reviews.comment: The class is not too difficult, but he curved our class down. His assignments  may have you do unrelated computer projects that are graded incredibly harshly. His schedule is inconsistent with the syllabus, and he does not reply to emails.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This course was both one of the least informative and yet easiest courses I have ever taken, not because the tests were reasonable but because the curves were incredibly high. Material we seldom ever worked with became the majority of the final, and everyone suffered because of it. He was also very insistent on getting positive feedback.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: While the course itself is laughably easy, in addition to the other reviews, he very blatantly used AI to create the last section of material, and has not bothered to remove the dead giveaways, often giving wrong explanations to students as a result. Although he has an impressive educational background, I'm skeptical of what he actually knows.

This professor flies by the seat of his pants, he changes the syllabus as he pleases, there is no consistency in expectations, he changes, adds, and drops major assignments all of the time and changed the weightings of exams midsemester. He is also vindictive, he asks for feedback and if you give any sense of negative feedback, he will get angry.

I had a 95% all throughout the semester doing well on both exams, he then proceeded to have us fill out a feedback form before the final, where he put content from classes not yet covered, so we all said he was great. He clearly wrote his own RMP when he was at Mizzou. If you like nuclear you need to tough it out for his classes. Good luck.

The content of the course is not that difficult. But professor Ibrahim's constant yelling and confusing analogies make simple concepts difficult to understand. You will need to dedicate a lot of time studying outside of class to succeed in this course. I remember feeling lost during his lectures.

Good lectures, but if you miss any class you are absolutely lost. Homework is hard but doable.Tests are much harder than homework and he takes pleasure in his own disappointment when his students have not practiced the niche topic he went over a month ago that has not come up again. He does not do notes well and there are no review guides or slides
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: BEST PROFESSOR EVER. I have never seen a professor care as much as Adel does. His office hours are the best ever. He makes the classes sooo interesting- I have never once spaced out in them. He cold calls to keep his students attention- and it works. His cold calling is not bad as you dont have to answer but just be more attentive. He is the best.

He is kind and funny. He made an effort to make us understand the concepts and learn a lot of things. 
He cared us with thinking about our busy schedules.

I will definitely take his class in Spring again.

Thank you Adel!!!!!! You are the best!

He is kind and funny.

I guess the reasons why his rate is low is because most of students who take 501 do not have accounting background. Therefore, sometimes his lecture is confused. However, if we get used to the accounting principles, we would be able to enjoy his class a lot.

I am taking his ACCY501 now.

When I first started taking his class, I was lost because of lack of my accounting knowledge.

However, when I ask him some questions, he teaches me well and responds me quickly.
Also, he sometimes gives us extra credit opportunities, and funny examples.

We have group pop quiz once a week. Overall, he is funny & kind!

The class was confusing and made no sense. Cold calls on students often. He tries hard to make examples to visualize concepts but ends up overcomplicating them in the end. You will struggle to memorize concepts just to pass the exam instead of actually understanding the concepts. Very helpful for homework help during office hours, though.

Ibrahim is an amazing professor! His lectures are very intense and really draining, but I swear he is there to teach you. The material is not easy, but he tries to make it as painless as possible. There's about one hw and in class exercise per week. Everything is open note. Not that bad, I swear!

His teaching style is better suited to textbook writing than explaining to students in that he delivers his knowledge in an extremely straightforward method that does little to help unearth additional understanding or provide greater context. Listening to this dry style over an extended period, it becomes difficult to latch onto the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Amazing lecture with tough tests.
Professor Adel Ibrahim is amazing.

He knows the material very well and really knows how to give lectures. Becaise of majority of accy lectures are boring, Adel will call students' names occasionally to keep their attention. 
He gives harder exams comparing with other sections to make sure that his students aware of materials the chapter. 
Adel will give curves base on percentile.

Worst professor ever. I'm an accounting undergraduate and this is the hardest accounting course I've taken. Homework/quizzes/peer review/group projects/lectures, the work load is excessive. Quizzes are way more difficult than they should be and he doesn't give enough time to complete. Would NEVER take a course from him again. Terrible experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Sink or swim. You must have a solid foundation in accounting and keep up with his lecture materials as he expects everyone to know it by the lecture date. Doesn't do a good job explaining concepts. He gets visibly frustrated and loud during lectures, then tries to justify his actions by saying he's passionate about accounting. Hard quizzes and test

Quizzes and exams were too long for the time allowed. Not unusual for class averages on quizzes to be 60%. It's not that material is difficult, it's that you don't have the time to complete the quizzes. I frequently ran out of time with one problem unfinished (20% of the quiz).Initially refused to provide specific feedback to groups on projects.

Lectures are okay but he prides himself on grading hard with a curve. The problem is that the quizzes are way too long for the time allotted so he basically tests speed rather than knowledge. So disappointing.

All material including slides and problems just taken from the intermediate accounting. Adel takes pride in setting very hard exams with the expectation of scaling. Again all test questions are taken from the book. This is the first ACCY course I have got less than an A. Often gets visibly angry/frustrated when the class doesnt get it.

Professor As could be a good teacher. He really knows the lesson. I think the problem with him is that he is not a good lecturer. He does not explain very well and the tone of his voice (really high like screaming) make it really hard to catch up what he is saying. So I will say he knows the lesson but dont explain it really well.
na

Adel is probably the best instructor I've ever had. 517 is one of the toughest ACCY courses, but it's worth putting in the time. I usually have a hard time paying attention in ACCY lectures, but Adel always kept my attention and definitely makes lecture worthwhile. He explains difficult concepts in ways that just make everything click.

Arguably the hardest college class taken alongside Fin 300. You'll learn a decent amount but there's a fine line between what he teaches and what he tests on.  Overall, it can make for a pretty miserable experience and I'd recommend avoiding this teacher whenever possible.
The way he teaches makes you doubt if what he said is real. Would better be a good actor rather than a teacher.

The professor is amazing and always willing to help students. The course seems to be difficult at the beginning, but spend time and efforts on it, and try to understand the logic. If you understand the logic and the way of thinking, the homework and exams won't be that hard. You'll really learn something useful from this class. Worth it!!

ACCY 517 & Adel are the hardest class I have ever taken, including FIN 300.  I have learned so much from ACCY 517 and it is the most beneficial class I have ever taken in my collegiate career.  He pours his passion into the subject, he slows the class down when students don't understand, he holds tons of office hours to help.
He's a really good professor, I don't usually say that I enjoyed a class, but I enjoyed Adel's teaching style and his lectures.

The class is tough, not because of the professor but the actual material. The class requires a lot of time and effort. If you don't want to put in the time and effort this isn't the class for you. What you walk away from the class is knowing how to analyze the FS of a company. A skill I will carry on with me for the rest of my life.
This professor doesn't seem to control his anger. He yelled at his students and I was always uncomfortable in his class
Worst professor ever!! Cant explain anything but ends up putting very difficult exams.. Never take any of his classes.. Screams in class all the time! Talks nonsense half of the time

He is the worst professor I've ever had. He can't explain things clearly, but always has high expectations towards students. I guess what he wants is just making the students fail at his tricky exam. He always spends half of the class time talking about nonsense. That's why he can't catch up the class schedule or finish the class on time.
terrible course. he truly loves tricks but can never explain things clearly. always make simple things extremely hard to understand. also, he YELLED all the time. HURT TO EARS!
unique style. really helpful, force students to think.
Worst teacher ever. Do not take any of his classes. Unorganized teacher, mean, can't teach any topic in accounting.
One of the best professors in accounting department.
Amazing !!
His style of teaching is better than most instructor. He prepares wonderful questions for exams and force students to think about what they know.
Talked down to the class, was disorganized, workload was inappropriate and unclear.
All her students dislike her all the way through the end of their degree or quit.
I don't understand the reviews here. She is such a sweet lecturer.
Very Bad Explanations
Cannot lecture

The professor herself is very nice, the lectures are meh. The problem is that the curriculum for 212 is so frustrating and so much harder than it needs to be that a lot of the time you're just lost unless you've had prior experience with the material. Stay ahead of each topic and self teach my friends

Overall she was a good professor, the materiel was well presented, however, there was a lot to cover so you have to go to lecture and do the homework's/practice exams if you want to learn it well/do well on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: The course is inherently hard. It is difficult to teach well, however her teaching wasn't anything special. Most of the learning is done on your own through prelecture and homework. She sometimes doesn't answer questions, but in an online setting with 100 people in the class, I can imagine it is difficult.

Prof. Sickles is a decent lecturer, but lectures only make sense if you watch the prelectures closely. Weekly homework problems are very time-consuming and midterms require a lot of studying to do well, though these are due to the course itself, not by any fault of the professor. If you put in the work, an A is attainable, but definitely not easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: While the physics department at UIUC generally has the same curriculum, Anne Sickles just doesnt teach it well. Her lectures are 95% iClicker questions with poor explanations, and the other 5% is random comments about the zoom chat. Not a good teacher. I had to watch other professors lectures to learn anything or teach myself. Very nice though.

Worst physics 212 professor at UIUC. Avoid her at all costs. I had an A-throughout the semester until the final. My section was forced to take it a week before the other kids, and these later sections were able to cheat. Overall, their average final exam grade much higher, but the phys department still wouldnt curve our section. Sucky situation.

Good at explaining physics concepts but not that great at answering questions. If you follow the pre-lectures closely, she will make perfect sense during lecture. She was not the course director so class-specific complaints were not her fault. She was a good professor and has fun stories from working at high-profile physics laboratories!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I don't know what all these people are talking about her tests being crazy hard, I got an A on each of the three midterms without too much preparation. Her lectures are a little light on actual content, so it's important to do the prelectures.

Maybe it has to do with online classes during the COVID-19 pandemic... maybe it has to do with the physics department. Either way, her class was horribly organized and she seemed to not know the answer to many of the students questions - the labs and discussion sections were also horrible.
Not a good lecturer

The physics department at UIUC does the same thing for each section, regardless of who is the teacher. Same HW, same lecture slides, same tests. However, Anne Sickles is by far the worse professor. She simply reads off the clicker questions, cannot explain ANYTHING, nor tries to. Her Exam 3 and Final exam is a full week before all other sections.

Her exam was terrifying. Yes, she told us to do the practice exams and everything will be fine. However it turns out none of the problem was even similar to that of homework or past papers. And what's ridiculous is when she tells you that you will be fine with the exam. Do not trust her words about exams.
Unorganized lecture and homework. Office hour helps little. She doesn't show any passion in teaching during lectures.
Boring lecture, just read Griffiths by your own. Exams and Homeworks difficulty are unpredictable and inconsistent.
Not a very good lecturer. Also the homework is wildly inconsistent in difficulty.
Unclear think flow throughout the lecture, concepts not explained with enough examples.

I found her explanations of problems unclear and hard to follow. All she did was read off the lecture slides that were made for her by the flipitphysics company. She didn't seem to care that much about teaching. Office hours were not very helpful, I would not recommend her.
Lectures are fairly clear, only complaint is that she'll interrupt lecture to tell the one kid in the back of the lecture hall to get off his/her phone/laptop. Tests are similar to homework problems.

Definitely take a class with prof Ali if you can! Super easy grader and responds to emails fairly quickly. There was a bit on an issue taking the final this semester with ProctorU and he got all of us refunds! Very kind person who wants you to grow :)

This class was sick. He literally just has what I assume to be a few bullet points and then talks for 1.5 hours. No prepared presentation, no white board, no nothing. He's really well articulated on what he talks about. I never did any of the readings and got nearly 100% on all the tests. Be engaged and raise your hand in class and you'll do well

Professor Ali is one of the most amazing professors that I've ever learned from. His classes are not structured around exams or homework; instead, they're about actually learning something and enjoying your time in his class. Be prepared to show up to all lectures, but he's such a knowledgeable and charismatic instructor that this won't be hard.

The class was very simple and easy. No homework other than the given readings but weren't really necessary. Tests were very easy if you went to the review session. Attendance is mandatory as the class is based on the discussions and questions presented in class.

If you have the opportunity to take a class with Ali, do it. He emphasizes that he wants you to leave the class having learned something and grown as a person, the grade is just a university requirement. Easy A class. This class is a lot of deep talks about the soul and reaching your highest self. Classes are always discussion based.

He does a great job in making what he teaches useful to everyone, you can tell he is engaged and passionate in whatever class he teaches. As long as you show up to class, pay attention and take notes you will be fine.

Dr. Ali is always ready to go into a deep conversation. He based his class question and answer style. Take notes and be focused. Highly recommended to take this class. Read and be prepared to ask questions.
Average and condescending. I'm not sure if she has the self-awareness to know that.

Prof. Kou is a pretty good lecturer imo. While she doesn't go beyond the syllabus, her lectures are straightforward and well structured. If you go to her office hours or ask questions after class, she answers them really well. The course itself is extensive, but that isn't something she can do anything about.

"ok" is the first word I can think of to describe. I went to Paul Kwiat's lectures a couple of times and he was more enthusiastic about the material and had more understandable explanations. Professor Kou does her job though. She takes time to try and explain the slides and does cool demos, but I would not say she's likely the best option.
She was alright.  Definitely have to teach yourself outside of class but that's pretty normal.  Just did clicker questions or demos in class and didn't learn much in the lecture. Kind of boring.
Actually, I don't think she is so bad. At least, her design of the course helps me easily pass the course as long as you can figure out the homework problems by yourself.
Prof. Kou not teach us anything, all we did in lecture was iclickers. Lectures are required though. Be prepared to learn everything outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I hate her and all the phys 212 course staff. Stupid style of examination allows for no partial credit and all or none. No wonder everyone takes this class over summer
Mid lecturer. The final was BS, and that could be an understatement

There was no real teaching that went on in her class. Students are expected to learn the material on their own and the lecture is just a place where misunderstandings can be corrected. She misspeaks so often during lecture that it makes her explanations hard to follow. I relied entirely on the textbook to get through this class.

Lectures are so stupid. They are the exact same thing as the prefectures however attendance is graded so you have to attend them anyway. I found myself either sitting through the entire lecture on my phone or would skip through the whole prelecture then take notes in class, it's redundant to have both. She's a fine lecturer but nothing outstanding.
Sometimes it felt like there was a disconnect between prelectures and lecture. Most learning comes from prelectures and discussions. The actual lecture period doesn't feel very productive

Lectures are just re-reading slides from checkpoints and prelectures. Answers to students questions are regurgitated from the formulas given on these slides and little input is given on how to conceptually grasp the material, which is particularly important in this class. Angela is nice and loves physics, but the lectures are mind-numbingly boring.
If you do the prelectures and checkpoints, the lectures become much easier to understand. Professor Kou was good at answering questions and the demos were interesting.

Does not have any idea of how to explain concepts effectively, nor any idea of how to listen and try to understand students' questions, nor any idea of how to pace the lectures so that we can spend time on important things that are harder to grasp. Monotone. Do not choose her lecture section, it's on a totally different level than Stelzer's.
He is a fantastic teacher and gives awe-inspiring lectures. He only gives the notes if you ask for them in class. He loves questions and is an incredible mentor.

Don't ask any questions regarding math concepts you would've learned in calc 2/3 or diff eq, otherwise you will be yelled at. Questions about concepts or anything else related to the class have to be as specific as you can make it. He doesn't decide what % corresponds with what letter grade, so the midterm averages were mid 40s. Have fun...

Avoid taking any important classes with this guy. He is unreasonably mean and irritable, and will personally memorize and form an opinion about every student in the class. Exam distributions range from 0-100 and are awfully skewed. Even with perfect lecture attendance, good luck scoring above a 60. There is no clear cutoffs either, he just picks.

No lecture notes u gotta take you're notes
Exams are extremely and unreasonably difficult
The guy is unreasonable, if u try and ask a question get ready for a grilling session
No notes sheet allowed for tests which is crazy for fluid mechanics
If the class average is a 40, somethings gotta be wrong with this style of teaching

He's a genius but not a nice guy. Gets to know everyone who regularly attends lecture by name, so make sure you show up. At the end of the class he ranks everyone by of total points in the class, and then he draws the line arbitrarily between who gets what letter grade based on if he thinks you deserve it. Don't take this class if you don't have to

This guy has no joy in his life. Lectures are terrible and boring, he's mean and arrogant, and expects you to know as much as he does. Old school lectures (chalk and blackboard) probably because he has no idea how to use a computer. This guy is not with the times at all. Grading is insanely hard but luckily there is a curve. Stay away

Pearlstein is not your typical professor at UIUC. But that's for the best, as he demands understanding of the material while also supplying thorough lectures. Homeworks are tough and that was the biggest annoyance of the class, but they aren't weighted for much and office hours are helpful. Take him if you really want to learn the material
Amazing Professor
I hate this dude. Heard a lot of strange allegations about him too. School should look into it more. Arne is not a saint

Does not use or post lecture notes and regularly makes fun of students who ask questions in class. Never makes it clear what content will be on exams and does not elaborate on grading. Does not care if his students learn the material, as his goal for exam scores is a normal distribution around 50%. I would not wish his class on my worst enemies.

Obviously very smart, but is an extremely intimidating person. Yells at students for asking pretty valid questions. Lectures are just derivations of equations that have very little practical use and homework is very different to lectures.

This man is literally the bane of my existence. It's like the anti-Christ has descended unto humanity and his name was Arne Pearlstein. He's very knowledgeable in his field, and does a lot of his lecture form memory. Conversely, he does not upload any lecture notes, nor does he work from a note sheet that people can follow.

High expectations from students. Killer exams, but his lectures are original and unique. He knows the material and can talk at length and in detail without great preparation. Lectures supplement reading rather than repeat it. Strict, somewhat intimidating, and does not take BS. Demanding professor, but best classes I've taken have been with him.

Great teacher and knows his subject(s). Committed to helping students understand the material. For ex., he created homework questions (pretty difficult) and the exams were a direct reflection of the homework. Also organized help sections for the homeworks.. But, yeah, he can be intimidating. And he expects you to know what was taught in the lecture
Notorious for being the toughest professor in the university. Talk in his class and get kicked out regardless of whether it deals with the class.
Writes an essay on the board during each lecture. NO correlation to the book material. Expects a class full of students such as Mr. Meyers. Test bends you over. Class is Class Impossible.

Different than most professors. Makes the course more about understand why things are the way they are, rather than just having you learn it to test it. Learned more in his class than any other. Go to office hours, if he sees you've made an honest effort on the hw and you ask intelligent questions, he will walk you through them.
Don't miss class and ask plenty of questions. Office hours are a must, extremely helpful if he believes you are trying. Very tough but certainly doable.

He makes exams very difficult, but definitely do-able. Be a good student, go to class, and most importantly, show him you care about the material and he is a very helpful. People say avoid him, but I learned more about problem solving and engineering than I would in any other class. He only expects out of you what you can accomplish. Don't be lazy

This professor is infamous among ME majors at UIUC for being the worst professor you'll ever take.  Bad at teaching (spent 2 lectures canceling units on one problem), bad at helping (yells at students and even other professors), bad at writing exams(average on the first exam was 28%).  Avoid at all cost!

Comments said he is too harsh while I should say the comments are way too harsh. Pearlstein is a very patient guy who would explain something you don't understand many times. Exams are hard but not impossible. I was worried when I saw the comments before taking this class but it turned out that the class is the best one I've taken so far.

Great professor, lectures are clear, homework questions are useful and helpful for exams. Exams are not that hard as long as you go to the lectures and do the homework. He also held question sessions weekly which are really helpful. He welcomes questions through email and answers them quite decently. Uses blackboard a lot. Like his way of teaching.

Everyone will tell you to not take a class with this professor, however he ties everything he teaches into the real world industry. He has more office hours per week than all my professors combined and yes, he WILL make fun of you for mistakes, but suck it up, you are there to ask questions. Exams are difficult, but definitely possible.

He's a grumpy old man that clearly does not know how to teach. The lectures are extremely boring. He wastes tons of time doing arithmetic rather than helping people understand the big picture. Frankly, he is quite an unpleasant person. Make a point to not take this class with him, as he turns it into a much bigger ordeal than it should be.

He prepares for this class the least of any professor I have ever had. He wastes so much time in class going over extended examples (which he does on the fly) that are not relevant at all to the material. He also gets angry very easily and is an overall unpleasant individual. NO PEOPLE SKILLS.

Class is extremely difficult and professor is definitely that stereotypical genius professor.  That being said office hours can be very helpful and he does offer lots of office hours/question sessions.  Definitely expect to work very hard in this class.

Where do I start? Worst class I have ever taken.  Going to class does not help, doing the homework does not help, going to office hours does not help... nothing helps. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HIM. He gave 4 people a 2 out of 100 on a midterm and said there wasn't going to be a curve because someone got 100.
DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS!

If you like to learn by having someone talk in front of you for 50 mins and write down every single word that comes out of his mouth then this may be the professor for you. We rarely did example problems. Exam are hard but he says there is a huge curve. He really makes the test so someone as smart as him will ace it
He talks straight into the board and the tests are extremely difficult.  As he puts it if you know the information (and by that he means to his level of knowledge) then you will pass.

Avoid taking any class taught by this man. He's your typical genius, arrogant professor that expects everyone else to be inherently brilliant. He seriously talks straight into the chalkboard for 50 minutes, and writes down every single word he says. Honestly, he is my worst professor throughout college thus far.
I was getting a B+ until he gave me a 0 on the third test because I was in the hospital with a 105 degree temperature!  He did not let me make it up and he gave me an F in the class.  He is a POS.

People would tell you to avoid taking him, but I think he was a good teacher. The avg on the tests are very low, but if you at least work through most of the problem, you'd curve pretty high. Though he'd take 3 weeks to  grade your exam, his trying to make sure he's fair in grading. His notes are good, help very much for exams. Gives extra off.hrs.
Grades harshly, does not help, going to class is worthless

The average on our two exams that he graded were 37 and 58. did not explain how his weighting system works. Funny at times, but sometimes unbelievably harsh. Laughs at some questions (mockingly which does not give a vote of confidence). At one office hour, went on tangent about units.Grading on homework is sick: +1 if it's correct -5 if incorrect.
dont expect to go to office hours for help, unless you want to be yelled at and called stupid!  this guy is a freak!
comes off as a hardass but in the end is a good guy.  its funny when he laughs at stupid questions too
No Comments
Brilliant professor and expects a lot from his students.
he once yelled at this kid for coughing too much.
No Comments

I'm not even sure why this guy is teaching the course, Besides the fact that he's basically just a text-to-speech for the lecture slides, he writes quizzes and exams that can't even be finished on time.

Ivan Abraham appears unprepared to effectively teach this course. Lectures are largely limited to reading slides, and the instructor often struggles to answer student questions. He's very disorganized with his teaching. It's disappointing because I love this course but I have to settle for this mediocre instructor.
He can leave you feeling super confused... not 'coz you aint smart enough to grasp..  He simply can't impart knowledge effectively. Spins fancy words, rambles, hardly gets to the point. Zzzzz

Nice lecturer. Slides/talks are often deadpan, sometimes witty. Does live coding in class. Knowledgeable, sometimes geeks out on advanced material zzzz. Exams are straightforward but weird: tests in a programming class are paper-based.
no idea how to run a class. his slide annotations are incomprehensible, and all his lectures inevitably end in a tailspin

I'd give him above average because at least he has slides. The first guy didn't even bother! However, he NEVER finishes a whole slide deck and the lecture predictably goes into a tail spin in the last 20 minutes with him rushing it.


complete mess | disorganized | rambling | chaotic | disjointed | unfocused | zero logic | Even the professor seemed lost in his own explanations

Abraham does a nice job lecturing on course material, though his accent is heavy. Slide are detailed, delivery is well paced with pauses and questions to check our understanding, often with a dry style of humor. Like other reviews mention, course could be structured better/different as it is too test heavy but that isn't in his control AFAIK.
well well well well
He communicates very well in lectures and has a humorous presentation style. Even through 80 minutes of lecture time he continues to keeps students engaged and learning

ECE 220 was structured very poorly. A significant portion of the exams are conceptual, but the lectures do not cover conceptual concepts enough. The homework is no help either, since it's only programming. During lectures, he would quickly type code on the screen with very little explanation, but expect you to immediately understand everything.

Terrible lecturer. He goes way to fast without explaining a topic well or doing examples with it. The majority of this class is just learning the material in discussions or outside of class. He spends more time going over proofs that you will ever use instead of showing us how the material is helpful at all.

ECE 220 Was not a well structured class , tests were made very difficult and tested on portion that was not taught in the course. Tests accounted to 65 percent of the course grade. Material was poorly structured.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class was managed quite unprofessionally. The professor did not provide any support to students and would neglect student concerns. He looked down upon students who who didn't understand course content. In classes, instead of explaining things sequentially, he would simply type the code and expect everyone to follow what he is saying.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pretty good lecturer
I walked into class and he threw a textbook at me, he then said I deserved it.
Please whatever you do, do not take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Loved Adam! Made me feel very welcome as a new education student! I hope I get the pleausre of taking one of his classes again!
He is the best professor I have ever worked with. Definitely recommended.

Adam is the best UIUC can get. I had for both EDUC 101 and 102 (the honors freshmen seminar) and not only was he a great speaker and lecturer, but just overall great person. Really get to know him - and he'll remember you. 101 was kind of a lot of meh busy work that got old, but if you put in just slight effort you'll get an A. Thanks Adam!

Adam is so cool. He's great at explaining things and always has a great answer if I have a question. I learned more from this course about how to teach math than any other course honestly. The work load isn't bad and he gives extensions if needed. He's the best!
Adam is the best! He is really funny and makes lectures very enjoyable. We learned about all of UIUC's resources. As long as you do the work you will get an A.
Amazing, he's such a great understanding teacher! Made me very excited to learn math again.

I felt so confident leaving this class. We used manipulatives and it felt like we were back learning as students which was something that was so beneficial towards using teaching skills. Grading was always on time, and I looked forward to his classes weekly.
Really good professor for EDUC 101! We learned a lot of the resources that UIUC has. As long as you do the work you'll get an A. He offers extra credit!
Adam was a great professor. We had weekly group homework and other than that just show up to class and know the material for the tests (midterm+final only) and you can easily earn an A.
Adam is an amazing professor. He really cares about the subject and his students. He is always nice, friendly, and supportive. Ive learned so much in his class.

Adam is the best professor I have had at the University of Illinois. You can tell that he actually cares about his students' learning and their lives in general. He is well prepared for classes and gives excellent feedback. He does an exceptional job modeling good teaching. Wonderful teacher, better man.

If you have an option to, take a class with Adam. He is a fantastic educator who really cares about his students and wants them to learn. If you're in a smaller setting with him, he will take the time to get to know you, which is really unique. He assigns homework and his classes aren't easy, but it's worth it.

He's the best!!!! I don't think I've ever had a nicer teacher.  Class is from 8-11 AM, BUT there's so much group workparticipation that the time usually goes by quickly.  The class is a lot of work but not hard!After like 1 week, you feel like you've known him forever.   Had us over for a BBQ at his house at the end of the year also which was fun!

Get Adam any way possible! (section M2) He is smart, kind, intelligent, and helpful. His classes are very educational, organized, and up to date. I highly recommend him. Do your work and you should get an A.

All the other reviews are making psyc 220 worse than it is. As long as you show up to lecture, take notes, and go to his office hours if you need extra help, you will do well on the exams and get an easy A. He is very fair with grading and will remove questions or give free points if he sees majority of the class got the question incorrect on exams
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Genuinely such a kind and caring professor. Really cares about the material and knows how to keep everyone engaged. Even though it was a 3 hour class I can say I learned quite a lot of material and retained a good portion of it. Made the final optional and the midterm was exactly all of the material he had on the review. Easiest 400 level midterm.

Professor Steel was able to make a 2 and a half hour class fly by with his interesting and engaging lectures. He is extremely kind and you can tell he really cares about his students. The exams accurately reflected the information on the study guide as well.

I found this class to be one of the most enjoyable ones I took this semester. Any material found within the lectures is on the exams, and the weekly quizzes do genuinely help in understanding the material. He is a very accommodating professor who genuinely cares about the success and well-being of his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Won't make exceptions for anything, even if your mom passes away!
He is the best professor I've ever had in my life. He really cares about the material and is very knowledgable.
Very nice guy but class is crazy difficult for a 200-level, like what everyone else is saying. But the first lecture is the hardest it does get somewhat easier.

Prof Steel is AMAZING he's very kind and easy to talk to. The exams were not hard at all and reflected the material on the study guide. Quizzes after lecture but semi easy. Very accessible via email and seems like he cares for his students!

Prof. Steel (while a very nice person) has no idea what an appropriate difficulty is for a 200 level class. For the first exam, no study guide was provided. He did provide one on the next exam, but it did not reflect the content on the exam. Lectures are too fast paced, and are disorganized. Prepare to learn everything outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: For a 200 level class, the content is CRAZY hard. Last semester I studied for 23 hours total in preparation for the first exam, and I still felt like I knew nothing on the exam. I still wonder how I made it out with an A. Lectures are incoherent (for beginners) and it's very unclear what you should be focusing on. Overall wouldn't recommend

He doesn't know how to teach. Exams test details that he goes over in 2sec+expects you knowledged on topics but he goes over them FAST and does not post/ record
slides before class. claims exam is based on study sesh but questions are 90% not on study guide. class too hard for 200. Prof unhelpful. Do not take if you dont have 100 extra hrs to study
Professor is really nice and funny but lectures a little pointless. Very easy to get lost during lecture because he goes fast, but just practice the review/homework, it'll be more beneficial.
Best lecturer in maths, only time in college I'd readily attend an attendance non-mandatory lecture.

Super funny, very chill and understanding. Practice exams prepare you super well for actual exams. Quizzes and homeworks are useful. Make sure to practice actually solving the problems along with the lecture notes instead of just looking at them.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's a very engaging lecturer and a pretty funny guy. Class isn't required but go otherwise you will get lost and fall behind. Exams were always fair and pretty simple compared to the in-class examples he'd give. The grading scale is also very nice since a 90 is the cutoff for an A. Definitely an easier class that any calculus class.
The exams are pretty nice and his lectures are somewhat engaging.

Exams are relatively straightforward, and the class weighting makes getting an A very manageable. Lectures can be worth going to, but they get confusing if you get lost in the first few minutes. However, Professor Manfroi is really nice and does his best to help if you ask.

Exams are on the easier side, and the grading is forgiving too; +90 is considered an A.

Lectures aren't mandatory, and not very helpful, in my experience. He solves multiple questions instead of explaining concepts, if you learn well in these conditions you should go. His handwriting is quite hard to read.

Cool guy in general though. GOAT

I think for this semester the tests gotten harder, because before people said it was really easy. I still have an A but haven't gotten a single A on any of the midterms. He is a great professor and is really nice, but some of the harder material does show up on the test and can be confusing sometimes, especially the multiple choice questions.

This class is relatively easy, I wish I could tell you more about this professor but I stopped going to lecture after the first test and only really looked at his notes to study. His tests are super easy and concepts are interesting. Light at the end of the tunnel after all the calc classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Goes quite fast in lectures, handwriting is often illegible (he jokes about it all the time). Exams and HW is easy, grading is easy but lectures can be long-winded and go over unnecessary stuff that is confusing.
He was an easy professor for sure, but did a poor time using lecture to get the information across. Stopped going to class after the second exam. He just does not know how to explain stuff.
His lectures are fast an sometimes it doesn't make sense but once you do the homework its very straight forward, come early to class and ask to play songpop!
He's pretty good at explaining stuff, and he's laidback and funny. Lectures are a bit quick though so it can be hard to keep up sometimes.

Don't get me wrong, this professor is very nice. He ends lectures early and the course is pretty easy. However, I didn't learn much from this class. It seems like he is lecturing b/c it's his job rather than b/c he loves it. If you just want a good grade for this class, he's great. If you want to actually learn something from this class, beware.

Don't get me wrong, I love Professor Manfroi, but not god as everyone tells. He's chill, lectures are clear and usually end early. My only thing is he has shorter and fewer office hours, and he's kind of fast through lectures but the exams are fair and he is great at explaining the content. Also be versed on music since we play song pop party

Manfroi made a class I expected to be difficult understandable in an engaging way. Be prepared to not be able to read his handwriting, but it's okay because he teaches at a very manageable pace. He can fit all the lecture info into his class without rushing anything, and class typically ends 5-10 minutes early. Highly recommend for DiffEQ.

He is not godlike as some suggest, but considerably better than other math professors here. Lectures can be helpful but I live by the textbook and practice tests, which I found to be more useful. Definitely pick him if you have a choice and plan on going to lecture
He's literally the best math professor I've had at this school.

Aldo is very good at balck board lecture teaching and will provide a lot of similar questions for a concept to make sure you understand the concept. He usually will end his lecture 10 mins early but the content is a lot.

absolute goat. love the grading scale (90.01 is an A). lectures could get a bit boring since he'd sometimes show the longwinded proofs, but he also is pretty hilarious and knows how to lighten the mood. he usually lets out early, but late afternoon classes finish even faster since he speeds through content, so take him early in the day
Great professor

If you have a disability he won't accomodate you unless you give him the accomodation letter early as hell so if it's causing you a bad grade and you don't figure that out until late you're screwed. Additionally, the website he uses for his tests are awful and cause you to loose a lot of points for no reason and his quizzes are very unforgiving

Professor Manfroi is AMAZING. This man can explain all the concepts with ease and make them more understandable than the textbook. He is also really funny and does small talk lecture (AMERICA'S CUP!!!). This makes the whole class a very entertaining and easy experience, but it still feels like we learn a lot. Best professor I've had at UIUC!
Best math professor I have ever had.
Greatest math teacher I have ever had. Homework is infrequent and extremely easy, almost impossible to fail this class. Also has amazing hair.
great professor, definitely recommend.

Prof Manfroi is extremely kind and caring and he makes his class so chill- he literally asks bonus questions about movies on his exams. Lectures weren't required but I went to every class willingly and took notes (only class I ever took notes in) because he was so engaging and passionate.

He was good, but bro literally didn't care about the math. It seemed like he just came in to teach, and not because he actually really cared. Also, the grading is pretty easy - the class could easily has a C or D average if he wants, so that is goated of him. But overall he just comes in to teach, not to actually be passionate about math.

I love Aldo. The course itself is not very hard. His lectures are informational. Although not mandatory, I still highly recommend u to attend. Not too much homework and homework is strongly related to exams. Only 90 points, u can get an A. Cheers!

Absolutely the best professor I have in Math department, much better experience than Linear Algebra with someone else. Practice tests and homework are really helpful for exam, and the lectures are great and clear with little efforts to fully understand the materials, and Prof. Manfroi is very chill and caring to his students.

"How's it going?" he asks us multiple times per lecture.
Very chill and laid back professor, always cracking jokes.
Everyone else seems to find this class very easy and get A's.

Manfroi wants you to do well and structures the class toward that. His lectures were clear, helpful, and understandable (until the last few weeks), and the homework reflected lecture and test material. Generous practice tests, grading, and test drop policy. Everything is also available online, but in-person lectures were more helpful.
Best professor ever!!! His practice tests were similar to exams. Super fair and kind!!
Best math professor ever. Never drop a single lecture of his.

Prof. Manfroi is great, he clearly cares about his students and structures the course in a way where almost anyone can succeed. I will say lectures were sometimes difficult to follow especially while taking notes at the same time, but with the help of examples, things usually became clearer.
Manfroi is really chill and makes the class feel fun instead of stressful. Also, the tests are easy As as long as you do the homework and pay attention in lectures.

Professor Manfroi kept a light atmosphere during his lectures but still kept them highly educational. He was always joyful and explained everything clearly. The class is much easier than Calculus at UIUC and is not a lot of work. The exams matched pretty well what was on the practice exams.

He's funny and carefree. In all his midterms he includes a movie question from a movie that students vote to watch. His lectures are very concise, clear, and to the point and he seems to not even put much effort into lecturing. Nearly all his lectures end early. He's just a talented lecturer and he tries to interact and connect with the students.
Use the note he give, that is really helpful.

So overrated. Lectures are very...ok? They're not bad but definitely not that great.  He seemed uninterested whenever I went to office hours for extra help, and was not that considerate about extensions. Class is soooo badly structured which made it hard to stay on top of work. Exams were super easy. But I've had way better math profs.

Funny quirky man. Explains things well (though with questionable handwriting). Textbook isn't mandatory but he gives you a PDF for the one he wrote for free. Just show up to lecture and take notes, you'll be fine.
Aldo is a great professor who is also very funny. Love him!
Take his class
one of the best professor, the exams were really similar to the practice ones
He's such a great professor and super funny. The class itself is not terrible and has plenty of practice exams provided, and the homework helps.

Professor Manfroi's lectures are very clear on what material needs to be known, and is very funny and friendly while answering questions. All exams on this class are on prairielearn and are very similar to the practice exams. If one puts in even some effort into this class while attending lectures, they should expect to do well.

Funniest prof at UIUC. You can tell that he loves to teach Diff Eq and he always comes to lecture very prepared. He will typically let you out of lecture 5 minutes early too. He will also try and make sure that he is entertaining, which is very good for a class that many majors have to take.

Second time having Manfroi, loved him both times. Very caring and smart professor. He'll periodically stop throughout his lectures just to make sure everybody's doing okay. Amazing person, best math professor

I can't give any other input that hasn't been given already. You can already see how goated this man is by the other reviews. Differential Equations with Manfroi was the most enjoyable math class I've had in college. I'm not great at math but I will likely end with an A (hopefully). Even if I don't, doesn't matter. He made the class great. 10/10.

My favorite professor this semester.  He was hilarious and was great at making the more difficult content digestible.  The tests aren't too difficult, they test for knowledge rather than try to trick you.  It's still a good idea to find extra practice problems on your own though.

The best professor in Math 241.  Highly recommended. The class is a lot of fun and the exams are not difficult if you understand all concepts and do the practice exams. I got 100 in the final and learned a lot from his class.
Profesor Manfroi saved my life from Math241.

I love having Manfroi so much that I also took Math 285 with him this semester. He is concise and I enjoy his lecture style. He really wants students to reach out and ask questions. He creates an inclusive environment for female students, which doesn't always happen in engineering classes.

Very good professor. He went a little fast during lectures but if you do the homework most of what he says will make sense. However, there are quite a few homework assignments and worksheets throughout the semester. Overall, he was pretty funny and seems to care that his students understand what he is teaching.

Absolutely amazing professor. Very engaging with his students and ready to answer any questions they may have. He was able to teach what should have been pretty hard material in an easily accessible and easy-to-learn way.
He was great. Made calc 3 so easy to understand and his tests are very fair.

I had Manfroi for Math241 and very much enjoyed it. His lectures are clear and his humor definitely livened up the atmosphere. Tests were straightforward so if you payed attention in lecture you were fine. 

At the time of this post his rating is a 5.3 out of 5.0 for some reason, but that is definitely a lowball compared to what he deserves.
Greatest math prof ever, it had been a while since I had taken any math before I took 241 so it was a little rough but he made it bearable.
The best professor I had!

dawg how this guy have a 5.1

good prof tho, would recommend

Manfroi grades as anything above a 90.00 is a flat A. His lectures are clear in terms of content, but if you don't sit close it is sometimes difficult to hear him. His tests are very reasonable and similar to past exams and practice exams. His sense of humor helps the class move along. Definitely take him if able, far easier than other 241 profs.
Funniest professor I had this semester. Very fair exams with straight-forward questions. Love this man so much.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Manfroi was a really good lecturer. Everything in the course could've been learned well via textbook, but his lectures were good enough that many including myself kept attending. I wish my grade was higher. I just suck at math. A's were 100% obtainable. Enjoyed enough to take Math 285 last semester.
Manfroi is the man! Enough said.

Extremely good professor. Everything he asks on exams is extremely reasonable and there are never any surprises. Very clear what you need to know. Lectures were decently engaging and all topics were explained well. He's a really funny guy and definitely cares about his students. My one complaint is that midterms were sometimes overly short

Loved having Manfroi for calc 3. He's really funny and cares that his students understand his lectures. His tests are extremely reasonable. The practice tests he gives are very similar to his exams and if you fully understand them you should be set for the exam. I'm already signed up for math 285 with him next semester.
Really funny guy. Helped me like math again after taking CALC 2. Really the tests were straight forward and for the most part didn't ask ridiculous questions.

the practice exams were so similar to the actual midterms, and manfroi was easy to approach and explained the material in a great way! way better than calc 2 if you had a bad experience with that, because so did i

Great professor. He provides a ton of resources to be successful. The practice exams he gives help u understand the types of questions he asks, and the actual exams are even a little easier. One exam gets dropped which is also nice

While Calculus III is not an easy class in general, Professor Manfroi did a great job teaching the complicated concepts in a digestible way. He was always willing to answer questions during class and office hours and while the class was a heavy workload (lots of homework, five exams, etc.) he always brought humor to his lectures.
I love manfroi's exams as they are quite easy to prepare for.
Amazing, 100/10!! Highly recommend, made calc3 the easiest and funnest class I've ever taken. Please take him if you have a chance and go to lectures!

Prof. Manfroi is a good professor and does a good job during lectures introducing the concepts, however, sometimes it can be a little dry and oftentimes not super engaging. The tests have MCQs each worth 5 points each so basically if you miss 2 MCQ problems but get everything else correct, you are down to somewhere in the B range on the test

Calc 3 is hard... In my opinion much harder than Calc 2. You have to memorize a TON of stuff. There is SO much material. Many times you will stare at a problem and not know even how to start it. Take this class over the summer at a community college if you can. I would avoid this class if possible. If you can't, find another professor. Good luck

He does not teach many things, and he only cares about the most basic part. If you want to learn something and  get a deep understanding of calculus, do not go to his course. Plus, there are only 8 problems in the exam so you may loss a lot of points if you just make a tiny mistake.

This man must have changed. For calc 3 he is terrible. His accent is hard to understand, and he is very quiet in lectures, very difficult to hear. He only lectures the easy examples. His midterms are very unfair. Our first one was very long for only 50 minutes. Our second one was shortened, so 1 wrong is a B. Unfair. AVOID this guy like the plauge.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Manfroi is AMAZING. If he's an option for a class PICK HIM. Manfroi was able to make an online asynchronous class fun and engaging. No other professor I had in the Frankenstein of a school year that was 2020-2021 even came close to being able to engage people with their lecture videos as well as Manfroi did.

Even though I had him asynchronously he made the experience still fun. The way he constructed the class made sure you would get an A if you did the given work. He was very approachable and accommodating during office hours. I wish I had him either online or in person because even reading some of his answers on Campuswire was just fun.
Amazing professor for DiffEQ, his lectures are in-depth he really knows how to explain the content.

Not even done with his class yet and I am writing a review. Best professor I've had thus far. Teaches the material so well AND he kept his lecture videos minimally short! Not alot of homework, but enough so that you understand what you're doing. Thought this class would be hard. It's not. Exams are very simple. If you're taking 285/86 TAKE MANFROI!
He was always funny and did a great job to stay connected with students during these tough times. Overall enjoyed the class and I am really not a fan of straight math! Awesome professor!

Online lectures were concise and full of example problems to prepare us for the exams. More importantly, this man Rickrolled us while learning about the Fourier series, so he definitely has my approval.

This professor made learning differential equations a breeze! In fact, I barely remember referencing the textbook at all, that's how clear his lecture videos are! I'll definitely try to take more of his classes in the future.
I ABSOLUTELY LOVE PROFESSOR MANFROI!! This dude is hilarious. He also doesn't assign more homework than you need to understand the problems. Will definitely be taking his classes in the future.
Amazing professor. Lectures are concise, specific and really well thought out. Exams and homework are really based on the lectures so that's a huge plus!
Great professor all around, highly recommend.
this guy is just amazing
Attend lecture and do the readings before-hand. Amazing guy
This guy is amazing. 90% of the time he finishes early and lets you leave, but you almost don't want to because this guy is so fun to listen to.
Honestly the best math professor I've had at UIUC. He's super chill and balances the material with humor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I honestly can't think of anything about this class I didn't like. He balances a chill environment with actually teaching the material well. Plenty of opportunities to ask questions are given and he genuinely cares that people understand. His office hours are flexible and he lets students set up appointments if they can't make the set hours.
The best professor I've had at UIUC.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: He is the great professor on this land. He is hilarious and loves all of his students and makes the class really enjoyable and easy. His practice exams are the only thing you need to get an A in the class
Definitely need to show up for class, but he let us out early 90% of the time. He is hilarious and I would take him again for sure.

I had the worst time with this professor for 115.  He was never organized, terrible at explaining and he made up his own math problems! sometimes the solutions wouldn't make sense and it didn't help me learn the material at all.  I spent the entire semester teaching myself from the book.  AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS
Loves students and enjoys answering questions and listening to us
Manfroi is great. He's funny, understanding, and explains things very clearly. His grading scale is also very lenient. Definitely take diffeq with him if you have the opportunity.

Aldo is a wonderful person. He makes office hours if you cannot make his. He is entertaining, friendly, and caring. however, his lectures are too fast and he writes lightly. It is very hard to follow along. His practice exams are honestly the only thing I used to study for exams.

Professor Manfroi is one of the best professors I've come across at UIUC.  He not only is a great teacher, but he also has an amazing sense of humor.  He is very organized when teaching and his explanations are quite clear.  I honestly don't think there is a better choice for a MATH285 professor than Manfroi.  He is extremely helpful.  Take him!!!

This is my first  semester on this campus. It is so great to have a considerate professor like him. He is easy-going, which means you can talk about things beyond math with him. His lecture is helpful and clear. He will teach some useful little tricks which are very important in Math. He is considerate, but you have to learn your own things.

This can be a challenging class but if you attend his lectures regularly, take notes during his exam review sessions and do the practice exams, you will do great in his class. He sticks to the textbook most of the time. First midterm was a breeze but then they do get progressively harder. The final was comprehensive but doable.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Its not as easy as people say it is. First exam is just like the practice (Easy A). For the next 3, pay close attention and remember the tricks. I would do the practice exams as well as reviewing the homeworks as I'd say I usually did worse than I thought I would just doing the practice exams. Small mistakes will cost you a lot of points!!

Most engaging teacher I have taken at U of I. He loves his students, he loves to teach, and he makes sure everyone is on the same page. He gives many opportunities to get a good grade in his course. If you are taking math 285, take Manfroi without a doubt.
Great, engaging professor. One of the best math teachers at UIUC.
He is, by far, the man for MATH 285.

By far the number one professor you want for Math 285. Tests are super simple if you go to lecture (no tricks, very straightforward.) Also, he is a cool guy who tries to make dull material not to boring, and you can definitely tell he knows the material inside and out. TAKE 285 WITH HIM!

285 is not difficult conceptually but the way the tests are setup it's easy to lose a substantial amount of points over simple mistakes. If I could do it again I probably would have taken it with a different teacher - I'm better at beating a curve than I am at not making algebra mistakes. Nice guy though.

Easiest Math I have ever taken in U of I. 3 midterms 1 final, the worst one will be 5%. Tests are same as the homework, as long as you do the homework, which is worth 20%, you will be ready for the test. I will take his class again if he offers any other classes.

Best math professor I've had at U of I.  Very clear explanations and examples with some humor thrown in.  The material isn't the easiest, but do the recommended hw and practice exams and you should do well.  Worst exam is only 5% of your grade, so if you do well on the 3 midterms you won't have to take the final.
He is super good. He explains super well and is pretty funny. Make sure to take good notes during class because he goes through all the types of problems you need to know very carefully.
He's got a kinda weird accent but it's not that bad. The class really isn't that easy but if you do well enough on the midterms the final is only worth 5% so it's basically a joke.
Manfroi is funny and makes dif eq, not the most exciting thing in the world, an interesting enough class. This class is very easy
He's the best.

Awesome Diff Eq professor! Definitely worth switching into his class. Funny and good at explaining stuff and really cares that you learn. Attendance not mandatory but definitely a bonus since class in entertaining!

Very clear when teaching and great at answering questions. Also good at balancing fun with classwork. Is especially helpful when you go to office hours. Use the book a lot for reading (assigned every night-not required, but highly recommended!), and homework problems. Material is difficult, but if you do homework and pay attention you'll be fine.

he explains things really well with lots of examples, gives you a practice exam that is the exact same as the exam, doesn't do plus/minus, and makes your lowest exam grade worth only 5%. best diff eq teacher!

Simply put, this is the professor you want for Diff Eq. There are some must avoids, and some not to bad profs. But Manfroi is the best at simply explaining the material and making you understand it. I took it during the summer so slightly accelerated. His grading system is extremely fair too. Also, no +/- which honestly I prefer.
clear explanations and not-so-hard exams. however, diff eq with Manfroi could be like a double edged sword because there is no plus and minus in the final grade (except A+).
Great professor. Great at explaining things and he's hilarious. Has a funny accent too. Attendance isn't mandatory but I'd recommend it for the sake of not having to read the book.

Great professor. chill, funny, and patient. very well organized and presents clear lectures. usually lets class out about 5-10 min early. really good and fair grading structure. thought exams were easy (understand the concepts). highly recommend Manfroi, one of the best prof/class i've ever had. he makes math fun and easy
He is very patient with questions and offers a lot of help! Bet teacher I've had! And he's so cute!!

Manfroi is a fantastic teacher. He manages to cover the material very clearly and in good time (we got out 5-10 mins early most days). He's willing to stop to make sure everyone follows and will answer questions. Homework is graded fairly, and exams reflect the lectures very well. Class was entertaining and helpful. Go with Manfroi if you can.
Test aren't as easy as reviews make it seem. They are fair.  Grading policy makes exams less stressful which is very nice.  Take good notes, do the HW, and enjoy having a fun/nice/good professor.

definitely take him for diff EQ. his tests are easy. although i think the grading scale is screwed up b/c i end up getting an A in the class even w/o taking the final. highly recommend him for this class!
GREATEST MATH TEACHER EVER!! EXAMS ARE STRAIGHT FORWARD. EASY A

Great teacher! If you have the choice of taking 410 or 415, take 410 with Manfroi! He makes sure everything is very clear. The tests are very straight forward (basically the same as practice problems). Not much homework & it's easy. If you go to class, all you have to do is a few practice problems before the tests and you'll ace them
Great teacher - fair, understanding, intelligent.  Not too much homework, test that actually reflect the notes, and great notes.  I would highly recommend this teacher.
excellent teacher. Explained concepts very well.

Good professor. Very nice and easy to approach. ALways willing to answer questions and constantly asks if things are clear or not. Homework load is very easy. Tests are straightforward. I'd stick with him.
GREAT! Best mather teacher I've ever had at U of I. Take his classes if you can. He's straightforward, easygoing, and incredibly nice!

Very clear, very straightforward lectures. He doesn't spend a heavy amount of time on abstract material and instead uses examples (with numbers!) to demonstrate which helps very much so. His exams are also straightforward. Incredibly nice guy as well and being in his class was very enjoyable. I'd take any course by him in a heartbeat.
Aldo Manfroi is the MAN! His exams are so straightforward, not any of that trying to mix you up or fool you. Its what you learned in class and nothing else, usually easier than the homeworks
Only math teacher I've had who has actually done examples with NUMBERS on the board. He's awesome. Take his class if you get the chance.
excellent teacher for 285! explanations were clear, always ready to answer questions!
manfroi's exams are what he teaches a rarity in math he explains very well and is open to questions during lecture
Lectures were always fairly interesting for a math class. He speaks softly but he's a good teacher and is really welcoming to questions. Tests are really straightforward

He is a terrible professor. He is lazy so he doesn't give out solutions to exams so we can fix our mistakes and his handwriting is terrible. I don't know how he got a job here. If the class you chose have him as the professor, you should get out of there asap and take it on a different semester if other professors aren't available.

Manfroi is a terrible, terrible teacher.  The concepts of this class really shouldn't be that hard, but his terrible explanations and mumbling speech along with inconprehensible handwriting impede any chance of actual learning.  Just memorize the equations, even for the geniuses it's their only hope.

Very difficult teacher for Lin. Algebra. Exams are different from material covered in class. He's got a slight accent. It's easy to understand. However, he is hard to follow and understand. I'd avoid him, I've heard there are better 415 instructors out there.

Not a very good lecturer. He was not very loud so you have to sit towards the front of the class. Proofs he did in class were not always clear and concise. His tests were easy, which made for an easy A, but that was the only thing I really liked about his class.

This guy is the best math teacher I've had so far at this school. He's explains class material very clearly and concisely. He's patient and helpful. Homeworks reflect exactly what you need to know for exams, and the exams he gives are always simple and straightforward.
This is probably the nicest and most enthusiastic math professor one could have. Grading is more than fair and he keeps you attentive. Genuine, nice guy.

He is the best math professor I have had here at the university. He counts homework for 30% of your grade (very very few profs do this here) and tests are graded fairly. He is also easy to understand,

Poor instructor. Homework problems never relfected the type of problems on the test, which made it very hard to study for. Belittled you a lot. These are very reoccuring characteristics I have found in most math classes at this university and Im a math major. If not, tell the dept. that they need better instructors.

Amazing professor. Young guy and very enthusiastic. He will do anything and everything to make this class easier to understand. The class may get boring but the professor does his best! Highly, highly recommended.

This guy is great.  He explained everything very well with a good mix of examples and theoretical stuff.  He made everything make sense and his tests were really easy, and since he was such a good teacher the class average was really good.  Take his class.

very organized and professional, but down-to-earth. explains things pretty well but sometimes doesn't give examples of everything you need to know. homework graded harshly but tests graded pretty fairly. doesn't give dumb quizzes every week. kind of boring, but relatively easy, considering it's a 400-level math class.
Out of all the 385 classes, I only heard of people from my class who actually liked it.  He's very good at explaining and overall a good teacher.  It's a hard class, but I'd recommend him.

Excellent professor, explained all material with examples in enough detail to understand without being overwhelming. Exam grading was very clear and reasonable, with ample resources to help prepare. Outside of lecture, very helpful during office hours and explains concepts one-on-one until the student understands the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Bahreini was pretty awesome last sem(Fall 2025), we got extra credit for submitting homework early, tons of practice problems, going to lecture is useful to a certain extent ( she doesn't do a lot of examples as she goes slow, but going slow helps everyone understand) her notes are awesome. Her course is designed really well.

She has decent lectures and uploads all notes and questions solved in classes. Main pro is the testing, stencils and given for all exams so you know exactly what to expect and many questions are recycled from homework on lecture problems. Ample time is given in exams as well, however she is not very good at explaining topics

Compared to other calculus courses,  Bahreini's calc 2 is straight forward and not difficult. She gives blank exam templates, hints, example problems, and practice questions that often end up on the tests. Tests can have one or two questions that are tricky, but there will be a question like that in the notes or practice problems. Overall the goat!
Very hard to understand at times

I retook this class for grade replacement and here's my advice. She isn't the greatest lecturer, but I don't recommend skipping lecture either; utilize other resources alongside lecture, like YouTube and CARE tutoring. 50 minutes just isn't enough time to learn Calc II content, so you need to do additional work to do well. Exam reviews are helpful
Very caring and engaging professor. Lots of HW and additional study material provided. Tests are fair and aren't weighted too heavy.

Bahreini's lectures leave much to be desired, but where her lectures lack, she makes up for in exam hints and extra credit. All homework is available for extra credit, she gives blank exams detailing the kind of problems, practice exams, and is overall kind. If you are willing to put in your own time to teach yourself concepts, I recommend!
kindest professor ever
Solid lecturer. Kind and wants her students to succeed.

She explains the topics during lecture well, but she often ran out of time during the lecture, and only completed a few practice problems.
I would highly recommend skipping the lectures(except exam review—she basically gives answers), and instead reading her lecture notes, and watch youtube videos on concepts that might be difficult to understand.

Prof Manijeh made 231 manageable but you really have to make sure you put in the time to succeed in the class. some people say this class is easy, while others are having complete difficulty. depends a lot on prior background (many here are STEM majors or minors and/or took some calculus in high school).
Literally awesome professor, you didnt go to UIUC if you didnt take calc with Bahraini

She's incredibly kind and supportive!!! Her class was the highlight of my semester. I honestly wish I could take Math 231 again just to be in her lectures. She explains everything so clearly and makes math feel less intimidating. Truly one of the best professors I've ever had!

I took this course last semester and absolutely loved it!!! She's my favorite math professor. Her lectures are super clear, engaging, and well-structured. She explains concepts in a way that actually makes sense, and she genuinely cares about her students' understanding.

She is not a very good teacher. Likely will be better off skipping lecture and read the textbook or watch youtube videos. She gives an exact study guide of the **format** for each midterm and final. There is EC for each homework if done before 24h of the due date. One thing that was annoying was that the hw was often a week behind lectures.

She's a very sweet professor, but her teaching style and lectures were not too helpful towards learning the material. I would suggesting watching Professor Leonard on YouTube instead and, then, work through her practice problems. Her exams were relatively similar to the homeworks/quizzes, so I would definitely focus on studying those.

Lots of opportunities for extra credit. Super kind professor, but her lectures are super slow and not effective - she goes through maybe two practice problems a class, you're better off teaching yourself. Tests are pretty straightforward and based on quizzes, and the class is structured well.

Bahreini is very kind and patient. Course content is difficult but her grading system is forgiving, e.g. there's extra credit points that can be earned on every homework. There's also a good distribution of grades and number of assignments, so no section carries a disproportional weight on your grade.
Don't recommend her for students with disabilities even though she's supposedly the better Calc 2 professor. She will ignore all accommodations and your emails regarding them.

Very kind teacher. The lectures are pretty standard and straightforward, nothing too crazy there, but she goes through many practice problems during class and answers many questions after class as well. She is a very nice person and also gives review sheets that are basically the test, just with different numbers. Also very generous extra credit

Not a good lecturer, straight up. Almost never went to lecture, was a waste of my time. But, she is very nice and a lovely lady. HW and Quizzes are easy, and you can get extra credit if you do it 2 days early. Also extra credit was provided for every test + final. If you don't mind teaching yourself w youtube, her exams are very easy

She was such an amazing professor. She taught the class very well and made calc 2 a breeze. She would take her time when explaining how to solve problems and didn't really read off the slides but would work through them. Wish she taught more classes genuinely the sweetest teacher I have ever had.

She just reads off the slides and recites formulas. It was more useful to look up lecture notes and watch youtube videos than attend lecture, which is what a lot of people did. Strongly do not recommend.

One of the nicest and most understanding professors I've ever had. The tests weren't bad if you kept up with the homework and did past tests. She also provides exactly what is on the test, so there aren't many surprises. The discussion worksheets can be difficult to understand, but the tests are primarily focused on the homework.

Came in expecting calc 2 to be a challenge, but Bahreini makes the class very manageable. Tests are extremely similar to homework/practice exams, what you're expected to know is made clear in lectures. A great professor and person in general, would definitely recommend.
I got a 100% on this course, it was thanks to this professors great teaching skills :) She is the best and is accommodating
this is class is awesomely good with Prof Bahreini she is kind
