An incredibly kind and sweet professor but not the best lecturer. She rarely does practice problems, and her notes are a bit messy. A lot of homework/quizzes but drops 2-4 lowest ones. Extra credit opportunities. Practice exams are exactly like the midterms/final. Take Bahreini if you don't mind teaching yourself calc 2 and learning in lectures.

Professor Esfahani is one of the kindest, most amazing and accommodating professors I have ever had. As someone with ADHD she has done an AMAZING job going through specifics throughout each lecture. She highlights the most important aspects to every lecture. She offers a ton of materials similar to the exams and she is super sweet.

Lectures were bad, but she got better along the way. Midterms weren't too bad bc they were almost just like the practice. She has a heavy accent but talked slow. She doesn't have the best lecture structure and it would be irritating. Be sure to go to office hours and grasp concepts you didn't get well bc they will be used throughout the  semester.

She mostly reads off the lecture notes, and doesn't really work through the homeworks. Most of the people taking this class would have some knowledge of Calc 2 beforehand, but I did not, and I found it to be incredibly difficult. However, the exams are based off of the homework and quiz questions, so they were OK if you understood the homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
She's very nice, but her lectures are ok. They're not recorded so it's difficult to catch up if you miss. But i've recently found Dobbs lectures online so have been watching those instead.

Class overall is very well designed though, with drops for hw, quizzes, and discussion. There's also a lot of practice content that resembles the actual exams.

I found it a little difficult to understand her through her accent but you catch on quickly. She just reads off her lecture notes and does not really work through examples. I say that you need to have a small understanding of Calc 2 to succeed in this class. She did have all of her lecture notes posted beforehand.

The test borrowed lots of questions from the quizzes and homework, so focus on these. The exams also had nice partial credit. Some of the problems can feel tedious, but Bahreini makes this class feel manageable.

Professor Bahreini is a very kind professor. She has a heavy accent and talked slow, so the lectures were a bit difficult to get through. She made it very clear what would be on the exams, which had problems taken straight from the homework. She also dropped our four lowest quizzes, worksheets, and homeworks at the end of the semester.

She is a VERY kind person, her lectures are ok. Go to lecture because she will tell you exactly what will be on the exam. Frustrating how many mistakes she had on things, like practice problems for the exam with incorrect answers.There was some sort of curve after the final bumping me up a few percentage points, so dont worry if you fail an exam :)
This professor is great. I usually take her course in the front rows, and she would always responds to the answers or the uncertainty from the students.

She's not the best lecturer, so I was at TA office hours every week because I had no idea what I was doing. Exams aren't terrible, and she tries to make them easier by using problems from the hw and quizzes, so study those. She's genuinely nice, but calc II is just super tough. Cried a ton this semester, but got through it, so don't worry too much.

Awful lecturer be prepared to learn from TA or youtube especially with midterm 3 and final. Upside is grading is fair if you make it look like you know what youre doing. Not sure if she plans on chaing cutoffs or a curve. I heard she hasn't in the past so well see. Also exam prep is generally never entirely correct and curveballs are thrown often.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Bahreini is pretty solid. Exams were tough but fair, other than mt1 which was very easy. Her lectures were decent but she is sometimes hard to understand. Gave decent practice materials for each exam as well. She seems like a caring person that wants her students to succeed and although calc 2 is hard, it's definitely possible with this prof

I take my first review back because of how accommodating she is with just about everything. She pushed back homework deadlines to let you study for the 3rd midterm and she omitted a question on the last one that significantly helped my grade. Take her if you have to take calc 2 TAKE HER FOR YOUR OWN SAKE.

Very bad at teaching but is fair with exams. She took time with the first exam material but then got lazy with challenging series units and skip examples as they are "very easy" but we would go over many examples of real easy stuff at the beginning of the school year. Id find a friend who took her last year to get the past exams.

Lectures very fast, and moves through units with the assumption that everybody has mastered the previous unit just from the one sped through lecture. Skips over many of the example problems within the lecture because they're "really easy" and the students will know how to do them, despite that not being the case whatsoever.

Professor Bahreini is not only a nice and caring person, but she is a very devoted instructor. She makes sure everyone understands the material. At no point did she ever make it seem like the student not understanding the material was a burden to her. In terms of examination, she was very fair and did not throw any "curve balls" on the exams.

Calc II is a weed-out class and usually challenging but Bahreini made it much easier. She bases the exams off of the homework on WebAssign and there is nothing on the exams that we didn't learn in class. Overall G professor.

Calc here is pretty awful but Professor Bahreini was actually a great professor. She's clearly very knowledgeable and genuinely cares about helping students understand material. Tests weighted heavily but were on paper w/partial credit and expectations were very clear. All notes/worksheets put on Moodle for review. Fantastic professor overall.

Bahreini may single-handedly save the reputation of UIUC math professors. The work in the class can be unbelievably hard, but professor Bahreini is the first instructor in the math department I've met who genuinely wants her students to succeed and earn good grades.
90% of exam Qs are very similar to WebAssign quizzes. Focus on studying those.

Good teacher, as long as you put the hours in you will do great in the class. I wish I studied more, because I know I'm more capable than the grade I got. Very caring, and she attempts to make the class as fair as possible, and has great study guides. For Calculus II (and any other math courses she teaches), I recommend choosing her.

Dr. Bahreini is, by far, the most caring lecturer in the Math Department. She really encourages students to succeed, and greets them as the enter the lecture hall! She posts everything to Moodle, and will spend time with students during office hours and explain every little detail. She might as well take over for all of Calculus II. Very satisfied.

Prof. Bahreini is the first one I've had for math in years that I haven't felt strong hate for. After nearly having to retake calc 1 (see Placzek's reviews for reference), I had super low expectations going into this class. She did a great job teaching us this semester even though she often had to go through complex material fast. She also cares!!!

Bahreini is probably the best professor for calc 2 which can easily become an impossible class with other professors but is manageable with her. Don't get me wrong, the class is by no means easy but in comparison to other sections of calc 2, professor Bahreinis class is very manageable.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Bahreini ensures you understand the things she talks about. She provides lots of examples to illustrate key concepts. Tests problems are from the examples. This is how a math course should be.

Probably the best Calc II prof at UIUC. Class is definitely a mess but at least it is clear she is genuinely trying to do the best job she can given the administrative restraints that are put on the course by the math department.

Tests are based on things he never mentions, lectures about, not in the readings he expects you to read, and not anywhere. The amount of sass is almost disrespectful at times and expects us to know everything before even taking the class. Furthermore, he leaves you in the absolute dark for exams and doesn't really explain what is being tested ever.

I'm taking the summer course (450). He talks a lot, but not much of it is actually useful information. He has a lot of filler information when he talks, and it makes it very draining to listen to him as he keeps talking about the same slide for incredibly too long. You end up having to completely depend on the textbook for this class.

The moodle is so disorganized and it's hard to find anything onthewebsite. You can't evenfindtheanswers to the studyguide on moodle. The exams are awful and mostofthe class got below 50% onit. The labreport average was so bad that he gave us another chance to do it and yet mostofus still didn't doitright because the feeback he gave wasnot helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: DONTTAKE This man does not teach at all expects you to know how to do everything. The tests aren't even part of what you do in class and the study guide never matches the exam. You can't even find the answers to the study guide in the course. The lab reports are so long and the feedback he gives are awful and don't even tell you what you did wrong.

This review is for MCB 450.

This class definitely makes you work to get an A, I had to read the textbook almost every day. Lectures are not too bad, he just goes on tangents a lot. For his tests you need to memorize the learning objectives. He's really helpful during office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class was a lot of fun and Dustin is one of the more relatable instructors you'll ever take from. The only warning I have is that this class is very research oriented and there is lots of discussion and writing involved and the protocols are often multi-faceted and complex. Be prepared to be really confused (and to learn a lot)!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: HK207 is a great course to take to fill a quant 1; it's not hard at all for anyone with even the most basic math background. Watch all the lectures on 2x speed, and do all the discussion posts at once, and then respond later. Makes workload much easier. Midterm was open, so was the final, both extremely simple. Great Class for a tough credit.
Best professor I've had!

Easy A online class, but the workload was a lot for 2 credit hours. Homework consisted of chapter reading, 1 discussion post on a 30 min-2 hour documentary, 2 peer responses, and a quiz/short project. Course lasted 8 weeks and 8 modules with 2 exams (no final cumulative). Lots of opportunities for extra credit. Overall, it was a solid class.

Was an easy class for 2.0 credits. Online so submit the work on your own time by Sunday, and it is eight weeks, and the course itself is not too demanding. But be prepared it is a lot of movie-watching paired with discussion posts. There is one project, a midterm and a final, and quizzes so a lot of test material. Overall a great course!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I really like her, this class would have been much better if it were in person because online was kind of difficult- there was a movie to watch almost every week with a 400 word discussion post plus two 250 word discussion replies due on Wednesday (400) and Friday (replies)
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: There are a lot of weekly assignments in this class. A discussion post, two reply posts (400 words and 250 words), a quiz and all the readings. You also sometimes have to watch lengthy documentaries to be prepared for the discussion (usually interesting and on Netflix). Overall would give this class a 3/5. It's not bad, but not my favorite.

No drops for attendance or homework. Attendance system is a random number generator. The textbook is full of typos so you have to constantly check the errata. No practice exams and the exams are very different from homework. Lectures are repeats of the textbook. Concepts are taught very slowly and out of order.

MY 4.0 GPA WAS RUINED BY A DISHONEST PROFESSOR! I asked during office hours about an attendance issue, and he said that attendance will not count for credit. He didn't put in 0's for attendance until the end of semester (otherwise at least I could've stopped missing classes after realizing something was wrong), costing 10% of my grade.

Dude gave no partial credit on exams, which is especially impactful due to tensor calculation errors that may propagate through problems. Attendance policy is completely unnecessary. We are adults here.
RIDICULOUS TEXTBOOK&HWs, ALSO EXAMs ARE VERY DIFFERENT FROM THE HWs

Took him for 398QIC (site doesn't have 398 as an option) in SP22.  Lectures were well paced and he did his best to simplify difficult concepts, and course structure was very well designed.  Was always willing to help during office hours and had a good sense of humor.  Wish more professors were like him.

Took him for 398 (rate my prof doesn't give the option).  Pre-lectures introduce you to the following lecture so you know what to ask about in class.  Explanations were generally well articulated, very friendly and willing to reexplain topics for you.  Homework difficulty is where it should be, exams were fair.  Top notch
Very inspiring teach. The highest rating professor in my heart by now.
Not getting an A solely due to not attending lectures, I wish Professor Doug could delete lecture and iclicker point and focus more on how well students understand the materials.

His lectures provided no information outside of the textbook but you were still graded on attendance and were expected to have already read. He had the homework due at 9:00 and the lecture started at 9:30, so you had to come a half hour early for no reason.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Lectures were not incredibly clear. Oftentimes it felt as though the material in the textbook was just being repeated, however topics that were unclear were not made much better by the lectures.

He and Makins traded off lectures, so I got to experience both of them. I personally learned better from Beck, but Naomi's lectures were definitely more fun. They both know their stuff and care about the students. It's hard to go wrong in the physics department.

He has a very straightforward teaching style. His lectures are simply PowerPoint presentations. His teaching is formula-based and not very conceptual. The prelectures are basically the same as his lectures
If you can learn from video 'prelectures' and a professor that doesn't teach you anything, this is the class for you.

Beck is a good teacher and a nice guy.  The entire class is taught off of smartphysics, which makes lectures occasionally pointless.  He has a lot of demos and enjoys teaching.  Maybe not the best teacher ever, but certainly not a bad pick.  The tests are easy to lose points on, though.  Make sure to check your + / - signs (they love those signs)

He's alright.. But what I do wanna say something about the class. This is a HORRIBLY designed course. It's way too ridiculous for a gened and, believe or not, most teaching is done through smartphysics. Let's just be honest, how many people can actually sit down and learn stuff from youtube videos?

E&M is a difficult topic but the system they use to teach it is great. I don't think it matters whether you get Beck or Gladding because its the system that matters. I didn't pay close attention in lecture but Beck didn't seem to explain things very well sometimes, and you could learn it all in the HW anyways.
Pretty good lecturer. Class is hard in general but he helped.
He is an okay prof.. the class is generally hard though.. and he does not really help make difficult concepts easy. the course would have been a lot better if it was curved though..
He is a smart professor and I found his lectures very helpful.  Do not take this course lightly, it will be difficult, but Beck's lectures help.
Definitely knew his stuff but made this very abstract topic extremely boring and difficult.  Very friendly, very approachable, but is not great at explaining concepts.

Prof is okay at teaching. However the exams are much harder than the content we go over. (And much harder than the given practice exams) 

If you want to do well study a lot. Read the Lumetta notes, course text book (don't buy it, just google the name), and course slides. Office hours and the official review sessions were also very helpful.
Horrible lecturer, not so helpful in answering questions

Great experience with this professor. 

GOAT of ECE 120
If i could i would give him a 0

Professor Bhowmik seems like a genuine and caring guy, unfortunately, he is an absolutely horrid lecturer. I only learned this class from MP's and office hours his lectures were that incoherent. His tests are also quite difficult, especially the second midterm, additionally the review materials he provides are not helpful in the slightest.
He is a good and caring professor.

Super nice and caring guy, really approachable and easy to talk to. That is about it though. He is simply terrible at delivering lectures. His lectures are incompetent, and he cannot explain any slightly difficult concept with any coherence. Avoid him at all costs, it will make ECE 120 much more enjoyable

Professor Bhowmik is a great guy, but his lectures aren't that great.  The concepts are not hard by any measure, but his explanations are confusing. His accent isn't that bad. I did most of my learning from Labs, HW, and Lumetta notes anyways so it wasn't a big deal. He definitely tries his best, but he could be better.

A caring professor who goes out of his way to help you. He cares a lot about his students and tries hard, but the fact is that he is just bad at giving lectures. He is awesome in everything except lectures and he has difficulty conveying ideas well. I learned mostly from Labs and HWs and fortunately the class itself is extremely well structured.
I like Professor Bhowmik. He supplied all the materials you need to complete the class. He tries his best.

Overall, the best way to describe Prof. Bhowmik is that he is a great person, not the best lecturer. His lectures cover what is necessary, supplementary materials are given, but it could better. However, outside of the classroom, he will go out of his way to help you understand material and tries his best to make himself available.
( I recommend!)
Not the best professor. His accent is a bit hard to understand and he cannot explain the concept well. Had to watch other professor's videos or youtube videos.
Prof. Bhowmik in my experience is a weak lecturer; however, I recommend him because he is understanding and very accessible outside class.
Bhowmik was an amazing prof and taught his lectures very clearly and concisely. highly recommend him for 120 and 220, this class.
prof was great at teaching during lectures and was very understanding of circumstances that came up. highly recommend him for 120 and 220.

Bhowmik is underrated. Idk if I'd give him a 5 if his rating wasn't so low, but he's a nice guy and an improving lecturer despite his really thick accent. I had to use the lumetta notes along with the lectures, but it was overall a solid experience.

Ujjal Bhowmik is a caring guy, truly motivated to see all students succeed. Many believe his lectures are terrible, but in my opinion they're really not that bad, and they always cover all of the material we need to know. Keep an eye out for his review sessions as you'll likely get general information about questions on upcoming midterms.

I'm conflicted. He is a super sweet person and he lays out the course very well. There are more than enough resources. But the lectures are less than ideal. I often find myself watching the online lectures instead since they're much more comprehensible. I feel more like I'm in an asynchronous class which I don't love. As a person though he's great.
unintelligible handwriting, terrible lectures, students reduce by less than half from first day, put your everything into changing away to take it with the other professors instead
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Bhowmik is not at all who you would expect teaching a top engineering school. His lectures are  inefficient and his teaching style is flawed. After the first few lectures, the amount of empty seats in his lecture visibly increased, and rightfully so, as spending 20 minutes studying would yield better results than an entire lecture of his.
Avoid at all cost, very poor at explaining concepts, and is inaccessible outside of class. The class isn't hard, learning from him is. Under no circumstances ever need extra help, you won't get it.
Very difficult to follow concepts. Does not explain the best.

Ujjal Bhowmik is a genuinely nice, kind guy with a great sense of humor. Please read the negative reviews with a grain of salt, they are from freshman ECE underclassmen that got smashed by their first relatively difficult ECE class. At times he can be a bit hard to understand but  he improved significantly as a lecturer. He has a great attitude
A bad lecturerer who rarely explained concepts clearly. The workload is huge and the tests are super hard. Would recommend taking other professors' classes even if you register his sections.
Homeworks were way too difficult, nothing like the lectures. Graders would cut points for very weird reasons.
An Awful Teacher. He doesn't curve exams and the exams are super hard especially for midterm 3 and final.
I learnt many thing from the class. He is a good professor.
Just Take Levchenko if you have not learn to program before, If you do know how to program, watch Lumetta's lecture on your own

One of the best professors I've had. He is extremely caring and makes sure no one has questions unanswered. He covers everything in lectures and will answer any question you have during class, after class, or during office hours. ECE classes aren't supposed to be easy. You need to go to more than just lectures to get a proper understanding.

This professor has such an unintelligible accent. About midway through the semester I only went to about a third of the lectures because I couldnt learn anything from him. He means well but doesnt know enough English and thats beyond the accent, its word knowledge. I learned more from TAs, the internet, and other students BY FAR.

Honestly don't know why people say he's bad. I think its just his accent, but his teaching is actually pretty good. Idk most of my friends agree with this. He's a nice guy and teaches things pretty well.
Just take Levchenko, you can drop in on any lecture.

If you really pay attention to his classes, you will notice that he does a good job of teaching you the contents. He is also a really nice person who is very flexible, don't know why people say he's bad.
He is terrible at explaining concepts and I always left lectures knowing just as little as I knew coming in. I would recommend switching professors.
Not a good lecturer. It's very hard to understand him, doesn't explain concepts well at all. I had to switch to a different lecturer. I advise you to do the same.

Very bad professor overall. Altough he tries to be professional and caring, he has the worst accent in the ECE department (cannot understand 90% what he's pronouncing) and not understanding the materials at times.

This is the only RMP ive written, he really should not be teaching 220. Took it over summer with him and he had to stop explaining the operation of the stack half way through lecture because he didn't know what he was talking about any more and a student had to get him back on track. Do what you have to in order to not be in his lecture.

Professor just cannot explain the concept clearly. His students have to do lots of self-study in order to catch up with the class progress. Honestly, I will suggest him to practice English oral skills. Also, I cannot understand why he would write the C codes in horrible handwriting on Tablet instead of typing them in the IDE.

I thought he really just didn't explain the concepts of 220 really well. Found his ability to answer questions rather cyclical and didn't help much. Ended up going to the other instructor when I could and him for the rest which was basically learning half a class. Shame
Best professor I've had yet. Explains difficult accounting concepts very well. Always willing to answer any questions or help you if you're confused. Gives a lot of study material for exams.

I personally struggled with this class, but Bo Ren is an amazing professor who truly cares for his students and making sure that they understand the material. It was his first semester teaching but he quickly adapted and has been one of the best professors and people I have EVER met. Overall, a great professor who makes his lectures fun and useful.

Literally my favorite professor I've ever had. The content of the course was difficult and heavy at times, but Bo explains things so well and makes every class entertaining. He was available to email or message at anytime of the day and always helped with homework, quizzes, and cases. Such a fun and caring guy.
The GOAT. ACCY 303 is a pretty hard class, but Bo helps make your life a bit easier. Lively lecturer and super accessible if you need help. He really wants you to succeed more than anything.
Makes simple topics appear difficult.

Hard class,you have to actually pay attention, watch lecture videos, read the content for multiple hours to grasp the content. He is the worst professor to explain topics for this class simply. He's overall terrible.If he could see his students fail in front of him he would be fine with that. He sets things up for it to be challenging and difficult
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not a good at explaining things

discussed difficult to understand topics, but broke it down over multiple lectures in a way that was accessible. The questions for a lot of the later units come from the textbooks, which can be found online for free. no final, just weekly homework (attempt) and bi-weekly "comprehension assessment" (5-6  problems, correctness)
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great professor, clearly takes time to explain class concepts and encourages class discussion. Structure of the class could be slightly improved, but it's the first time this class has existed so pretty great for that.

You can tell Prof. Batzloff wants to teach.They consider feedback, prioritize learning, and offer a lot of office hours.The material is hard but much easier to complete if you watch lectures, go to class, and work with a group.The only con is they didn't teach for two weeks of the course.They assigned us textbook readings and problems-no lectures.

Batzloff was friendly, but I found the course content overly theoretical with insufficient real-world examples. There were no examples of final projects provided. However, it was mentioned that the course is still in development, so it may be worth trying. Prof. Batzloff is not a strict grader, and attending class and putting in effort is enough.

Professor cares about students outside of class and wants us to succeed. Class will not be difficult if you don't have a good group to work with/friends. Flipped classroom format, in class you work on worksheets with groups. Comprehension assessments every other week and videos to watch before class. Only started taking attendance halfway in sem..

The class can be very difficult and challenging at the beginning, but as long as you do the work and show up to class regularly you should be fine. The format of the class is different: you watch lecture videos online and work on workshop problems in class. Going to office hours is beneficial. Professor gives lot of extra credit opportunities.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He explained things very well and as long as you take notes in lectures, it's pretty easy to pass the class

Manages to make econ less boring for sure. Could pass the class without ever attending lectures but the examples he gives makes it worth going,
plus he gives great lectures and isnt afraid to point out how relevant it is to modern topics.  Great guy definitely take him!!

Truthfully didn't really go to class or try my hardest. That being said I think the criteria is really clear and prepping for tests is just on how you study. Get his workbook, it has a lot of practice tests in them that aren't on Canvas. Homeworks every week can be boring but worth it. Also don't need to spend too much time.

Professor Buckley is a great lecturer, he makes the concepts interesting and understandable with real world examples. I attended every lecture and barely had to study. He also has a great sense of humor.

Very passionate and kind. Has a whole lot of prep in canvas and not too hard to follow. Post lecture slides on canvas too so don't have to show up in person. Just make sure you study and you'll be fine. Tests weren't that hard or tricky.

Econ 102 w/ Buckley is a light course, structured incredibly well. Each week you have a lesson assignment and a lesson quiz which both total around 15 to 20 multiple choice questions and optional worksheets. Extra credit is provided. Professor is nice and sound quality depends on room sound system it has nothing to do with how he sounds.

Just like 102, 302 with Buckley is a great course. The content is just an extension of 102 with calculus. Buckley is once again a great lecturer. Content may get dry at times, so make sure to stay on top of things by attending discussion (but are optional). Homework is unlimited attempts which is nice. Overall with minimal work one can get an A.

Buckley's econ 102 class is super light, insane amount of opportunities to get your grade up and extra credit. Seems like a super solid dude as well, easy-to-follow lectures and the guy knows what he's doing. Highly recommend the class

Overall, the class was pretty light. I only needed to study the day of the exams and that was plenty. Attendance isn't mandatory and he posts all the slides, which explain the concepts nicely and there's even extra credit for just taking midterms and doing other practice assignments, which I thought were helpful studying for the exam anyways.

Overall Professor Buckley is a great professor and makes Econ and concepts very clear and understandable. You sadly dont interact with him, since it is a giant lecture, but the work load is easy and fair.

He is a funny lecturer. You don't really interact with him since it's an auditorium lecture. Attendance is not required (and not really necessary if you can learn from the slides/tutorials). Homework is simple and weekly, taking about 10 minutes or so (including the quiz, lesson assignment, and tutorials). Many chances on assignments. Easy tests.
You don't need to go to anything, but go to the discussion if you're not understanding anything, because they give a full summary of the lectures with practice that looks just like the exams
His lectures are very good, using real world examples. He grades based only on online quizzes, assignments, and exams. His Microecon class is incredibly easy, and he teaches it very well.

Class has various resources to use such as book, tutorials, tutor handouts and lecture slides. Their was various extra credit and grade boosters opportunities. People who rated him a low score, I guarantee did not use their resources to their advantage. Please study throughout your week as this was helpful for me. Overall his class is very easy.

Very easy class. Barely gives homework and what he gives is very simple. Only knock on him is that he needs to speak up during lectures and sometimes gives too much examples to where it can distract you from the main points. Overall, he's pretty solid.
He explains things great. Makes the material easy to understand. There are weekly quizzes and assignments but it is tolerable and the workload is definitely manageable. He also has great TA's.

Don't need to show up to lecture to succeed. Very very good online materials and practice to learn the content on your own. Seems like a chill guy too. Tests should be light assuming you did practice.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: The students bashing this class are simply bad students. This course was so easy. Homework had quizzes which were 2 attempts, and lesson assignments unlimited attempts (all MCQ). 3 tests and a final, and technically you could just take the final and it would be worth 75% of the grade. Good lecturer with useful examples. He does speak softly though.

I thought he was very clear with what we needed to know. I took AP Micro and Macro in high school and did not receive credit for the AP exams, finding them quite challenging. However, Buckley is incredibly easy to understand. Exams are not required to be taken, but will make the final worth more if neglected. Very easy A for me.
Super easy class. It takes skill to not get an A. Could barely hear him during lecture and read off slides so never ended up going. Notes posted online

Even though I currently do not have a good grade in his class, I am still so interested in what he has to teach. I feel as though if I were in this situation with other professors, I would hate the class and the professor him or herself. However, he is so engaging in lectures that I feel myself learning a lot from him and loving the course.

The professor was extremely rude and did not care that I was experiencing a health difficulty. He told me he didn't understand why I don't understand his class even he speaks looking away from the audience and slams you with information in a tiny lecture. Even Econ MAJORS failed his class.

Worst class I have ever taken. Speaks so quietly during lectures it is difficult to understand him unless you're literally in the front row. TA sessions were not one bit helpful at all and were a COMPLETE waste of time, test's had material that was not even remotely covered in class. AVOID AT ALL COSTS take this with Diilani i've heard much better.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: King Buckley.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures are boring and long, basically reads word for word off slides. The test prep assignments are not helpful during the test. He makes the test questions more confusing than need be.

This class was harder than expected. The homework was very difficult because it gave you no feedback and you have to start over until you get your desired grade. The exams were okay as long as you studied. The book with practice exams can help. Lectures are very boring and he basically reads off the slides.
Easy A as long as you attend the lectures and complete the weekly quizzes and extra credit assignments.

Class was based on homework, 4 total exams, and optional extra credit. While attendance isn't mandatory, the lectures are worth going to, since Dr. Buckley is great at explaining. Homework was incredibly tedious and annoying to complete, but not too difficult overall. Doing the work guarantees a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Can't go wrong with Buckley :D. If you go to class and are attentive in the lectures, you'll succeed. Most of things he taught is very valuable and he uses a lot of real world example to help you understand the concepts. 

You get what you put into the class!

This class will take 20-25 hours per week. There are few resources for this course, and the tests are tough!! The recorded videos are made well. However, they give very few instructions on how problems are carried out. Overall, this course is miserable. Take an alternative if you can.

I had Buckley for 102 and 302 for the econ minor. He is great. Very clear criteria, posts all notes/slides on Canvas. HW was annoying: unlimited attempts but have to start over with like 10+ different problems. Did not give HW answers. Never went to lecture and easy to learn on your own.

Very easy class, professor knew what he was talking about. Despite being good at what he does, his lectures were pretty boring, but the content was very easy to understand and pretty intuitive. He had great grading policies and an A was pretty much guaranteed if you somewhat paid attention. Great guy overall.

I've had Dr. Buckley for ECON 102 & 302, and he is one of my favorite professors.  He has very clear grading criteria, and offers many resources to make it easy to learn the material.  Unlike my other professors, he leads his own office hours to help answer questions.  He is definitely a professor you should try and take if you can.

He is a great lecturer. I felt bad for him because only a few people were showing up to class by the end. Pretty much all of what you need for the exams is posted to canvas. I didn't always show up but when I did I always felt glad that I went.

I went into the class knowing nothing about economics and came out of it somewhat knowing basic economics. What I learned about economics was thanks to TA handouts and the assignments on Moodle, not the lectures (since I fell asleep in about 75% of them due to how boring some were and how quiet Prof. Buckley's voice was). Chill grading system.

For ECON102: The course was well structured and followed the schedule and syllabus. The lectures were detailed although sometimes boring. All the required materials are accessible online (PPT & notes), there is a textbook (~$30) but not mandatory. Only complain would be that there was always a delay with grading, for all 3 midterms and the final.
Buckley explains the topics very well and provides more than enough resources for one to succeed in the course.

Buckley is friendly and passionate about his field, but his teaching style left much to be desired for an advanced course. Lectures overall are largely unengaging. Material is not too difficult, essentially his Econ102 but with calc. However, the difficulty of the exams are not accurately reflected the coursework and hw is mind-numbingly tedious.

Surprised that many found his lectures to be hard to follow, as I thought he was a very good lecturer. He always answered questions in depth as well. Homework can be tedious, just make sure to start it early. TONS of extra credit. Some of the tests had weird questions tbh, like there'll be a random question about something that wasn't even covered

Buckley does some things right, but he also misses some things. He posts guided notes, has an optional attendance policy, and gives easy extra credit. HW is tedious but you have unlimited attempts. Discussions are terrible - TA had no idea how to teach. No additional materials to study for exams - stare at your notes! Tests get harder as you go.

You can't learn from the lecture and be prepare for so many extra time spending outside the classes. This professor use a very lowering voice to speak in the auditorium, so you are easily distracted by other things. But one good thing is that the grading system is chill.

I had a really bad semester when I took this class, but still ended up with a B- with nearly no effort. Attendance for lecture wasn't required when I took it, and he uploads every resource you'll need in Canvas so you can learn everything on your own. Everything is organized extremely well and he even offered tutorials you can do for extra credit.

Lectures were some what boring to listen to and I would often not pay attention. There are weekly assignments and quizzes that taught me the material. The exams were not that bad, but you do need to study for them. Pretty easy extra credit is offered. Buckley's class is very organized, and he gives out follow along notes.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I really enjoy taking his classes! Would love to taking more of his classes!

Buckley is a great professor, has a great personality, and wants you to succeed. The way the course is organized is excellent and easy to understand. Guided notes during lectures and quick and easy assignments. Exams weren't too hard either. Overall, I wish every class was built this way.
Great professor, answers questions thoroughly after class, and also teaches well. Would recommend

easy to understand the material. going to lecture isnt required but always helps. I got by studying for the exams on my own and it was fairly easy to do so. not much homework and he is an easy grader.

You do not need to go to lectures. They are pointless and repetitive, I would just do the notes on my own time. During lecture it is hard to hear him, his tone moves up and down and I couldn't seem to understand much. The practice test in his textbook are nothing like the tests, and the tests are nothing like what is learned in class.

Buckley is a fun lecturer, but all of the slides can be found on Moodle so a lot of students don't go. The exams were fair and you can buy a review packet with a practice exam for each midterm and final. The only other work are weekly quizzes (lowest 4 dropped) and assignments with unlimited attempts. Up to 2% easy extra credit is also available.
His lectures tend to be boring when he goes off topic. Whenever class is almost over, he will start speaking fast which leads to no time to write down your notes.
This professor will teach the basics of Microeconomics and does a very good job at it!

Prof Buckley's lectures are just him reading off powerpoints that he posts on Moodle. Everybody shows up to the first two lectures, but later they realize that the lectures were useless and it was just easier to look at his powerpoints online. The rather easy weekly quizzes are nice because they force you to study so that you are not behind.

The material is thought provoking. He streams using zoom in case you're out sick. The math & models aren't any more complicated than intermediate courses. I didn't make below 94% on any assignment or test. Doesn't require a book; he provides you the slides. He responds to e-mails and is willing to talk outside of class. Few assignments.

The class structures are pretty messy and unorganized.

Somethings on the syllabus were not true or contradicting itself...

Econ 302 used this alternative website called "learn" or "Moodle" instead of canvas and the UI was horrible. 

Just did not feel the effort / enthusiasm from the professor as much.
soooo many quizzes and exams, this class is supposed to be very easy-i took an equivalent at parkland- but the way he teaches and the structure of the course make it hard to pass and keep up.
Lectures are pretty easy to understand but I was drowning in hw and articles to read on top of everything
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Excellent lecturer for Economics. Anyone with very little knowledge about microeconomic concepts can learn very well from Buckley. Class is also structured very well with homework and tutorial assignments giving ample amount of practice opportunities for students. Exams can have tough questions at times but they are very reasonable.

Pretty easy class. Mr. Buckley is a good lecturer and the subject is actually very interesting. You can do the homework as many times as you need for a perfect score before the due date and if you get the optional workbook it has some very useful practice exams. Exams are also very straightforward without any curveballs/trick questions.

Overall, Buckley himself is a good professor. He teaches well and I did learn a lot in his class. However, the amount of effort I put into the class did not reflect in my grade. The tests and quizzes were super easy to mess up on, and one little mistake can tank your grade. I really expected myself to get an A in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: 100% recommend taking! Unlimited takes for hw with ample extra credit - take advantage! Buckley's lecture/slides made it very easy to understand the content. Exams very closely mirror hw. If you miss class, everything you need is in the slides and once you take the 5 min quiz for discussion you're good to go.

Econ 102 was very easy. The presentations and many assignments(a lot of easy extra credit) were available on moodle and helped me ace all my tests. Everyone rarely went to the lectures and in the discussions left the class within 10 mins of starting after giving the weekly 5 min test. Its the easiest class. Highly recommend to take this class!

This class is incredibly test heavy, 70% of your total grade is based on exams. There are weekly quizzes, 4 of which were dropped for my class. There is a decent amount of extra credit. The exams are optional in the sense that if you feel like you arent solid on the material you can skip it and the weight will be transferred over to the other exams

Professor Buckley excels at presenting his information in lectures, keeping us engaged and being very straight forward with exam content. If you do the weekly assignments the questions are near exact to exam content. Extra credit is abundant and he will make accommodations if you ask him early enough. lecture slides are posted online with answers.
Econ 102 is a very easy class in general. Prof. Buckley is an easy grader and his slides are easy to understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Buckley is by far one of the best professors I have met on campus. His lectures are very easy to understand & he certainly has a passion for the topic. I took Microeconomics in Fall of 2021, & I would recommend him to anyone needing to take Micro. Attend the lectures/do the extra credit he offers, it adds up & can totally save your grade!

DO NOT take this class if it is not required. If taking, be prepared to take weekly. quizzes. Also, lectures are him going over his slides, so feel free to miss. Exams are hard because they are all application problems . My advice is to study the definitions so you can easily apply them to problems. There is a lot of extra credit tho.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Such a good professor. I took micro in hs and didnt do well, but I am passing this class with flying colors. My favorite professor. 10/10

Homework is graded for accuracy. TIme-consuming class, lectures are posted in video format but are filled with irrelevant material. Exam material is relatively easy but is made unnecessarily more difficult due to course structure. All exams are proctored making the class unnecessarily expensive.

I took Econ 302 with Buckley last year and will be taking Environmental Economics this semester. One of the classes I'm looking forward to the most. Actually I look forward to all my Econ classes next semester :P

The class is test heavy, every test (there are 3 plus a final) is about 20% of your overall grade. If you don't do good on the exams, don't expect to pass. You get graded on a few things: exams, math, *concepts, and *vocab. In the lecture we don't learn, we all just follow the examples on the slides. Lots of sleepers, he's truly boring. Never again
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Some of the pros of this course include the practice exams and extra credit opportunities. That being said, while the professor is very passionate about what he teaches, the lecture format of the class is extremely boring and very ineffective at teaching. In addition, the lack of frequent homework assignments makes studying difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Buckley is a great guy. His lectures can be dry but he does bring in interesting real-world examples. The class is very test-heavy but they aren't very difficult. Make sure to do all the extra credit and practice the math problems before the tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I wouldn't recommend taking his Econ 302 course, unless you have no other choice. Take a different professor's Econ 302 course if you can! Professor Buckley does somewhat of an okay job going over the math used in the course, but does not go over any of the conceptual stuff. Overall, he does not teach this course well, at all. He's not a good prof.
Great guy, very passionate, go to lecture and get the workbook and you will do great as well as learn a lot about econ.

He is clearly smart and knows what he is talking about, but his teaching style is just not very good for a large lecture. His powerpoints are endless and not very helpful. But the hardest part is that there is little homework and it does not correlate to the tests at all. The practice tests are somewhat helpful, but more practice is needed to prep.

Lectures are very boring with a lot of extraneous info. There are a lot of materials to study and learn from. Definitely go to discussion and buy the work book and you'll do fine. Lectures as aren't totally necessary but at least study the slides
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: exam is so hard
If you buy the workbook, you'll basically be set for the course. You can study the practice tests in the workbook for the midterms and final. Overall, decent professor.

ECON302,482,490 - One of my favorite econ professors at UIUC. The classes he teaches all follow a very similar format. The workbook(302) is very helpful as it has sample tests/exercises that resemble the exams in class. There was extra credit in all his classes. Lectures may sometimes be dry, but he is very passionate and is just an awesome guy.
He should more time explaining the math portion.
Hes okay. He is good at teaching what is going to be on the test but his political views on business regulations are extreme.
One of the best professors I have had. He gives so much material to get you ready for all the exams and the final exam as well.
Excellent teacher!!!

Do the extra credit each time you do a homework assignment so you don't have to cram it all in later. Went to class probably three times. Wasn't a good idea because I had to study for like 30 hours to have any idea what was going on on the practice final exams in the book. And the final was way harder than the other exams. Still got an A tho

I thought this class would be an easy A, it wasn't. Buckley is good teacher, but the lectures are dry and hard to focus on. I 1000% recommend getting the notes packet and not slacking. Do as much of the extra credit as you can and study! Discussion was optional, but I advise that you go.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: He is incredibly enthusiastic and energetic. He is a great lecturer, even though the material itself is really boring. Try to go as often as you can because he will often include really interesting historical segments regarding the subject at hand. I did not go to office hour, but he seems like a very kind and approachable guy.

He actually cares about students - there's just so many in this class that it's hard to really connect with him in any meaningful way. He makes economics fun. If you pay attention in class, do the extra credit, and use the study booklet you should have a fairly easy A.
He's a good professor who definitely is very passionate about the subject, but the class was way too basic and easy, especially since you get so many practice exams that are very close to the exams.

ECON 302 is just not a hard course. I found his lecture quite tedious because he digs on to something really easy, but doesn't cover that much important stuff (like he has 50 slides of doing derivatives but just skims through important concepts).
He thoroughly explains simple concepts but does not prepare you for the super difficult exams. He offers LOTS of extra credit and the online homework is super easy.

Professor Buckley is a fantastic lecturer. If you go to class and the discussion sections a majority of the time and do the workbook problems, you'll get an A. He's also accessible outside of class if you need any help. He genuinely cares about his students.

Bryan isn't the most exciting lecturer, but he is good at explaining the material. Class is optional and he posts all of the lectures online. If you take his class, I recommend doing the extra credit mastery points on the homework site.

Buckley is a decent teacher but wastes so much time in lecture talking about the simplest concepts.  However, the tests are difficult and full of tricky questions. He does not curve tests. He lets you skip tests, but that is a poor strategy, and I have no clue why he offers it. He goes off on tangents about his opinions. Be ready to teach yourself
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Buckley is a great guy. In his class there are 4 exams (1 final). If you want you can skip all 3 exams except the final, it just puts all the weight on the final. If you are someone who skips lectures but is willing to study before the exam, do the practice exams they are just like the final. 100% on the homework is so easy, and its 30% of ur grade

Easy A+ class. The lectures are long and boring but you do not need to attend them. Work on his past exams in the workbook and you will do well in the class. It is easy to get 100% on the homework and he also gives a lot of extra credit.

SUPER EASY! Just do the some problems on the workbook and review the lecture notes before test you will be find. He really gave so many extra credits like 5%. I got an 95 on this course but end up with a 100% because of the extra credit.

He is a good teacher and has incorporated some good teaching techniques in order to make the material interesting and clear cut.  His ECON 302 Final was tricky but his mids are medium hard, few small tricks to keep you on your toes.  He is a good teacher who has embraced tech (lectures slides odf) and personality to make learning smoother.

VERY boring lecture (especially when they are longer than 50min) but overall his exams are fair. Homework will take about 10 minutes to complete so make sure you are actually learning the material as you go along and not cram everything before the test.

Does the best he can with lectures given the dry material. Homework is a piece of cake and worth a decent amount of the overall grade. Be sure to pay attention in lecture and do the practice problems, as the test a based very closely off of these.

He is one of the best econ professors here. The lectures are actually really helpful and he is great at explaining things. Tests are not tricky if you go to the lectures and discussions. Buckley is the man

Great lecturer, is extremely clear in presenting the material and goes through many different examples on the power points he posts online with step by step instructions on how to solve it. Always goes into great detail on questions and is passionate about the material. Only downside is there's little interaction with students

Professor Buckley is great. I find econ boring  but he's great at explaining things and making lectures interesting. He always finds a way to tie in real life situations with the example problems and show how certain economic decisions affect us. You can tell he really cares about his students and teaching. Exams aren't hard if you go to lecture.

Bombed the first exam. studied the practice exams in the practice book, got 98s on the next two. My final exam grade is unknown, but I got an A in the course. Do the homework, pay attention in class and do not fall behind.
Swaggy B is dope. Tests are decently hard, final was impossible. Homework is the easiest 30% you'll ever get. Take Vazquez for the easy A, take Buckley (or DIianni apparently) if you want to learn.
He posts all the needed material. You can skip lectures and discussions. However, do the weekly practices and read the lecture to get yourself ready for the test.
Multiple attempts for homework. You can definitely get 100% on homework. Tests are not hard at all, as long as you read the lectures and do the weekly practices. He posts all the needed material.

Not a bad teacher, but he's a too aligned with rules. I accidentally missed the final for my section, asked if I could take the final with the other section, denied me. Thought that was a little harsh especially when I explained to him I was a senior and needed the course to graduate. If you take him, be sure to keep up to date with class.

He really tries to make class fun but I just find intermediate microecon super boring. The 1.5 hour lectures are too long and I literally stopped going to class after the first midterm. Tests aren't too bad but you need to study really hard if you don't go to class. Some say he's easier than Dillani, some find Dillani a better teacher.
He cares about his students and actually TEACHES. Recommend him if you actually care about learning.

Plain and Simple, Dr. Buckley. He is a man who is VERY interested in economics. He tells you what is on the midterms, because his lectures are structured around them. TA Wkshts are helpful for FRQ on midterms and online HW is for Multiple Ch. Coming to the midterms, they were fairly simple. Overall, Dr. Buckley does his job and does it well.

He taught pretty well and his lecture videos were very helpful in understanding the readings. It was honestly just a lot of work, from video lecture notes, to live lecture assignments, weekly annotations, video assignments, and exams. The main thing I didn't like was having to annotate a long article every week, but class was thought provoking.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: This class is awful. He says he gives you a optional zoom. THAT SHIT IS NOT OPTIONAL. You have to go. Also the way everything is structured sucks. This is a gen ed but he treats it like its the entrance to the military.

Good lectures and the exams were easy enough as long as you read the texts/ watched the lectures. Heads up for a bunch of Canvas notifications but they are mainly small daily assignments. Tons of extra credit and regrade opportunities so nothing to complain about here
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: this was a really fun class! i especially like the peer reviews, but some students clearly submitted chat-generated answers (peep those mad down below) which made my efforts to comment on their work feel useless, but overall fun and I learned a lot :)

Entertaining and easy if you're willing to put in the effort. It's an ACP class, so expect essays for an exam, but note that the essay is easy as long as you know your material. For example, what are the views of the philosophers in question and have an example or two prepared. ProctorU is terrible, but it can't be helped bc of ChatGPT cheaters.

Worst class ever taken. Horrible course structure. Disorganized. This guy hold his “lecture” on time that's different than class you see on self service. Nothing on syllabus is kept. 8am proctoru essay exams that are completely bs. Do no take this class.

This class is very doable. 2 readings (annotations) every week which takes about 2 hours. The 8AM essays exams were annoying but the TAs grade pretty fair and the regrade was generous. I averaged an 80 on the first ones and 88 after regrade. There's 7 pts extra credit too. The class is disorganized though so you need to stay on top of it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: There are some problems with the online format (ProctorU bs) but compared to other online classes I've taken, this has been much smoother. This is a ACP course, so we have to write pretty frequently, but the essay exams are short (
I would not recommend this class. The professor and course were unorganized. Assignments and homework were made up along the way. Assignments did not have a set schedule for due dates

Fun lectures and teaching style, very interesting material. Really nice guy, very easy to talk to and intelligent. Does not answer emails all the time, janky final exam. You'll need to put effort into your papers, but it's worth it because it does develop your philosophical writing skill.

He told a lot of fun stories but the homework was actually insane. Depending on your TA the class can be miserable. Definitely would not suggest taking this class if you don't like writing 1,000-word essays as your final during a class period.

Way to much homework given for a philosophy class that is 3 credit hours. Almost an hour of homework for every other day and it is really irrelevant homework that is due unless your interested in Philosphy. If you want an A you really have to try. Also 2 hours to write an 1,000 word essay for final exam which is insane.

Don't take! Way to much homework is given for no reason and he made 2 homework assignments due the same day as an exam. Exams are the WORST! 3 1,000 word essays are the exams and you have to do it on ProctorU which I understand but it is impossible to write an 1,000 word essay in the little canvas box without being able to do anything else.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: As long as you do the assignments and give them their due diligence, it's hard not to do well in this course. Doing all the assignments gives lots of extra credit and essay rewrites make getting a good grade pretty stress-free. I thought the readings were mostly fair for an intro PHIL course. It does get a bit tricky when it gets to Mill and Kant.

His teaching style is decent, and CS124 is an easy class if you are keeping pace. Most of the quizzes are easy, especially if you took a practice quiz, and you can make up missed quiz questions later for full credit. The tutoring system is also very well thought out and works great.

MPs are just vibe coding... 
you can't learn anything
How does he have a higher rating than Nowak, lol? Honestly, both of them are pretty rough CS instructors at UIUC. If I could, I would rate him a 0

The professor is almost never present in person. The course is essentially fully online, so you end up teaching most of the material to yourself. When you email the professor, you typically do not receive a response (or he responds but in an extremely rude way). He does not know how to respect students. Never take this course.
MP basically was intro to vibe coding. But eventually you gotta teach yourself!
Professor Challen is a really good teacher and cares a lot about what he is teaching. As long as you do the work and dont fall behind, then you will succeed in this class.

A fully-online* introductory programming class offered in two languages, probably easy for people with prior coding experience and challenging otherwise. The class is well-designed for an online class, but sometimes not engaging with too many videos. Changes coming next semester include (probably) required in-person discussion sections.

The CS 124 website is pretty helpful to learn all of the basics. The quizzes were alright, allowing for 1 retake the week after. However, the Machine Project at the end of the semester was harder than it needed to be. We didn't even apply what we learnt in the first half of the semester. It was just telling Claude code to pass the tests.

The class was easy since I was not new to programming. However, the project was a bit difficult. Make sure to do well in the first few quizzes since the last few can be hard. There is a quiz every week and no final exam. The quizzes offer a re-take of the previous week's quiz. I do think that he and the tutors can be kind of rude sometimes.
i mean its alright, a fine intro course, kotlin is chill and you get to vibecode

This course is overall very easy, especially after taking AP CSA in high school, but it's definitely still beginner-friendly. The course did not properly prepare for the MP at the end of the course, which was definitely the most frustrating part. Also, he and almost all tutors were often very aggressive when students would ask very valid questions.

Quizzes are 70% of course grade, but you can make corrections on them the following week after you take them the first time. MP is stupid and really complicated, but Challen is very nice and his videos are very informative and teach you well. It is very important to stay on track of daily lessons to keep up in the class.
Good lessons on CS124. I took APCSA in high school but didn't understand anything but I understood everything that was taught in this course

I took java in high school and the course was pretty easy, I capitalized on the quizzes and was able to not do much on the MP, MP was very confusing and the CA's sometimes lead you on a stray path rather than properly helping. Don't wait till last second on the MP, start early so you can get proper help.
Never saw him once. the whole thing was online

(Had prior Java experience) The everyday lectures and weekly quizzes were fine. Grading was fair. The MP was atrocious. Thrust into android programming without explaining most of what we were doing was recipe for disaster. Stack up extra credit from early deadlines so you can avoid doing the last parts.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Weekly quizzes and daily homework. Not hard as long as you put in some time and effort. I had some pre knowledge and slacked a bit but still was on the border of a B+/A-. The MP is hard but doable, just go to the in person tutoring at the CIF (online tutoring isn't very helpful).

It's a pretty manageable course as long as you put the time and work in. I did learn a lot and the lessons were usually pretty clear with multiple explanations for topics. There's a lot of time commitment with daily homework though. The MP was also a pretty big jump in difficulty from the work done throughout the semester.

Geoff, in my opinion, is a decent professor. He makes most things clear and gives you plenty of resources to succeed. This course is A LOT of work though. You'll be doing daily assignments and homework. It can be difficult but it is possible. My best advice for taking this course is to pay attention, ask for help, and google things occasionally.
decent - MP's are being improved -

i really enjoyed this course. the difficulty of the course depends on your experience coming in. i had no experience in cs at all but i found i could learn everything i needed through the course generally. BUT, some things aren't explained really clearly in the lessons and you may benefit from outside resources and def the tutoring site...

I think that this is a pretty good course, with good and helpful videos, but it is definitely intense. You can take it as an intro class but it will probably require a fair amount of work if you haven't coded before. I think that the machine project is somewhat poorly managed, but it is very doable especially with the extra credit offered.

Challen is not a bad person, he does offer alot of quirks for extra credit. However this class is ALOT of work especially if this is your first coding class, NOT beginner friendly and moves at a fast pace. There are weekly quizzes which are alot of work. If you have never coded do not take class along with 3 tech class u will be miserable.

Fully-online daily lectures, lessons, and homework. Weekly quizzes that aren't super difficult. Class is easy for the most part, the semester project can be difficult if you're new to coding.  Online tutors are always busy so go to the in person tutoring. Utilize course forum, as you will prob never speak to challen in person.

I get a high grade but I will not say I ace in Java after this class. Only finished 40 percent of the lectures because I was dissociating of his metaphor all the time, not very intuitive and it's definitely better turn to Youtube. Be aware of BIG lines when you want to get some in-person tutoring for mp, which for many of you have to do.

It is an online lecture based class. If you are new to coding, please for the love of god go to the in person discussion to get help if you need. Online tutors do not help at all. Other than that, no matter previous experience, you will be fine if you put the work in. If you dont put work in, you will suffer even if you are good at coding.

This is a very good course that teaches you how to write and implement codes. Although there is a quiz once a week, you'll be fine if you review the lessons carefully and do some practice problems. Professor Challen is a good instructor, and he often answers questions I posted on the forum. The grading policies are also very lenient. Easy A.

Great instructor, made learning Java fun and informative. Most of his homework is easy but some will stump you. Excellent material to prepare for exams. The final MP for the class is the most difficult aspect of it which you may find to get stuck on. Depending on class TA's you may be helped or provided little direction but the project is doable.

Geoffrey Challen (previously gwa) is a character. My favorite pastime is logging onto the forum to see 80 new questions with only 10 one-word answers by Geoffrey which are at best useless and at worst rage maxxing. Do you know how infuriating it is to wake up in cold sweat after reading the words "what debugging steps have you taken?" everyday.

As someone who's taken AP CS A before, I have no idea how anyone in CS 124 w/ no prior Java experience could succeed. The class is structured in a weird way, and the final MP deals with topics that we have never learned before. Geoff gets angry whenever someone asks for help, ignoring half the questions about grading in an attempt to be mysterious.
very hands off and fast paced

Challen knowns a lot about what he is teaching however CS 124 is not a course for a student who has no experience in coding. The course goes at an insane pace, weekly quizzes that are very hard to study for, the TA's aren't helpful, and at the end of the semester you are thrown into a final project that is really confusing with little guidance.
Lectures were clear, and while the prof doesn't provide much 1-on-1 help, the weekly quizzes and challenging MPs kept me on track. I found Unstuck ai super helpful for quiz prep - saved me some time.

Funny how previous classes had starter code and the Homework was worth more as a percent of grade, therefore you're getting punished for taking the class later, funny aint it? Love the way the practice quizzes aren't representative of the exam, so sad!

He clearly knows his stuff and can teach it pretty well, but it's just confusing when you get to the project. Generally, I usually felt pretty well about the course until the project, but still, it wasn't that hard bc of AI.
absolute stressful and awful experience

He was chill but gave no guidance whatsoever. We had to do a lot of things, and since everything was horrendously taught, you were on your own. The MPs were hell because they threw you into Android Studio with zero preparation, which wasn't very pleasant. Thankfully, we could use ChatGPT(it was allowed for the MP) because it made no sense.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Probably won't have face to face interactions with Geoffrey, but his lectures were clear for the most part.

CS 124 itself was structured decent. Expect weekly quizzes (2 hours of studying), + 1 hour of daily homework/lessons. There is a useful help site as well.

The prof. takes feedback and cares and is decent w/ his videos, but sometimes his material is irrelevant to quizzes. For Machine Projects or hard HWs, use AI because lessons aren't good enough.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: The course was online, but the only interaction from the professor was his reused recorded lesson videos from who knows how many semesters ago. The course was not objectively difficult, but it was often hard to pay attention and get through all the content in the daily lessons. Not all the topics were covered in detail and were often pretty long.

This class is structured to be as hands off as possible for Geoffrey. After creating the initial content its essentially reused work semester to semester. And this is honestly a good thing. Geoffrey is not helpful and doesn't want to be. He clearly wants to put off as much as he can on the 200 different Assistants and collect his paycheck.
He's cool

I had almost zero experience in programming, and this was the perfect intro to cs for me. prof challen always go above and beyond when explaining concepts. My favorite memories from this class are when he takes 10 minutes explaining a concept, then write the whole logic in one line.
He's a great professor also a nice person. The syllabus is kind. The challenging tasks are the MPs, also the weekly quizzes. Overall, you work hard, you will get an A

This course is pretty difficult for those who never coded before, I think I spent around an hour minimum everyday on the lessons to keep up with the coursework. It is straightforward if you do the work, but what was frustrating was that when I was sick, the profressor didn't offer many accomodations and recommended that I drop the course instead.

As someone who did not have any previous coding experience, this course was difficult for a supposedly "introductory course." There are daily lessons that took around 40-80 minutes and quizzes every week which are straightforward if you keep up with the work everyday, but they don't offer many accommodations to missing work when you are sick.

Even if you didn't take APCSA, which I didn't, the class is still very easy and it's not hard to keep up. Expect 40-80 min of work a day. Love the help site and the help staff! Some of the walkthroughs should be abridged, but most were fine. Quizzes are very straightforward, and you get drops. I would definitely not call it a nightmare.

The class is completely online and asynchronous. Daily homework assignments, weekly quizzes, and no final exam. Towards the end of the semester, there are 2 MPs. These were tough because support is hard to find and they're new. The website was very well organized though, and many drops were offered for homework and quizzes. Def recommend!

Class is decently easy as long as you keep up with daily lessons, the TAs on the help site were great. Easy to see how to earn points but finally grading scale happens at end of year but most will get an A so don't worry. Lecture recordings felt longer than they needed to be, and the MP could use some better explaining but the TAs are great help.

The course itself is fairly easy. Tests are pretty straightforward but daily lessons were too long and repetitive. The professor offered no support through the forum or help site. He also lacks compassion. I had a medical condition at the start of the semester and inquired about a 1 day extension on homework and he suggested that I drop the class.

CS124 is a class set up for you to succeed. If you put in the work, you can easily get an A (no A+s). However, in terms of support from Challen, don't expect any. He will almost always direct you to the tutoring site; however, the TAs and CAs on there are extremely helpful especially when the MP is on, queue is on the longer wait time though.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I had Java experience before, though it could be difficult for those without. All interactions are with course staff at not Challen, MPs were hard (don't delay those), and voice recordings got old fast, but the course is designed for everyone to get a good grade (with a good amount of drops). So you can learn a lot and get a good grade (avg. 95%).

Extremely slow and repetitive. You have to be incredibly patient to listen to someone saying the same thing for over 15 minutes. Problem is (and this was a challenge for me), during those 15 minutes, he does say some important things in the middle. So after losing attention due to repetition I needed to rewatch the whole thing.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class is a good introductory class, but if you haven't taken APCSA, it can go by really fast and it can be hard to keep up. If you have taken it, then it becomes a lot of review and not that worth it. The lectures can be annoying at times and long, but they are easy enough. The HW was never too hard to manage and the help was good too.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: My first CS course, a living hell and a nightmare. Would not recommend it for people who are unfamiliar with programming and have a decent workload. Time-consuming, daily homework. Many people found this course easy, but there are also many who are struggling through it. Poor explanations, doesn't know how to teach.

Nothing to say about the professor; from my point of view, those lectures are clear and straightforward. CS124 itself is easy, so I never went to the office hours. The most challenging part is the quizzes since there is nothing for you to prepare with them. Generally, if you put effort into it, you will get a decent grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
This is a coding class that encourages you to not test your code. All the assignments are hosted through the website but he doesn't let you copy / paste so it's hard to test it in an environment that actually allow unit tests.

I honestly thought he was joking half the time because the stuff he was telling us was so wrong.

CS 125 in a nutshell:

1. Challen builds own website.
2. Part of website doesn't work.
3. Someone reports that part of website doesn't work.
4. Challen gets mad about it and doesn't want to accept responsibility.

Easily the most egotistical teacher I've had in my time at Illinois.
the videos are annoying. make sure to watch them at no less than 1.5x speed to save some time
