He's okay. Lecture is understandable, not too much homework, office hour avaliable for everyone. Just the attitude is not great, he always seem to have a bad day, and when you meet him in OH he will not explain further about anything he said in the lecture, even some of those are confusing.
There were very few times when I felt lost in this course, even as a beginner to Java. Just make sure to do all of the homeworks. Grading policy is very fair.

If you put in work this class is an easy A. That being said there is a lot of homework and weekly quizzes, although I thought the class was totally fair and think anyone can succeed. Do not get behind on homework and the MP, try to do all of the homework a bit early and take advantage of the help site. I chose Kotlin, and enjoyed the class greatly.

Get ready for a world of pain. 
A new Lesson every day from Monday to Friday, each due the day after it is scheduled.
Each lesson is 45 minutes of reading and watching the lecture, and the homework itself takes half an hour upto 2 hours.
Do not fall behind on your homework or quizzes.
Work in groups, and make sure to get those MPs in early

despite having decent lectures, he provides zero actual help. The course staff are not well versed enough in CS to provide adequate help to all the people in the course, and Challen is pretty much MIA when you need him. Also, the MPs are nothing like what we learn about in class but he expects you to know how to do them yourself.

Challen has created the CS124 website to have a HEALTHY buffer of course assistants and TAs to provide help to the students with their computer science problems. This is good; however, if you have an issue with something that the help site cannot solve, good luck because if you contact him directly he will just push you back to the help website.
Provides little to not help on instruction. Students that do not already have a background in computers should stay far away.

I mean the mps were too hard for me. Every week we have quizzes and we had to study online, so inefficient and so much work of research needed on our end. For those that doesn't have a lot of time and no prior experience should rethink wether to take this course.

The course as a whole is pretty good. Challen's videos and walkthroughs are the best and he is quite personable. On the forum he does come off as condescending and definitely has built a hierarchy so that he can have as little interaction with students as possible. Overall, CS124 has a beautiful website and a great overall structure.
You do not know what you're signed up for until week 4.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This was my first ever CS class and I actually really enjoyed it, despite its difficulty. Biggest problem for me was the time sink, but I did learn a lot! Challen himself sometimes seemed a bit rude on the forum, and the MPs made me feel incredibly lost. I would recommend using the help site a lot. It's an easy A if you just put in the time.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Challen is super passive-aggressive & practically non-existent through the course, leaving the job of helping to the TAs and CAs. They work on a help site, but it can take hours to get help, especially towards the end of the semester. His motto is basically "don't let students ask for help." He also spends more time on Reddit than answering emails.

CS 124 as a class was relatively easy, if you have experience. Some hw problems were tough, but that's expected. What isn't expected is Challen's arrogance. He is so passive-aggressive to students on the forum - it's like he hates being asked questions. He takes criticism personally, or as an attack on the course. The MP was also very tedious.

Moving CS 125 to online teaching is a milestone of Computer Science education at UIUC. I like his quote: "Do little bits every day." This is so true and it will always influence me and my future computer science career.

Avoid if possible. Online daily lessons & homework that get progressively harder & take hours to complete followed by a weekly quiz. Watch Colleen's lessons for better explanations. Be prepared to wait up to 1.5 hours to get help from TA's as you try to meet the deadlines. Challen also has a condescending attitude towards students on the forums.

Had prior coding experience and this class was still fairly painful, purely because of how inaccessible help is at some points in the semester. If I didn't have a TA I personally knew by pure coincidence, then getting help would've taken hours every time I would've needed it.

Professor didn't really exist all semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
Professor Challen is a very smart man. He has set up an impressive ecosystem on his CS website with multiple voices explaining a concept and ample practice problems.

In terms of coursework, his course is fast-paced. Some advanced topics are taught in this introductory course but in an approachable way. Quizzes are difficult; take the practices.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: I had no prior CS experience. If you find first 8 weeks hard, drop because second half is way harder. ~45min online lecture + hw that takes 30min-3hr 5 days a week. MP's explained terribly with 50 people waiting for help at one time often. Watch Colleen's vids, she better at explaining. Prof is nice, but typical CS guy who can't hide his arrogance.

Professor Challen's lecture videos are super helpful with understanding the materials and preparing for the weekly quizzes. The TAs & CAs are also super friendly in answering questions I've throughout the semester on homeworks and the MP. Great class overall!!!

I had no prior coding experience & I learned ALOT within one semester. There is a lesson & homework assigned everyday, with a weekly quiz. There is a help site that you can join for help, that honestly saved me loads of stress. You are allowed 9 homework drops & 3 quiz drops. He is a great teacher but have not seen or spoken to him once.

Class is somewhat easy if you've had prior experience in high school, but likely more difficult for people new to CS. The site the class is on is very impressive and it is somewhat convenient to get help. The course can be done in Java or Kotlin. Just put in the effort and it will be rewarding, especially so for the newbies.

Geoffrey Challen is a great professor. He is caring and very knowledgeable about computer science. The class is beginner friendly but is a harder adjustment for people just learning to code. This is acknowledged by the three quiz drop and nine homework drop policy. Stay consistent, don't skip lessons, and do practice problems, and you will succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Easily my biggest disappointment with the class was the staff, especially in regards to Prof Challen. Inside his videos he seems fairly approachable, but outside of that (and in forum posts especially) he is outright condescending and rude. For a beginner class, the way he treats students who don't have prior knowledge leaves much to be desired.

The class is quite large so I don't blame issues with how it is taught/handled solely on the professor. Unless you have already become comfortable with coding prior to the class, expect at least 10 hours a week on this class on the lighter weeks. Weeks with MPs, expect ~20 hours if you are having any issues. Quizzes become extremely challenging.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Quizzes and MPs are ridiculous. This class with make beginner coders depressed and declining mental health. I am turned off to the CS minor now. I'm never taking a CS class here again.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: We have 127 people (Instructor + heads + TAs + Associates + Assitants) of cs124. And I post a question on t he forum, no one is willing to help. Just let the question stay there and let me lose points.

this class has gotta be worth more than 3 credits. there are lectures and homework every weekday that take over an hour to complete. not to mention this class is daunting for me as i haven't coded before, even though the class is literally an introduction to computer science. safe to say i'm turned off from a computer science minor

I had success in lowerlevel CS classes but this one is terrible. Class is great until week 3. Hw is everyday and is 2+ hours to complete. Quizzes are downright unfair and prep with TAs is guided in the wrong direction. He is also condescending on the forum.  He doesn't consider that we have other classes besides this one and expects 30 hours/week.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is a good prof who is passionate about teaching CS, HOWEVER CS 124, is said to be a beginner course, which as a beginner, I did not find super beginner friendly and oftentimes found myself lost on the MP. The quizzes were ok, but I found them kinda hard if I am being honest. For anyone taking 124: Please learn a little bit of java beforehand.
Prof. Challen is just wonderful. He is caring, responsible, and passionate about teaching!

Understands that beginning Computer Science in college can be really scary and provides multiple lesson videos that slowly build up beginner skills. At the same time, Challen reminds you that what you put in is what you get out of this class. If you don't put in the daily work, you're not going to understand what you're doing.

To succeed you really just have to stay on track with the daily assignments. The class is also quite forgiving as there are numerous quiz and hw drops and coding problems do not subtract points for a wrong attempt. You also have a very clear idea of what your grade is which is very helpful. A lot more good to be said such as the help site.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not that hard for java beginners (especially looking back from cs128 lol). The MPs and homework are challenging but manageable. Put in effort and you will be fine.
Easily killed my interest in programming.

Just want to share some thoughts about Professor Challen: He did a great job in making our course website more stable. Then he gives specific suggestions to all my problems posted in the forum. I know computer science class is abstract and even tough for some of the students, but as long as you start studying, it will be ok.

Easily one of the worst professors I have ever had. Changed CS125 for the worse and made it impossible to do as someone who did not know CS coming into college, while being advertised as a class with no prior CS knowledge.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: great professor; knows his stuff; it's an easy class if you put in the effort

CS 124 is permanently online, so its format is quite different from other courses. That being said, there's a lot to learn and I think Geoff is a great prof with extensive experience and knowledge. He's active on the forums and answers all student questions, whether they'r directly related to course material or not, and the lessons are interesting.

The class is broken down into daily tasks. You have a lesson everyday (relatively small lesson) and all you have to do is complete the homework lesson at the end of the lesson. It's not that bad, usually took me 1 hour a day depending on difficulty. Just stay on track, don't fall behind, and you'll be fine.

I took this class in 2018 and assumed when I dropped out that I wasn't meant for programming. However, after returning to programming years later I'm convinced this is the wrong way to teach an introductory class. You should really have prior programming experience before you decide to take this.

Professor Challen know his stuff but is rude and condescending if you need help with anything. everyday the help site has over a 3 hour wait and even then you dont get the help you need. Professor Challen basically tells you to figure it out yourself and does not actually help anyone. Difficult class with hard quizzes and very hard MP.

He's a pretty great teacher honestly and I've really enjoyed learning Java through his lectures. The HW and quizzes can be kinda tough so I recommend studying the past week's lessons to prepare. However, I can't recommend it enough if you're willing to put in the effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This class moves real fast tbh. Like it's 3 credit hrs, but I (a relative beginner to cs) put in at least 12 hours a week for it, and if there is a week where i don't then i get like a 68 on the quiz. Basically a loooot of work, but at least you know you are learning cool and life-changing skills. Even if it sometimes feels like u want to just cry
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Class is moderate hard and so as the quiz, but like this professor so much that this is actually the first time that I figure how to code.

I've graded him once before and now my opinion has changed. Quizzes are practically hard and homework can be tough to decipher. Says that you can succeed even with no experience, but I strongly think that you must have extensive knowledge of computer science to pass this class. Do not take this course if you have no experience.

One time I entered Professor Challen's Zoom office hours to get help setting up my MP, and he chastised me for interrupting his "social hours" with the students (I was the only one in the room). He teaches CS concepts very well and definitely knows his stuff, but I don't like him personally.

On the outside, he seems friendly and caring. But if you really get to know him on a deep, personal level, you'll know he's disrespectful, rude, and full of himself. And I don't take those words lightly, especially when I'm writing a review here.

Geoff is what I wish every instructor was. His lessons cover absolutely everything you need to succeed, replies instantly on the forum, cares about the success of the students, grants extensions, and encourages you every step of the way. The learning environment for CS 125 is set up extremely well to provide you with all the help you need to learn.

The class website is very well-designed and the grading is generous. The MPs are very difficult for people with limited prior coding exposure but the staff is always there to help. Quizzes are difficult at times but the lowest three quiz scores are dropped from the final grade. Highly recommend for anyone looking to learn basic programming skills.

The class website is good, but the quizzes are a little hard and the MPs are downright impossible for people who are beginners at coding and completely disconnected from what we are learning in regular lessons. Would most certainly not recommend for beginners despite the fact that this is an "intro" course. Getting an A is a struggle.

Most people I know either love or hate Challen. I'm in the first camp. He's the best professor I've had at UIUC. Challen is extremely well organized. He is upbeat and intelligent. I'd never coded before taking CS125, and I'm getting an A. If you actually take the time to study the daily lessons then the homeworks and mps are pretty easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Awesome class

By far my favorite professor at UIUC so far! The daily lectures were perfectly paced and interactive! Having taken another CS class that isn't going as well, I really miss the structure of CS 125. Super easy to get feedback on MPs or general help with the content.

I took this course because it was supposed to be an introduction to computer science. However this class expects you to already be experienced with thinking like a programmer beforehand. I ended up spending hours on homework that should have taken a few minutes at most. Quizzes are very difficult and MPs are even worse.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Geoff doesn't like students sending him individual emails but it makes sense because you can literally get the same questions answered on the forum in 5 minutes or less by him or by the staff. His lectures are very thorough and helpful and he's very passionate and knowledgeable about CS. Great professor who's always got your back.

He is the best professor I ever met, really. The daily practice is great. The Mp is a little bit hard, but if you work hard and spend enough time it would be great. However, the quizzs and exams are hard, if you want A, you need to pay more attention to the homework and Mps.

Claim's "You're not alone", and tells you to get help when you need it. He repeats this in nearly every lesson but when you go to get the help you're shamed or given a sarcastic comment. He's quick to blame students for not understanding something. I would never take a class with him again. Seems to despise his students
He is the best. MPs and weekly quizs could be tough, but you'll be OK.

He is a very good professor and he knows how to help his students very well. I was brand new to programming and this class really helped make me understand it more. The class is a lot of work and you need to watch the walkthroughs the Professor puts online to understand what is happening in the class.

Need to have some experience in CS or a friend who can help you out! Otherwise, you may spend hours on the daily homework problems! You get several drops on different assignments, but you will not do well in the class if you don't have what I've said is needed.

Geoff's teaching style is incredibly clear and concise. The daily lessons are structured well in content and also delivery. I admit, for someone truly coding for the first time - the course will take you 1-2 hours every day. If coding isn't for you, however - it's best you realize it as soon as possible. (class median: 93)

I was definitely a beginner in CS, but Geoff is an extremely caring professor and does a really good job of explaining all of the material. The class is hard though, with homework due every day and five lectures a week. Luckily the class is well organized and has lots of office hours, but even still the quizzes and MP are a real pain.

If I have to rate him in detail I would give a 4.8 for him, but there is no option for it. He's the one of the best instructors I have for fall 2020. The workload is really heavy since there are 5 lectures per week and also a HW followed after the lecture. The only bad thing about this course is the machine project. Would be better if he removes it
The best CS prof to ever exist :-)

One of the best run/structured classes you will ever find. Do not be fooled by the "Intro" title because you should be prepared to put in many hours into MPs as the semester goes by. However, he is definitely NOT reachable or accessible as a professor. He has a lot on his plate. I have heard he can be very unfriendly when you reach out personally.

Geoff is a great lecturer. He made you work for the class. Though the MPs are hard and extremely time-consuming, it was all worth the timeone of the best courses I have ever taken at UIUC. You would learn a great deal from the class. Take the class if you have time and are ready for some challenges.

Excellent guy! He wants every student to succeed and will do whatever it takes for them to appreciate the subject. The class has a good mix of fun and hard work. Put some effort and I guarantee youll get an A and even pursue this field for life. Do take this class, even if you have nothing to do with CS.

The class was straightforward. Go to lecture, do the daily homework, start MPs early, go to office hours and ask questions. This is not a low-time class, but it's very worth it. You definitely need to go to office hours to finish the MPs . The homework was incredible. Wish I still had access to the homework problems honestly.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Fantastic teacher and human being. He really cares that students succeed and has organized CS 125 with daily homework assignments that really reinforce the concepts. Super innovative approach that I wish more of my other professors would take with the online forum and mobilizing such a large course staff. You will learn so much in this class!!

Sometimes he goes too fast for beginners, but listens and changes his style according to feedback. The MP's are challenging and don't have much to do with the material. The only thing is that I wish the weekly CBTF were not 30% of the grade; he said they were only to make sure we were doing the homework, so why make it make or break our grade?

Geoff is a super great teacher and really cares about his students. I had almost no prior coding experience coming into the course so the quizzes and MPs were very challenging to me. This class is definitely hard.

The MPs are irrelevant to the lectures and the prof is not good at teaching. Either he has typos in the ppts or he would just mention sth and say that we would learn about that later and then never talks about it again.

Great professor, very passionate about CS materials and thinks for his students. I was a beginner in programming and I did have a bad time in the first few weeks, but it's not hard once you get used to it.

Geoff is really nice and he's passionate about computer science! This class requires a fair amount of work but it's an easy A as long as you do your work. The MP of this semester is harder than previous ones but he gave an extra drop for MP and apologize in class. I went to his office hour once and we had a really nice conversation.
It is very easy to get an A/A+ in his class, but there is a decent amount of work to be done. HW problems everyday and MPs biweekly. If you put in the work, you will get an A guaranteed.

The guy genuinely knows what he is talking about and I enjoyed studying under him. You will have to get used to the fact that he does only wear one t-shirt but I guess it just adds to the stereotype of Computer Science Majors; although, he is unusually fit for a person in CS.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This man is strong-minded, but won't act on impulse. We made an APP to recommend easy gen-eds, and while Geoff doesn't personally like the idea, he gave fair feedbacks of our project. He moves the class a bit fast, making the 4-credit-hour-class appearing like 6, but his lectures are decent. About MPs - beginners have to go to office hours!

Genuinely cares about teaching and his students. Sometimes he went too fast and this was very frustrating for beginners. However, he responds and changes according to anonymous feedback forms, which I really appreciated. There is daily homework and mandatory attendance, but also a generous drop policy.

His lectures were absolutely amazing, I know my stuff but I still learned a whole lot! His lectures are mandatory and you do have to keep up with the slides to get credit, but you also get 10 drop lecture attendances! His class isn't the easiest but he does his fair share to make an A worth what it is. If you work hard, A isn't an issue.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Geoff's CS125 class is fantastic. He has improved it constantly through feedback, and it will only get better. The class is hard, and will be a huge challenge for beginners. However, towards the end of the course, you will know all the work was worth it. He really cares about every student and really made the 9 AM lectures bearable.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: Really good prof, seriously.

Very good class. Everything is structured extremely well, and it's clear that Geoff has/is putting in a LOT of work towards improving the class. His lectures were very comprehensive, and whenever I didn't understand something, I could just look back into the lecture video. He cares a lot about his students, and the grading is very generous.

I had no coding experience coming to this class. Not gonna lie. This class was tough. Spent more than 10 -12 hours a week alone on Machine Problems. However, Geoff take feedback pretty well and will hear his students out. Going to office hours consistently will help you do well on this class. Class doesn't get easier, but its gets better.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: Don't get me wrong, CS125 is hard even to the ones who have taken ap in high school. But I honestly think geoff tries to guide us to understand the concepts. Also his grading system is really generous. I absolutely bombed my first midterm(68) and still end up getting A( 96) overall, he also gives lots of extra credits

There is no doubt that this is a hard course. Actually, it is the hardest course I have ever taken, even much harder than 400-level math courses. However, I do think Challen is a good professor. He is passionate about teaching, and always willing to give students help. The MPs are hard, but it turns out that we can learn a lot from this course!
Super hard midterm. Hmmm. Proving Fermat's Last Theorem is much easier than his midterm.

Geoff is awesome. He really cares about his students and is extremely helpful. I wasn't sure if I wanted to do CS but now I know I do. A word of caution: this class is hard and moves quickly. It's easy to get an A if you stay on top of it though.

Geoff clearly really cares about his students. You can really tell that he has put in a lot of effort to make this class accessible to as many people as possible. Don't get me wrong, this is not an easy class, but there are many resources available to help you do well.

He is patient and very good at explaining topics . There is a lot of work, but if you do it and study for quizzes/midterms, then you will get an A. He is very active at answering questions on the forum. The CAs/TAs are very helpful. There were few bugs in the assignments. The class was very well-organized.

CS 125 is more time-consuming than other intro courses, but Geoff has put a lot of effort into making it accessible to anyone (I personally had a little Python and no Java experience before taking CS 125). Daily quizzes, weekly homework, and biweekly machine problems (MPs) prevents slacking, but it's ultimately rewarding.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: This is one of the most amazing courses I've ever taken. It's REALLY hard for a starter to surive this course, but after coming along a long, long adventure, you find yourself harvest a great deal. The grading for this course is also generous, as about 50% people can get A. Take this if you love CS, or leave the seat for those who loves it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: For fall 2018 CS125, Indeed it's a little bit too hard even for people who have learned AP CS before, but it's worthwhile to work your ass off and learn a hell out. ECs were given. Geoff earns my respect at least. Really fantastic lecturer. :D

I don't really understand the Geoff hate on here. The class is tough, no doubt about it. But coming from someone with very little previous experience, his class is doable. He's also super active on the forum and on reddit which can be really helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: awfull
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I have never seen someone put so much effort in to teaching his students. Geoff really does care about teaching us and the amount of work he puts into it is reflective of that. Having a learned a little java prior to entering this class I thought I would be in for an easy A. Instead, I ended up learning SO MUCH. You will work for an A, but worth it
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: If you go to class, follow the slides (to get participation points), do the daily hws, work on the MP early and get 100 for all MPs, then you will do well on quizes and midterms. Since the grading components are really nice, if you work hard, you will get an A. ps: MP are worth a lot, make sure to do them and get 100 on them.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Explained things clearly and covered a lot of useful material. Only now that I'm at a different university do I realize how well taught CS 125 was. Some people love to hate on him because of the course difficulty, but it's worth it and you can definitely do it as long as you don't procrastinate.

Geoff gets a lot of slack in this class but he actually works incredibly hard to make this a better CS class/school. Before this year 125 was a very easy class, especially for those with experience. Now, however, it is difficult, and extremely difficult if you don't have experience. Be prepared to sell your soul, but you will learn a HELL OF A LOT.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: writes buggy code and teach in an idiotic way.

I enjoyed his lecturing A LOT. I think he is pretty talented as a lecturer. He expresses things pretty clearly. EVERYTHING MAKES SENSE in this class. In Spring 2018, we had more than 100 CA to answer our questions and help with MPs But don't need to worry about grades that much, most people got A at last. There are several extra credit opportunitie
Extremely arrogant.
Awful experience overall.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Challen is very passionate about teaching and he tries to make sure that help is always accessible. The grading is quite generous as 12% of students in Spring 2018 got 100% or higher in the class. However, the MPs are challenging and I would not recommend taking this class unless you are prepared to invest hours of work every week into the MPs.

While the course has a bit of a learning curve at the start, it is COMPLETELY worth it to get over the hump. Professor Challen runs his class well, and I learnt a lot in the course (much more than if I had taken CS 101). I went from having no knowledge of CS, to making a working android app. Highly recommend CS125 with Geoff - makes class great!

CS 125 was a challenging by rewarding class. I don't think the current rating of him (2.2) fits what I saw in my class (Spring 2018). Notes, quizzes, and MPs were all relevant. Everything was solvable, but not necessarily easy. You will learn a lot and have it be relevant knowledge in his classes. Put in the work and you will be fine.
I got mental illness after taking his class. His class is funny but the homework tortures me a lot. I am a freshman and I hope my rest of the college life can be better.

he is a nightmare. His quizzes involving coding and no particle credit is given. He announced all of this after the drop deadline makes me have no choice. He first said the final is on may 11th and said no traveling excuse for conflict. So I bought a new flight ticket with a really expensive price. Then he changed the date.

The professor writes bugs and sometimes make mistakes, but he never admits. He also talks a lot of useless words and forces you to listen during class, such as how many Android devices are around the world, and this is in the quiz! Not a introductory at all. If you are good at CS, take the proficiency, if not, take CS101 instead.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: This guy is awful. Never take his class.

It is indeed a tough class but when I come this far I feels good and get more adept at doing MPs and quizzes. As long as you devote enough time in and follow Prof's instruction carefully you would definitely achieve a desirable grade.

If you're good at math and have experience, it'll be a breeze. If not, it'll be hard. Prof. is a nice guy, his lectures DO pertain to the MPs, his test cases DO work, and he does care (unlike what other have said). Never been to office hours, but seems its crappy. If you're struggling, get help EARLY, and best from friends (make friends btw).
Although his 125 class, as a introductory cs class, is little bit challenging, he is definitely doing a good job as a cs professor and designed so many online systems useful to students.
This prof sucks.
His CS125 is not beginner friendly at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I admire his passion for CS and desire to make this class into something great, but this class is an intro class for CS and supposed to be accessible to those who haven't coded before but it is very far from that. Availability of office hours is amazing but it is often hard to find help there as it is so busy. If you want an A, get ready to work.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: The worst professor in this university!!!!!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: This is the worst professor I've ever seen in my life. Worst course. Lowest grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Yes the grading policy wasn't clear enough. But this is the first semester after he redesigned the course so it is understandable. He is definitely trying very hard to improve this course and he is doing perfectly fine. His lectures are very inspiring and this course has office hours running by CAs nearly 10 hours every day from Monday to Friday.
Worst course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Honestly speaking Geoff is an awesome professor. He has been trying to redesign CS125 since fall 2017 and made great progress so far. I agree that this is class is hard and the workload is heavy, but this is necessary for CS students, especially those who don't have previous coding experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: This course is not that hard overall, but the problem is that nobody really gets attendance points. That is 5%, and no matter how well you do in other parts, an A is impossible.

Now I know why everyone just chuckles when you say you have challen this semester. I'm not saying he is horrible, it's just the system. Attendance is absurd and nonfunctional. This is DEFINITELY NOT an INTRO to CS & MP is not related to lecture & office HR is crazy. The school says it is Intended as a first course with no prereq except math. LIES!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: The attendance system is really stupid. I attended every lecture but found the record showed that 8 lectures were missed. By the way, we are just at the half of this semester. I can't imagine what participation would I get at the end.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Unorganized Office Hour, Unclear documentation, Buggy testcases, Poor infrastructure
This course is too hard for beginners. I don't recommend students to take it without any background of cs. The lecture is never related to MP.
His teaching is not freshman friendly, but he puts a lot in improving the course.
Please, this is freshman level course. Don't make it too hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Incredibly easy and well designed course. There is a fair bit of work since it is an 8 week course, but assignments have videos to guide you. Exams while on ProctorU are open note and open lecture videos, so there is no reason to get an A in this course. Also tons of extra credit.

Honestly, I think Professor Choate's lectures were very fun and engaging! I have no background in meteorology and this class was not related to my major so it was nice to learn something new. Professor Choate makes all the deadlines and expectations for her class very clear, just do your work and take advantage of extra credit opportunities!

Super nice professor! Exams are tough but manageable with studying. Lots of extra credit opportunities if you come to lecture. Very knowledgeable about the subject and made me want to take more atmospheric sciences
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Love Jessie. I absolutely recommend this course, especially for ATMS majors. Everything is due Monday and so you structure your week yourself. SO much extra credit it's difficult not to get at least an A. It is a good amount of work (usually 8 hours a week) but it's simple. Help videos on every math/excel sheet that basically give you the answers.

This is my first online class I've taken, so I don't have much to compare it to, but I loved this class. Other reviews are heavily exaggerating the course load (imo), but this is an 8-week course, so expect to have to work more and faster. Has help videos for every math problem on the problem sets. Lots of extra credit got me from a B to an A-

DO NOT TAKE! There are like 3 lectures per week and each lecture there's like 3 separate videos that are like 30 minutes each (4.5 hours) and then there's readings, on top of that there's a midterm and a final but during the midterm week she still assigned the normal course load and I was struggling mentally and sent an email and she ignored it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: While the course isn't necessarily difficult, the amount of expected work is extremely unreasonable. Even with playing the pre-recorded lecture videos at 2x speed, a week's worth of assignments can easily take 8-10 hours of work. It's not hard to get a good grade, but the devotion it takes is laughable given the context of this course.

ATMS 120 is great for non-stem students looking for a science+math gen ed (Science & Quant 2). It's super easy and Professor Choate offers an insane amount of extra credit and provides step by step instructions for every math question. Only issue is that she tells you to buy an expensive textbook but we never used it, so don't bother buying.

Great, engaging lectures. SO much extra credit, would technically have a B but because of EC earned an A. There is a textbook but is completely optional, I didn't buy it and was completely fine. She posted weekly lab help videos that went step by step through all math. Highly recommend!!

Offers tons of extra credit, covering a subject that is both interesting and useful. 

She is exceptionally knowledgeable and passionate about the topic, delivering what are truly the best lectures ever.

Small class teaching, attendance not required, but attending can earn a lot of extra credit and guidance. 

Relatively challenging material, but the grading is fair. 

Extremely knowledgeable and passionate about the subject, gives the best lecture ever.

She is sooo super sweet. So much extra credit, I never did too hot on the labs but am able to still get a good grade because there is many many opportunities for extra credit. This is a great option for your science and quantitate reasoning gen eds.
She has amazing energy and gives out a lot of extra credit

Truly the best professor I have had at Illinois. Super caring for her students and was always there to help me out during office hours. Gives so much extra credit (All the answers are in the textbook but it is not needed as you can find them on quizlet). Do yourself a favor and take this class, its amazing.

Professor Choate is absolutely phenomenal. Even in an extremely large lecture she never failed to ensure that everyone was able to keep up and understand the content. The class itself can be challenging at times but is relatively easy. You're mainly graded on weekly labs with 3 unit tests (non-cumulative). Choate is one of the best profs I've taken
SOOOO much extra credit. Do yourself a favor and take this class. The labs are kind of annoying but they're fairly easy. Exams are all multiple choice and study guides are very thorough.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Jessie is amazing. If your looking for an easy A Quant. 2 course, look at ATMS 120. Its lecture heavy (no discussion sections), but the workload is light and the lectures are engaging. LOTS of extra credit (over 100%), tests are incredibly easy, and she walks you through every lab question. Little to no math, only on the labs. Plus you learn a lot.

This class is an easy quant 2 credit. Jessie gives lab help videos for all of the labs that take you through it step by step, even the Excel parts. Labs take about 2 hours to do. Lectures are 1 1/2 hours but she always finishes early, the longest lecture has been just over an hour. She points out iClicker questions that are test questions as well.

Best prof ever! She walks you through all of the math, best way to get QR credit! Exam reviews explain everything. She recognizes this is a gen ed for almost everyone, and focuses on making sure we get the credit and learn that is useful outside the class. It is obvious how much she cares. Extra credit can bring up your grade by a full letter!

One of the best professors at this school. I took it over the summer in 4 weeks. There are 4 labs and thats it. Took about the whole day to finish all lectures for a given unit, take notes, and complete a lab. Missed the first one because of a family emergency and she let me turn it in a few days late. Also rounded up my 89. The math is fine.

She is passionate about her class and makes lectures entertaining! The only drawbacks are the lab reports which the math is challenging at times. However her help videos have helped me understand the math portion, she is active on forums too!. Tons of extra credit opportunities, if you aren't taking advantage of them then your grade will tank.

Professor Choate is one of the best professors I've encountered so far. She's really funny. Literally gives away the answers to exam questions if you participate and engage. Since the course is asynchronous, it will require a lot of work on your own time.

Was alot of work for a gen ed 100 level course. You have to do a lab every week which is very time consuming. All her TA's grade her work, so it depends how tough they grade. Overall, I wished I chose another course.

Professor Choate is fine, but the class was unreasonably hard. You have to know how to use Excel very well, which I did not know. Lots of extra credit, but doesn't make up for the other work. Do not take this class unless you are literally so good at excel and math.

Prof. Choate assigns an unreasonable amount of work. I took her course the second half of the semester, and she was still making us watch lectures during finals week. For a 100-level gen ed course, devoting 10+ hours a week to lectures, labs, and studying was way too much to ask. Lectures seemed like they could have been shorter, same with labs.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Choate is amazing. The course is a lot of work but you get used to it. Great gen ed option. There is no math on the exams, only concept-based. The weekly labs have math but she has so many office hours. She also creates online Q&A forums. Tons of extra credit, up to 5% bump on overall grade.

This 8-week class is graded based on 2 (open-note, open book) exams and 7 labs, which is great if your notes are complete. Labs are math-heavy and you should be prepared to have your math skills challenged. However, you can supplement with easy extra credit, including freebie points if you pay attention to emails and watch the weekly review videos!

Lectures are pretty long but that's mostly because you have to watch them online. Tons of extra credits so no reason to get a super bad grade. Prof. Choate is very helpful and very active on discussion boards to answer questions.

Great prof, always there to help you out when you need it but for a 100 level gen ed, this class is unnecessarily difficult. Exams extremely hard and proctored. Labs difficult but again, she walks you through tough parts and has a lot of help available. Even though material was somewhat interesting, wayy too much time spent on a Gen Ed.

I took this class for honors credit and found it very interesting. There is some math and involved but the professor and TAs will walk you through it with examples. I learned a lot about Excel in this class. The lectures were very enjoyable and iClickers are used for participation grade.

I took this course as a gen ed course in person thinking it was gonna be easy. The exams were always super hard and I failed them all. The labs were okay but there was a lot of math on them and they were graded pretty hard.

Professor Choate was great despite the bad ratings I read on here before. There was an insane amount of extra credit and she gave videos + q&as to clear any concerns. Honestly a pretty easy class. Some labs were harder than others but nothing insane. Just dont leave labs till last moment and youll be good.

Having taking the class online, all materials were clearly organized, and she was very open to questions and always elaborated when needed. Labs can be done fairly easily as long as you pay attention during lectures; she gives all the information in a clear format and communicates her expectations clearly.

The class was fully online, but everything was organized well enough so I could find whatever material I needed. She understands a lot of students take this class for the Quant. II requirement and tries her best to make sure nobody is left behind. Overall, she's overall very affable and approachable, even if the class itself wasn't the easiest. :)

This professor is an expert in her profession and is super interested in what she studies, however, this class was extremely difficult. It includes a lot of mathematical prowess and I had to spend hours googling how to do the math because her explanations didn't help much. The lectures are super boring too. She tries to help and EC helps though.

Jessie is very passionate about teaching, and is clear about grading. The labs take awhile (longer than the 2 hours she says), but they're relevant to the course and helped me understand the material. If you have any issues, she is quick to reply and posts weekly help videos for the labs. The videos can drag on, but its a great class I'd recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Posts videos of the lectures and doesn't do anything. you have to teach yourself everything. all the homework (labs) are all pointless busy work that have nothing to do with the class. like why do I have to find how many hail reports there were in 2019, who cares. such a waste of time. if you put in 650 hours into this class, you'll get an A

Lots of homework/ labs and very long lectures, but overall the material was very easy. If you don't do too well on the tests there's a lot of easy extra credit quizzes you can take that will heavily boost your grade. Professor Choate is also very easy to work with in respect to late work/ technical issues.

Fall 2020, COVID semester, 8 week course completely asynchronous. Two big tests (online proctario but open note + open internet). Most of rest of grade is labs, work with other people and you'll be fine. Spent abt six hours weekly on the lab, would've been easier if I watched the lectures maybe. DO THE EXTRA CREDIT. Office hours if you need help!!

For this semester, the class was 8-week long so lots of material per week but Jessie is a GEM and her lectures are very interesting! It clearly shows that she's passionate about the course content and she cares about her students. There is a lab and discussion due every week that take some time. Don't buy the textbook. TONS OF EXTRA CREDITS!!!

She is not as bad as some people say she is. She does care about teaching, even though I personally do not find the materials very interesting. This semester exams only make up 45% of the total grades and during COVID-19 she decided to let us do open-note exams and that really helped. I got away with an A by mostly reading the lecture slides.

I really liked this professor, she was super nice and helpful. There's a lab due every week. Definitely attend lectures, there's always multiple i-clicker points. Exams aren't too hard if you take notes during lectures (slides are also posted on compass) and do the study guides. This is also coming from a non atms major.

Boring and bubbly lectures that are not worth your time. Could easily just pass this class with google. Couldnt explain anything - but does fill in the blank science. Take this class if you dont care about learning.

I heard this class was an easy A so I expected to spend most of my time in lecture zoning out, but Jessie is a gem! She has a lot of passion for the subject material which makes lectures very entertaining. Tons of easy extra credit available and Jessie is very approachable & knowledgable. Just don't buy the textbook for ATMS120 and you'll love it.

Lots of extra credit opportunities, most of which require just a Google search (making the textbook pointless). She's a newer professor, but gives excellent lectures, clearly cares about her students & the subject, and honestly was amazing. This class was definitely a highlight, and she's one of the best professors/lecturers I've had.

The lectures are boring, dry, and hard to follow, and cover topics you will not have yet covered or will ever focus on in CHEM 236 - despite her husband teaching that class. She also relied on the TA for EVERYTHING, including course logistics. At least she never skipped class, though her exam averages were almost as low as in CHEM 236...

Labs and their reports were fine enough, but the exams were ridiculous. Averages never exceeded 50%. People will try to tell you lectures are unimportant - this is untrue. Majority of the exam questions were either best answered with preexisting 236 knowledge or little blurbs from lecture

Professor White's lectures can seem dull, but they're essential to pay attention to if you want to do well in the class. The exams are very tough, and you are given a limited time. For the lab reports, the grading falls on your TAs, so it depends on how generous they are. Professor White is very kind, and she'd give out free snacks before lecture.

The knowledge of the course is mainly taught by TA and the professor gives her lecture by simply reading the PowerPoint. Completely a waste of time if you go there.
 The first exam had  the average of 64, which was higher than TA's expectations. So on our next exam our average was 46. It's dreadful. 
Be sure to complete past papers before exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: It's safe to say that Professor White has mastered the art of making learning organic chemistry an exercise in frustration and futility. If you value your sanity and GPA, avoid like the plague.
Tests are hard, tests will cover things that were not emphasized. Not sure if this is normal, but there were only ten lectures worth of content.

Tests are based on lecture material, but unrealistic in terms of scope. The professor knows the material, but the TAs know more about what is actually going on in lab than she does. Memorize all HNMR, C-13 NMR, and IR chemical shifts/group locations in advance if you want to succeed on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is honestly not as bad as everyone says she is. I found her to be a pretty alright professor. Her lectures are easy to understand if you have a background in the topic already, but they may be a little fast if not. Otherwise, it wasn't bad. Much of the class is run by the TAs, and they are extremely helpful and kind. Tests are hard, though.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Don't let the other reviews fool you: you will be okay! Labs are much easier and much better planned than 203/205 and the examswhile difficultare manageable. Prof White never once yelled at my class and the TAs were caring and accessible. I learned a lot.

The labs are not too bad as they are graded by TA's. Her lectures are not extremely useful but will give you around a 510 point boost in tests if you know the material. She's notorious for not replying student's emails, but the TA's are extremely helpful and dedicated. I learned a lot from the course, maybe not too much from her specifically.

Professor White can be very intimidating.  She often jumps into the material without giving her students any background on what she is going to be teaching.  The lectures weren't very organized, and she assumes that her students know way more than they actually know.  I would not recommend her, but she is usually the only one teaching this course.

Christina White is the kind of professor to specifically say something won't be on the exam and then make it 25% of the exam. She loves to stand in front of her unreadable mechanisms and get upset when anyone gets something wrong. She does not have office hours and does not make herself available. Belongs in a lab and out of the classroom.

She is extremely intelligent but her teaching methods are not the best. According to her, if you're taking the class you should already have an introduction to organic chemistry. Her exams are extremely long and convoluted. Work hard!!!

She is not a nice person but an ok professor. She's a good lecturer but very mean to students and most did not like her. Be prepared for lab exams - they're difficult and she does not curve. She has yelled at us several times and brags about her research. But very possible to get an A with lots of studying

She is a really horrible person. Extremely mean and degrading. The class itself can be enjoyable (esp compared to old chem classes), but the only thing that save me were the TAs. Be prepared to study for her lab exams and don't expect a curve. Try to avoid her.
I'm in love...

I enjoy Orgo Chem and I had taken Chem 236, the lecture course, in the previous semester, so the material wasn't the issue. Biggest complaint is the professor herself. She has no interest in teaching at all which she explicitly stated to me in a research group interview. If you are looking for a real teacher look elsewhere. Avoid her at all costs.

Practice exams are nothing like the actual exams and you genuinely cannot predict what will be on it. You have to read 2 30- page scientific articles at the end of every unit and he expects you to read and comprehend all of it. Any math questions are coated in layers of unit conversion. Good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
Although the Exam2 was easy everyone got good grade,but cannot disguise the fact that he doesn't like teaching. He made the class hard. The extra reading materials are not helpful and are unrelated to the content they are just additional boring things you have to memorize to prepare for his exam.
A middle-tier lecturer among the MCB faculty.

Professor Kim is the best professor I've ever had. I took the class Fall 2024 and his class prepared me very well for the MCAT. His office hours were super informative too. He'd ask us to send in questions beforehand so that he could prepare slides to explain concepts in detail. He's also super willing to meet with students outside of class.
most unorganized proff ive ever had. good luck

You read 3 10-page research articles and other articles serve as lecture. He only goes over the figures and expects you to read and digest the rest. That becomes your test. Don't feel prepared for the MCAT because there is no clear curriculum of Biochem. He is very nice though! No curves despite 66% average and no one got 100% on the first exam.
Very good lecturers that is engaging. But material seems a bit iffy and you can't predict at all what will be on exam

Professor Kim is truly one of the best lecturers I have met-he makes the course so engaging for us! Even though the content feels a bit tedious sometimes he makes sure we understand every bit of it....he has always been really helpful whenever I emailed him outside class-he even helped me get connected with a research opportunity in biochem!

For this professor it's a shot in the dark of what is on the exams. He only cares that you know the random weird factoids that he expects you to figure from reading the textbook, covers 5 chapters per exam of dense content + lecture mat. The office hours were strange since you have to know the question you want to ask before the lecture was taught.
Exams are tougher than the content he lectures about. He expects you to memorize specific details from articles , which is not reflective of the material.

He often asks the class what the answer to what he was saying. He makes us read the textbook which is old and arbitrary. His tests did not really test on the overall concepts but more specific details. He occasionally cracked a funny joke, and overall he seems like a good dude. Spect.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Lectures were fun and inclusive, It is mainly the class discussing our assigned reading. Grade is made up of a few assignments. I enjoyed the readings and never felt like I was assigned too much.

Would take every single class he teaches if I could. Incredibly silly guy, very engaging classes. Everyone in my class became friends because of the positive class atmosphere Crespo fostered!! If you need to take Engl200, Ramon is your guy!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Fun professor and course is definetly better if you really read the material. Chill class overall and you can learn a lot
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He was a phenomenal professor who was really passionate and well-versed in the content. The course was presentation based and student led, but every silence gets used as an opportunity to raise a new perspective the class had not yet discovered. The class is much more enjoyable if you thoroughly read the content!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I loved this class. It had nothing to do with my major but I genuinely feel like it was one of the most worthwhile courses I have taken at UIUC. At first the content was a little daunting, but after establishing a good writing format for the papers this class flew by. This professor truly cares about the content, and was honestly so inspirational.

I loved this class! The style of the class was that we would read the material before class and then during class we would have the chosen presenter present a slideshow on the text. Toward the end of the presentation were to answer discussion questions On Fridays, we didn't have class but instead had to answer weekly writing prompts.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I had Professor for ENG270. It was online due to COVID but it wasn't any less easy to follow. If you do what's expected, which isn't much except for watching films, writing one page papers and participating in the discussions, then expect an easy A. Professor's presence was a joy and discussions were interesting if you're into environment films/doc

I had Professor Ramon Crespo last semester for English 373 and I couldn't have been happier with that decision. He gives you ample opportunity to show what you've learned throughout the course. Participation in class discussion is really big but it's pretty straightforward and as long as you add to the discussion and support opinions you'll be fine

ENGL 116 would be a great option for people with a genuine interest in reading classical literature. Thanks to Professor Ramon Soto-Crespo, one can enjoy this class without any relevant background; the secrets to success are to read, to think, and to think more. The coursework is moderate and can fit in a busy schedule. Fame awaits future writers!

Prof. Ramon is a professor unlike any other. You will learn so many things while having a blast and being unable to control your laughter. He engages all his students in the discussions and preaches a classroom atmosphere where everyone can share their thoughts. Couple essays, couple exams, everything manageable and nothing over the top!

I am not usually a fan of American literature, but the variety that Soto Crespo had us read this semester was fantastic. Most of the readings are on the website, except for two novels that we had to buy and read. The essays are short and graded easily. Every student has to present on a reading. Tests aren't hard. Good intro level course for English
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Mr. Soto-Crespo is a wonderful professor. While you do read many things, they are often short stories and he gives you the PDFs instead of making you find them yourselves. He really engages the class in discussion and makes you feel like your opinion matters. Overall, one of my favorite classes so far. Just do the reading and you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: I really loved this class! it's largely just a group discussion class with a few page 2-3 papers and one presentation that I found pretty easy. Besides that and reading, there's no other homework. Ramon is a funny and interesting prof who really enjoys the class and makes it fun

Sweetest Professor I've ever had, you can tell he really cares about people succeeding. Unfortunately, Chem 104 is just an extremely difficult class if you're not pre-med or stem-heavy in the major department. I've studied for weeks, got failing grades.

Very good teacher, actually cares about his students. Really nice when getting to know him outside of class. Homework and prelecture assignments are pretty easy, but quizzes have hard questions, so studying really matters. Overall, really recommend and would take again! Offers retakes on quizzes too :)

He makes learning CHEM 104 simple and straight forward. He's also genuinely a very nice guy, especially if you see him in office hours. I would recommend getting to know him. His homework is very light but the quizzes are pretty tuff. They can take multiple steps but if you understand the material you should be able to get an A.

His quizzes were difficult and you really had to interested in the material to keep up. Our quiz averages were consistently around 60 and is what he expects. He does give the class a bit of a curve tho. Not a bad teacher, he was really nice, just a difficult class.

Excellent professor, this course has biweekly quizzes that can be retaken the following week for an average of the scores taken. Don't skip office hours, be sure to do the practice quizzes, and memorizing specific problems won't get you very far here, you need to understand content. The discussion worksheets reflect the final more than the quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Ray is great. Super nice and helpful. I'd say that the online 104 class is more difficult than taking it in person, as it's easy to get behind on the lecture videos. The 6 quizzes at the CBTF are difficult, but he curves the class nicely. Final exam was a lot easier than the quizzes.

Good guy. Definitely seemed to care about his students and was funny. Tests were kinda hard and always would put questions that majority of class would get wrong. But, they were doable. Just wish the class wasn't so heavily weighted on tests.

I loved Professor Ray, he always approaches students with kindness rather than judgment. He doesn't make students feel stupid for asking a silly question. Grading criteria are clear, discussion wrksheets are completion points. Workload can be pretty heavy as you have a pre and post-lecture every class period. Quizzes have review questions everytime
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He's a great professor, really cares about his students and being a good educator. I like that he provides supplementary videos to the lectures. On some quizzes the questions can be a bit hard to understand but that might just be on me.

Great lecture and teacher, the tests were unreasonably hard in my opinion, lowkey fried me every other week when I knew the material but make some stupid mistake to get zero points in the CBTF. Still cool professor and enjoyable class, class is mandatory and requires physical iclicker.
Dr. Ray is such an amazing person and teacher, his class is just HARD, I can't even rate him poorly because it has nothing to do with him, the course he teaches is just inherently hard.
I will admit professor Ray is really friendly and gives off major dad energy but I think sometimes he does that to throw us off on how hard his quizzes are because they never reflect his kindness
Ray Ray slays the house down. At times, bit of a diva—but a chem to it. He knows his stuff. I'd say he's funny similar to Trisha Paytas and Anna Stawowski !!
holy goated

CHEM 104 is tough, but Ray is a great lecturer. He provides a lot of useful resources, and is generally excellent at clarifying concepts (although this varies on the content). Pay attention, use practice materials, genuinely try, and you should be fine. What made CHEM 104 difficult for me was the amount of tests, not Professor Ray.

Honestly this class is not that bad. I really like the quizes every two weeks as it forces you to stay on top of the material. The most helpful thing for this class to to find past tests and practice tests and study from those. He pre-lecture videos are also really helpful so don't really need to go to lecture. Also good teacher.

Ray is the best professor I have had at UIUC. He is such a kind guy that really cares about his students and their success. The course itself is pretty hard, but he is such a great lecturer and makes the content actually understandable and doable. 6 quizzes instead of 3 exams, which is great for this type of course. I strongly recommend his class!

Dr. Ray has been one of the best professors I've had. He is not only excellent at explaining complex material clearly, but also brings humor into his lectures, making them enjoyable. It's clear that he genuinely cares about his students' success. While the quizzes are challenging, if you put in the effort, you will do well.

Dr. Ray is the coolest dude you'll meet in the chem department. If you need help, find him before or after lecture and he'll be more than happy to explain. His lectures make sense and are closely related to the coursework, so show up! His tests are difficult, but study well and consult people who took the course before and you should do fine.

Ray is amazing. He is one of the kindest professors you'll have and genuinely enjoys his job. The class is hard but he does a great job teaching the material and posts videos so you can look back on lessons. If you are struggling go talk to him!!!!! He gives great study tips and is super helpful in office hours.

Oh my god dr ray is everything you could ever want or need in a friend, human, or teacher. I wish he taught every single class i needed. He is funny, personable, caring, humble, and very reasonable. Discussions were actually helpful and there were 6 quizzes with lots of practice and retakes available instead big, anxiety inducing tests. LOVE him

I just love this professor, the goat for teaching concepts especially the ones i won't forget for a long time, the lectures are really fun especially for an intro class ! the quizzes are doable until you practise a lot
Not gonna lie, this class is hard. I struggled because there are just so many quizzes that we get no break. It makes the semester go very fast.
The class is difficult, but Dr. Ray is a very good lecturer and such a caring person. His explanations are clear, and he is very accessible outside of class.

Literally had to write a review at 4 pm on a Tuesday simply because I love Dr. Ray. He makes life so much better because chem is hard but he makes it digestible. Super caring, super fun. Great lectures. Respectable man. Love him. God bless Dr. Ray. Thank you Dr Ray!!!!!
He is overall such a great person! He is a great professor and you will learn a lot from taking his class.

Dr Ray was an amazing professor. He knows how to teach the material and make concepts easier. Be aware that the quizzes are hard, and more often than not you'll find yourself running out of time to complete it to its entirety. The final is fair, but you do have to dedicate quite a few hours to studying. The curve at the end really saves you.
Professor Ray is very clear when going over lecture material.

Great person, cares about students. The course content is tough, but not his fault. The only issue is that he's not a very organized person, so that makes navigating the Canvas difficult. Have study buddies for this class and read about the topics online. The homework is helpful but overwhelming.

Ray makes a 300-person lecture feel cozy. He's caring, funny, and explains concepts clearly. The tests are tough, but quizzes are doable if you study hard. unstuck study was a lifesaver for extra practice.

Dr. Ray is extremely helpful and invested in students' success. If you pay attention in lecture, practice, and attend his review sessions, you can do well. The material is pretty difficult, and the grade breakdown weights quizzes heavily, but Ray's explanations are clear and he provides plenty of resources. Time-consuming class.

PROS: final is hard but its impact is forgiving, RETAKES, good teacher and has study sessions every week, provides extra practice sheets that prepare you well for tests, passable class if you study and take advantage of retakes
CONS: lecture notes can be hard to follow, grading is very test heavy, your TA matters
I struggled a lot in this class, but Dr. Ray was very gracious and helpful every week. goat

Dr Ray was caring and funny. He explained concepts well and gave plenty of resources to practice. He clearly knows what works for students and has good availability. I enjoyed the many demos that were performed during lecture as well. Tests are worth 82% of the class with little extra credit, but the quizzes were not hard if you studied well.

Dr Ray was caring and funny. He explained concepts well and gave plenty of resources to practice. He clearly knows what works for students and has good availability. I enjoyed the many demos that were performed during lecture as well. Tests are worth 82% of the class with little extra credit, but the quizzes were not hard if you studied well.
Manages to make a 300 person lecture feel like a high school classroom. Chill and funny guy!

I really enjoyed this class. I felt like lectures were easy to follow and the fact that they are available online is a huge weight off of your shoulders because if you miss lecture or don't understand you can go back and rewatch it. Dr. Ray is really nice and cares deeply for his students. One of the best professors I have ever had.

Overall, I really appreciated all the help that Ray gave me. I started off the class really confused, and close to having to drop the course. But, go to his office hours, and really put in the time meeting with him one-on-one, he really helps in giving advice on how and what to study if you need. There was a lot of work, but it was all helpful.

Class was hard as hell, every quiz made me lose a part of my soul, but Ray restored my hope for the next 2 weeks before I had to take another. Lectures were well organized and overall helpful and Ray is good at explaining and offering additional explanations outside of class. Overall, a win in my book.

A fantastic professor who is kind, caring, and is amazing at lecturing. Offers a ton of resources to help students with chemistry. His quizzes are a bit difficult, but the retakes/worksheets are helpful.

Dr. Ray does a pretty good job at teaching the content, as well as making good analogies to help us visualize what something is. There was homework due before every lecture. His quizzes were pretty hard, but manageable. Most of the course was based off of the quizzes and the final.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: As a professor, Dr. Ray is very good at teaching the content! He engages with students well and is available to help outside of lectures. Although he is a good teacher, the quizzes are harder than the practice, the final is worth 30%, and there is a lot of homework. I learned a lot from him, but I definitely struggled in this class.
The class he teaches is quite difficult if chemistry is not your strong suit, but he is very accessible during office hours and over emails and wants all of his students to succeed.
Great teacher who really cares about his students. Explains difficult concepts in an easy to understand manner. Would definitely recommend for CHEM 104.

After taking Dr. G's class in CHEM 102, I found it hard to continue my existence on the planet Earth. But Dr. Ray single-handedly restored my will to exist. His lectures are very organized and understandable after watching the pre-lecture. His quizzes are a bit hard but with the LON CAPA practice problems, you will be well prepared.

After 102 I was terrified, but Ray is seriously one of the best professors I have ever had. Lectures are recorded if you miss one you can watch them on your own time. He gives you a lot of resources to prepare for quizzes. Do the practice quiz problems and the discussion worksheet over and over and you'll be fine. Also go to office hours!!

assessments aren't easy but if you understand lectures & homework well enough to explain it to someone else rather than just memorizing the steps then you should be good for the quizzes. He has very thorough & efficient pre-lectures and posts all of his lectures online. He's also available to answer any questions and gives almost identical retakes.

Clear, helpful explanations are given during lecture, and can also be found in concise online videos. The expectation for what you need to know is clear and straight forward. While he likes to include what he calls "kitchen sink" questions, the quizzes are reasonable and resemble the practice content.

Dr. Ray is a great professor who truly cares about his students. The quizzes are quite hard however and are not very representative of the practice material that he hands out. The nice thing is that he offers a retake and allows you to see your old exam so you can review what you got wrong and ensure you ace the retake, he then averages both scores

This class is very quiz-heavy and rigorous. Topics were challenging and sometimes, the quiz questions did not reflect the material being taught in class. Dr. Ray cares for his students and is available outside of class, but sometimes lessons were crammed and not all materials were covered during the course of a lecture.

His tests/quizzes were extremely hard. I am a good test taker, but the CBTF sucks. His practice problems were always way easier than the quiz. Lectures are a mess and its hard to take notes. He does have good prelectures that you have to take notes on. Lots of HW but it is a chem class so that's expected. Andino is the better choice IMO

This class was stressful especially with the quizzes being every other week, and the I hate the grading that the CBTF does because one small mistake drops the grade so much. Ray seems pretty nice and has good relationships with the students, but it was hard to understand concepts from lectures, and the quizzes were so much harder than the practice.

Don't get me wrong, Ray is a great guy, but his class is hardddd. Chem104 was a requirement for me so I had to take it and the biweekly quizzes killed me. His lectures are informative but I still struggled with the content. Beware this class is really difficult with Ray. If you reach out, though, he will be glad to help.

Christian Ray is a well-respected figure in the community. He is a strong lecturer, with a ton of experience under his belt. Yes - his exams are fairly difficult, but many of us persevered, and can actually say we learned a ton once all was said and done. He was recently recognized for his excellence in teaching with a huge award. Go for him.

Solid professor, but if this is your last chem class/ the topics don't actually matter for you, go with the other section for an easier time. I think everyone who said that you can just do practice problems had access to past PL quizzes lol. Get a good TA or ask friends for their TA's resources since they know the problems to focus on :P

One of the best chem teachers here. Chemistry is a hard subject, but he's a really good teacher and explains things in detail. If you have questions, he will answer with really good explanations. He provides practice quizzes and his office hours are very helpful. If you put in the work, you will be rewarded.

This class is hard for someone who doesn't love chemistry. I am an animal science major, so I just need to get chem out of the way.  Prof. Ray has weekly review sessions and reasonable windows for office hours. even though I ended up with a C, I actually learned a ton.
You can tell Dr. Ray cares about teaching. Though the class is difficult, he is always available and wants to see students succeed. I wish he taught more classes!

Dr. Ray was an amazing professor. He is so good at explaining things and he genuinely cares about everyone. If you are struggling in his class you can always find help. If you put in the effort in the class you can pass!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Most grading  quiz-based, but you will never run out of practice problems and quiz prblms are 99% of time from these practice problems. Retakes avail (takes avg so may hurt) Lectures were fine; type to be personable. Check out TA Jessica Xhumari's youtube for review. If you have a chance, take her section! (super helpful & review sessions galore)

Ray is a pretty easygoing professor who is also happy to talk with students outside of class. His lectures were decent; although I have seen better lectures before, he generally did a good job of helping me understand the material. He also gives retakes for the two midterms and partial credit on multiple choice questions.
