A lot of people like to give Dr. Ray more credit than he deserves because he is a really nice guy. However, as a teacher, he is not good at teaching the material. He spends way too much time talking to his students about his personal life and not enough on actual explanations of the material. He is also hardly available outside of class to help out

Not sure how Ray gets such high ratings especially after this semester. Expect everything you learn in lecture and in the practice quizzes to be NOTHING like the actual quizzes. Almost everything we learn in class has no reflection on his quizzes. Also 82% of your grade in this class is made up of quizzes and the final exam. Ruined my gpa

The lectures are very basic compared to the rigor of what is on the exams. The exam and quiz questions have many curveballs and the grading is very harsh. I think the average on the quizzes is usually in the 40s and on the exams is usually in the 60s.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Lectures on thermodynamics were difficult to understand and didn't do a good job of teaching the more abstract and conceptual stuff that a lot of students struggle with. Quizzes only have 3 questions and sometimes the questions are almost impossible to get right on the first try. Class is basically just meant to wreck premeds GPA.

Prof Ray isn't a bad professor, just not fantastic. Definitely cares about his students, but lectures are very basic-level and just showing up in lecture is definitely not enough to do well. Test questions are difficult but doable w/practice. I would recommend taking notes on the optional Openstax readings + doing all the practice problems offered.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Ray is a nice person. However, he has some policies that I found unfair. The class is 82% exam; he doesn't curve/give extra credit, but gives retakes which is helpful. Also, he's a good lecture (not bad/not great). The class is hard, threw curveballs. He is better than the avg chem professor at UIUC. I recommend lots of practice!

Dr. Ray is the best 104 Professor. He uses analogies to teach, personal anecdotes etc.. which makes understanding chemistry much more manageable. His office hours are also very helpful. Jessica Xhumari is the best TA who does 6 hour review sessions before the final, and she is probably as good of an instructor. Ray + Jess are your key to success
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I had Dr. G last semester for CHEM 1o2 so Dr. Ray was an amazing 180 turn. You can tell that he enjoys teaching because his lectures are very engaging, and he is great at explaining complicated concepts. The biweekly quizzes can be challenging, but he gives plenty of practice problems and materials. I recommend taking the merit section CHEM 199.

Dr. Ray is amazing. Easily the best professor Ive had at this school. He makes CHEM 104 feel like such a relief after taking 102. As long as you truly understand the content from his lectures (pre/post) and do the practice quizzes, this class should be an easy A. The retakes are also very helpful so make sure you always get them done.

he's great at explaining things and is easy going. he understands that we have lives outside of schoolwork. his quizzes can be tricky, so definitely study his provided practice questions. there isn't a textbook, he teaches you what he wants you to know. overall great guy and professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Amazing lectures--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: amazing

Dr. Ray was a decent instructor but I was very disappointed in him towards the end of the course. He promised an overall class curve, fell through and refused to round grades that were .5 or higher from the next letter grade. Thats just unnecessary considering were in a global pandemic and he did nothing to account for this when grading.

DO NOT listen to the person below me, there were no specific instructions for the extra credit and talked about a curve the whole semester just for him to disregard it. Great teacher for the most part especially when semester progresses. He just did not follow through with his promises and around 30% of the class have to retake this class again.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Ray is genuinely my favorite professor! He made chemistry so easy to learn because he would give you pre-lecture, post-lecture, and even host Zoom lectures. If you want to succeed, take advantage of them! Yes, he did offer extra credit but our class didn't follow the instructions, so it's fair that he didn't give any. I completely understand him!

This is a challenging class and requires hours of studying. I strongly recommend taking a merit discussion section while taking this class because having a great TA will make a huge difference. Dr. Ray offers retakes for each quiz, and also holds review sessions every Friday to help with digesting difficult material.

Although I can see why many of my peers were upset about our final grade situation, I genuinely really liked and respected Dr. Ray. I think he is a great professor and made chemistry as a subject much easier for me to understand. I did much better in this chem class than I have in any other, so I am grateful to him for getting me through it.

I think I would've been better off I take his class in person, I don't feel that online was his thing. I myself went through a lot of personal issues with family members passing away and I just feel that sometimes he was dismissive towards the concerns/issues of students. Great guy, but I just don't really know how I feel about him online.

THE BEST professor Ive had at UIUC. He really shows a passion for chemistry and wants his students to succeed. He isnt out to fail you, if you work hard and show it you get the grade you deserve. Wish there was a Dr. Ray in all of my classes

Ray was great/fair the majority of the semester. That being said, I lost a bit of respect at the end. I ruined my mental health studying hours each week, getting above average on every quiz. While a B+ is a fine grade, he had promised us a class curve if any of our quizzes were deemed unfair (we had a 55% average on one). He did not follow through
Great professor!

Dr. Ray has been one of the best chemistry professors Ive had. I know some of my peers are upset that there wasnt a curve overall in the class but he did curve some of our individual quiz scores. He was always available to help us, got to know a lot of us on a personal level during the pandemic and empathized with us. Overall, a wonderful prof.

Instead of exams there is a quiz every other week. The quizzes are difficult and are worth many points so you will still end up studying a lot of material and spending a lot of time every 2 weeks. Would not recommend taking this class with other difficult classes because I had to sacrifice a lot of time for this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had so much respect for Dr. Ray before the final. He is a great person. However, this class specifically has ruined my mental health. He promised a curve to the class but did not follow through. This class makes me rethink my choice of attending the University of Illinois.

For the majority of the semester, Ray was a great teacher, very understanding and fair. Unfortunately, he ruined that in the end. After promising a *class* curve all semester, he only selectively gave one out. Although C's and above are in a better position, it is not fair to be that successful w a C-A during a pandemic & not get the same reward.

Was not accommodating at all during a pandemic, he has curved the class in past semesters but would not do so even this semester even though he knows majority of us were struggling and would not round up even if we were so so close to the grade we needed.

Knowing that we are in a pandemic, he still refused to give us the extra credit points that many of us needed to raise our grade. Aside from that, many of us had internet problems yet he was not very accommodating and said it was out of his control. Many of my classmates agree that this class ruined their GPA and could possibly ruin medical school.

This professor is not accommodating especially not during a global pandemic. He wants students to fail so he can keep a bell distribution of grades. Dont take this class. Not sure how others gave him 5 stars ...

Dr. Ray is a great professor who does an excellent job explaining concepts in an easy to understand manner. He is very patient with people who ask questions and takes the class at a very reasonable pace. His test questions are very fair and as long as you keep up with learning materials, you should do just fine,

Best professor I have had thus far. He has a lot of quizzes, but he is very clear about what he expects you to know and will give you plenty of materials to prep with. He is a genuinely great person and cares about his students a lot. If you have him you're set!

Ray is awesome! He really cares about his students and going to office hours is EXTREMELY helpful. The practice quiz questions are basically the real quizzes which is great. Definitely use the practice materials given on lon capa. There are 6 quizzes which makes it easier to stay on top of materials. Lectures are really engaging and fun:)

Dr. Ray is one of the best professors I've had so far. His lectures are engaging and fun and he's really good at explaining difficult topics. He genuinely cares about his students and makes sure to gauge understanding on difficult topics. Overall, amazing.

Professor Ray is a perfect example of what all professors should aspire to be. He actually cares about his students, and is so incredibly talented at taking a lot of difficult material and condensing it into easily understandable chunks. He is fair, he is cool, by far the best professor I've ever had. Do anything you can to get a seat in his class!

Dr. Ray is such a great chem teacher. He made me not hate chemistry for once in my life and actually made me curious about it instead of dreading it. He GENUINELY cares about his students and works with you. One of the best professors I've had at UIUC.
Ray is an angel sent from above. He was born to be a chem professor. He made me not absolutely hate chemistry. Do whatever you have to do to get into his section.
Very kind and understanding. Biweekly exams are exhausting and the practice problems arent reflective of the actual exam
His lectures were enjoyable because he didn't just speak nonstop about chemistry for 50 minutes like a textbook. He included life stories and demonstrations that made concepts much clearer.

Ray was great at taking difficult or abstract ideas and making them manageable, and not seem so daunting. I would strongly advise going over the prelecture content he provides online, it only takes a few minutes, and it really helped me.

Ray is probably the best general chemistry professor on campus. His lectures are crystal clear, and the quizzes are very similar to the practice quizzes he gives. He will give a generous curve to the class if needed, but as long as you pay attention to the lectures and review all the practice quizzes, you will do great in his class.

LITERALLY THE MOST AMAZING PROFESSOR EVER!!!!!! I was able to pay attention in every single lecture - he has such a great teaching style. Made me love chemistry!!!!! Also such a nice guy in general. He makes it so easy to follow what is going on in class.

He genuinly cares for all his students and makes an effort to make sure everyone understands the material. His lectures were extremely enjoyable and not boring. Lastly, His teaching style is amazing. I love this man for sure!
one of the best professors I've ever had

The lectures were always very interesting, and he very clearly taught the material needed for the exams. He gives you everything you need to be successful in the class, and he sometimes brings his kids to lecture.

He is a great lecturer. His tests are more straight forward then CHEM102. Don't need to go to class even though there are clickers because he posts video which are easier to understand on LonCapa. Ray also is good at explaining and answers all your questions thoroughly. Great teacher

Dr. Ray is the best chemistry professor I have ever had. He genuinely cares about his students which was evident on the first day when he made an announcement about caring about student mental health. He is vey busy but usually is available outside of class and puts in a lot of effort to help you succeed.

Dr. Ray is the most amazing professor that I have ever had. His lectures are easy to understand because he takes scary topics and breaks them down. Take good notes and do all the practice exams. Make sure you ask if you do not understand something! Dont let questions build up. Hes always available before and after class. Great prof and class!

Ray was a gem -- if you have the opportunity to take him, I HIGHLY recommend you do so. This is not to say that his exams were easy (they were rather tricky), but he was FAIR throughout the entire semester. As long as you participate in lecture, take good notes, DO THE PRACTICE EXAMS, and just spare some time throughout the week, you will be gold.

Dr. Ray is one of the BEST professors one could ever have! Do not defer the opportunity to have him as a professor. His exam questions are slightly convoluted and tie multiple things together, but as long as you understand the concepts, it's not all that bad- he does not try to trick you on exams! He will go out of his way to help you succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Honestly the best professor I've has at this University so far. I found him extremely funny and was very knowledgeable about the subject. Exams need a pretty big time commitment to do well, homework is relatively easy. Put in the time and this class is very doable.

Makes you work extremely hard to keep your grade up in the class, seems to be easy to pass, but with what grade you want is to be determined how hard you work and understand the material. Tests sometimes don't exactly reflect the material taught in my opinion. Overall, would not say this is an easy A, but an easy pass is definitely a possibility.

Dr. Ray is the best professor I have ever had. His lectures are easy to understand and he does not speak too fast. His office hours are wonderful and he will remember you by name. The project isn't bad as long as you get a group of people that actually do work.

Dr. Ray is a good professor but his exams are pretty difficult. Don't think you can pull your grade up with them for the most part. Do all the little homework assignments and get them all right and you will have some padding for the exams. He has a group project for some lectures that sucks so make sure to start early on it.
You need to go to lecture, and he can be intimidating at office hours, but he's a nice guy.

Professor Ray is an amazing teacher. His gives a lot of resources to help prepare for tests and his tests are similar to those materials. He doesn't curve, but he does give every student a chance to "earn a curve" by doing an online retake with multiple tries. These helped my grade a significant amount!

Dr. Ray is extremely knowledgeable, and always seems to love to come to class. He makes lectures interesting, and is always there to help you if you need ANYTHING. I wish I could take every chemistry class with him as my teacher - he is awesome!
I thought he was amazing!  Really great at coming to lecture early and had good office hours at the CLC.
Gives you everything you need to do well on exams but they can be a bit tough. easily one of the best professors i've had so far on campus

As much as Ray was a very nice professor, he was extremely difficult. He was a good lecturer & I performed well on homework and quizzes, but majority of the class grade is from exams. He states at the beginning of the semester that he challenges you on the exam with questions you haven't seen before. Easy to perform poorly on them & kill your grade
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: This is my favorite professor to date. Given that I am just a freshman, take that with a grain of salt, but I have previously taken Chem 104 in high school and retook it for a grade boost, and the way he teaches the concepts is very similar to my high school teacher. He really walks through exactly how to do problems and makes lecture fun.

The only redeeming quality of this class was the online lecture videos. He talks constantly about his own life, and never replies to emails. He is late in posting on Ion Capa and his grading system is very test heavy. He is a nice guy but really shouldn't be a teacher. Puts little time into the class.

He's a good lecturer but that's about it. Takes weeks to reply to emails. Is always late up loading exam material therefore, giving students very little time to study. The answer keys were littered with mistakes. You will end up knowing more about his personal life rather than chemistry. Would not recommend.

I thought Ray was an exceptional teacher because he actually made the expectations of the class clear, so you knew exactly what to study. He gives you all the resources you need to succeed. If you're willing to put in the work then you should do great!

So long as you're willing to put in the work and keep up with lectures, you should do fairly well in this course. Ray explains the material thoroughly and provides additional resources, such as his pre- and post-lecture videos. Definitely the best choice when it comes to Chem104. Never have I enjoyed chemistry this much prior to taking the class.
Good professor overall. Project was fun, and we learned a lot from it. Lectures were clear and he is very approachable. Will try and work his hardest to ensure your success

I had high expectations for Ray, but was kind of disappointed. He had lots of office hours, but they weren't as helpful as I'd hoped. He's an ok teacher, but there were many areas where we were left to fill in the gaps/find the unwritten rules. For as hard as the tests were, he needed to have been more thorough in his teaching and homework problems

Dr. Ray is such an amazing teacher. I didn't like chemistry very much going into the class, but my opinion has entirely changed. He explains very well and there are a ton of ways he presents the material. You have to put in work to do well, but the material is so well presented that it's not hard to keep up. I couldn't recommend anyone more.

Took 104C which has a group project which was interesting but rushed. Ray wasa great teacher and did his best to get everyone to understand material. Glad I took this section. Never used the textbook but it was free!

Up until I took this class, I hated chemistry. I used to do terrible in even the easiest high school chemistry class but this course has completely changed that. The professor is super friendly and turn chemistry into something you can enjoy. He holds office hours by himself and clearly understands what each student is having difficulty with.

One of the best professors I've ever had. Extremely personable, brilliant, and knows what he's doing when it comes to teaching. Lectures are enjoyable. The material is hard, but he explains it clearly and thoroughly. Also extremely helpful at office hours. For CHEM 104, take advantage of the optional problems that are available online.

He is one of the best professor I had during my time at UIUC. Yes, his class is hard, but it's chemistry at U of I so that is expected. He lectures clearly, and is also clear about what is expected of you. If you go in to office hours, he is the most helpful and genuine professor you could have. I would recommend him as a professor any time!

Amiable character, but absolutely disastrous as an instructor. He overstates this fantastical concept of "critical thinking", which turn out to be quixotic when exams are expected to be completed in blitz speed this giving little room for "thinking time". Quite ironic indeed as he expects one thing, and does not provide the resources to meet them.

Professor Ray was a great lecturer. He reinforced the material presented in the pre-lectures and his tests and homework was right in line with what he was teaching. He's been the best professor I've had at the U of I. I would definitely make you schedule work around having him as a teacher.

I took him after having a semester with Overcash who was pretty awful. Ray's a great teacher and really cares about his students doing well. He also offers retakes and all the tests. Keep up with the material and you should do fine. He also posts pre and post lecture videos online which are very helpful. Best teacher for 104

I took this class when he taught chem 204, but the material is pretty much the same as 104. He is the best option for 104. Tests are hard, and he claims he doesn't curve, but he does. He offers retakes as well. Just keep up with all of his material. An A shouldn't be hard to obtain at all.
Enjoyable to go to class.
He's one of those professors where you learn more about him than the actual course.
Ray is the best teacher that you can get for Chem 104. Work your schedule around his availability. He makes things clear and exam resemble what is gone over in class.

He is the best, he teaches in the most simplest terms, however his tests really test the concepts that are learned in class. SO YOU BETTER KNOW YOUR STUFF. Watch the online videos because they really help, do all your homework assignments. Also he makes class really FUN!

After a terrible semester in CHEM 102 with Jamieson, I didn't think I could look at chemistry ever again. But Ray actually made me have an interest in chemistry. He is literally the best chem professor I've ever had!I wish he could teach all of my chem classes. If you have a choice, PICK RAY!
Took his chemistry class my freshman year. He is a great professor

He was a good teacher and the class was pretty easy with him. He was new to the class and his tests are pretty simple. You just want to make sure you go to his review sessions because they help especially before the exam
Best Professor I have ever had.
He is a fantastic professor! Just amazing! He really cares about the students learning. His   tests are difficult but really put in perspective what the material is about. Take him!

Dr. Ray is probably the best professor I have had at U of I. He really loves teaching and cares about students. He is always willing to help. Also, the class isn't that hard. The online quizzes were the hardest part, but he lets you work in groups. Overall, he's a great professor.

Favorite class I've taken in college.  Dr. Ray is a great professor and is super helpful during office hours.  Go to office hours the day or two before exams and ask how to do the practice test Q's and you're golden for exams.

He's not that bad. He's really nice, cares about making his students learn, and is really helpful. You don't need to go to class, because he posts lecture videos online that get straight to the point and are exactly the same(rather than sitting in a 50min class)He even curved our class at the end!

One of the best professors I've had. You can tell he has enthusiasm for the subject. Real nice guy and his exams are fair. If you make sure to understand the homework you should be getting an A, the material isn't that hard. The only thing I found annoying was that sometimes the review sheet for the exam wouldn't be posted until two days prior...

He offers multiple ways for you to learn the course. Online lectures were very efficient and well done. Of course, like many chem courses at UIUC, material is not homogeneous, so expect much harder exams than homework and quizzes. And for Fall 2012, half the class is --not-- failing, more like 10-ish% after curve.

Dr. Ray probably the best teacher I've had at U of I.  If you ever struggle with a concept, go to his office hours. The class is not hard if you put the time into study and do the suggested practice problems. He writes hard tests  but offers online retakes that can get you point back for the original test. This man was meant to teach chemistry.

Chemistry is something I really struggle with, and the only reason I'm doing so well is because Dr. Ray is a fantastic teacher. It is a difficult class, but he's absolutely wonderful. He tries to put everything at students' fingertips so that they can do as well as possible. He really cares about his students.

He's really nice but a terrible teacher. He doesn't explain things very well and expects you to learn most of the material on your own. His online lecture videos are sort of helpful but he doesn't go into much detail.

Professor Ray was one of the best professors I've had so far at the University. He's passionate about teaching and is extremely helpful when it comes to helping students understand the material. He makes pre/post lecture videos which really help. Practice problems; that's the key!

I hate Chemistry. Dr. Ray is incredibly nice. Never met a teacher who cares so much about his students. He wants you to understand more than anything. Everyone warned that he was hard but honestly I thought his exams were very fair if you actually study and do practice problems. Extremely helpful during office hours. I wish he taught Orgo.

If you go to his office hours he really makes an effort to get to know you and find a way to help you out that works for you.  He tailors his explanations to your learning style.  With all this, he is a difficult professor and his midterms are challenging, but doable.  Much better than Hummel.
His office hours should really be used. He is extremely helpful when people just ask. He also has lecture videos that are greta for studying and catching up if you miss class.

Really wonderful. Loves to tell stories but also teaches in a nice manner. Numerous examples and an abundance of practice material. Exams are exactly what he warns you they will be. Overall, awesome class. Dr. Ray loves his job and it is apparent through his teaching.

Dr. Ray is awesome. He definitely loves his jobs and is interested in what he teaches. He is always willing to help and does not want his students to fail. His exams are a bit rough but he gives great practice problems and practice exams. Just go to class and take notes.

Nicest guy I've met yet. Feels bad when we are confused or don't understand. Loves to teach. He'll help you with any problem you might have. Exams are fine if you study decently. Uses a peculiar system to get points back once you've taken them. His video lectures online are gold. Answers questions during lecture. He is such a good person.

Great Professor! He helps you if you have ANY questions, and his pre/post lecture videos are AWESOME! This class is totally doable if you put the effort in! Watch the videos, attend lectures, and do the practice exams, and you will do just fine! :) Dr. Ray is by far the best professor I've had thus far!

fantastic teacher. Very good at explaining hard concepts and answering student questions. Easy to approach and relates well with students. You really want to have Dr. Ray if you plan on taking Orgo, his class lays a wonderful fondation for CHEM 232.
he is literally the best. i love going to lecture because he's funny and clear and very willing to help. tests are hard, but you get a chance to make up some points for each test

Couldn't have asked for a better professor. He asks a lot of you in his exams, but what he gives you is outstanding. He has pre-lecture videos (5 min. long) so you're prepared for lecture, post-lecture videos in case something isn't clear, and any question you ask him will get answered. Don't take anyone but Ray for CHEM 104.

The guy was great for the first month.  Then he had a baby and everything went downhill.  I was in the online section and he was supposed to post videos lectures every week.  Ended up being three weeks behind schedule.  I had to learn the entire three weeks and the current week of material for an exam within 4 days.  I'm an A student, he burned me.

Good lecturer! Very clear, and explains most things well. I worked pretty hard but did not dedicate as much time as I could have to the class - don't make that mistake! On a regular basis you should watch the pre-lecture videos, go to lecture, then review the lecture. For exams, review notes and take the practice tests.
Great professor! Always open to answering your questions. Exams can be a little bit challenging but if you go to your discussion or his Friday tutoring sessions you should be just fine.

Really great teacher. He goes out of his way to make sure you understand, but the catch is, YOU have to ask him. Never had a question go unanswered. Online lectures are exactly what is covered in class so it is very good review material. If you study and actually ask him to explain stuff to you, he will. Great class, hope to have him in future.

Dr. Ray really wants his students to learn and tries to help as much as possible.  He also personalizes the class which is cool.  The material is hard (especially orgo) but go to class and study a lot.

Watch pre-lecture videos before lectures, read the book for what is unclear, go to class knowing what is discussed that day, participate during it, DO NOT BE AFRAID of asking questions, watch the post-lecture videos, go to his Friday tutoring, go to office hours if you must, and you'll be golden. Made me love chemistry and pursue biochem.

He talks a lot, but he's adorable, easy going, funny in a dorky way, DILF. :D He writes difficult exams, but he is an AWESOME teacher, and he puts the lectures online, which are really convenient and concise.
The best professor I've ever had. He actually cares. Office hours help a ton.

good lecturer. very clear, and explains everything well. Got an A+ in the class, didn't work exorbitantly hard. Watch the prelecture video, go to lecture, then review lecture. Then just review notes for the exams, and take the practice tests. Tests are not that hard provided you are not an idiot. most of the test problems are exactly like homework
great professor. his lecture video and notes help a lot. glad to help his students. exam is kinda difficult however if u have done the previous exam u should be fine

A bit pretentious and annoying. Course material and test material didn't always match up. Felt unprepared for tests, had to show up for class but ironically didn't learn too much from them that would actually benefit me on the test.

Great teacher. ALWAYS open to answer questions. His lecture videos are very helpful, and he holds review sessions every week. It's a tough class, exams are difficult.  If you put in the work and take advantage of all the practice problems, review sessions, and lecture videos you should be fine.

If you don't like chemistry, this class is tough. Class attendance and discussions are helpful, but not always good enough. I studied for every test and could only achieve a C or D. Quizzes were somewhat difficult. Do the practice problems from the book. Doing  minimum work will not get you a B. Tests resembled the practice ones and were fair.

Hes a cool professor to talk to, but hes really not a good teacher.  I found his exams to be unreasonably hard despite studying.  Hes a fairly nice guy, but I would definately recommend Yerkes or Shapely...He expects you to go out and learn alot of stuff on your own.  I felt like I put in alot of time into the class but was not happy with my grade.

Ray is definitely a cool guy who is excited about teaching and relates well with his students.  Nevertheless, his exams were very difficult.  And since they are multiple choice, it is really easy to lose points fast.  I would for sure recommend Ray to anyone taking chem 104, but be prepared to study hard.
Great teacher. I could tell he really cared about the individual success of his students and would go out of his way to help.

I truly felt that he taught better and in a more efficient manner in his lecture videos than his physical lectures, so I rarely went to those. When finishing exams, make sure to check those over because every question is .7% of your grade. Because of the unreleased criteria he uses for the grading scale, nobody really knows his grade until the end.

Dr. Ray is tough, and if you miss a lecture its easy to fall behind & hard to catch up.  Sometimes its hard to understand, but he's always open to questions, if u keep them specific.  His curve is generous, so as long as you try you should be able to pass.  Be prepared to put in a lot of work and be sure to have a friend you can work with..it helps
Really helpful, funny, and reasonable. Go to lectures and things will come much more easily. Be prepared to learn plenty about his family!
Great teacher. He explains subject matter quite well. Exams are tough tho. Be prepared to work hard on org chem.

This professor is the best I've had at UIUC. I hate and am terrible at chemistry but he made it completely enjoyable. He cares about the students and does everything in his power to get his students to understand and learn the material. He will challenge you on exams but he does well in preparing you.

GREAT TEACHER. He made chem so much fun. People in other reviews bag on him, but honeslty they probably didn't do the practice problems or watch the NUMEROUS lecture videos that he posted, and they probably didn't go to his review sessions that he holds every friday. GREAT TEACHER. Ignore the negative posts. Take him if you can!!!!!

If you can get in his lecture, TAKE IT. He is a great professor. I hate chemistry, but LOVED this class. He has tons of lecture videos and prelecture videos, practice problems, suggested problems, and ton's of help available. Go to lecture, get your clicker points, WATCH the videos he posts, and do the suggested/practice problems and you've got an
I love love love him. I hate chemistry but you can tell how much he cares about his students and puts in so much effort to help you succeed!

Excellent professor in all aspects. The best lecturer I've had. Not an easy class, but any problems you have you can post on the compass site, and he will probably respond on the same day... very helpful. Also manages to make chemistry fun. Highly recommended.

Great professor! Actually makes me enjoy going to lecture. Gives you all the tools you need to do well and thoroughly understand the material, including tons of practice problems. My best professor at this university so far.

All in all great teacher. His power points are organized and his pre lecture videos are very helpful. Also has a lot of suggested problems to help prepare. Exams aren't bad at all, wayyy easier than yerkes' exams. I actually enjoyed going to class. And he's a hot live version of Doug Funny...
Best Professor I have had so far here!!! This lecture videos he posts on compass are so helpful.. if you miss lecture you have no problem catching up! I love his class!

He's one of the best professors I've ever had. Very good at teaching and really cares about how his students are doing. Puts a lot of effort into this class! Exams really test you, but LONCAPA, quizzes, and the curve should boost your grade.

Dr. Ray is a good professor if you go to his office hours, prepare your questions in advance, and ask him directly outside of his lectures.  The problem is that his office hours are often extremely filled because he is quite popular, so it's very hard to get his help when there are so many people surrounding him.  He is nice, but overrated.

Great lecturer. Responds well from student feedback and genuinely cares if you don't understand concepts or need help. The only drawback is that he really tests you with his exams. But if you put forth the effort, I'm sure you should do fine in his class.
An ok class. Exams can be tough though. And Ray is not very helpful.

Dr. Ray was one of the best professors ive ever had! i never have written a review on a professor before but Ray is worth commenting on. Excellent teaching, not exactly easy material but Ray is very fair and if you go to class (which i would HIGHLY reccomend for chemistry) and put in the time/effort you will be fine.
Taught material well. His tests were hard, but the curve made up for my poor scores on the tests and I received an A.

My favorite professor yet! I loved dr. ray! Although the material is not very easy, he does everything possible to help you learn and gives plenty of opportunities to have things explained outside of lecture. Go to lecture and discussion and its a pretty easy class. Dr. Ray is AWESOME!

Dr. Ray is 'friendly' and interesting and, it seems at first at least, genuine. But if you read his 'teaching philosophy' on the Chem dept website it is all spelled out. To me he did not appear spontaneous; it was as if he was too mechanical. After a while the 'novelty', if any, wears of and you almost start wishing for a more genuine professor.

Take Chem 104 with Dr.Ray!He is a great professor,and very down-to-earth.Favorite prof so far!He does challenge you,but pay attention,do suggested problems,and take advantage of his online office hours and the TAs to master it.The class is curved since the tests are challenging.He is really good at explaining complicated things.He made me like chem
Dr. Ray is my favorite professor thus far in college. He's cool, smart, and his exams are very straighforward as long as you understand the material. Overall he's a cool professor.

He is well liked by students because he does not have an attitude problem and appears enthusiastic about what he is doing. He is not,however,very good at it. He often relies upon prepared notes, sometimes seem a bit confused -and it is not too difficult to loose focus during class. Some demos are all style and no substnce. Overall, he is ok.

Ray is a fun-loving guy. He is a great guy but I'm not sure if he's a great professor. He seems to prejudice one over the other and his tests are extremly hard compared to the tests of the other Chem 104 professor.
Dr. Ray is a great teacher. He made going to class worth the effort. He also provided a great pre-organic chemistry base before heading onto organic chemistry.

Rey makes his class interesting and fun to attend which is very refreshing after CHEM102 which made me hate chem. His lectures include lots of demos and he clearly explains everything which is unique to this campus. Definitely recommend!
Amazing!  Be sure to do suggested problems, lectures were helpful
One of the few entertaining lectures ever! I actually loved going to his class! A good advise for this class is to do the suggested problems and read his notes and you are good! Now I love chem!
Terrific instructor with energy, enthusiasm, and the rare ability to connect with an auditorium sized class.

Dr. Ray is a really good guy, he is very helpful and very approachable, Class is tough but if you do practice problems, go to office hours, do the readings, you should be good. This class is all about how much effort you are willing to put into it. If you are gonna take this class, I would say take it with Dr. Ray.
Dr. Ray is an amazing teacher! Loved this class.
I absolutely looove him!  He was so great and chemistry ROCKS!!  The class is awesome and he's hott!!!!!  He's also good at teaching :)

Super awesome chemistry teacher.  Seriously, if you have to take this class make sure to get Ray!  Not only does he make chem really interesting, he's just overall a great teacher.  Do all the suggested problems (trust me you'll be thankful on the exams) and do practice exams and go to class!  Chem 104 is nothing but a good time with Ray!!!!

Dr. Ray is an awesome professor.  He absolutely loves teaching, and he always talks about how it is his dream job.  I'm really glad I had him instead of Dr. Yerkes.  She bores me to death, and doesn't really teach.  Dr. Ray keeps you interested.  You should see his lectures.  They are packed full!

Dr. Ray is an amazing teacher.  He's incredibly helpful, and as long as you do the suggested problems, go to every lecture, and understand the material the class will be a lot easier.  Before exams review suggested problems and do all of the old exams!  Dr. Ray is extremely helpful, so use the Chem 104 website frequently.  Awesome class!
Fun teacher; last day of class is "blow stuff up day." Tests have the usual tricky questions. In comparison to Yerkes: Yerkes teaches better but Ray is more entertaining.
All around great guy. He loves what he is doing, is always willing to help you, and always has some sort of demo during class. If you apply yourself, it should be an easy A.
good lecturer. somewhat interesting, good demos, seems like he cares about students

He's GREAT! Lectures were actually understandable and he had GREAT demos! Obviously it's a hard class- but with a lot of work you can get a great grade. Ray made things really clear and he's a really nice guy.
Good guy, definitely makes lectures worth going to. A lot of cool demos, explains things well, but he doesnt just lay everything out in front of you.  Exams are fair and curved well.

The corey matthews look-a-like really makes class interesting, so that you wish there was more time. You don't receive a grade for homework, but you HAVE to do it to do well on exams! Take quizzes seriously, too.

Great professor.  Makes a real effort to constantly improve his teaching.  If you read the book before lecture and do the problems he recommends you doing, you will succeed.  I never thought I liked chemistry, but now I have a definite interest in it.
Great professor!  Had easily understandable and very enjoyable classes!  Also, gave fair exams. Definitely recommended!
Dr. Ray is amazing.  Definitely one of my favorite professors.  He makes boring chemistry interesting.

Very enthusiastic professor!  Extremely hard tests, but the curves aren't too bad.  I would definitely go to office hours to catch up on stuff you don't understand.  Prof. Ray will only get better as he gets more experienced!  I would definitely take his class!  Go Cubs!

Dr. Ray is fantastic. I am terrible at chemistry and was excited to go to lectures... he makes the material interesting. Office hours were very helpful--he was great at rephrasing things I didn't understand and was really interested in my success. It's a tough class, you have to put in the effort, but Dr. Ray is a great professor.
Very interesting professor, always brings in current events that deal with what we are learning. Great speaker. Hard exams.

Dr. Ray is great.  He does fun demos and his teaching methods are awesome.  His focus for research is improving chemistry teaching, so he definitely knows his stuff. And he's great at chemistry too, but thats really just a bonus.

He was a super nice guy, however his lectures were so broad that when it came time to do the homework, I was lost. His tests are super hard and I feel like he almost sets us up to fail. Overall, nice guy but not so good at teaching.
Hard tests, but very fair grader and generous with curves. Lots of demos, good analogies in class

Nice teacher but not all that clear. Instead of having power points he writes his notes by hand on the board which really reduced the amount of information you can learn in class. But go to lecture because his test questions are derived directly from lecture. The 1st test is hard just to scare you and the rest are easier- final was very easy!

Dr. Ray was amazing. Sometimes in lecture things could get a little confusing but he tried really hard to give real life examples. Go to his office hours! It really helped me understand better plus he is really fun to talk to about other stuff not related to chem. I think he will get better the more he teaches.  Best chem teacher I have had
very helpful and enthusiastic. lets you know exactly what will be on the test. beware of all the whiners in chem 104.
Entertaining-makes you want to come to class; helpful in office hours; takes personal interest in students' progress.

Nice guy but if you take the class learn concepts more in depth then what is presented in class. Tests require that you know ALL of chem and he teaches only about 20% of it in lecture. You are better off staying home and reading the book.
he was a good teacher, pretty helpful. didnt mind if you came into his office at anytime.
Even though I'm doing really bad in his class, his lectures were interesting... I enjoyed taking his class, and I may be taking it next semester too! :(

Not ready for lecturing at all.  I learned more in my discussion than in lecture.  Horrible explainer and doesn't give any examples for anything he explains.  He hardly prepares you enough for the tests which were hard.  I wasn't great at chemistry in the first place and he did not help at all.  If only I had known this before...
No Comments
Great (young) guy, but would barely cut it as a TA.  Definately not ready to lecture.  Explanations are confused--he clearly does not have much experience with Chem 104.

Lectures are just reading slides for 1.5 hours for a 1-point quiz, labs are 15 minutes of reading slides and "you're free to go", the homework has more reading and writing than a literature major. Halfway into a class called "Database Concepts and Applications", we've only covered basic CRUD operations and joins.
Really bad, AVOID!!!! He does not respond to emails, late with grading too.

Doesn't actually teach anything that the class is about, instead expects us to learn through a certification website you could access and accomplish without this class. Lectures are difficult to get through and don't go over anything important for the assignments or papers. Grading rubric isn't clear and he won't provide feedback points taken off.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
Very bad professor. 

If you need recommendation letters, do not ask Craig!!!
Craig promised my friend a grad school rec letter but never submitted it. Despite multiple emails and visits, Craig didn't respond or upload the letter on time. My friend missed a great school's deadline because of this.

Not clear with instructions and picky about specific details, like needing to include animations in presentations. She also doesn't research the material well and/or forgets basic facts, such as confusing Dubai for a country, relying on students to correct her. Constantly invades students' personal space and is very rude, always interrupts students
Professor really tries to keep us thinking. She asks interesting questions and get us involved in lectures. Group projects!

Don't take her class. She's not the most reasonable grader and unwilling to hear student perspectives. She singles students out all the time. Lacks passion about the course content. It's time to retire.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Her lectures are very good! She has group projects and she does something when someone in your group who is trying to take all of the credit. She is very nice and she gives good feedback.

Gets argumentative with students, secretly adds assignments to canvas without telling, lots of case readings, class was very unstructured. Overall, find a different professor if she is your professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Terrible professor and terrible person overall. God awfully disrespectful to students, exam questions are nothing related to the course material. Expects you to read six 15 pages reports in the span of two weeks in addition to the many other hw we already have. Worst class I have ever taken at U of I and I just completed Math 257 this semester.

Unacceptable behavior to students and approach to teaching. If you dont want to teach then dont. Class structure is nonexistent, material is blurry and irrelevant and she overcomplicates things. Lacks any basic understanding of the syllabus and materials that she literally created.
She made a subject I was passionate about boring and dreadful by being aggressive and rude, having an unstructured class and being argumentative with her students along with utterly disrespectful

My mental health was ruined for a semester. This class was my worst this semester and so unnecessarily hard. I didn't even want to go to office hours because she was so condescending. Not to forget her content was very unclear and she just expected you to get it right away.

Insanely disrespectful to students, its clear when she is annoyed by their presence. Unstructured class, lousy assignments, clueless of her content and syllabus and makes the class way harder than anyone else. If you love yourself, don't take her. I wasnt even planning on it but the section I was in got switched to her.

Martinez needs to retire. Her behavior is awful, grading is mood-based, tests are oddly specific and she is very rude to her students. Her lectures are boring, slides are blurry and you never learn anything really. Take with literally any other professor for 449.

She has to be losing her mind. Adds random assignments to Canvas, grades capriciously, forgets her own syllabus, makes the class and tests 10x harder than they need to be, doesn't prepare enough, argues with students when they give her feedback she asks for, her feedback to students is degrading them and she invades personal space.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Needs to retire asap. Martinez asked for feedback and got so defensive when students responded. She doesn't even know her syllabus, and she added extra assignments for no reason. This course has so much busy work, and the tests are so stupidly specific. The worst part is that she always interrupts students when speaking to get her point across.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: awful....the whole course is boring, and the assignment is hard
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: This professor is straight up psycho. She doesn't make her expectations visible or clear, berates students in the name of feedback and has very blurry and messy slides that are extremely vague. Her lectures are just reading off her slides.

Asks for suggestions only to argue with you and never implement them, blurry and messy slides that she complains about, doesnt talk about the material besides what's on the slides, disrespectful to specific targeted students and it's pretty tough for those of us who arent targets to watch. Has requirements that nobody knows about

I didn't even ask to be born as a POC so you can imagine how frustrating it is when I get treated differently and extra rude by my professor. Always late, degrades students, outdated material, nonexistent instructions. She could have an undiagnosed mental illness because she talks about things that aren't on her Canvas at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: Always 10 minutes late to class. Very negative and unhealthy environment for participation. She is malicious, evil and rude to students if they don't follow her nonexistent, nowhere to be found directions for assignments. No sympathy to students, out of date teaching materials, "feedback" is degrading the student if theyre POC.

Someone said that the only people who like her are frat boys and that is very true. She treats them like human beings while treating everyone else especially WOC like subhuman. You will never be prepared for success with her guidance (or lack thereof). She shouldnt be teaching a literal capstone class. She is not a subject matter expert.

The recent reviews do so much justice to her poor teaching of a course that could have originally had potential. People with other teachers actually enjoy the class and have better GPA's and interest in the subject. Her slides are useless, she doesnt review with you for exams nor give you resources and she wants you to fail. She laughs when you cry

Her grades arent based on your understanding, its on how much she likes you. Which is easy if youre white. You can study all you want with her misleading slides and study guides she steals from other instructors and you will still fail her exams because she DOES grade capriciously. It's like the pyramid on Dance Moms for BADM 449.

Lacks empathy for students, poor slides, outdated guidelines, unclear expectations, RUDE to students, stares at you, doesn't care to know your name unless she hates you so that she can grade you capriciously.

Incompetent professor. Heavily underqualified, it is a wonder how she got her degrees. She has very outdated standards for consulting slides and very strange criteria for evaluating others. She is very mean to students who are from the other side of the globe. She is insensitive to students personal situations.

She uses the students and class as an outlet for her pent up anger and rage which is very unprofessional. She is a lesson to students on what not to expect in the real world. She screams in students faces to the point you can smell her breath, as someone said earlier. Her slides are just a decoration because no learning happens in her class.

She uses the word "sucks" a lot for being a self proclaimed academician. She lacks professionalism and shows up late while calling out students who do under her breath. She doesn't even know her own course. She lacks content knowledge and misguides students. It's hard to focus on material when she spends half the class complaining about slides.

If you're a Gies student, chances are you have heard about someone complaining about her class this semester. She gives her students unnecessary work for a business core and humiliates them by constantly being RUDE. Her lectures do not help and no need for retention.

The stress from her class is something I talk about at my counseling sessions. I have never been beaten down by a professor like this. She has no idea how to present and just reads off slides she complains about. She spends 99% of the class berating students. She makes out of pocket comments. She makes texts extra hard. She tricks people on exams.

The way she graded exam 2 was suspicious. She never gave statistics. She slams the door aggressively. She complains about her own slides, rude and disrespectful to students even if they have jobs and expects everyone to cater to her whims and fancies. She is full of entitlement and privilege.

If you are a POC or international student do not take her. She will make you uncomfortable especially if you are East, Southeast or South Asian. She assigns many assignments at the same time and doesnt realize we have other responsibilities. She has no desire to be there but her paycheck, and that reflects in her teaching.

She doesn't even properly remember the names of the cases she makes us read and analyze. Rude to students with many snide comments. Constantly disrespectful to everyone. If she has a bad day which is everyday, it is for everyone in class. She is confusing in class. She does her own thing with the frat boys cheering on while everyone else suffers.

She doesnt respect students or their personal space, lacks basic empathy, has double standards and grades capriciously. Nobody knows her expectations. She picks obscure exam questions. She screams at students. She stepped on my foot and never apologized. She is on a huge power trip because her lectures are unprofessional but shes tenured.

She invades my personal space during class all the time and comes close to my face when giving lectures and I can smell her breath. Her lectures are not engaging and its unclear what the takeaways are. Her expectations are in her head, not the syllabus. Her canvas is disorganized and chaotic. She purposely makes sure some people miss the attendance

Her expectations are not always written out, you apparently have to be a mind reader to know. She says statements that would normally be friendly in a condescending manner where you know she means ill, not well. Rude and disrespectful to students. Embarrasses students publicly. Suspected of racism by many students. Complains about her own slides

I had to leave for the restroom during class due to a women's health occurrence. She gave me a nasty, menacing look when I came back, then cold called me and then held me back after class and yelled at me for disruptive behavior. She doesn't post slides on Canvas in time. Doesn't help prepare for exams. Doesnt guide us what to study.

She cant teach, she doesn't want to be there clearly and she doesnt want students in her classroom. If she does want them its to humiliate them. It's not a learning zone. She complains about everything. Her slides make me yawn. She thinks shes Gordon Ramsay of business school except Gordon Ramsay knows what hes doing. Martinez doesnt.

Sometimes I forget if this is a class or a bullying session. This professor is a bully and incorporates into her lessons. She shows up late, complains about slides she made, uses fear tactics, sends mean messages on Canvas and keeps her page very unorganized. She purposely picks questions we have no way of knowing the answers for

Me and my friend of the same ethnic group were constantly targeted in class. She did not spare feelings and is a mean person along with inconsiderate instructor. Boring presentations and repetitive assignments. Feedback is more of her problems with your personality than your work.

She focuses a lot on what her students do WRONG and if they do something right it is immediately followed by a rude comment. Doesn't know how to teach but does have a PhD in how to make sure students are horribly underprepared for exams and projects. Her TAs are more competent than her and they're literally students.
She makes everything 10 times more complicated than it needs to be and ruins students mental health. She is insanely rude and tricks you on exams. She likes to make her students feel inferior

I was aware that she didn't have a good reputation before getting stuck with her class, but she's 10x worse now. Questionable teaching methods, outdated slides and constant negative energy. She is a hater to all students. She loves international business but hates international students and POC.

I have certainly developed stress lines from taking her class. She stress me by cold calling and insulting me. She has no empathy for her students and complains about slides she makes so nobody can focus properly.

Her material is ineffective, she is intimidating and all her students are scared to come to class. She threatens students, disrespects them, and makes sure to give them unnecessary hurdles in exams. She enjoys seeing her students fail and is not afraid to fail students.

She stared me down for going to the bathroom during her lecture when I had a stomachache. I couldn't miss class anymore because I already missed two. She sends aggressive messages on Canvas and makes snide remarks in class. She mutters things under her breath which are likely mean. Her lessons are useless for exams and life. DO NOT TAKE HER

The night after I presented her, I couldn't sleep because her comments were hurtful and personally directed, not constructive or relating to the material. I had an exam the next morning too for another class. She cold calls POC a lot. She always comes late to class and intimidates students. Nobody feels safe in her classroom.

She might be senile because she cannot teach without targeting and hurling insults at students as if they are also part of her nonexistent lesson plans. Purposely chooses obscure questions for exams to spite students. Low exam averages. Everyone who has her this semester is complaining on here and for a reason. Raises her voice and screams.

She is not a testament to the values upheld at Gies. Older professors typically come with a wealth of knowledge and she has none. Her slides are heavily outdated and she can't go a class period without complaining when she made them. She always tells international students or POC to speak louder even when they aren't quiet.

I have had so many sleepless nights because of how rude she was to me in class. It makes it hard to focus on her presentations which are not informative anyway and have nothing to do with exams. Retention of learning is hard when your guard is always up in class.

She is the single worst professor at Gies who intentionally sets students up for failure. She doesn't believe in helping students or giving them study plans, cannot accept her mistakes but will berate students to the point where they go home and cry. Her slides are awful and an eyesore. She can't teach without insulting students.

She is a very intimidating and rude professor who oversteps boundaries and uses the privilege of her power to tear down students. She was mean from the beginning but the worst as class ended. It was a sour note to conclude my Gies experience. I struggled but was scared to get help from how she was humiliating in class. She was ineffective.

Gives little guidance on what to do for projects and set students up for failure for exams and doesn't believe in study guides, says that she purposely finds and makes obscure questions for exams. Her exam averages are super low and she doesn't believe in extra credit. She has an auto failure of the class if you miss more than two days of class.

This professor does not care about her students, she makes students uncomfortable and attacks students that are international and people of color. She calls out students for not knowing the answer and makes the learning environment feel hostile. She belittles students and makes them not feel welcomed. Has a negative energy and comes to class late.

Like the last post said, she will be hearing from my therapist. This experience will cost me a lot in therapy. I felt dehumanized in her class. I had no motivation to participate knowing I would get humiliated let alone study or pay attention. It's not like she gives effective lessons anyway. The Harvard Business cases are way more useful than her.

I'm literally considering therapy at this point because of her and her classes. I worry that her rudeness will be a barrier to me getting the most out of my professional life. Whatever I do after graduation certainly won't relate to her poorly executed lectures and slideshows and inability to effectively convey material.

There comes a time when a professor needs to stop teaching, and that is now for Martinez. She brings her negative attitude to class and instills fear among students. She demonstrates poor character and business professionalism. She yells at students for the same things that she does. I have cried after class so many times.

Just look at her ratio of reviews here and how an overwhelming majority are 1 star. She is a bad person and a bad professor. Everyone knows you don't take personal issues out on students. Most of my classmates are uncomfortable. She doesn't encourage her office hours and if you go you aren't welcome. Her presentations are disorganized and boring.

A few people have described her as being vindictive and I think that's definitely the word here. You can't even focus on her poorly executed lectures because she's so focused on creating enemies out of her students. Very revengeful professor. Ineffective lectures, poor blurry slides, horrible learning environment. I don't feel motivated to finish.

After you take this class, forget everything. You will never use it in the real world. If anything, you might think mean people are nice because this professor is a level of cruel that was incomprehensible to me before taking this class and getting disrespected by her every Tuesday and Thursday.

I'm thrilled people are spamming this because it is necessary to prove how many students were affected by her class. It was a traumatic experience for me as an international student. Every time I talked she told me to speak up or yelled "WHAT" with a rude look on her face. It's already hard enough being an international student.

Do not expect any consideration if you take her class. She definitely takes advantage of the fact that she is tenured. She is a professor with genuinely no knowledge of what she teaches. She made a snide remark about girls who show up late to her class when she does that every single class, so you can guess how ineffective she and her lectures are.

The professor is like a high school mean girl who never aged out. This is the mentality she brings to her lectures, along with coming to class late and being extremely unprofessional. She puts in no effort and is monotonous. I cheer in my head when her lectures are over because she talks nonsense and is very mean. This class is required sadly.

I'm not even motivated to do any more work for her class because she has made the material and lectures very unenjoyable. I probably would have cared more if I had a different professor. I was one of her targets this semester and I am mentally drained. Gies needs to stop hiring professors who have no manners.

I've never seen a professor get so much joy out of seeing her students so miserable. She is so happy when students are upset. People can't focus properly in class because of fear. She creates a fear-based   learning environment. She has been given a very long rope.

Blunt and rude are two totally different things. She thinks shes blunt but shes RUDE. Unable to effectively teach material but can humiliate students well. Lacks basic etiquette. Teaching style is not suited for any educational institutions. I haven't been able to sleep properly in weeks because of her rudeness when I participate in class.

If you get stuck with her somehow, drop the class or wait to take it with someone else. There's no issue with the course itself, but with the way she teaches it. It's business policy and strategy with a side of intimidation, humiliation, rudeness, insults. If she targets you for something it'll be for the whole semester and reflect in your grades.

I took her class a couple semesters ago but I heard from my friends in class this semester that she got even worse than when I had her. She was very disorganized, showed up late to class and also still did target quiet students and non-native speakers, but it seems like it got much worse now. Her exam averages were always low and material sucked.

Now that the class is finally over, I can share my thoughts. She does not show professionalism when coming to class 10 mins late or being rude to students and grading based on her mood towards them. She actively makes threats to students. She makes the material confusing and if you admit your confusion, she will make fun of you. DO NOT TAKE HER.

She lacks any sort of professionalism and is extremely rude to students. If they mess up, she will be nasty. It's not a proper environment for growth and she criticizes some students to no end. How she treats her students and conducts classes is very unlike the real world. She should be on a reality TV show for rudest professors in the school.

She is a shame to Gies. I'm sure the recent outpour of reviews has to do with the Reddit post about her recently and rightfully so. Many students have been holding in their feelings the entire semester. She uses her position of power to intimidate students and portrays a totally different side to her colleagues. Her lectures are ineffective.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There are tough professors who genuinely care for their students' professional development, and then there's Martinez who cares to make sure students feel unprepared and unsuccessful especially if they fall under a certain demographic. Unstructured, disorganized, and always late to class. Cold calls students for the purpose of being mean. DO NOT.

Martinez has problems separating her personal feelings from her work life. She brings her personal problems to work and takes them out on students who don't follow her rules or she just doesn't like. Usually, if you fall in this group you are a POC or international student. If she asks for suggestions don't give any. She will argue with you.

She was very vindictive towards a student who violated her class policy by significantly lowering their grade. She took her anger out on that student constantly for the entire semester and made sure they knew they were unwelcome. Maybe if she valued her students, they wouldn't violate the no tech policy. Nobody even wants to be in her class.

Her teaching style is disorganized, threatening and rude and her ability to convey the material is poor. Students who struggle don't feel comfortable going to her for help because she makes them so uncomfortable. She complains about slides she made and stares down students who she feels like who sit in a certain row.

I personally struggled in this class throughout the semester and I couldn't even feel like I could ask for help because of how intimidating she is in class. I feared if I went to office hours, she would make fun of me since she is so rude and insulting in class. She is unclear about grading and expectations and shows up 10 minutes late every class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: She is a nightmare of a professor. She has certainly violated policy multiple times by talking about her political views in class and being RUDE to certain groups of students. She is shady about her grading policy, her Canvas is disorganized. She wanted students to come to her class in hazardous weather and used profanity.

Good luck if you want a professor who respects you and actually cares for your feedback. She will argue with you if you give her feedback but when she gives you personally directed criticism, you cannot talk back to her. She does not respect students, she wants to humiliate them. I don't think her supervisors have any idea how she is in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She can turn your otherwise quality Gies education into an unpleasant experience. Taking this class with her will ruin your mental health, sanity and self-esteem. Her grading procedures make me anxious. She will be extra hard on POC women. She takes her pent up rage and describes it as academic criticism but it is targeted and filled with hate.

I don't understand how she talks to students the way she does and gets away with it. It is very hurtful. Her class gave me anxiety and still does even though I'm done. She has outdated materials and teaching methods and loves to disrespect students. She wants us to participate but shames us when we do. Doesn't prepare us for tests. AVOID HER
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: Her class gave me anxiety and still does even though it's over because she is very intimidating to students who do not look like her. Questionable grading procedures, rude in class, cold calls students, tries to trap students and attack them for not studying, deceptive exams and makes the class how she wants it. DO NOT TAKE HER
Don't take this professor, she is very mean and lacks empathy for her students. Her class gave me so much anxiety as she would cold call on poc.

She has no passion or knowledge in her field, no real world experience, grades on her own whims and fancies, misleads students on purpose, gives repetitive assignments, loves to embarrass students she doesn't like, very RUDE and MEAN. Her behavior should not be excused. Most students in Gies know about her and her issues.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: Ambiguous grading policies, personal attacks to students especially ones not like her, unwelcome learning environment and cold-calling used to shame students. Likes to interrupt students just to get the glory of saying "YOU ARE WRONG." Encouraging participation only works in a warm environment, not one where you are afraid to be wrong and learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I've never seen a professor this disrespectful and inconsiderate towards her students, especially those that are not like her. If possible, avoid taking classes with her at all costs, even if it means waiting a semester to take it with a different professor. She is very mean, makes you feel unwelcome, and overall unpleasant to be around.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I haven't taken her, but I have friends that do and they always complain about her being politically incorrect towards students. Someone posted about her on Reddit too and many people commented that their observations are valid and they heard about her. My friend said they felt very targeted in her class and it ruined their mental health.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: This professor has no experience in the corporate world yet disguises her very rude and targeted feedback to students as "criticism they won't give you in the real world that you need to know." She uses her personal issues with students and takes them out in academic pursuits saying "that's how academia works." Unclear expectations, poor structure.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Someone called her feedback and grading "capricious" and I think that is very true. Her grades and feedback aren't measured by students' effort, but rather by her problems with them. If she catches a student on their phone or looking down or not making eye contact with her the whole time, she will seek revenge through comments and grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She uses threats a lot during class and makes students extremely uncomfortable especially POC and international as many people have said. She encourages participation but is dismissive if you get an answer wrong and makes you feel bad about it. She has personal issues with students and makes it seem academic. She is highly under qualified.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: If you're a POC or international student, avoid taking her class. She will target you especially and ask you to speak up or pretend she cannot understand your accent. She has made comments about international students before, particularly Chinese and Indian. If you're white you're fine. She tells students to think, learn and grow but we can't.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I am so glad to never step foot in her class again. Different professors for the same class have different and more clear grading procedures than her. She doesn't prepare students for exams and uses outdated material. She thinks she's being funny when she's actually making students uncomfortable.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: She is very rude and targets certain students in her feedback, making very blunt comments and not sparing anyone's feelings. Unclear and ambiguous grading criteria, perhaps even capricious. She has no corporate or real business experience yet teaches about business policy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: One word to describe Candace is insufferable. There are tough professors who are good people, and she is not. She lacks empathy and consideration and makes her students anxious. She complains about slides she didn't make, so why can't she make them? Her TA's do more work than her but she gets paid a handsome salary. She should be reported.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: My group had a presentation last week for her class. We received very harsh feedback from her and then she held one of my groupmates back after a team meeting with her. I later got to know that she gave that student individual cruel feedback and berated their performance and accused them of not contributing to the project when I know they did.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Expect critical and harsh feedback from her, not constructive. It seemed like it was more anger directed toward the particular students rather than helping the team improve for the report. Questionable teaching methods and grading. Office hours are useless and she just wants to leave, why even hold them then? She complains about slides consistently
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I didn't even plan on taking the class with her, I signed up for another professor. Then it got changed to her, unfortunately. Unclear grading procedures, rude during office hours, rude to students and in class and makes very snide remarks and comments. Her class environment is very uncomfortable and nobody in my class even likes going.

The 5 good reviews she has on here are clearly ragebait. She never fails to make her classroom an uncomfortable environment for everyone. Her instructions are very unclear and her grading procedures are unfair and put students into a box. She has "good" and "bad" students stereotyped in her mind and treats them according to how she sees them.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She offers feedback that is extremely critical, not constructive. She was very rude to a group mate of mine who is ESL and used note cards. She is consistently rude and disrespectful to students and gives repetitive writings with unclear criteria. Her grading policies are unfair and questionable. I dreaded this class everyday.

I am so thrilled to leave this class tomorrow and never take it again. This was my least enjoyable class of the semester, and I learned that a professor can really make or break the experience, in this case break. She singled me out many times during the semester and loved seeing students be miserable. She was very rude during office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She is very rude during office hours and in class, singles students out, stereotypes students and puts them in boxes, does things her own way and strives to challenge students in ways that harm learning. People are scared of even going to her class and it is reasonable why. She should not be in a classroom, she should be a women's prison warden.

She constantly disrespects students and makes them feel unwelcome. She does not prepare you adequately for exams. She offers repetitive assignments and takes off points from students. She categorizes students as underachievers and targets them during classes. She has referred to underdeveloped countries in cases very rudely before.

Just thinking about going to her class gives me panic attacks. It has been an uncomfortable semester in her class and she has singled me out publicly for being an underperformer in her class.
