She gives me menacing stares during class, not to forget gives very repetitive assignments and never explains directions. Takes off points without explaining.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She complains about exams and slides that she made. Participation is required which is fine until you're a quiet person, non-Native speaker, or not confident in your answer. Disrespected students continuously throughout the semester. Essays are repetitive; she is unclear about any requirements. Absolutely DO NOT TAKE HER.

She made a student cry in class today. She always complains that she doesn't make materials for the class, so why not make them then if you hate them so much? She is extremely disrespectful to underperformers and has referenced her political views in class before which is extremely unprofessional. She makes $162,000 too many and does no work.

She is extremely unprepared and on numerous occasions has shown certain tendencies against East Asian and Hispanic students. While other students are on their phones during her class which is no technology, she will only single out these students and go out of her way to ensure an unenjoyable class experience. She singles out struggling students.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Boring lectures + unorganized canvas + unorganized slides she "teaches" off = a disaster of a class. She tests us on the textbook she hates, so the info we have to know is cherry-picked and given to us last-minute. The cases are boring to read & many of the assignments are given no structure on what to do but graded very harshly. AVOID THIS TEACHER
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor was always late to class and her slides were outdated making it hard for students to follow. The exams had content on there that was unrelated to class material bringing exam averages down. There was also excessive writing assignments that were repetitive for no reason. She was also rude to students when giving feedback. DO NOT TAKE HER.

This class is an easy A compared to some of the other teachers. I have had friends with a different teacher and their gpa was much lower than mine. We all took the same course but with different teachers. Prof. Martinez gives good feedback on assignments. If you participate in class and do her easy assignments. It's not hard to get an A.

She is predictably late to every single class, class structure messy and she is noticeably unprepared. Outdated standards for consulting slides. Her slides are blurry, messy, and unclear. RUDE to everyone and makes students uncomfortable. Workload unreasonable for business core class.
Do NOT take her. Exam is hard. Doesn't listen to advices. A lot of writing assignments.

Makes unwarranted comments to students during class and interrupts them as they speak. Asks for feedback and then challenges students and does not take it into account. Very tough grader and not very approachable. Does not prepare you enough for exams/gives you resources at the very last minute. Do not take with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor is a tough grader but she is very helpful on what I got wrong and why. Class is animated and lectures engages you! Professor has a very good ability to convey the notes/lectures slide to our everyday life. 100% would take it again!

Rude to student, like to interrupt during others talking just to tell you, you are WRONG. The slide deck is very useless and messy, most thing are screen shot from wherever. She doesn't care about students opinion, not willing to take suggestions. Don't take hers.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She does not care nor respect students. Will go on long tangents regarding slides that she does not like yet still made. Asks for advice but will not take anything into consideration. After there was an 8% curve (from problems didn't have a right answer) the average was still a 78%. Very argumentative and will not listen to anything students say
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor is very refreshing and shows a lot of effort in class. Cares about students and cares about the class. Professor has clear instructions with homework, readings and projects.

Late to class every day. Complains about the slide deck that she uses without fail, every class. Very negative about everything and is rude to all students. Never teaches us anything more than what's on the slides. Forced participation with horrible feedback. Do not take.

The class in itself is an easy A but the professor has disorganized outline of assignments and unnecessary directions on final projects. Her feedback is very Kurt and cuts people off without giving them the chance to give their thoughts. Feedback is more critical than constructive

The professor often arrives late, cuts off students and presentations rudely, and gives feedback lacking empathy. The course design is unclear, with outdated materials and overlaps with required courses, offering little on multinational enterprises.

Always 10 minutes late to class. Assigns more homework faster then she can grade it. While she is upbeat and encourages class participation in a healthy way she is curt and rude to students if they don't follow her nonexistent directions for assignments. No sympathy to students, out of date teaching materials, feedback=degrading the student.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Very disappointing; She lacks class structure, arrives 5 minutes late to every class without fail, sets an unreasonable amount of homework and assignments, pretty rude to all students, has zero sympathy towards students, has out of date teaching methods, fails to give reasonable feedback. I would never this professor again. She is paid $150,000 btw

This class and professor are a disgrace to an otherwise fantastic business program. Prof. Martinez is kind, but I found her to be disorganized when it came to the structure of the course. Expect excessive busy work, long/drawn-out lectures, and unreasonable attendance expectations. I'm surprised this course ever made it out of the pilot phase.

I really enjoyed Professor Martinez. She took her time connecting material to topics outside of a traditional business sense. It was a refreshing experience that increase my overall happiness and optimism about the field.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Inspirational

reviews.comment: BUS 401 curriculum seriously needs to be reconsidered. The class was a waste of time and one of the least valuable business core classes offered, even compared to gen eds. None of the material relates and the whole course was a captive zoom audience with no purpose. Professor was overly aggressive with participation that did not to the material.

She's a good instructor, but no chill at all. She will call on people out of nowhere and force you to participate, which is understandable to a certain degree. Made it very clear during the first class that she does not like extra credit. Not very compromising and came off as unreasonably strict at times.

This class was flipped (lectures online & then you go to class & discuss/build on to lectures). She is very knowledgeable & knows a lot about everything. 2 exams (with 2 take-home essays), 10 image assignments (easy), 4 economist assignments (longer, but not hard), a group project with paper, & other things throughout, but a fun class nonetheless.

Very gracious to students during class, explains everything in a clear and simple way, and wants everyone to do well. I think the structure of doing lecture videos outside of class and collaborative work in class works really great and is a much more effective use of class time then vice versa.

Not the biggest fan of the "lectures at home, work in class" thing, but he is very very helpful whenever you have a question. The tests are very similar to the practice tests and he makes sure you understand at office hours. You will have to watch the lectures on Canvas and take a quiz after but its not bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: good class, very group work focused, plenty of opportunity to get grade back up

Stat 200 with Dr. Clement is a very easy A. He uses a flipped classroom which was intimidating in the beginning, but it's nice being able to look back on lecture videos to study for midterms. He also has class discussions about the work we do in class, making it easy to get an A on all the activities. You can tell he wants you to succeed.

The class functions on a flipped classroom structure. There is lots of opportunities to practice and ask questions. Kit is a delight, always willing to help and explain concepts in easy to digest ways. He makes learning approachable. I have previously taken a stats course, but I don't doubt that someone new to stats would succeed under Kit.
helpful and patient
Tons of helpless assignments and in-class works.

The way this class is structures is honestly unrivaled. The way Rstudio and TinkerPlots are used to develop a very intuitive understanding builds a very strong foundation for future statistics courses. As someone who's done stats before taking this class, I'm not too sure how this class is for a new stats student, but I'd say you're in good hands.
Enjoyed listening to him yap in class and in video lectures. Fun guy to be around and a pretty easy, laid-back class. Often able to get out of class 5 - 30 minutes early too.

Super caring teacher who wants everyone to succeed. The main parts of your grade will be in the in-class activities so it's important to go to class, and these are completion grades. There's only two midterms and no final, midterms are definitely outweighed by the homework and in class assignments.

Kit is an amazing professor. He piloted a new, collaborative learning format for STAT 200 and it was an instant success. He cares for his students, is open to feedback and improvement, and designs the course with the intention of his students succeeding. His online videos were informative, and he was very accessible inside and outside of class.

This guy cares about teaching and about teaching effectively. He cares about his students and has compassion and flexibility for their circumstances. His course can be annoying at times, but that's because it's built to pull you towards success and understanding. He's always trying to improve—it makes him one of the brighter minds on this campus.

Kit is a great professor, great personality and makes his lectures and videos very engaging. Class goes over a lot of great and interesting statistics topics and R code. Tinkerplots assignments were great visualizations for understanding topics. Very clear grading criteria, generous grading, and plenty of EC. Great professor and awesome class!

Kit is one of my favorite profs I've had here. You learn by watching video lectures outside of class, and class time is spent working in small groups on assignments based on the videos. He's a really nice guy and very approachable during office hours. Relatively easy course if you've taken some sort of stats class in the past.

Kit is one of my favorite profs I've had here. You learn mainly through the video lectures outside of class, and the class time is spent working together with a group of about 3 people on activities based on those lectures. This really worked well for me. The course is relatively easy if you've taken some sort of stats class in the past.

Kit is one of my favorite professors I've had here. He is very upbeat and made lectures exciting to come to, and he was always super accessible outside of class. Video lectures are watched outside of class, and you would work in groups of around 3 during class to finish related assignments. The class is well structured and I thoroughly enjoyed it.

Kit was a very good prof. He clearly cares about Stats and his students. He does lecture on videos that you watch over the weekend and Tuesday and Thursday are small group (3ppl) homework assignments. Makes an interesting style but can be difficult if you get paired with a bad group.

I really admire the way Kit designed this class. There's no textbook, lectures are recorded videos that we watch for homework, in-class group work is productive and always doable, there's many assignments but they aren't burdensome and it makes it so nothing has too much weight on your grade. Overall great class procedure, lectures, and workload!

He was a good teacher, but I found him a bit boring in lectures only. He is a sweet person during office hours. He low-key rocks. But during lectures, the talking is way too long, and especially hour-and-a-half classes can get really long. Paying attention was really hard sometimes, but if you can handle that, he's the best to go to.

Kit is by far the most understanding professor you will ever meet and for that alone he deserves every star possible. He was easy to reach outside of class and very easily accomodated me when I missed a quiz and THE IN PERSON FINAL! Seriously, this guy is an absolute gem of a professor and you would be lucky to have him. The class wasn't too bad.

Kit is SUCH a great professor. Made stats fun and engaging, truly a great guy who cares a lot about his students. His lectures can get a bit long, but he's early in his teaching career and really takes feedback into consideration so I don't foresee this being as much of an issue in the future. Highly recommend!

I wish everyone could know how great prof clement is! He's funny and cares for his students a lot. As long as you come to his office hour he would spare no effort to help you better understand the content. Always patient and easy going, a professor really loves education and math.

I had Professor Clement for STAT 100 and I thought that he was a great professor. While sometimes it's annoying to have mandatory attendance to just sit for a lecture, Professor Clement takes a different approach and makes the class more interesting. Majority of the points come from attending and participating in the class.

My favorite professor at this school so far; you can tell that he's a genuinely very nice person who looks out for his students. Although our lectures were long, I appreciate how much he tried to keep us focused through relatable examples. My only complaint about his system is his attendance checks, but he's mostly lenient on that as well.

Professor Kit is amazing! The lectures are long (1hr 20 min), but he tries his best to break it up with practice problems (although he will stop if not enough people are actually working together). The homeworks and tests are straighforward. Office hours are great and the course staff is very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: One of the best professors when it comes to teaching an introductory statistics class. His guided notes allow you to focus on the material and not worry about writing everything down. 10/10 would take his class again.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Good lecturer and knows his stuff but it can get boring. The lectures are long and the information is thrown at you. Needs more collaboration and discussion to get to know people. Office Hours are amazing with helpful people. Doable HW every week and easy enough midterms with lots of help. Lectures are available online and a forum for questions too
He is a great lecturer and provides fill-in-blanket lecture notes for the course.

No point in the lectures, apart from getting the answers to the worksheets. The 10 CBTF quizzes were very easy and based directly on the homework and worksheets. The class needs a major redesign as the prarielearn workspace coding is very slow and crashes constantly.

One of the most unprepared lecturers that I have had at UIUC. If you are taking him get ready for him to stutter through his unprepared notes and then introduce you to material that you do not need whatsoever. His class is not laid out well at all and his students are constantly correcting his errors. Overall, very disappointing class.  Good luck

Lectures are just him coding. Nice guy, but class is super unhelpful. You watch him code, then try to code in groups for worksheets. Lectures did not effectively teach the concepts that come up on homework. I think stats requires a good conceptual understanding of the material, but if you don't have that already, 202 might get hard.

Consistently unprepared & confused during lectures. Unable to answer questions or explain material; especially coding. Often contradicted the syllabus & gave misleading advice on major assignments. Teamwork issues dragged down individual grades & ignored repeated complaints. Unfairly removed extra credit for students who completed all their work.

This professor is very nice, but get ready to learn everything by yourself. He often make mistakes in lecture and he continues typing in R when the rest of the class are clueless. Tests structure is awful, questions weighted heavily and for most questions you only get 3 tries if not 2, imo doesn't reflect how much you've learned.

He lectured by mumbling while typing into an R-markdown. He never taught the key concepts of any units, and many students had large fundamental gaps, which made in-class work and tests hard. His syllabus said that the lowest of the 4 midterms could be dropped, and without announcing it to the class, he changed it to only the lowest of the first 3.
He is supportive all the time during the semester.
He clearly did not know the material, and office hours are basically non existent. A nice guy but lectures are extremely useless and the guidelines for the project make no sense.
Work in groups to solve worksheets for attendance. Plenty of ways to get help. Exams are fair.
Prof didn't know the material very well. Learned everything through the answer keys of the worksheets and the review sessions. Final project isnt too bad.

I've never been compelled to add a rating on this site until having Dorothy as a professor. The criteria she uses when grading essays is a joke. She asks for your personal experiences then faults you when your own experiences aren't "interesting" enough for her. The mean for each assignment hovers around 60%, it makes no sense.

If you don't write the way she likes and wants with her very unclear grading criteria, you'll be getting 40-60% on each assignment. She provides her own feedback on your own study abroad experience. But the greatest joke is that you can't even resubmit an assignment with a failing grade for a higher grade after implementing her feedback.

This was the least useful class I've taken at UIUC. We've already studied abroad, now being forced to take this class for a grade, and now we are just writing fluff and bs for a few hours each week so that we can show that we've learned and thought about race, class, and other ideological stuff? What is the point of it?
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: My first iMBA course. Fantastic professor who clearly loves teaching and often begins with a virtual campus tour. Lectures were engaging, with interviews in the MOOCs and interactive live sessions. Standout lessons on product differentiation and bidding wars. Highly experienced and enjoyable lessons. I didn't want to miss a single live class.

He is not up to par of an MBA program. There are 5 star ratings within this forum, but they seem "sus" because all of them have been written on May 8th (not trustworth). The lecture doesn't provide important info. The assignments are based on principles he made up and not used world-wide business. You're forced to use his made up concept.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lecture heavy, but they make sense if you go deep into understanding.
His class is a pretty easy A. It's easier than most undergrad classes.
Interesting class and interesting teaching method
He is professional and has a good teaching style especially for class being online
He's really not that bad, you just have to get to understand him and his frameworks. He is definitely a subject matter expert.
I enjoyed his class. People just don't like to do work or think but compared to some underqualified professors I have had, his lectures are refreshing.
Unfortunately, you can't avoid this guy. His study guide makes no sense. The exam is way off from what you have practiced in homework. Awful. Additionally, his lectures are pointless.
Most boring self centered professor ever. Class drags by and he always goes over time. Teaches primarily off of opinions and frameworks he made up.
He is insufferable, rambles on and on about topics of a subject that would be very interesting, if he wasn't the one teaching it. Not sure why UIUC has him teaching, despite such a bad reputation.
super hard class and no way to get good grades from that class. I'm not too fond of his teaching way and I would never recommended it.
bad teacher and bad teaching but I don't think there is another teacher teaching this class. They sign this class name to another teach so students can sign up. He is too bad.

He made Strategic Management a very hard course when it could've been an interesting one. His grading style was so random and unfair to the point where he had to give us a grade curve every time. There were no accommodations for people who had internet or technology issues even though it is an online masters program.
Just finished the course. Never seen such a bad educator. Disgrace of a prof for UIUC
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Live lectures are useless, self-serving, and disjointed. Lectures always go over time, which is difficult for working professionals. More details are passed out in office hours rather than in classes. VERY subjective grader. More time is spent trying to write what the TA will give credit for than actually learning. Very disappointed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: I wish I could attach a picture so you could see how I scored a 100 % on every assignment except the final exam. The mean on the final exam was 70 % and the instructor sent an email acknowledging the exam was designed for students to score low.  Do not take this professor if you value your GPA.  He didn’t cover materials on the final exam.

Deepak Somaya teaches his own theories that he made up in his mind. Ex. VARS Framework. Nobody in the world knows what that is. He teaches students to forget about the SWOT analysis and take his unknown VARS framework to analyze. This guy is teaching unknown theories. Beware! Never had a teacher like this guy before. LMFAO
Live lectures are point-less. Graders grade papers as if it were an English class.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Group projects

reviews.comment: Professor Deepak Somaya is a complete Fraud.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This is hands down the worst professor I've had at UIUC. I genuinely couldn't stress hard enough to AVOID HIM. Lectures were disorganized, instructions for difficult assignments were unclear, and his tone often felt rude and condescending. The overall teaching quality was far below expectations and made the class much harder than it needed to be.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This guy is the most vague professor at UIUC. He goes on 30 minute rants about topics that are not relevant at all to the lecture. No one knows what is going on in the class ever. He still doesn't know a single student's name in my class and it's week 7. None of the lectures build off each other and are all over the place.

He is the single most unorganized person I've ever met and can't even keep track of his lectures. He forgot to assign the midterm exam and made us take it the first class back after spring break. Gives ZERO instructions on any assignments or projects. I learned more from chat gpt over the course of a week than I did from him in an entire semester.

Try to stay away from Kennemore's class. While he has a lot of industry experience, he lacks control of the classroom. I often left lectures confused because of how unorganized everything is presented. No syllabus or rubric for the final project. All over the place! I will say he is responsive to questions but often very vague.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Walking blind in the middle of the street in open traffic is safer than going to this class, to keep some sort of sanity in any semester going forward, do NOT take Kennemore, he has the most vague instructions for assignments and final project possible, most of the class never knows what they are doing at any point in this semester. Never again.

Review for 442: Notes are riddled with typos, and serious ones. No solutions to homeworks released, and releases midterm practice solutions the day before the exam. Seems very last minute on all things.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: First day of class, the professor posted his whole and very detailed notes. This was perfect as it allowed me to follow along with the lectures better. He really made me enjoy the subject at hand (Mathematical Biology). He has numerous office hours every week too.
He is the BEST professor in UIUC. He is so kind and patient towards students. His lecture notes are really clear and test grading is fair. He wanna every students to be successful in the class.

Professor Cooney was a really good lecturer. He explained concepts really well, and was super accessible outside of class - through email or during office hours. His exams were quite straightforward and quite easy if you studied his review material well.

The tests are very fair if you prepare for them and the class overall is very fairly graded. Dunfield is also great at drawing and a good lecturer. He does explains things a little hard though so if you aren't strong at math expect to put in a lot of work in this class.
Great lecturer, but explains things too fast that I couldn't understand

Lectures and notes are pretty good. HW has nothing to do with what is on the exam, practically don't spend too much time on them. Use practice exams to prepare, the earlier the better. Overall, I would say his exams are challenging. He is professional and gives amazing stats about the class.

The homework on Webassign is quite difficult (if you do it yourself...). Dunfield himself is incredibly smart, but I didn't like how much he focused on the proofs instead of applications. I learned best going to office hours. Make sure you do well from the first midterm onwards. I did pretty mediocre on two midterms and ruined my chances at an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This was my second time taking this course and he definitely showed me that having a good professor makes a huge difference. His lectures were super engaging, and he provided multiple examples for each new concept that we learned. Overall a super cool professor and really cared about his students and their wellbeing.

Certainly a tough class, but Dunfield is extremely accessible. He's the most passionate lecturer I've had (although I am just a freshman), and he was helpful outside of class and understanding of extraneous circumstances.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great lectures including sketches of proofs and drawings to help visualize concepts (he is very good at drawing). Prof and TAs are available often and lots of resources to help are online, including lots of practice tests that are similar to actual tests. The homework can be very time consuming. ~$90 online textbook is required.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: professor dunfield is by far the best calc 3 prof. his lectures were easy to understand and he is really good at drawing. all the course resources are also easy to access. his exams are quite straightforward if you understand the content.

Prof. Dunfield is very theory-focused and rarely works exam-style examples, which can be tough for average students. I recommend Professor Leonard plus PLT/Merit. Many skipped lectures but still did well using posted past midterms/finals. Homework was much harder than exams.

If you need to take Math 241, take Dunfield. All of the professors cover the same topics and have the same exams. Dunfield's lectures are by far the best of the lot. His drawings are amazing, and he does examples, but he mostly spends his time covering the reasons why something is true, which helped me understand it well. DO THE PRACTICE EXAMS!

Lectures are clear and well-organized, with a strong emphasis on theory. He is very helpful and approachable during office hours and willing to explain concepts in different ways. Exams reflect the material covered, and his practice materials align well with expectations. Overall, a supportive professor who cares about student understanding.
Watching videos of the topic instead of going to lecture saved me loads of time as lecture at times was on things not on the tests. He is very approachable and has many resources

Often times skips over foundational material leading him to be "too smart" for the average student. Lecture is often wasted on useless information that has never been on tests and homework is far more difficult than the actual midterms and final. The midterms and final make up about 80% of your grade so bombing one jeopardizes your whole grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: 90% of lecture is drawings, 10% is computational/applicable demonstrations. I never learned in lecture. 85% of your grade is exams. I just watched Professor Leonard the whole semester, put his videos on 2x speed and you'll learn 10x as much in the same amount of time as lecture.

For the LOVE OF GOD I hope he puts out lecture videos, this hell house of a course will make your fundamentals terrible if you are not constantly in lecture, and that gets hard sometimes. If you get behind, prepare for the spiral of not knowing the content because there is so little to go off of digitally, the notes are quite sparse.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This rating is for the class structure. No lecture videos + the way we follow the textbook is NOTHING like any online video or resource. Makes it really hard to study if you miss a lecture or get confused during one, and the notes are just "too smart" for people without context on the concepts. Lectures are good, IF you understand them during.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He a good professor, very proof heavy and definitely teaches smarter than the average student, but the homework is really helpful and there's several opportunities to learn content in different ways

Far too smart for the average student. Often skips over foundational concepts, doesn't show steps for examples, and overcomplicates simple ideas. Honors HW is difficult, especially without help. The exams aren't too bad. If you are very confident in your calculus abilities and want to learn as much as possible, take his class. Otherwise, avoid him.

Very good professor, you can tell he's an expert in his field and is passionate about what he teaches. Honors homework is tough but fair, and it does help in fleshing out your understanding on the subject. My one issue is that he's "too smart for the average person", often skipping the fundamental and basic ideas and leaving you very confused.
best math professor ever

Dunfield wants his students to succeed. He's extremely generous with his time and extremely approachable and friendly when answering questions. Very good artist too (important for math) and provides lots of visuals. Great for those who love math and are good at rigorous math language.

Extremely beautiful drawing. Excellent lextures. Best Notes ever. It is an honor to take his lectures. Although the participation is not mandatory, it is still hard to find a seat if came in late. He explained everything patiently, and always willing to prepare students ready for the exam.
GOAT. Just take his class and you will know.
he is good at drawing

He basically has no time to teach you the proof, and shifts fast in topic. If you do not have good foundation, and pre-learned knowledge, you need to spend more time learning by yourself. Nonetheless, he is an interesting professor, and he is welcoming during his Office Hour.

241 students are so lucky to have dunfield as an option this semester. Take him! He is the best professor here. Genius but still can explain things in a way that makes sense. He is passionate about math and just all around a great guy. Class is super organized - never been given so much resources/feedback from a professor.
AMAZING!!!!! He is going to be away at a workshop next week and I am so sad. He is the best professor of my life!
Best professor of my life.
AMAZING PROFESSOR!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Nathan is an awesome professor! He cares about students and wants everyone to succeed in this course. His lecture is very clear, and he will upload handwritten notes for every lecture. The course is tough, but attending his office hour is super helpful! I will definitely take his course again!

Prof Dunfield was an amazing professor who taught the class really well. He really liked the concepts and had amazing drawing skills that are not to be messed with. The lectures were kind of awkward because he'd ask questions and wait for someone to answer and no one ever wanted to answer, so don't be afraid to talk. Office hours were very helpful
Super nice and patient professor willing to explain everything until you understand. Hold several office hours per week, and willing to extend office hour when students' problems are not solved.
Amazing lecturer. His lectures motivated me to get up at 8am.
Great lectuer, though he goes a little fast sometimes. He really knows his stuff too, and is great to ask for help. Easily the best professor Ive had here so far
Professor Dunfield is the best math teacher I've ever had in my life. His office hours are very useful and he takes the time to ensure that you understand the topic if you seek the help.

Amazing professor. So many resources (online lecture notes that he hand-wrote, worksheets and exam solutions, course diary, practice exams, pdf of your graded exam, office hours, etc etc) that can help you achieve the grade you want. Also, I really enjoyed his subtle humor.

Worksheets are harder than tests, but they are very good tool for me to get a big picture of new concepts. Practice exams are much like the real ones. The course is not easy, but u'll feel much easier if your professor is Nathan. The grading is fair. The overall grade will be curved. So dont worry. PS: Hes in suits every day! Like his beard : D

Calc 3 isn't the easiest class but Dunfield is exceptional at teaching its many concepts. The lectures are very informative and easy to understand, and exams themselves are very fair, and you get adequate preparation with many practice exams. Additionally, they are graded very fairly and quickly, and you get to see exactly where you went wrong.

Sometimes I think he is so talented and nice that he should teach sth much beyond Calc 3. If you can please take his class. He explains everything in lectures and hes very approachable outside class. He is patient and ready to every question. Hes also excellent at drawing 3D pictures to help you understand the geometry in calculus.

Dunfield's lectures weren't boring, but sometimes they had no big picture and were confusing. He also sometimes went off topic and taught us things we didn't need for the test, which was annoying. Discussion worksheets were way harder than the tests. Tests were almost exactly like practice tests, so focus on studying from those. Fair grading.
Nathan Dunfield is amazing.

Prof. Dunfield is by far one of the most talented professors I have had so far. He makes a effort to connect with students, especially in a large class like MATH 241. For example, I went to  his office hours once and he remembered my name. Additionally, his lectures are phenomenally clear and handles in class questions exceptionally well.

Dunfield is one of the smartest people I have ever met in my entire life. He is an amazing lecture and while he lectures quickly, they are clear (he is amazing at drawing the 3D shapes). His tests are very difficult, but the practice exams are great prep. I am not great at math but I managed to get through.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Dunfield has very clear lectures and is always ready to answer any questions in a succinct manner. All of his drawings are incredible and make learning the concepts much more straightforward. Office hours can be quite helpful and he makes the effort to know you by name if you stop by and ask questions. Take this professor!!!!!

He's so sweet! I took calc 3 as dual credit, didn't do so well, took it here with Nathan and he's a million times better than my prior instructor. Really helpful discussion worksheets and sends you a PDF of your exams so you can see your mistakes and how you are doing in calc 3.
An awesome professor who is always enthuastic in the lecture. The lectures are instructed clearly and organized well . He's extremely helpful and accessible to students. The notes are really helpful.

Dunfield is probably one of the smartest math minds I've ever seen in my life. Because of that, at times it can be hard to keep up with his lectures and his way of explaining things can be more complex than it needs to be. However, he's a great artist so his visuals are super helpful.
Very good prof, clear and insightful. one of the best ive had during my time at Uni
Perfect sketching just make 3-D much easier
Very good professor. Gives clear lectures and really cares about students. Though the course is tough, you will probably end up with good grades.

Awesome lecturer, very knowledgeable about the material (used to teach at CIT). But he sometimes goes too fast in lecture. Pretty helpful during office hours, HWs and exams are decent, not too hard not too easy. You will be able to do well if you put time in it.

Awesome professor. The class is a lot of work, but he provides you the resources to get the grade you want.  Regularly attending lecture and discussion, doing homework, and going to office hours/the Calc III tutor room will allow you to get the grade you want.

The class's discussion and homework tend to be difficult, but they aren't worth too much of your grade. They're where the problem solving learning takes place, and you should take them seriously, but not worry too much about grades so much as the learning and practice you get from them. Dunfield is a wiz when it comes to his drawings on the blackboards, and he explains the basic concepts very well. I never had any problems with him. He taught at Harvard and Caltech for awhile, so no surprises.
Fantastic teacher. Super fair class. I honestly can't say anything negative about the guy. Awesome beard too. I would 100% recommend.
Best Math professor I've ever had.
He's obviously an incredibly intelligent individual. Possibly too intelligent to be teaching calc III. None the less, a nice guy.

Great professor! He is amazingly well prepared and has intimate knowledge of the material. He's hard, but reasonable. He offers tons of office hours and curves on tests. If you come to lecture prepared, do the homework, and ask questions, he is a very helpful resource. I wish all professors were like him.

He was absolutely fantastic. I came in from Calc BC and my teacher was great. Nathan was almost as good. Very clear purpose and excellent explanations EVERY SINGLE DAY. people seemed to have trouble with his tests, but the curves were huge. I thought they were perfectly fair and not too hard. Take his class for sure

Nathan is a good professor. He's patient and funny in a nerdy way. His tests were fair and pretty straightforward. He draws excellent pictures and teaches at a fairly slow rate. The only thing that's sometimes lacking is the ability to energize us/break the fourth wall during lecture, but it's 8 or 9 AM so it's not all his fault.
Nathan is the best math teacher I've ever had. He makes calc 3 easy by always making sure his students understand each topic. Definitely try to get him!
He's the best! Definitely my favorite math professor I've had. He's great at explaining things, but his drawings WILL make you feel incompetent because he's awesome at them.

Probably the best calc professor on campus. His exams are extremely difficult, but that could have just been this year because hes previous exams were much easier (he showed us his previous exams before each midterm and the previous ones were much easier). However, although his exams are extremely difficult he gives a pretty generous curve.
Great teacher that draws awesome pictures and is very helpful in answer questions and giving explanations.
Great teacher east midterm
He makes the most amazing graphics.
This teacher is a straight up boss. He is the best drawer ever and can help you very easily. He is a great teacher to have for math 241.

Teaches quite clearly and patiently. And draws nice pictures. He really prepares his lectures and discussions. His exams are not difficult; just cover the basic definitions in class. Go to the class, do the hw, and you will be fine.
he's pretty awesome
great teach, interesting lecture,very professional

He really cares about our learning. He is always willing to answer questions, he checks in with students about the pace of the course, he always gives feedback so students know how they are doing relatively, he has extensive office hours, he offers outside material for extra help. He is the best professor I have had.

He's a very hard professor. Attendance is not mandatory, but you definitely need to go to class to understand. He's tough, but he's willing to help his students during office hours. If you put in the hard work, you'll learn a lot from him.

Dunfield is very patient and helpful at office hours, but his lecturing and overall teaching ability is sub-par. It seems like he tries very hard to teach well but unfortunately failed the entire semester that I had him for.

If you are just aiming for a high grade, I recommend you not take his class, because the homework will be really hard. But you can learn a lot from his class, especially the way of thinking and the method of proof. He is also very helpful during office hours.

Very passionate and knowledgeable professor but he makes the class 1000x harder than it needs to be. Literally came to UIUC with a whole new curriculum of this class and makes it 1000x harder than the other sections for no reason and is very confusing.

I absolutely loved Prof. Dan. As boring as the topic of theoretical computer science and discrete math can be, Prof. Dan made the lectures flow really smooth, introducing topic after topic in a non daunting way. Course was also structured really well so that I didn't have to struggle once in the class.

Professor Dan was an extremely approachable figure in what would have been a difficult class without him. Office hours clarified not just problems but intentions, to the point to which you could effectively infer what would be on the next test. A genuine person, very particular and articulate. Would recommend his section over those of Evans/Fleck.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The grading of homework and exams is a little weird, and I got used to that for a month. It is not that bad, actually.

It's nothing like STAT107 where you know in the labs if you're things right, there is no checkpoints in the labs for this course so you could get an answer that works, but if it doesn't align with exactly what the TA's want, you lose points and you can't gain points back. Questions on exams are worded so odd

Very unorganized class

I think these reviews make Julie out to be the devil. Julie is an incredibly kind person, who gives lots of extra credit and assignment drops. She is also a very capable lecturer. The flaws of 207 are that the homework and test questions are vague, and graded harshly. However, it is very easy to get an A in this course if you put in some effort.

While Julie was a kind professor and gave lots of extra credit and buffer points for things like homework and labs, the overall content was very disorganized and confusing in lectures. The tests were very confusing and often very subjective when it came to the answers. If you complete your work and get all extra credit points, you should be ok.

Terrible experience taking the class. The questions on the exams or so confusing to understand, and I feel like she doesn't exactly acknowledge how confusing and terribly written they are. I should never have the guess what a question is asking. If there is any other professor, take it with them. If not, good luck soldier.

Genuinely so much to say. She never has clear grading criteria, and when asked about it in lecture just says that she doesn't know. How do you not know, this is literally your class. She vibe grades based on how she's feeling that day.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Points in exam are deducted for nothing. Awful wording, making a lot of confusion both in homework and exams, but she refuses to answer any questions during the exam.
Great teaching, wonderful lectures, and very easy to understand
She is a wonderful professor, has a love for the game. Diptani is also a top-tier prospect hes one of the best TAs I have ever had.
She is an awesome professor and loves teaching statistics. Yes her homework may be demanding, but she and her tas are always very helpful. The class itself is easy money if you study the materal

Everyone is hating on her, but low-key, she is not bad. The class itself is boring and lame, but she is a fine professor; she is understanding and kind. So, I don't understand the hate watching fr. There are so many resources for this class; I don't get the hate.
The worst prof
Very disappointed that the University has not fired her, she seems nice and all but truly abhorrent at her job, genuinely would not study under her if she paid me to do it.

She is the professor I have most vehemently hated in my educational career. Every online resource claims questions should be differently answered than her solutions. She admits to writing questions badly. Asked her about a question on the practice exam and her response was I don't know the answer, it's badly written hopefully I don't do it again.

She is the worst professor of all time. Doesn't teach, leaves half the material for you to learn, and she isn't flexible with time changes. I would never recommend taking her class, the practice test answer keys aren't always the best, and other sources say it's completely wrong. Test formatting is horrible, STAT 107 is so much better.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Oh my god does she yap. Her lectures are not helpful, she doesn't explain anything really and if she does she just breezes over it. I don't know how these lectures are an hour and 20min because I walk out every day knowing less than before.

Awfully structured class. The most vague, convoluted questions then she gets upset when the responses vary. Frustrating to be around tbh. Having the same hw split up into gradescope and jupyter notebook (when they could be easily combined) is extremely frustrating.

I know you take stat207 because you have taken stat107 and you really like stat107. Because you like stat107 and you think you gonna like data science and success. NO This prof gonna flip you thoughts, the lecture is useless and not understandable. You would not know what the question is asking on hw/quiz/exam/lab. Me no like ds anymore

Julie is an extremely nice person, very caring/friendly. Her lectures are extremely boring, making her not the best lecturer, but as a complete professor she is pretty good. Lots of extra credit, lots of review material, willing to give extensions if necessary, puts effort into her class, is accessible outside of class, and cares for her students.

If you come to this class with some statistic background, e.g. AP stat, you will be fine and be amazed by how ds is used for analysis in statistics. Prof. Deeke is great professor in lecture, caring for her students. Grading is reasonable and even you mess up something in homework it's not a big deal under drops and extra credits.
This class was well organized and lecture note heavy, but if you complete homework and attend lectures it should be a solid A

This is not a weed out class by any means, but after taking this class, you will know whether you want to continue data science. Lecture info is useless when doing hw and labs, & tests are basically an essay competition. However, there is a lot of leeway with your grade and she is sweet & personable. As a person, I enjoy her. As a prof, SO BORING
Disorganized, no relevance between homework/labs and exam

Lecture materials and labs are very surface-level with no reasoning for the steps taken. Questions on exams are vague and misleading, but she refuses to answer questions during the exam. She has also admitted to not knowing the answers to her own problems. Outwardly kind and helpful, but not good at her job.
terrible professor

She's not the worst professor, but she is not good. There are a lot of hit or misses with people liking her. She is very very nit-picky with HW and lab grading, the TAs in lab are so useless (they just sit there), but she does EC with i-clicker. I wish I didn't drop last semester cause I could have taken with Ellison. Deeke made me like DS less.
BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD INSTRUCTOR! NEVER GONNA TAKE HER AGAIN!

Lectures are vague and seem filled with boring concepts not closely tied to the material. She provides past exams, but explanations are unclear. Some questions need additional knowledge of politics. I've taken 5 Stat 4xx courses, but only 207 feels like a liberal arts course, with many concepts to memorize.
Grading was very very hard. The lecture has little correlation with exams.

Does not create proper notes when teaching. For example will create wordy explanations or interpretations of problems instead of teaching students how to create their own, and does not answer questions if asked on discussion boards. Shame that she is the only STAT207 professor.

As mentioned by a litany of other comments here, Professor Deeke is an outwardly kind person, but her lectures are scatterbrained, our in-class "notes" don't correlate with almost anything seen on the test, and she grades homework with a hypercritical eye.
She is nice but awful at teaching. Her grading criteria are also unclear and subjective. Me and my friend could have the same answer but graded differently.

This professor will be like: Interpret the following situation, but what she won't tell you is exactly in what fashion to do it, filing a complaint soon with the statistics department, disregarding my education on another level!

Professor Deeke is a very nice woman, but she is not a great instructor. Her grading criteria is very unclear and very subjective. Campuswire and office hours aren't super helpful either, because she isn't great at explaining answers or what she's looking for. I got the best help from my lab section TA and CA.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: this professor is so incompetent, she will demand so much to only have your grade depend on her interpretation, so sad 😞

Lectures are rushed, and does not correlate well with exam. Some exam point deductions are not even legit reasons. 4pts off for not mentioning one term on a question. Also, how am I supposed to know you are supposed to include it? Also, she gives us a blank notes sheet but does not want to post the filled out version? Weird

Prob one of the worst professors on this campus ngl. She skips through like every other section in each module with notes and doesn't resurface it unless you speak up about it. I took this class KNOWING how bad the prof was. I was told since my freshman year that stat 207 would be mid, and yeah that totally bit me in the butt now. Labs are okay.

Deeke is a really nice professor, but the course is structured pretty poorly. The labs and lectures are disjointed and the best way to study is to use past exams. It's not a hard course, but there was a lot of confusion in instructions. Grading is generous, though, so it's a very doable class- just not enjoyable. Do not take if not passionate.

(STAT 420) Unlike some other reviews, I actually really enjoy Prof. Deeke's lectures. I find them really easy to follow, my only critique is coding could be a little more in depth. My biggest criticism is that she is a VERY picky grader. I would get C's on tests I was sure I aced. Overall I just wish she was more clear on her grading expectations.

Most of the class is coding-heavy but the midterms & final are comprehensive. Tests are nothing like the review materials provided. Very strict grader and is nit picky when it comes to the homework. Made the final project along with a two-part homework due a week before the final exam. Some of the TAs/CAs are no help at all. Only take if required.

Terrible exams, explanations, TAs/CAs (in my experience, lectures, and homework. Give us a project and two homework assignments due on the same day, and less than a week before the final. Not good at answering questions and there is absolutely no late work tolerated for some reason.
Terrible lecture and wording is so hard to understand, the content is not design for us to understand at all.

This professor is terrible. She will describe the simplest things(we know how to subset columns in Python, why are you explaining that?) while completely ignoring explaining statistical concepts (always saying "I don't have the best answer to that question") and on top of that, she always admits that her questions on exams are worded terribly.

Deeke is an awful professor. If you did not take AP Stats in high school good luck. Most of her work is done using python (homework and labs) but half of her tests are on paper. She also does not have a test rubric. My friends would write down the same answers as me for the test and one of us would be right and the other would be wrong. Slow grader

She's not a very good lecturer (goes too fast) and isn't very helpful when students have questions. Expects students to be able to perform well on vague on-paper exams when almost all of the graded work is python jupyter notebooks. Overall, the workload is manageable but she still piled on a final project and two-part final exam in the end.

Lectures were long and often uninformative. All graded work was on Python but she suddenly introduced written exams which were vague and extremely difficult. Questions on homeworks, labs, and exams were worded poorly.

The "Life Happens" policy is very fair and makes it very easy to get a good grade in the class if you do all the work. The class starts off very easy and then eventually becomes pretty difficult.

Lectures can be long and a little bit boring, but if you pay attention you'll be fine.

I had Professor Morris for ENGL200. He assigns interesting readings, which are enhanced by his lectures, and he provides very useful feedback as well. I was always excited to come to class. He also is not a tough grader, so if you put in the effort, you will absolutely pass with an A.

one of my fav profs ive ever had. the type of person that you could see yourself sm0k1ng a cig w while talking about poems and papers and such...
he's the type of person who could have been a spy in his lifetime, and you'd never know. 
10/10 great prof, knows his stuff, best feedback, writing improved exponentially. am gna miss him he was the best

Dave is the kind of great teacher you'd only expect to see in movies—humorous, easy to get along with, very understanding about project deadlines and absences, and always insightful to talk to. He actually made me enjoy a class I was forced to take.
One of the best teachers I'd ever had. He's a very generous grader, and his feedback is very helpful. If you have the opportunity to take him, do it; you won't regret it. 10/10
RHET105 was as boring as you would expect, but Dave made sure we didn't have to do more than we needed to. He also let us out early almost every class. If you try in this class, you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--So many papers

reviews.comment: Dave takes the time to make sure his students are engaged, gives extremely clear instructions on what hes asking for on assignments, essays can easily be done, very flexible with extensions and honestly a great class to boost gpa!
Honestly he's just amazing like, he's super chill but just make sure your participating and doing your work
Amazing teacher, even better person. Gives interesting lectures and you should definately consider taking this class.

Professor Morris's Intro to Film class was by far one of the best classes I've ever taken. He was understanding of the pandemic and extended deadlines on papers if you asked. If you like film it is an easy class and full of interesting discussions and lectures.

He is an amazing professor. RHET 105 being a gen ed, he is fully aware of that and does not make you do more than you need to. He is very lenient with deadlines and gives amazing feedback on all your papers to make sure you get a good grade. I would 100% take him again if I needed.
The best

If you have the chance to take one of his classes do it! His lectures are interesting and discussions are more of a casual conversation. He is very understanding when it comes to papers and if you just email him ahead of time you can get an extension on anything without an elaborate excuse. If debating between instructors CHOOSE HIM!!!

My favorite Instructor, I have had so far. His lectures are engaging and he clearly explains the topics you need to know. I had four papers for this class but he gives excellent feedback and we were able to revise our first major paper. He is really nice, funny and genuinely cares about his students. I would highly recommend taking his class.

My favorite professor in my first year. He is a tough grader, but if you keep working and try to rewrite if you need to, you can still get an A. It is a tough process, but I found it worthwhile eventually. He is very knowledgable and alway be patient with students. He inspires me a lot.

Great, fun professor. He does not grade that easily, but he makes sure you get the grade you deserve from the class. 3 long papers for the whole semester and they basically determine your grade. I learned a lot from him. He also allows revising and he is very lenient on deadlines. Just make sure to stop by at his office hours with your draft.
Great professor. He is patient and understanding. The grading was not easy for me in the beginning, but he allows revising and regrades. I learned a lot.

One of the best professors I have ever had. Really thorough with everything, enjoys what he teaches, makes an effort to get to know his students. The best part, he is very lenient about deadlines and rewrites. Basically a cool professor who you can learn a lot from.
Chillest Rhet Instructor Ever. Very Lenient on deadlines, and doesn't grade very harshly.

By far the best teacher i've had. He is very understanding and if you can't turn a paper in on time, as long as you approach him ahead of time he'll give you an extension. Get this professor by any means necessary.

Awesome guy. great class. very knowledgeable and insightful man. i can say i walked out of that class with something new learned or causing me to question something and it is because of his teaching. honestly, great class and teacher

Awesome teacher, very understanding. Allows re-writes for higher grades. Explains everything thoroughly. Makes discussions interesting. Only a few major papers, the rest are small. Go to class - get an A.

Dave is one of the best instructors I've had. He is INCREDIBLY understanding. With all assignments, he completely understands if you need a little more time and will not penalized you. He also allows rewrites and takes only the higher grade. He's kinda funny and knows what he's talking about, too. Definitely a good way to get an advanced comp!

By far the best teacher I've ever had.  Dave is funny, approachable, and just a great teacher.  He made the class really interesting and he isn't smug at all like some other professors.  Bottom line: RHET 105 is required, so try to get Dave as your instructor and you will be very happy you did

This man is a rock god - oh yeah, and an instructor as well.  He shreds on the guitar and on my papers.  If you mention The Simpsons or any seminal prog rock band around Dave, then be prepared to receive the "schooling" of your life.  Some say he's today's Tom Sawyer but I say he's my prof.

20 years later. I still remember her as the worst professor in my entire time at UIUC.  She issued us novels instead of textbooks as a way to learn math.  No one learned anything from them.  We all had to leverage the labs to actually learn stats.

Took ONL section. Was impressed with the course structure. Her lectures go off of a workbook, id buy it (around $20). Homework is very fair and is often exactly what is done in lectures. . Lectures can be long, but the ability to pause and pick back up later is a major plus. I wish she taught stat 400 so i can have a similar experience there.
took the class online so lectures and homework were all asynchronous. class was pretty easy and professor is amazing!

Lots of homework, some of which feels quite tedious. The instructor does a good job of presenting the material using examples and working through problems. The exams were easy and there were plenty of extra credit opportunities.
very helpful preparation for higher level stats classes. great teacher and course
Plain and simple, she shouldn't be an instructor at the university level. It's one thing to not be able to teach, it's another to not show any effort or care.

We were online for this semester and she just reused lectures from past semesters. Doesn't have personal office hours at all. Literally is getting paid to show her videos from last semester, doesn't even have an office hours, doesn't know how to clearly explain concepts. She's a nice person but should not be an educator for this class.
Great professor! I took her Stat 200 class. She gives lots of extra credits and almost every problems in the homework you can find simliar examples in her lectures.
She's ok. I do wonder if she deliberately kept some mistakes in the book though. The mistakes have been there for at least a year because lectures last semester showed that there were mistakes too.

I took STAT100 with Karle and LOVED it and got an A+ easily. Thought I would enjoy STAT200 as well but I was mistaken. Fireman's teaching style is completely different. She does not speak clearly and her thoughts are all over the place. Definitely not as easy as stat100 but it is still overall a fine class. Same extra credit policies as 100.

I was in the lecture that she taught and there was a small amount of extra credit offered for showing up (the extra credit offered in the class is pretty useless and does not improve your grade). The lectures were also posted online so you could watch them for a refresher or if you skipped class. Super conservative and does not hide her politics
Online lectures were good and very clear. Caring and a funny professor, if possible, take online with her rather than in person with a TA, the videos are clutch.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Great course and teacher. Covers lots of material, well organized, clear lectures.

I can't stand how sloooooooooowly she explains concepts, by the time she finishes talking I forget what she was even saying in the first place. The lectures could easily be 50 minutes, but she takes 80. I also frequently wonder whether she fully understands these concepts during her lectures, or is just reading off the book...

Taking Fireman's STAT200 this semester, and I'm pretty sure I would like to take her classes again. She is organized in explaining stuffs, and provides detailed study guides for each exam. It should be a fun class even if you didn't have fun with math in the previous, and it prepares me well for stat400/410.

Online lectures are very clear and easy to follow. Class itself covers a lot of material and can be a little difficult but she prepares you for the exams really well and provides lots of resources for studying. Require work to do well but I learned a lot in this class.
Wonderful and kind
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Fireman is very nice but sometimes confusing in the way she explains things. The material isn't too hard though and you can figure out what's going on by following her examples in the textbook. All lecture videos are posted online so attendance is not mandatory but you can get extra credit with clicker questions.
Highly recommend! Great class, very well structured and interesting.

Hated this class as an online class. You just watch lecture videos that were actually done by Karle Flanagan (who is great). Homework is extensive and tests aren't that hard, but just overall boring and I forgot I was in it most of the time.
Fireman is okay at best. Just pay attention and you can get a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Awesome!
This class was easy as long as the lectures were watched and the homework was done.  If you do what's required it's an easy A.

Fireman is really helpful & she uses interesting examples.  Still like in any math class the temptation to fall asleep is real.   A lot of people say it is easy A, but I guess that's true if you're naturally great at math.  I had to work for it but it wasn't impossible.  You can guarantee like a 2% overall grade raise if you do all the bonus work.

I think she is a fairly good lecturer. Sometimes, it gets really boring and tiring going to class, she is really funny and makes Statistics interesting. It is worth taking the course. I recommend it. You can just watch videos at home if you just want to take a nap.

Professor Fireman is a little weird and funny at times, but her ability as a lecturer fell short of my expectations. She's very friendly and offers plenty of extra credit, but she's not very well-spoken, often struggling to explain and losing her train of thought. I had previously heard good things about her, but her STAT 200 lectures were a bore.

The class is easy; I managed to get an A without much effort. That being said, Fireman is an absolutely dreadful lecturer and her speaking style often contains a great many words and little content. She tends to dawdle on examples and I often fall asleep during the class. However shes a funny lady and very relatable. Gives lots of bonus credit.
Love! Dr. Fireman is awesome (and hot)!

Very helpful, clearest math educator I've ever met. Very passionate about teaching. Wrote the textbook herself. You will learn concepts, not just formulas. Go to office hours! You will get an A if you go there, they will explain everything. Videos of the course are offered online so you can miss classes and not fall far behind.

The class is interesting and fireman creates most of the material herself. She teaches in a way that gets my attention and I wish most teachers taught like her. She understands that different students learn differently and that we do not like those boring text books that take forever to get the point across.
She is amazing! You will learn a lot of valuable things about Stats
Best class I've ever had! It is easy because she is making everything so clear!
She has been my absolute favorite teacher to have here on this campus. Super easy course and it's fun to be in class for once

If you have to take stats at U of I make sure that you have Fireman. The easiest class. You don't even have to go to class. TONS of extra credit. She posts lecture notes and videos on her website. Tests are super easy and just like the study guides
Hilarious.

One of the easiest courses on campus. Ellen Fireman is hilarious, so even if attendance isn't mandatory, it's still fun to go to class(?!) Exams were exactly like the practice exams. Warning: Don't take this in place of ECON 202 if you are a Business major or thinking of transferring to Business...
Never have to go to class and you can easily get an A. If you do go to class you get a lot of extra credit. Super easy

Doing the workbook, homework and massive amounts of extra credit are sure to earn you an A in this class. Fireman is a little quirky and might be too slow for faster paced learners, but all the same the class is relatively easy and practical as well. Definitely recommend for those needing a QR class or a GPA booster.
Fireman was amazing!!! Probably my favorite math teacher I've ever had! She was really clear and trust me it's not hard to get an A whatsoever!
She is just a great teacher. She really knows her stuff and does a very good job of explaining things. The tests are also easy, as long as you do the practice tests.

One of the best teachers that I've had at U of I. She truly cares about how students perform in her class. I think that it is a testament to her teaching style that so many students think her class is easy. She also makes stats interesting!

One of the easiest classes I've taken so far! Fireman gives you every chance to ace the class, including tons of extra credit, around 7 practice exams per exam, and goes over multiple examples per topic. I have never paid attention during her class (except when she is saying something ridiculous), but if you do even 1 practice exam it's an easy A.

Fireman is kind of a crazy hippy, but you can't help but love her. She tries to entertain you every class and use relevant examples for statistics. If you take at least practice exam, you should be able to get an A on every exam.

Such an easy class, all you have to do is go to class, pay attention, fill out the workbook (copy her notes) and youll have an A. Homework was annoying, but if you do the practice exam there is no way you cannot get an A on the tests.
The HW in this class (Stat Math 161)  was rediculous. A third of the class disappeared by midterms. DON'T TAKE IT!

Super easy class. She is a very good instructor. I'm sure you've been recommended this course a thousand times as a easy gen ed. Yes, this is the best Quant. Reasoning course as far as easiness. Yes, just take it.

Fireman was GREAT!! I really want to stress that this class definitely isn't a easy A or Blow off class. There is homework twice a week. And that homework prepares you for the exams. But she also gives study guides for each exam. Exam 1-3 are fairly easy (If you study). But there is a 100 question FINAL!

Excellent prof.  She wrote the book which is a spiral bound print-off that sells for like $18, so you save money.  For someone like myself who doesn't care about stats, she made the class really interesting because she discussed other subjects while explaining the material. Now I teach a college level course and I strive to be like her.

She really simplifies the material. Gives study guides that are literally like the exams, no trick questions. Also has tons of opportunities for extra credit. She's funny and has quite some stories to tell. Doesn't really answer emails.
this was a great class. Ms Fireman is a wonderful person and very interesting. Loved it!
She is great!

She was very effective at teaching and using real-life examples to foster understanding. There was tons of opportunity of extra credit. She sometimes goes on long tangents which can be frustrating when you're sitting in an hour 20 min class, but she's quite funny at times.

she's tries very hard to help her students understand the material. she's hilarious and seems to be really passionate about stats. she gives bonus points out like no other. homework is always relevant to the material learned in class and the exams aren't difficult either.
Honestly best professor I've had! She is hands down hillarious, super helpful and I actually understood math for the first time in my life...Go Fireman

Overall a fair and easy class that gets the information across in a simple way. You learn a lot and your grade will be good. Lectures must be attended to get the notes and the weekly homework is pretty easy. There's lots of extra credit and the exams are very fair. Oh and for her age Fireman is hot!

Super easy! I stopped going to lecture after the first test and still got an A in the class.  All notes are posted online, but I know tons of people went to class just because she is HILARIOUS.  Best professor I've had at U of I so far!

Overall Fireman is a good prof.  She gives lots of extra credit opportunities, and though she goes off topic a lot and you can barely read her writing, you can get through her class pretty easily with a little effort.
Very easy, very nice.
I love this woman!! She is the best professor you'll ever have at UIUC.! You have to take STAT 100 with her; it's a must!!

Easiest math class you will take. Tests are exactly what you expect. She goes off topic A LOT, so if you want to skip lecture, you'll still be fine. Just go to the ones you anticipate to be difficult to understand. Highly recommended class.
AWEsome teacher AWESOME class
you have to try really REALLY hard to not get an A in her class. She is sooo funny and by far, coolest professor at U of I
Fireman is the teacher to go to for Stats.

EXCELLENT class. This was so enjoyable and I learned so much. She is a great professor. Every lecture I cracked up at least 10 times. She is one of my top 5 favorite people ever. Plus, the other Stat 100 prof is hard and boring from what I have heard. Take this class.

I can't describe how much I loved this professor and this class.  I am by no means a math person but because of Fireman, I can honestly say that I enjoyed Statistics.  It's definitely easy and  I would recommend attending every lecture to fully understand the material.  It's worth going anyways because she has hilarious stories.
By far one of the best classes and professors I've ever had! I don't like math... but Fireman makes Statistics tolerable (and easy)!
Easy teacher. Notes are posted online every Saturday. Test comes straight from the lecture notes (the little book she sells). Easy A.

Easiest class I have taken on this campus. I went to class the first day and never showed up again. Follow online by doing the Homework/EC and Fill out the Notes. Tests are straight from the study guides and practice tests in the notes packet so if you can do those, hello A+!
She's a cougar! But she's a cougar with some good info! She guides you through the class with ease and really makes sense of the material.
Great class, really easy and Fireman is crazy. But in a good way.
Super easy class. Fireman's awesome and full of hilarious stories.
She is really cool

Incredibly easy class. I've never been that good at math, but I've gotten A's and an A+ on all of my exams. She teaches everything in a way that you can easily comprehend and uses real-life examples. I never liked math, but this class is pretty interesting. If you need a stats class for your major, take this section! DONT BUY THE TEXTBOOK.
Very easy class.  Professor is helpful and very nice.  Do the homeworks and show up on occasion and you'll get an A

Great instructor.  Attendance is probably not mandatory, but her quirky sense of humor and excellent teaching ability make it worthwhile to go to class. Homework is online and very manageable, and exams are easy. The incomplete lecture notes are quite helpful. She looks and sounds a bit like Maud Adams.

She is an awesome teacher.  She looks great for her age and she can teach.  Best of all, there's no textbook and only a lecture note packet.  It's not a super easy class, you still have to do the work.  You CAN get a B in this class if you slack off and don't keep up with the lectures and homework.  Take advantage of extra credit surveys.

I love math, so naturally this class was a good fit.  Professor Fireman took the class to a completely different level though.  She is an incredible teacher that explains things clearly so that everyone can understand and do well.  She genuinely cares about her students, makes lectures fun, and has an unbelievable life story.  Take her class!
REVIEWS EVERYTHING, ANSWERS ALL QUESTIONS, GIVES EXTRA CREDIT, ACCEPTS LATE HW
You learn so much and don't even realize it. Such an amazing teacher. She really cares about the success of her students, and is always more than willing to help. TAKE HER CLASS!
Fireman is great! As long as you show up more than half the time and learn some of the initial concepts, it will be a breeze.  She is very good at teaching and has interesting surveys.

She is by far one of the coolest teachers i have had so far. sometimes she's a bit ditsy and she gets destracted easily. but overall she's a really caring and honest teacher. she doesn't want to fail anyone if she can help at all. but besides that, the class is pretty easy and I hate math
She is amazing
Awesome professor & awesome class!  Fireman is a hottie!
She ROCKS! Love her. Unless you are an idiot, you can get a supereasy A.

As easy as it gets - the only people who didn't get A's were either huge slackers or just really really dumb. Fireman's funny, smart, and a very good teacher. She looks like a hot college student from the back, but her face definitely gives her age away (warning to all smokers).

Extremely easy. If you can't get an A in this class, you shouldn't be going to UofI.  I'd say she's somewhat attractive too...keep in mind though, if you were to look up "old cougar" in the dictionary, you'd see her picture (ie, attractive, but old...and I mean old)

I would definitely recommend this class! I am not a math person at all, but even I ended up with an A. Homework is very easy, and she posts notes online at the end of every week so you really don't have to go to class. So worth it.

Ellen Fireman is amazing!!! I only went to the first 2 lectures of the semester and I've gotten 100% on the exams and the homework.
