She's really clear on the material and all of her notes are posted online, so there's no reason to go to class. Do the practice exams!! They're exactly like the real exams. she's a babe too
amazing teacher!! I hate anything to do with math but she made it fun! Just do everything in the book you buy for her class and you should at least get a B in the class.
Awesome

if youre going to take stats, take it with fireman!!! i rarely go to class and have gotten A's on all the online homework and exams so far. Her lectures are pretty boring, but she puts the notes up online. And I would take the class with a friend. It made life much easier when I could study for the exams with a group.

Amazing class...I actually enjoyed statistics by the time I was done. Makes things VERY clear, and lectures are always entertaining. She's hilarious. And always willing to help you, or direct you to a place where you can get help. Easy, but study for tests. And do all the extra credit, I would've gotten an A+ if I had!

pretty easy class, but don't take it as a blowoff, you should study, it doesn't have to be that much but go over lectures before you do homework and study a little before test and you should get an A. I got a A- because I completely blew off all the hour exams but when I actually studied for the final I smashed it. DO EXTRA CREDIT, SO YOU GET AN A!
She makes the class relative to every day situations. I highly recommend this class. DO BONUS PROBLEMS.

AMAZING!!!!!  DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS FROM ANYONE ELSE!!! Lectures are VERY entertaining- she is a crazy lady (in a good way) but there is almost no need to go if you don't want.  If you cant get a C in this class... then you HONESTLY do not belong at this university and you should go back to Jr. High. My test average is 97%. AMAZING class.
love her
crazy manurisms but a great teacher
She was so much fun and the class is easy, take it as a senior for an easy gen ed!  Three tests and you have to take the final.  Lots of online homework.

i went to a total of 3 out of like 30 lectures and got an A.  class is not hard but you DO have to put SOME time into it a day or two before the tests.  She gives you everything you need to get an A.  her practice exams are just like the real ones.  definately take it with fireman.

Alrite, I went to the first lecture and didn't go to the rest...just seemed so boring to me.  The lectures are online.  The class isn't incredibly easy(I would know, I'm asian, I went to the tests totally blind, not a good idea)...you actually have to do the homework and study.  Study a day or two ahead of time and you'll do fine.  I got an A. Easy
Great help in getting math requirement completed.  Hottest woman on campus, and she knows it.
it's a taker
She is pretty much amazing
easy class. take it
easy class, awesome prof.!
Best class @ the university so far...she gives tons of xtra credit and has very fair exams!
Great Teacher!!! Probably the best in the school. The way she teaches is very helpful. Really can't miss the class.
Super easy class.  She is really fiesty.

Rated one of the best professors by the DI for a reason. She makes math interesting and cares about her students. She participates in student activities, like Greek Charity/Philan events, which shows how much she loves her students.
If you are going to take a math class, take statistics, and if you are taking a statistics class, take it from Ellen Fireman. She is the best teacher ever.

She is a great teacher and her class is really easy if you study and do your homework. she explains really well and she is helpful. also, very funny lady. I LOVE HER SHES GREAT> make sure u take her class EASY A

I LOVE HER! By far the funniest and easiest class I've ever taken.  She give ex. credit out the wazoo, and most of the ex. credit is surveys.  Homework can get tricky, but the TA office hours are really helpful, and you can take the homework as many times over as you want until it is due. Take the practice exams!!!

I suck at anything that has to do with numbers (I'm an English major), but she was so good at explaining the material that I found it easy to understand. If you need a math credit and you suck at math, take stats with Professor Fireman- you'll be glad you did.

Very clear and straight forward teaching! you will realize STAT's not that horrible at all in her class. Go class, study her lecture, and do practice exam you can get an A on exam. Very helpfrul teacher! take SATA with her!

Hilarious lady with really random funny anecdotes and she is just so cheerful and so hot. Believe the others. Do not take Stats with anyone else, and make sure you take Stats for ur Quant class. EASY!!!

This was one of my favorite classes! I am horrible at math, and I got an A in Stats thanks to Prof. Fireman! Don't take Stats with anyone other teacher! There is only 1 online assignment per week, so the workload is very manageable. You learn a lot in the class too.
I detest math. no, abhor it. Ok, I just hate it. And I liked stats. You will like it too.
No Comments
Very very helpful I passed and I can hardly add let alone subtract.  very fair  she is the only reason I managed to pass my Quantitative Reasoning class and graduate.

I loved this class.  She made it interesting.  Lectures were very easy and laid back.  She is the nicest lady and hot too for someone in their 50's.  Do NOT take stats with anyone else.  My favorite professor at the U of I no doubt!
TAKE THIS CLASS.  trust me.   i am a pre-med student and this is the most unbelievably easy A you will ever get...
Super easy, tons of extra credit, really easy to understand
Wow. Best class i've ever had.

Easiest class ever! I don't even go to class b/c she posts lecture notes online which are clear and easy to learn from. Going to class isn't bad either, b/c she has a great personality and has an extremely good body for a lady in her 50s.
this is such an easy class and teacher, if you dont get an "A" your retarded
Easy, easy, easy!  And she has such a cute personality.

I think I'm in love!  She is too funny, but at the same time gives great examples so you understand everthing.  Her lecture notes are all printed up in a notebook, so there is no rushing to take down notes.  She is GREAT!
Only math class I ever liked. Tests were hard though.
No Comments
She's retarded...but the class is easy

awesome!!!!! i love her!! shes so cute and so sweet. shes like this mid aged woman in great shape with so much energy and makes the class so interesting because shes so good at explaining and soooo funny. if youre going to take stats GET HER! its really easy and i hate math. she genuinly cares how her students do.
Great professor, interesting class even if you dont like math, explains everything very well so exams are super easy!
Ooooooooo Heleeeeeen! Awsome teacher! From the very first day of class you'll know that you picked a good one. Extremely fair and gives TONS of extra credit that is easier than pooping!
Awesome teacher!!

Best teacher ever! She is so fair in her grading and the tests really arent that hard because she explains everything so clearly that you actually understand the material. Plus take advantage of bonuses and extra credit- which she gives a lot of.. be prepared to laugh a lot in class- she's a funnt lady.

She has to be one of the best teachers that I have ever had! She is so nice and is VERY fair on her grading. I think she is too easy on the grading, but I am not complaining. Tests could be a little tricky, but very fair. She is also looks amazing for a 55 year old! A little crazy though (ditzy a bi
Best math teacher I've had, explains stuff really well and you know just what to expect on tests b/c she gives you old ones and the test you'll take is just like those!
Easy class! She is very nice and actually cares about people learning the concepts, which are not difficult at all. Just study a bit and you'll get an A. She's funny too!
Wonderful teacher.  She is very caring and always wanting to lend a hand.  If you need to take stats, DEFINATELY take it with her.
very understanding
No Comments
Easiest class ever.  You have to be an idiot to not at least get a B.  Just go over the examples in the book and you should be fine.  Lectures are borring so I didn't feel like going.
she is the best math teacher I have ever had, explains everything and truly cares about her students grades
Her class is so boring!!  She goes over the same thing for a whole hour, just doing example after example after example.  Going to the class is pointless.
awesome teacher!
what everyone says-clear explanations, interesting examples, straightforward,funny...
explains clearly, very patient and makes stats fun!
Very easy.  I think people just say she's hot because she has a pretty nice body for a 50 something year old.  Wish I had that kind of body :(
Explains things so that anyone can understand. Course is very organized and logical, plus it's useful. Loved the incomplete notes.
Fireman is cooky in the best way, she makes math semi-fun and can make an entire lecture hall crack up. She is EXTREMELY fair.
No Comments
what everyone has said- she makes the class fun by using real examples and by having a sense of humor, plus the class is a breeze.
She is a great teacher and actually makes stats bearable.  She relates problems to real life and has a sense of humor.  Many opportunities for extra credit.
Great teacher, never thought I'd ever actually enjoy a math class.

She is one of the best profs I have had here...she makes stats easy to learn. She dresses like a teen and is kind of flaky but she can be pretty funny. I don't know why people are rating her as hot though...the lady is like 50 something guys!
No Comments
Good teacher, but still dresses like she's 15
She makes learning statistics very simple.
Ellen Fireman is an awesome professor. She explains everything so that everyone understands and is my best professor.
one of my best professors
Good teacher, easy to follow.
She is a really good teacher and will help anyone understand the material.
No Comments

So easy
When are your office hours this semester?
No Comments
Great body, good teacher

No Comments
No Comments
MPs and exams need a lot of effort, but grading is fair.
He is memorable for playing music before the lectures start. What I appreciate about this professor is that he responds to students quickly on Discord. I thought his lectures were just okay.

looked at prof solomon's slides from previous semesters instead of evan's useless lectures. loved the extra credit, hated that labs and mps were due so close together (sunday vs monday), exams were okay
He is definitely very knowledgable on discrete maths, and his lectures were very easy to understand. The grading was awful, but I guess that is how 173 is structured instead of him being mean.
He is a highly knowledgeable professor.

I took his CS173 and CS225. He canceled the drop for a quiz, added a tutorial section in CS173, and increased the percentage of exams in CS225. In short, he sucks.

Btw, he cannot give good lectures. His explanation to CS225 lacks logistics. He cannot even defend his own proof when some students ask questions in CS173.

I would rather transfer to purdue then take this class again. This class sucks, he does not teach, there are 500 kids in each section and 30-50 kids go to lecture, that should say something. Self taught myself the entire class based on the online videos.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: It wasn't a bad experience throughout the course. But brace yourself to teach yourself as this course is designed to teach yourself. Tests are 90% of your final grade but they were straightforward in some cases. Sure it's a tough class but not unachievable.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Pretty good professor, makes lectures interesting, but the class is very hard and very exam focused
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do not take "his" CS 173 course. 90% of the course is exams and the professor does not teach. Materials are taken from the courses previous instructor, with 10-20 pages of reading and 1-2 hours of videos. Professor recommended students to skip teaching days which were based of the instructor walking through two problems by hand and nothing else.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This is probably the toughest course I have ever taken, but I have learned so much. Evans definitely tries his best, but there are so many other students that he can only do so much. Ninety percent of the course is exams, and there is a shock during the first couple of exams as you get used to the course rigor.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor does not know how to teach for his life. For the most foundational cs class at a top 10 cs program, i expect the professor to be constructive, engaging, and competent. This professor seems to not fit any of those descriptors

He does not post slides and often makes mistakes in lecture. Exams and lectures are riddled with spelling  mistakes. I emailed the professors twice about an issue I had and nobody responded. Lectures are barely relevant to the MPs and are insanely boring. Tough class, but rewarding if you put in the work. He just makes it hard to learn.
D1 yapper, nobody likes the goofy ahh music in lecture

lectures are literally awful. how do you lecture with BLANK SLIDES to 900+ people. this impedes my learning heavily and he does not have clear talking points whatsoever. better use of my time to self study out of class, disappointing for such a good cs program at uiuc.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: His lectures are very boring, and he has a habit of playing weird music at the beginning of the lecture. He replies on Discord faster than emails, and he's on Discord 24/7. His lectures are irrelevant to the MPs and exams.

Professor is knowledgeable but provides boring lectures that are better off learning yourself. Lines for TAs during discussion were way too long. Documentation for MPs can be unclear at times. Overall, the professor is overrated and has an ineffective teaching style.

As a person, he is good, but as a lecturer, he cannot lecture to save his life, and I am not exaggerating, but the sheer drop in the amount of people who show up to lecture during the first few days as compared to mid-semester is sad, given the class size. You are better off learning content from online resources. The MPs were hard but good.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
He rejects MANY ec final projects (also directly relating to A+) without a good reason. Our group has written a really complete and organized proposal but NO ONE can prove that they'll fully stick with proposal.
His lectures are slow, just watch them at home if you can.
MP instructions aren't clear. Start only after you understand everything.

You know, I heard a lot of good things about this guy before I took CS 225 - to be honest, I was really excited to take this course because everyone hyped this class and Carl Evans. Unfortunately, Carl Evans has been making CS 225 more difficult every semester, and the quizzes in CBTF are meant to buck you over with gotcha questions.
Honestly one of the most overrate professors here at U of I. I was a little disappointed with his way of teaching and leading the course.

Very easy A, lots of extra credit, fun final project. You will learn a lot of useful things through the class, although there is a fair share of work involved. Find yourself a good final proj group and you will enjoy!

Professor himself was very chill but lecture was kinda slow. Goes over easy concepts too slowly and hard concepts a bit too fast.
pretty easy due to a lot of extra credit but a fair bit of work
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
Hardest class I ever took. Also, single most beneficial class I ever took.
Give him the time he deserves!
Go 'head be gone with it!
The MP/lab starter code is as messy as it gets. He is just not a tidy programmer.
Great professor, tough class for 241 so don't expect it to be a walk in the park but he is very accommodating of students
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great lectures, and always available. He runs a highly active discord server where he regularly answers questions. The lectures were also engaging and well structured.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: There are 100 points extra credit. OH take a long time to get through sometimes 5-6 hours. It is a difficult class and the MP's, Lab's, and Exams are hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Decent lecturer who excels at course administration. Assignments for the most part are well structured and designed to set you up to succeed. Office hours have historically been awful for this class (a byproduct of how large it is), but he tries to mitigate this by being active on the class Discord. The class is very well run otherwise.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: CS225 is a great class but you could definitely learn all concepts online. Lectures were held on Twitch and were pointless tbh. Heavy work. If you're not dedicating at least 15-20 hours weekly, then it's very hard to pass. The MP's, Labs, and Exams are all challenging. It's a weed out class, so make sure to put lots of effort from Day 1. OH suck!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Lectures are honestly useless if you have taken CS173 and CS126 before there is no point in attending them because it's not mandatory and he goes off topic so many times. If u need to learn a specific thing he records his live streams and uploads on twitch which is helpful! Overall prof.'s lectures meant nothing to the class

A great professor for CS 225, did his best in Fall 2020 to keep things going smoothly at all times. A lot of people complained about office hours and other things that were hard for him to control, if you look past that he seemed like a really great guy and professor!

I have to admit that this class sucked. The lectures didn't make sense and that there was so much work. Office hours is a hit and miss at times. Be prepared to wait in office hours for at least 5 hours and the TAs not be able to help you throughly with the issues with your code. I felt like this class didn't teach us proper debugging tools.

The work in this class is not too hard but there is just so much. And if you need any type of help, it's just not there. They go on and on about all the resources available but honestly, nobody answers the Piazza and OH is always hit or miss (usually miss) with long waiting times.

Professor Evans is extremely responsive outside of lectures. He's always helping students on the class piazza/discord and is very approachable. However, lectures are oftentimes unorganized and poorly paced. I watched the recordings of Professor Wade's CS 225 lectures instead.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Great professor who truly cares about his students! This was especially evident during the COVID-19 pandemic in which he went above and beyond to make himself available all the time and ensure fairness. Although his lectures may not be the best, they were pretty good and along with the amazing assignments, learning the content was never a struggle.
Horrible- unnecessary discrepancies between local tests and online ones in POTDs. Quizzes are like all or nothing. Disorganized. Nightmare professor overall.

Had Evans for CS173 and got an A but was really hoping not to have him again, hes unhelpful and kind of a jerk. Had him for CS225... Again, got an A because I taught myself everything, didnt go to a single lecture because I knew from 173 how terrible a professor he is.

Pretty dreadful lecturer honestly, fortunately, the notes posted online are decent enough to help you through the labs and MPs if you're willing to put in the work to understand it. As with many CS classes, stackoverflow may be your best friend for this class

Carl is incredibly nice and easy to talk to, so its a shame that hes such a bad lecturer. Had him for 126 and 225, he added almost nothing to the course that couldn't be found online or from previous semesters. Tries his very best to teach and is sincere about wanting feedback, but just can't get it right. Just very unclear and disorganized.
CS225 is the ultimate easy A. And the contents have been taught really well.

I had him for both 126 and 225. He isn't the best lecturer, but is decently understandable, unlike some professors. He is fairly reasonable . His courses were all well established, so they ran based more on how they were set up than based on his preferencese.
Absolutely horrible. Had him for 126 but now stuck with him in 225. Don't bother attending his lectures, because he has zero teaching ability. I would like a refund on my tuition...
Average lectures, although sometimes on interesting topics. Knows his stuff, doesn't teach it very well.

This course should make sense. Fleck's book is very well-written and that's the only way I got through the course at all. Every single class, Evans appeared flustered and disorganized. His explanations didn't make sense and he couldn't stand up to students that challenged his proofs. Lecture confused me more than it helped, so I just stopped going.

His class was very funny, but the lectures weren't really worth the time it took to attend. The Maps weren't extremely easy, but definitely doable. The MP difficulty is really going to be based on your code moderator though, but you'll learn what they want quickly and ultimately it does help you improve your coding style.

This class was managed extremely poorly. Nearly every MP assignment had a bug in it and Evans would acknowledge it and not do anything about it. He's a funny guy though. He misspelled the words "peppermint" and "lettuce" live in front of us and that's when I knew that I was in for a treat that semester. The class itself isn't difficult though.

His lectures are fairly detailed and he's good at explaining confusing topics. The work load is extremely heavy in this course, with weekly projects ranging from 5 to 15 hours a week. The rubric for projects are not often explained well.

Even though I haven't taken too many CS classes in UIUC, I believe that he is the single worst professor I'll meet. His lectures are boring, full of professional vocabs, no intention to explain things the easy way. His rubric is vague, but the TA can just take off points whenever they want. Homework load is HUGE. Expect at least 10 hours a week.
Memorize Angrave's wiki and get an "A"
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I think Evans is a pretty new professor, and was teaching a course that changes a lot. Still, I think he did a great job teaching the course: his lectures were mostly clear and useful; he was funny, but not to the point that it detracted from his lecturing; and he made a HARD course interesting and doable. I HIGHLY suggest ATTENDING his lectures.

He only reads off slides and his lecture slides are uneffective. After the first exam, half the class dropped including me. He said it himself that the class was not going to get easier. The concepts were not even difficult, I felt confident in the material, but the exam covered nothing that was going on in class. Do not take his class.
Gives bad lectures with useless lecture slides that don't relate to course material. Gives extra credit to curve a bit as well as simple(ish) exams.
Test materials are mostly not from lecture slides.

Only reads off powerpoints, which consist of screenshot from textbook. Lectures are useless, midterms doesn't correlate to difficulty of homeworks, lectures. Would never take his class again unless necessary. Should stop teaching.

This professor's course is tough. Last year's exams were Excel-based, but SOA said it was too easy, so now there are 3 paper midterms with 5 questions each. You need 85% for UEC credit, but one mistake ruins it. His lectures are unclear, reviews are useless, and exams don't match. Passing the FM exam outside school is easier.

I've had Icaza a few times and I enjoy his classes. For 210 I thought he just read off the slides too much, but I did get an A. For 406 and 411 I think he did a great job of explaining the concepts and giving us study materials (past tests, annotated slides, etc.). Homework was fair and he's very clear about his expectations. Good dude.

Lecture is lengthy it is and all he does is go off powerpoint slides. Class itself wasn't too hard as long as you do the homework and review you should be fine. He suggest to do the reading, but it isn't that helpful. You can still pass with an A without doing the reading. He is very easy to access outside of class and helps break concepts down.

Personally, I did not enjoy having Icaza as an instructor. He would only read off of powerpoint slides (mind you this is linear algebra...) and overall it was waste of time. I will say the review days for exams and final exam were helpful and made studying for and taking them easy.

Overall I thought the class was well structured and not too hard. Professor Icaza is a kind professor who will help you with material. My only complaint is that the lectures rarely go much deeper than whats on the slides. It would be so much better if he focused more on practice problems and participation than just reading what's on the slides
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Excellent professor, hard class, & I asked a lot of questions, but each time he responds, the answer is always detailed & direct. As long as you take the time to study for his exams, you should be fine. I highly recommend attending lectures. He goes over examples and concepts with clarity, and the lecture is always open for questions. Definite 5/5.

Half of the time you will have absolutely no clue what she is talking about. She's a professor whose main goal is to seem as smart as possible even if that means losing her students along the way. I wouldn't recommend unless you want to smash your head into your computer.
This professor is really understanding and willing to help students who are struggling, she's kind and as long as you're respectful and show interest in the course it will help you a long way.

Very condescending, discouraging, and often made racist and elitist remarks that went unchecked. She overloaded us with 500+ readings, gave contradictory directions, and created a hostile, unsafe learning environment. Completely unfit to teach future social workers. Hazing dressed up as “rigor”. Get ready to do phd level reading/assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The way that the course is structured makes no sense, grading is hidden so you really have no idea how you're doing throughout the semester or what you should do to improve your grade. When asked for clarity or transparency on grading she will walk you through her "pedagogy" which is basically "you are who you are" like what?
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Awful, worst professor at uiuc! Is such a tough grader and thinks she knows everything. I have a medical disability, and she was not the most understanding. Don't take this course unless it is required.

Shocked to see bad reviews, as she was one of my favorite professors I've ever had. Was a difficult grader, but also a sympathetic one who was very kind with extensions when needed. If you engaged with the course, she engaged with you. This class requires a lot of effort and a lot of talking, but if you engage it will stick with you for a while.

Although she gave a couple points of extra credit, it didn't matter in the long run because it's actually impossible to get a 100 on ANYthing. And at some points in class it got very very awkward because she would have us basically compete to see who was more privileged than who, and you had to participate.
*Only take if ur a social work major!!!

Literally the worst professor I've ever had. So condescending, only knows the word pedagogy. So rude to her students, wants and encourages trauma dumping in her class, and rewards those who do. Incredibly unclear with everything she does. You couldn't pay me to take her again.

Dr. Tel made me not want to take another social work class ever again. She barely taught any content and instead preached her "pedagogies" and was extremely dismissive. She was rude towards me on many occasions, and would not answer any questions about her unclear expectations and grading procedures.

Dr. Tel made me not want to continue with social work. Unfortunately, this is very sad as SOCW 200 is a beginner intro course to the field of social work so she should make her students feel welcomed instead of feeling intimidated. Furthermore, since SOCW 200 is a required class you have no other option but to take this class with Dr. Tel.

Don't let her turn you off of social work! She is very repetitive, and does a lot of talking for little content we learn. She is an extremely picky grader, and the paper length for the assignments are at least 6 pages, while the final paper is 8-12 pages.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: SOCW 200 almost made me change my major because I found classes with Dr. Tel to be so tedious. She was incredibly patronizing and yet dismissive of students. It felt like she was trying to dissuade us from going into social work. If I ever have to hear the word "pedagogy" again I might scream.

Wish I had never taken her class she was rude and tried to make her class life-changing, but that wasn't gonna happen. Her website was very confusing and disorganized, we barely learned any content, and mainly focused on individual aspects of ourselves. I found it very hard to find the motivation to do anything and the lectures were so boring.

Dr. TEL was very kind and understanding when I had to go on a week-long grievance period due to family loss, and in true social worker fashion helped me out greatly and understood my struggles as I was going through a grieving process while going (and not going) to class. I'm surprised to see negative reviews, she is very caring and understanding.

THE BEST CLASS I'VE TAKEN. Tara's theoretical and philosophical knowledge, as well as her dedication to her students personal and professional growth, influenced me to transfer to the School of Social Work. I wish Tara taught more classes because I'm yet to have another professor as motivational, insightful, and respectable as her.
Tara did a great job at giving an introduction of the social work profession. The class is really easy. There's just three online exams (one is optional) four papers, and one research project.

The topic and information was relevant and interesting. However, the TA's help more than Tara. When she was in the classroom, she cut students off when they were talking in discussions. She doesn't answer questions very clearly. I also got the impression that she did not respect her main TA and was often rude to him in front of students.

I found her pretty irritating. She calls out and praises the same 10 students in a class of 60 and tries to create a "life-changing" experience through her class that just doesn't happen. Lecture is really boring save for videos. The papers are annoying but there are only a few. However, she grades easy enough and the class itself isn't hard.

The thing that really bugged me about this class is how slanted it is. It is so one sided and Tara, I found, favored the students who reflected her beliefs. It got so frustrating because of her intolerance (which is ironic, given we've been taught to be tolerant to everyone and everything) of opposing beliefs and avoiding diversity of opinions.

Tara is a control freak. She DEMANDS that this class is transformative and builds it into the curriculum. If you do not have an amazing experience with her, it will affect your grade. Her class feels 3x as long as it actually is. Don't let her turn you off of social work though if you're taking this class to see if you like the major.

Really disliked teacher and class. Text is terrible, topics are boring and self-explanitory. Piles on homework that I didn't find helpful. Thinks pretty highly of herself and sometimes evades answering questions.

LOVED TARA! she does a great job with this class. she really tries to engage her students and does everything she can to make class fun. she's really understanding, but her midterm was really hard. overall i would totally recommend this class to anyone. only 6 homeworks all semester!! and one project worth 70% of grade but she loves anything you do
Overall Great Teacher!!!

Wonderful class, wonderful professor. Professor Larrison is the most helpful and understanding prof. I've had so far; makes a great effort to know her students and to include them as much as possible in the course material. Easy to get an A if you attend class regularly and keep up with readings. LOTS of fun activities and chances for bonus points!
I was in a class with this instructor where she did not respect the pronouns of another student, and I find that concerning. Everyone in class should be respected.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ester is super kind and is willing to help you learn Spanish with a smile. It is clear that she is knowledgeable on the language, but also has a passion for the pedagogy that goes behind teaching people how to speak a language. Grading, assignments, and participation where all super fair. Just put the effort in, and you will receive high marks.

Professor Perez Landa teaches a difficult class if Spanish isn't your strongest subject. However, she understands that not everyone is fluent in the language and just expects a lot of effort. You meet in person to discuss and most of the homework is online.

Ester is quite nice and will work with you as long as you put in the effort. She can be fun too so though I don't enjoy foreign languages, with her it was alright. You get what you put in for learning languages.

Avoid, he doesn't care about you.

Clearly in the job for a check
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Chris Evans should not be a professor. He rallies up freshman TA's that barely squeezed an A in his class to do all the work and grading for him. Don't bother emailing him, he only answers when he pleases

It's sad to see people in this position at such a good university. I strongly advise not to take his class.

Terrible teacher and even worse human. He hands out all the work to his 14 freshman TA's. Do not take his online course, you will learn nothing. It's all pre-recorded lectures because he doesn't take any time to get to know any of his students. If you try to email him, expect not to her back for days. Don't waste your time; find a different prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Chris is awesome. The online section of the class is hard. In-person is easier. All grades are given by TAs. Not much lenience for late work.

Really good professor. He's definitely passionate about what he's teaching and tries to instill that into his students, which is amazing. There aren't many assignments in the course which is somewhat daunting when talking about grades, but there were plentiful extra credit opportunities throughout the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof. Evans is an awesome teacher. He is always very engaging in class and wants his students to learn. He offers a lot of opportunities to get more involved with journalism outside of the class and his class involved real-life reporting. I enjoyed this class so much that I am going to take a class with him again. TAKE JOUR200 WITH PROF. EVANS!!!

He truly cares about students and understands what it means to be one. His lectures are extremely entertaining, and he is a very understanding a caring professor. For CLCV 114, his study guides were very similar to the test, and his policies and expectations are very clear in the syllabus. Also minimal homework! Could not recommend more!

first off this is for CLCV 220 but it wasn't an option above idk why. the prof is really nice, caring, and can hold convos on anything even out of class. lectures were rlly chill and ppl get really engaged, but u don't need to pay attention or anything bcuz the graded assignments r basically completely separate from lectures. it's just attendance.

I had Professor Garcia-Molina for Intro to Ancient Greek Culture. The workload is light, and his clear class policies make exam prep easy. His lectures are the highlight, driven by his enthusiastic personality and passion for the topic. Would definitely recommend.

His notes are all over the place. They're technically based on the textbook, but when you look at the textbook, you have no idea what's happening since he diverges from it a lot. Always gets lost in the lecture. Everything is in groups. I hear the same goes for CS 555. His lectures are a waste of time; don't take class with him. Please

He's always confused.  Not clear at all. Literally will say things like "quiz this week," but you don't know if it's for a specific day or for the same day he said it. Homeworks are long, and the lectures don't help. Group projects and the homeworks are in groups. Doesn't help if you don't have a partner. Takes marks away if you do them alone.

Absolutely horrible. His lecture is just him reading the slides. Not to mention his slide is absolutely terrible and full of nonsense. He simply mix his lecture notes with another professor's slides and put slides of several lectures in one big pdf with more than 200 pages. Good luck finding one single formula in it.

This professor is terrible at lecturing. His slides are full of words and he just reads from them without actually spending time to make students understand the material. You will always get lost in his lectures. Please avoid him!

Terrible professor, doesn't teach at all and doesn't care about the students. Entire class is just him reading straight from the slides without explaining anything. Doesn't do any worked out examples for a math heavy class. Shows demos in MATLAB even though exams and homeworks are in Python. Doesn't make sense at all. Avoid him at all costs.
This prof is horrible. PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE avoid him

Not a very good lecturer, assigns extremely time-consuming homework even if you have gone through all the lectures. Oh, did I forget to mention that they literally assigned a homework due over fall break.

He is a very smart and knowledgeable  professor. During the lecture, he often extends the material beyond the textbook. However, it seems that he is not very good at conveying. It's very likely to get confused during his lecture and you really need to spend time home to get understand.  But anyway, the exams are quite easy.

Examlets are worth a huge part of the grade, but they are pretty easy to study for with the given practice problems and past examlets. Lectures are a little helpful in solidifying material, but most of the learning comes from the textbook readings outside of class. She explains concepts in practical ways for students to understand.

Class isn't the best structured, but also not the worst. It focuses on BREADTH more than DEPTH. Quizzes are hard and test tiny details. MP's require self-learning Python.

Best advice: If you plan to take several AI-related courses (e.g. 446/447), then taking 440 first provides a good background and is useful; otherwise choose a different course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She might have been a great teacher in her prime but she lwk doesnt remember anything anymore. She constantly forgets her content even though she wrote the textbook. The 90% weightage of tests is really great!!!

Notes/videos/free textbook are great. Grade is fair but 90% tests with a few questions so easy to drop big on one mistake. Horrible lectures...seems to have forgotten her material? Spends 10 mins fighting the projector then the rest trying to remember how to solve the problem. Lots of mistakes/long pauses, says she's "demo-ing the process."
it is good
Prof Fleck is a great professor and her lecture content is amazing. It was too much of a load with an MP every week and a quiz every two weeks. Learnt lots from her lectures though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I am giving her a 5 star because she adjusted the A cutoff to 92.5%, so that I got an A. My lecturer of this course isn't even her. The course structure of this course is ridiculous, so it is not completely her fault. As a CS+ student, it is mandatory to take this course, so forget about avoiding it.

Ignore all the positive things that people say about her. After a long time of anything, you get worse at the one thing you were good at. That's exactly what happened. Apparently she used to be great, but nope. She is not the best. Close to worst.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: One of the best professor's I've had since entering college. The website is a labor of love, it's comprehensive, thorough, and so thoughtfully constructed, including ample study resources, clear guidelines on how to succeed in the course, words of encouragement and a wealth of information on supporting resources.
Genuinely one of the nicest and smartest professors I have ever had. Her textbook is amazing and she is extremely helpful in office hours for questions.

Get ready to read! :D

For real though, she assigns readings and recorded videos for homework for one week and only uses the lectures as an opportunity to go through examples. Do not expect to learn anything from the lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: A lovely person. This is one of the most manageable classes I've had especially at the 400 level, and it has taught me a decent
Good professor. Lectures are very worthwhile.

Professor Fleck is a very passionate educator. My small issue is how she runs the class in an outdated way versus modern ways classes like CS124/128/233/411 are run. Quizzes on Moodle instead of the much better Prarielearn? No practice quizzes? Using outdated piazza instead of Campuswire?

I had Prof Fleck for CS440 in Fa22. Quizzes were kinda bs but doable if you studied a bit for them. Overall for an introductory AI class I learned a lot. The class was relatively easy and rewarding and I enjoyed her lectures. MPs werent too bad at all. Negative reviews are underclassmen CS kids who have never had a difficult class in their lives.

Has the mindset of a know-it all and only covers topics she is interested in, aka linguistics. She is not approachable for help. Her course does not offer enough resources to get help. 30+ students and 1 TA for 1 hour of office hour is normal. Paid non-trivial amount of $ for her course, and still stuck in AI of the past. Not worth it
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Quizzes are really hard. They have nothing to do with the MPs, and they test everything in lectures, even some very detailed things(specific person and dates). Requires a huge amount of memorization, and after memorizing everything you might still not get a good grade. Think carefully if you are not good at memorization.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She has very limited ability to handle 4xx level CS courses (She's okay in CS173 cuz the material is so easy) She made concept very confusing and cannot explain things well.  I would not suggest taking any classes with Prof. Fleck, she just cannot explain theory and concepts well, she should teach CS101 and CS173, which is more suitable for her.
She is kinda slow in her lectures, but the content is amazing, and she's a great teacher.
After doing 7 quizzes and 7 MPs, even writing two 8-page papers, you're still in the middle of nowhere in AI world.

Prof Fleck is very enthusiastic about teaching the content of this class and it makes the lecture videos enjoyable! The content can be difficult at times, but the free textbook is very useful and it's easy to get answers on the forum.

The single worst professor I have encountered at UIUC. She is a terrible lecturer who can't explain the simplest concepts without confusion (just like her in CS173). She has no regard for student ability vs performance either because she changed every aspect of the course from the previous instructor to make grading much harder. Just plain terrible

Well, to be fair, she is a nice lady and really helpful. However, homework is too difficult and useless. You have to spend several hours figuring out the TERRIBLE tricks. While the exam is easy, you still need a lot of time to practice and have to take care during the exam. You have no chance to fail if want A. If there's an easy choice, why not?

The way she does lectures on the chalk board in my opinion give it a really unique experience that makes you get up at 9AM. She's extremely caring and reachable. But, the class could be better structured. Having tests every week only allow for basically no mistakes at all because the exams are so short, if you want an A.

One of my favourite professor at UIUC. Really admire her teaching style. She really cares about students and put their learning on first priority above anything else in the course. Was very helpful with the assignment and strike right balance between giving small hints rather than the answer itself.

Lecture did not have attendance, and the textbook material was repeated with the same examples. The key is to attend the lectures near the end of the semester. Grading was tough at best, and grading for makeup examlets was extremely tough. Final in our year was way too difficult, with a wrong question.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: The most unfair course I have ever come across
Extremely helpful (but not required) lectures. Mini tests every week but lots of online material to prepare. Textbook is a free pdf. Easy to make mistakes however so be careful.

Margaret is fine but I think how this course works isn't fine. We have every exams every single week and we are expected to be taken at most 2 points off for every one of them. They also make the questions on exams vague sometimes. The materials are not hard but they make it hard and a bit sick. I would take prof instead of taking this class.

Fleck is a good prof but sometimes gets a bit heady or goes on tangents which makes understanding the basic concepts harder.  I would say reading the textbook and teaching yourself in this course, doing practice exams (!!!), and such help more than lecture.

She's a great teacher and clearly cares about her students. Her textbook (posted online for free) is excellent at explaining things; many students get through the course with no issues without going to any lectures just by reading the textbook.

One of the best class I've taken at University of Illinois. All of course material is ready at course webpage, always communicates and answers questions by piazza quickly. If you really want to learn discrete math, don't hesitate to take it with Fleck
Very good professor, relatively challenging class.  Very helpful when you ask questions and definitely cares about her students.
She is a very helpful and nice person always there for the students, I just did not find her lectures very helpful for the tests

Super friendly and a great lecturer. You can tell she really cares for her students. She goes out of her way to let students make up missed work and is always adjusting her course to teach it as effectively as possible. She wrote the entire textbook for the class and made it available to students online for free, which is greatly appreciated.
She's a good teacher in an easy class, but she rambles a bit and can take forever to get through material.
I went to the first couple lectures of the year. Was very bored by CS theory. Didn't go to any more after that and still got an A- in the class pretty easily.

She is a really funny professor, and she asks your questions really fast. Also, the attendance is not very important, but the discussion section is really helpful. The mini-homework is a little bit hard, and there is an examlet on every Thursday. It is an easy A class.

CS 173 is a math course and has a slightly dry material that tends to make people slightly bored in the class. However, Fleck does an amazing job at teaching it. She's funny and incredibly smart. No midterms. Quizzes every week though. I would definitely recommend her. She's the person to know if you're into discrete math.

She is very knowledgeable and would periodically diverge from the lesson and discuss other applications of concepts. HW was very easy. However, you should check HW answers with a friend. To do well on tests, just make sure you study for understanding and not completion. The textbook, which she wrote, is very to-the-point and easy to understand.
Very helpful professor. Good person.
Very helpful professor. She wrote the textbook (which is stellar) for the class, and is very good about answering student's questions directly. Definitely recommend taking her.
This was an easy class. Lectures are pointless... Read the book and do the test from previous semesters and you will be fine.
If you take CS173 at UIUC, take it with Professor Fleck! Awesome person, cares about the material and if you attend office hours, helps you understand it.

Mediocre professor. Nice person but lectures tend to drone a bit. Combine that with the already boring course material and the 9:00 am time slot (true during my semester), it was very difficult to stay awake.

Wrote fantastic textbook, and makes it freely available to students online as a pdf. Spread this textbook around on the net, so any student learning this stuff has access to it. Impressive person, math genius. Look up some of her research papers. Honors assignments were actually fun, cool. Not worth going to lecture though, very boring speaker.

Very nice professor, very helpful and really cared about her students. Sometimes she goes too slow and becomes boring, and the course doesn't cover too much information. Textbook is online and she constantly updates it and is helpful. Weekly homework to keep you on your toes. She loves Harry Potter. Join the honors section, it's pretty interesting!

Really good teacher, you can tell she really cared that we understood the material and her textbook is very good and consistent with her exams and hw problems. The class isn't easy but it's not too hard, just the right amount of challenging. If you take 173 definitely go with Fleck, she might be the best professor I've had
Bleh. This class is not fun. If you have to take it, Fleck is the better of the two instructors. Textbook is not required at all, there is a free one online that we use.

No need to go to lectures, you'll learn just as much by reading the online lecture notes. Discussions are absolutely essential. Office hours are very beneficial. Also, don't start the homework on friday mornings like I did.

Go to office hours! This is the only way to do well on homework and get tips for the exams and quizzes. The lectures are posted as PDFs online, read them carefully, twice, and you'll be fine. The content of exams and quizzes is always made known and is very clear - no surprises.
Fleck is very nice and kind of nerdy. She is very approachable and is willing to help you with anything. Everything is posted online, including solutions to all past exams and homework.
Marge is the nicest lady ever but her lectures are like nap time and sleep every class very boring. Discussion is very helpful though.

The lectures were a little dry, but Margaret certainly tried to keep them interesting.  It's an easy class, so the staff has to resort to trick questions to distinguish students.  Fortunately, you can use the material from the old class pages to prepare for quizzes and exams.
The class was not easy, especially at the end, but she was a great teacher and helped as much as she could. Also tried to keep the class alive.
Very nice instructor. Always try to help her students. Is obviously a geek but very kind
No Comments

She's a nice person but I personally have a really hard time following her during lecture.  I end up just reading the lecture notes, which unfortunately are hardly notes, but written out lectures).  I'd probably have done better in the course if a different professor taught it, to be honest.
She seems really friendly, but I can't follow her lectures at all. Other people seem to do okay, but I learn way more in the discussion sections.
It is an easy course. She explains things clearly. She is a very nice and friendly professor.
Very quirky teacher, no question. Very manageable class for natural math students. Wears a tyedye shirt every day to class. I'd recommend for sure.

She's one of the nicest professors you'll probably ever meet, and extraordinarily helpful if you go to office hours. The class is hard as hell, but she tries to make it as bearable as possible. Plus, I dig the tie-dye shirts. Totally awesome!

Humm.. actually I was surprized when I read those bad comments below. I think she is pretty nice, try to explain hard concepts in a easy way. Although there are many important concepts in the textbook, as long as you are following her lecture notes, easy A+ is not a problem
Great lecturer if you ask me  Always up for answering questions but I nailed the class so I don't know how it would be otherwise
Great physics 211 teacher take him if you have the chance to.
Amazing professor

Farina was great at lecturing and was really fun in all of his experiments. He made you want to show up to class and actually made physics easier to learn. The style of the course is a lot of work and can be a little rough but hes very accessible and gives you lots of resources and help. As long as you do the practice exams he gives you its easy.
Probably the best PHYS211 lecturer. Take him if you can. His demonstrations are great. He makes lots of jokes during lecture and encourages students to participate.
Professor Farina's lectures are entertaining and very digestible. This class was not difficult, but I still felt like I learned a lot.

A great professor.
Definitely recommend
He's the GOAT: engaging lectures, oftentimes hilarious, and deeply enthusiastic about teaching the class.
Really friendly and great professor

Farina was an amazing and funny prof. He explained topics thoroughly and answered questions clearly. He made lots of jokes and made the class comfortable enough to ask questions. His lectures and teaching style were basically the same as Prof Stelzer's, so probably don't worry about getting 1 of them over the other for PHYS211 for those reasons.
His lectures were very engaging and the tests were difficult but manageable with proper studying.

Farina is great. Lectures are hilarious with him, and he is great. If you have questions, he is accessible after class to answer any questions and genuinely cares about you. He just joined the Physics Department here this year, which is why there's not a lot of ratings for him.

Farina is very engaging in lectures and extremely friendly. He stays after class to answer as many questions as possible. He is also very friendly outside of class if you see him on campus. Despite being new here, he seems to be settled in very nicely on campus and would 100% recommend taking his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Farina is an amazing lecturer. He always clarifies ideas that were confusing in the prelecture assignment, and is always open to explain these concepts further if asked to. Furthermore, he is hilarious and makes the class excited on the most dull days.
Best professor ever!!! Lectures were very interesting and explained all topics very well, left most lectures with a very good understanding of the material, even if I went into it confused.

- Very engaging and interesting lectures (mandatory attendance).
- Weekly workload/studying is minimal. 
- Lots of resources for help.
- Exams similar to past papers.

Professor Farina is awesome! His lectures are really fun and he has a talent for explaining things. He is also hilarious during the lecture and very accessible outside of class. He really cares about his students doing well. Definitely one of the best professors I've had here.

He does not try to give credit on assignments. If you are somewhat wrong, you're getting many points off. He likes to hear himself talk. He will repeat himself over and over as if you are in grade school. He seems power hungry and he wants you to call him "doctor". Least favorite by far.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This was an easy A of a course. Only a brief discussion post, a 300-500 word paper, a 5 question quiz, and some reading each week. Probably the easiest course I've taken. Dr. Fredericks also put effort to make it that way, he explained a lot through email and was very accessible. Although its an online class, he makes the class better.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: Asynchronous online class. There's a weekly narrative paper (at least 250 words), a quiz based on the readings (provided to you, no need to buy a textbook), and a discussion post with at least 4 replies on the Packback platform, and a final paper (at least 500 words). As long as you follow all instructions, you should get an A in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: Every week you would do a reflection and a quiz (open book/a provided RG, but timed). Prof is enthusiastic, but a tad bit pretentious. Expect almost every class to be a lecture with some form of class participation and instead of a final exam, it's a position paper. Also beware, he randomly calls on students.

If you're looking for an easy A, this class is it.  The homework takes a long time, but it was easy.  He's not as bad as the comments say he is.  Just remember to not miss a single deadline, and follow his instructions properly. He is strict, but if you do what you're told, you can get away with a high A or A+.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Brand new professor at UIUC, classes are significantly harder than they should be, which is not uncommon for new teachers. I wish Robyn still taught these classes. Randomly calls on students to answer. Also quite boring with lots of lecturing.

Made us pay a $40 Packback fee to "get access to the full platform", then micromanaged every single sentence that we could write. PB is already ridiculous, and if you're not going to give creative liberty for us to "earn" the curiosity points, then why use it in the first place? Overall, really overbearing and slightly pretentious.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He's a great professor and very considerate of students. I love his teaching style.

257 is awfully designed due to the course design. As a lecturer Felix was great, and I would have given him a 5 if it was for a different course. He is a very smart guy and always helping out on the course forum. Only complaint is he scrolled through notes fast, but lectures and notes were recorded.  Would 100% recommend him for another course.

** FOR MATH257 **
This class just tries to do too much with all the coding plus teaching basic linear algebra. You are completely on your own for all of the Python, and only left with lectures for concepts. Tests are helllla hard too. Prof. Leditzky was a good lecturer, but I would personally take the online section.

Incredible. 
Very direct and clear explanations and lectures. Very approachable outside of course as well.

*NOTE RATING IS FOR MATH257: Prof. Leditzky has a thick accent and is difficult to understand at times, what was more problematic was his hard-to-read handwriting during lecture and poor explanation of topics. This class overall is a pain, tests are nothing like the homework with a heavy conceptual focus, and the coding is very irritating.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I absolutely bombed MATH 347, so going into this class was quite scary for me. Professor Leditzky really took time to explain problems and answer questions in a way that students could understand, and it was because of that I could truly understand the concepts rather than simply memorizing them for exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Prof Leditzky is amazing. His instruction is clear and concise. As the first 400-level math class I've taken, the course materials are not too difficult, and I can understand most of the concepts without a solid background in previous courses. I learned a lot in this course, and he excites me for future studies in the subject. Definitely recommend.
well designed course, great lecture
Gives a lot of practice exams. Study them and do the extra credit and you will do great

Class is very easy if you go to lecture and take notes. When it comes to exams,  he gives practice exams that are extremely similar to the actual exams. Do as many as possible and you will succeed in the class.
Fisher is the Accy GOAT! will miss his " Go Illini" at the end of every lecture
He is a wonderful professor. Honestly liked this course and everything. He is caring and supportive. Not much to say, simply good. Go Accy!!
ACCY GOD!!!!!!!!
very straightforward class, do all the work and it's not hard

Good attitude and energy in lecture. Well-structured course with plenty of materials to help students succeed. Lecture slides AND videos available. Plenty of support from TAs in office hours and discussion sections. Good homework and practice questions that foster deeper understanding before the exam.

Omg mitch fisher my king. so sweet. explains everything very well. plenty of resources to succeed. lots of extra credit. easy if u pay attention and follow along  in lecture and do the readings. so nice and a great prof, take with him if u can!

He will give you an excellent groundwork for accounting at this university.  Teaches it in a clear, cohesive manner.  Pay attention in lectures, engage with the examples and you will learn a lot with him.  Tests are not too hard as long as you study.  He loves his job and is passionate about accounting so it makes it funner to learn.

Tests are very heavily weighted and challenging, study well. Don't underestimate them as many failed the first exam. Fisher explains concepts well and has iclicker for review in lecture. TAs are very helpful. Class is challenging but doable, prepare well for the exams. Best part is that there is a lot of extra credit you can earn.

Did a very good job at introducing students to accounting. The coursework came very naturally to me, which is not the case for everyone. The class moves very fast covering almost every aspect of basic accounting, so be prepared to catch up if you find it difficult to understand. All lectures and discussion sections have graded attendence.
I have learned more in this class than any other course I have taken at UIUC. Because of this class, I am planning on switching my major to accountancy. Thank you for a great semester Prof. Fisher.

He is easily approachable and happy to help students. However, this class was difficult and quite boring. Lectures were very long, and because there is so much content to learn, you really need put a lot of extra work to learn everything if you want a good grade in the class. Overall, he's a good professor but prepare to put in a lot of work.

Professor Fisher is an excellent professor for this intro to accounting class. I struggled in the beginning, but once I started paying better attention in lecture, I saw a turnaround in my performance. Make sure to take notes, as it will help a lot on the homework (there is a lot of it). He is a nice guy and cares about what he is teaching!

the class was ok and homework+discussions prepared us for exam well. But the lectures were so boring, Fisher just goes over steps but I feel like he could have explained better. I literally fall asleep every single time. also the required textbook was very expensive

Professor Fisher is a phenomenal professor to learn from if you are taking your first accounting class ever. He explains concepts in a very clear manner where concepts are very easy to grasp if you pay attention in lecture. The overall course structure is very defined and it is definitely possible to do well if you put in the time and effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I felt that while the course itself was not bad (the homework was good preparation for exams) I could not stand going to lecture. I only ended up attending lecture about 3 times the entire semester because the way Mr. Fisher lectures comes across as if he's talking to elementary school children. To be fair, it seems like some people appreciate it.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Awesome professor. The class is definitely fast paced and can be difficult at times, but Fisher does a really good job teaching and providing the necessary resources to do well. If you put in the work, the class is definitely manageable and you should be able to do well. You can tell he cares about his students and wants them to do well.

Was a pretty good teacher. You need a good base of basic accounting knowledge to do well in this class. Debits&credits, journal entries, etc. He was a good teacher and you don't really need to read the book if you pay attention in class. I thought he was a strong teacher, but not super compassionate and didn't seem to curve the tests.

BEST TEACHER AT UIUC!! Prof. Fisher is extremely caring and wants his students to do well. As long as you prioritize this class, you will get an A. The exams can be challenging but study a lot, make sure you get 100% on all the homework, and do all of the extra credit. GO TO THE LECTURES AND DISCUSSIONS TRUST ME YOU WILL GET AN A.

Professor Fisher is very friendly and approachable if you need help with the material. Lectures aren't mandatory to attend but the material builds off what you learn each lecture so it's easy to fall behind if you do skip. There's a homework in the textbook and a set of problems due every week but there's so much extra credit you can earn.

One of the best professors I had at UIUC. Very funny and knows what he is teaching. Even though Accounting is hard, it was possible to learn easily since Professor Fisher did such a great job on the lessons. However, if you skip class, I bet you will fail, and he gives out a ton of homework so beware.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He is a really nice guy but he treats everyone in the class like a child. I felt like I was in grade school again. He would ask a simple Iclicker question that takes 30 seconds to solve. He would give us 10 minutes and get mad when we finished the problem early. He treated us like we were ten which was frustrating. Overall, he was a nice guy

Mitch is a pretty good professor for ACCY201, but the course itself is very lecture heavy. If you don't come in with prior financial or managerial accounting knowledge, be prepared to practice the different kinds of problems a lot! There is some extra credit available so take advantage of it. Lectures are mandatory because of iClicker questions.
This is one of the least understanding professors I've ever had. He did not adapt well to the covid-19 pandemic like many other profs did, and was extremely unhelpful during these hard times.

Professor Fisher is one of the best professor Ive ever had. He truly cared about his students and spent a lot of time making sure that we understand the content. He replied to his emails in a timely matter. I had him during the covid19 semester and his transition to online class was very smooth without any interruptions on our learning at all.

Mitch is great, he was very considerate during the pandemic. You just need to do all the work to get an A. It is really easy to get all your points from homework, lectures, etc. Study really hard for exams using past activities and exams and you will be fine!

Arrogant unexperienced professor. Very obvious that it is his second year here. Don't know how people can say his lecture slides were helpful bc he doesn't know how to explain basic concepts and instead goes into way too much irrelevant details that just make you more confused than before. Tests are bs, take any other accy professor if u can

This guy is honestly amazing. It's not too hard of a class if you actually pay attention and don't get behind on work/bs it. Pretty basic accounting tbh so it's a moderately easy A. Fisher is great tho. Im taking his class during the pandemic and the amount of care and consideration he's giving is great. He really loves his job, students and field.

He's a nice guy, but man it's a boring class. Imagine Toby from The Office teaching a class. Not to mention, accounting is extremely challenging for someone who has no experience with it, and this class is ridiculously fast paced.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Fisher is great- he has REALLY good lecture slides and is good at breaking things down to make sense to people who don't understand ACCY- he really cares about his students. The only negative thing I'd have to say is there is sometimes too much content covered & the exams are so difficult (coming from a student who studies a lot)

Mitch is a great guy and has pretty interesting lectures. He's funny and tries his best to keep lectures engaging, even though accounting itself is boring. Attendance is optional but going to class prepares you really well for the midterms, which are a large percent of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Fisher is very caring! He posted a lot of stuff on compass and is very patient with teaching. However, the class is a bit boring. Too many materials are introduced at the same time so it could be challenging. Other than this, an overall great class.

I literally love this man, he cares so much about his students and puts so much time and effort into making sure we understand the course material whether in lecture or at office hours. I took his class last semester when it was his first year here and he was already on top of everything, hats off to my boy Mitch, would definitely recommend him.

-Lectures were very boring and hard to pay attention to. -Do ALL the extra credit (It's time consuming but can give you 3.5% of your total grade back if you do it all) -Exams have HUGE weight of grade -Do the discussion H/W & go to discussion (pts. add up) -Understand HOW to do each kind of problem -Studying: Lecture slides & discussion activities

He really takes the time to work through and explain problems in class. If you show up to lecture and do all the homework you shouldn't have issues. Overall friendly guy. Take advantage of all the extra credit

Seemed to enjoy teaching and cared that the students learned the material. But, tried to teach too much material, which hurt the overall effectiveness of the course. Also, the final exam was worth around 40% of the final grade.

This was Mitch's first year as a UIUC professor, and I think he killed it. It is obvious he cares so much about his students. I think there are kinks in the format of the class to be worked out, but nonetheless, great professor. Mitch, if youre reading this, keep it up. Also, dont make the test worth 40% please.

Mitch is an awesome professor! He is new, so it took him a while to figure his stuff out but he is a very effective teacher that listens to feedback. He made ACCY 201 super informative and easy. Yes, the final is a lot of points and he is kind of a goofy guy, but he has given us really great opportunities to earn easy points.

Professor Fisher is a very good teacher. He cares about students very much by providing every week videos and prepared well for every class. Every homework is useful for the course and you can truly learn things from him.

He is very organized and provides a lot of examples and problems, so students could constantly practice. He truly cares. He puts so much work into his course, posting all the solutions and walk-through videos online. The grading is very fair. Overall, a great professor. Take him for this course.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Great guy
Does not seem very prepared when planning ahead. A lot of the grade is towards tests. Also, seems to try to hard to be funny, which has the opposite effect.

I've found Mitch to be a quality professor. His lectures are very thorough, allowing his students to understand all of the topics. He currently teaches an introductory class, so some may find it a bit slow, but it the pace is reflective of the level. Only negative is how test heavy the class is, which the midterm worth 30% and final worth 40%.
Lots of not very good T.V./movie references. Also, for the first several weeks the class moves very slowly, continually repeating the exact same stuff.
Very harsh grader compared to other professors. A lot of hidden requirements for speeches he wants to get a full score on certain categories. Too harsh for a communications beginner class.

He was the best professor I have ever had. He makes learning fun again. I had him last year, and I wish I could have him again! I see people from the class he taught all the time, and we never fail to bring up how badly we miss taking his class.

This class was not hard at all. As long as you follow the rubric for each speech you're guaranteed an A. I am definitely not the best at public speaking but was still able to do really well. Truthfully, if you believe that he is a tough grader you are not following the criteria that he hands to you... also he is very nice!
Not a cool guy.

This professor TRIES to be funny, but harsh regarding grading. He's very very strict with format and is not tolerant. Took this non-native class, and he was strict with international students. Class is organized, but I feel like he's not the best at teaching, and we rarely did speaking activities compared to other classes. But gives a lot of info.

He tries to be funny and personable but his instruction and teaching technique is very poor. He doesn't really take correction or criticism well. Isn't considerate about mistakes or poor communication on his part and has a more "it is what it is" attitude when it causes a student to lose points rather than being lenient due to it being his fault.
Funny Teacher, interesting class, but his grade is as low as ****.
This dude is quite funny, but the score given is low like shit.
BEAST MODE
It's a good class, just please please please make sure to dedicate a lot of time; it's definitely a big heavy lecture and workload class

I really enjoyed him as a person, he was super goofy. You get a point for every lecture you attend so it was worth it to go. Gave an extra credit assignment every week and the discussion homework was completion. The final was worth 40% and the midterm was 30% of the final grade so be sure to actually study or your grade will massively drop
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: First I have to admit Professor Taghaboni-Dutta is very knowledgeable. My low rating is because she interrupts and talks over students who ask questions only to not fully answer the question. She also says homework should take 5 - 10 mins, yet several others said they took at least 3hrs on each assignment. Unrealistic expectations from her.
One of the best teachers at UIUC. Her stats class finally made me understand how statistics works, after having taken it on before in high school and college.

I'm so confused by the negative ratings here... Her classes were so great in my opinion and I've enjoyed learning from her.  She challenges you for sure but isn't that better than a boring class where its all minimum effort? Its graduate levels... 
She's been so great and is one of the best professors I've had in the program.

I have taken every graduate level class by Professor Taghaboni - she's an excellent teacher and provides real examples within her lectures to allow you to think through problems in an interesting way.  She encourages critical thinking skills and wants to teach you how to think about things not just do things. 
She doesn't put up with B.S.!
Never in my life have I had a professor where I do not understand anything she says. Truly, a marvel. She can speak for hours and no cohesive idea will be communicated.
Hate her class. Do Not Take Anything with Her
She is the worst teacher no more comments. Do not take her elective class. She is a tough grader
Do not take this class as a first class in Gies even if recommended and considering take only this class in a semester. The amount of coursework is absolutely insane and they grade very hard.
Never take her if you have chance.
Don't take her class at all. Bad teacher and bad class. Do not take the class, please.

She is okay as a teacher but her grading is too harsh for a class like this because it is elective and people take it to pump their grades she screw my who group and they got mostly B-. You need to spend hours on this class and her quizzes are too hard only the last one okay.

Don't believe in any good review for her because she might write it for herself. Please don't take the 589 class. It is not worth it because you won't learn anything from her and she grades roughly. it is an elective class. Take something more important.

This is the second course taken with this professor. Yes, the grading is overall easy, but the lectures are way too time consuming compared to many other courses. Too many unnecessary videos. Consicion and more application to real world experiences would be key to improving the overall learning experience.
