Professor Taghaboni-Dutta's way of explaining project management tools and techniques is very easy to understand. She makes the class interesting. Group assignments are a bit tricky and you may not get a lot of hints during open office hours however that's totally up to individuals' expectations.
Overall a great knowledgeable professor.
He can be disrespectful.

Professor Moussawi cares about his students and his field of study. He always asks provoking questions in class that lead to thoughtful discussion. He is playful in class which relieves some of the emotional burden that some topics discussed caused. There are powerpoints gone over in lecture, a quiz almost every week, and 2 papers-1regular1final.

Professor Moussawi is quite literally one of the best professors I have ever had. His class is the most enjoyable parts of my week! You can tell he truly cares about what he is teaching and his students! He is very engaging in lecture. Every time I leave his sections I am so inspired about the topic! If you can, definitely take his class!

Class was great. He's very passionate about what he teaches&helpful if you reach out individually. The only thing I didn't like was he'd get cranky if less than, like, 10 people volunteered to answer a question. I don't enjoy speaking in class due to social anxiety&I think the class environment could have been better equipped for that kind of thing
Very engaging and energetic. You can tell he cares a lot about what he teaches! If you get the chance to take a class with him, 100% do it.
Amazing professor

Took his class during this pandemic and he was awesome!! Super fun class, the chat was always active and the final project was not hard at all. I got to do a playlist for the final project, which I thought was super awesome and unique. The class topics were always very interesting and he always kept us engaged and nothing felt boring
Disappointed in the usage of ai for this class
Lots of ai :(

I'm in the action learning nutrition section and it's honestly my favorite class. We do hands-on activities every week for our weekly assignment so attendance is required, which can be annoying. But there are drops for flexibility. It's super fun and we usually get snacks or even a full meal each class! Not sure my grade. I hope it will be an A.

I am in FSHN120 and think it is good. She is really enthusiastic and assignments make sense, no blatant ai, however I have a friend in FSHN101 and they were showing me their assignments it's actually insane how much ai is used. So if you have a choice take FSHN120 not FSHN101

This level of AI use should not be allowed. If UIUC expects students to uphold academic integrity and follow AI policies, professors should also be held to the same standards. It's looks extremely bad on the university when stuff like this happens, and is a waste of a lot of student's money.
Ms. Gist really cares about her students and is an awesome teacher ! Please take this online course!
The FSHN Nutrition course made me really reflect on my health choices. I learned a lot and I have actually changed the way I eat to better my well being. I recommend this class to my friends.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The professor & class leverage AI in the eBook made for FSHN 101. She is very responsive! In her lecture video, there was a small error when discussing the historical context of egg consumption. I emailed and she kindly apologized for not clipping out or updating that part during the video editing (she is human). She then updated it. She listens :)

I love FSHN 120 and how it steps me through the learning allowing me to correct any misunderstandings I have before moving on to the next thing. The chapter starts with a short video quiz (unlimited takes), a definition quiz (3 takes), then there is a reflective assignment and then some introspective questions that ask how it applies to my life.

I am extremely disappointed by this class. The professor was nice, but many of her assignments were created by ai. Also multiple times when I received feedback the grader would point out that they liked something that wasn't even the subject of my paper/video.

This professor used 100% used ChatGPT for assignments. I also noticed many inaccuracies in her textbook which is required, including “humans have been eating eggs for 6 million years.” This lead me to not know what was real and what was false. If you want an easy A this class is for you but AI could do just as good.
Just do the work and you will be just fine. The professor is very flexible, caring, and gives lots of extra credit opportunities. She makes it very clear that she WANTS you to succeed.

I loved Ms.Gist's class! Her lecture videos were very informational, easy to understand, and allowed you to absorb the information properly. The class does have a lot of assignments due on a weekly basis but they are all pretty easy to do and if you plan ahead it doesn't really take too much time.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She was my professor for FSHN 101 and I'm 100% certain that all of the assignments were created by chat gpt and copy and pasted into canvas
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: One of my favorite professors at Illinois, I've taken 3 of her online courses now just because she makes them really easy to succeed and the course content is always pretty interesting. Would definitely recommend taking class with Toni!

She was really understanding when I broke my leg and couldn't come to class for a long period of time. I was able to still keep up and learn content online and get a good grade. She's really enthusiastic when she teaches and cares about her students. 
I would highly recommend this class.
Great professor, clear grading criteria and easy to earn your A if you do the work assigned!

Professor Gist is very sweet and cares a lot about her students. I learned so much from her FSHN 120 class, and I highly recommend taking this course if you need to fulfill a science gen ed. The class was asynchronous, so it was not stressful at all. :)

FSHN 120 might be the easiest class I've ever taken. No exams/quizzes. The professor is very nice and welcoming, but she doesn't really "teach". Everything you do in the class can be looked up except for the "Semester Step Projects" which are also super easy. If you're looking for an easy class to turn your brain off in this is it.

She is so sweet and her emails to the class were all motivating. She also sends out announcements when there is free food in Bevier Hall for students to get. This class is super easy, only about 3 very short quizzes and a STEP assignment each week and many extra credit opportunities. It is definitely do-able on top of a busy semester schedule.

Great class, prof, and TAs. Toni cares a lot about her students, even the ones online. She's very knowledgeable and approachable, I look forward to working with her more. Literally designed for you to get an A, I had a 120% for most of the semester and I learned a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Toni is very funny in lecture. We had videos to watch before class and played a kahoot every lecture and it was fun. Textbook is super helpful is you follow it. Short assignments that aren't hard before every lecture. Lots and lots of extra credit to take advantage of as well.

Professor Gist was one of my favorite professors and helped me decide to major in food science and human nutrition. Her course is a great introduction into the field. You will get out what you put in!

This professor is AMAZING! She goes out of her way to ensure that you understand the course topics and hosts so many office hours by herself or with TAs. She also uses inclusive language so that no one feels left out. I appreciate that she always used real-life examples, which helped me remember the course topics. Highly recommend!

FSHN 101 Online with Toni Gist was great! There are tons of opportunities for extra credit and you will succeed as long as you do the work. She is a very caring professor who truly wants her students to succeed.

Toni's class is a great experience - she's super engaging and really wants you to succeed. The workload is manageable, and there are plenty of extra credit opportunities. I found unstuck ai to be a helpful study tool alongside the course materials.

Amazing amazing professor I cant recommend her enough. Super easy class, tons and tons of extra credit its nearly impossible to get anything less than an A. Like 4 assignment every week but theyre easy and you usually get a couple attempts and if not there is so many resources to help.

Definitely one of my favorite professors here. This class was mandatory to take for me, but it ended up being one of the best that I have taken. I have learned so much and I am so grateful I got to take it. Dr Gist is so caring and her feedback is always so thoughtful and you can tell she takes her time. I have learned so much about myself in class
Toni is awesome and this class is very easy to get a good grade in.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She is honestly one of the sweetest and most caring professors ever. I know many people who have taken this class and so far I have heard no complaints. She cares about her students mental health and overall well-being (and that even shows through the screen - since I took her online). I loved her class and thought it was really interesting!

Great Professor and class is really easy. There are a lot of extra credit opportunities and she does her best to make sure you pass! Learned a lot in this class and she made things easy to understand. Not a lot of work just some quizzes throughout the week. She gives like 2-3 weeks of "wellness' where you could get extra credit.
Amazing teacher!!!

Super easy course, but still learned a lot. As long as you get the work book and write in it, you're pretty much guaranteed an A. I stopped going to lectures a month into the semester and still got an A.
She also offers a Jame's Scholar opportunity which is easy, too.
Super easy and accessible class. The prof and TAs were very available and willing to help students whenever we had questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: I really liked Toni! She really cared about her topic and wanted to help the students. There was a decent amount of homework, but nothing too unreasonable. She also gave automatic 9 hour extensions, and was very understanding about late work. There were multiple opportunities for extra credit throughout the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: FSHN 101 has been the easiest class I've taken at UIUC. This course satisfies a physical science credit, so if you don't want to take physics or chemistry, take this. This course is set up in an organized way so you know when things are due. Coursework is easy and there is a lot of extra credit. Toni is very nice and responsive to emails.

She really cares about her students, even if there are a lot of them. The grading is clear and straightforward, and it isn't difficult to get an A. She lectures very well and always makes sure to answer student questions. She is also very inclusive of everyone.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: GREAT professor. Very caring about mental health, which is prioritized extensively. FSHN 120 is a very fun and simple class with useful real-world information. I highly recommend to anyone looking for an easy class or to gain some basic but practical nutrition info (no biology needed).

Such a great professor! Lectures were always fun but still helpful. Workload was manageable and exams were always open book. She is also a very caring person and is always there to help her students. Plus, she lets you meet her dogs!! Definitely take her class as it also counts as a gen-ed.
Toni is very considerate, and the class is easy if you keep up with the workload and finish assignment on time. The class is very structured and organized.

Toni is an incredibly nice professor who is always willing to help you out with any issues. It's clear that she also cares more about getting you interested in the content than grading it, as she is very generous with her grading. 10/10 professor - I would love it if more were like her.

She is extremely helpful and understanding. She provides you with all the resources you will need while also challenging your knowledge and creativity. Her course was easy to navigate and understand, and her grades were always on time and graded within reason. One of the best professors I have ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very considerate and caring for your success in FSHN 120 online course. Easy to reach out to for assistance in the course, as well as general health and nutrition inquiries.

Professor Gist is super flexible and caring for her students with the amounts of assignment drops in the class to accommodate emergencies or busy week. Her online lecture videos are also very straightforward and easy to follow along in the note packet. Heavily recommending this class either as an elective or gen ed. :)
Amazing
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class is manageable and engaging. Professor Gist makes the material entertaining to learn. The coursework is relatively straightforward but allows for creativity to do whatever, which I enjoy. I would definitely recommend FSHN 101 or 120 with Toni!

The class was super easy to navigate. There are two weekly assignments and four quizzes throughout the semester counted towards your final grade. The quizzes are challenging but there is plenty of extra credit to achieve a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: As long as you do the work and put the effort in it is easy to get a great grade. The professor is amazing with communication and wants all her student to succeed. You learn a lot and the work is actually fun to do especially because in a lot of assignments you can choose what to research.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Toni is one of the best professors at UIUC. The class is very laid back and easy. As long as you keep with it (which is very manageable) you will get an A. She really cares about her students and wants them all to succeed. She makes this class enjoyable while also learning. 3 assignments a week that are so simple. And she drops lowest grades.
Great choice to take FSHN120 as a Gened course!
She tries to get all her students to succeed
Great guest speakers and easy quizzes and assignments that are very informative and fun to do.

I took it last semester, and it really made it good. You are not required to go to class, but if you do, you would receive extra credits. For the exams, as long as you finish the notebook provided, you would be very likely to be fine. But remember to memorize all the information on the notebook.
Super nice and understanding. Definitely one of my favorite professors. This class was pretty easy since I took it for a gen ed requirement. There is a lot of extra credit and it's easy to do well.
Such a great teacher, I'll probably take another class taught by Toni in my remaining years at UIUC

Professor Gist is so nice and helpful! She answered emails fast and stayed around after class for questions. There was a good amount of homework each week, but not too bad. To get an A in the class you just have to reach 900 points and then you can stop the class.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Tony is very understanding when it comes to grades, and is clearly interested in the topics she teaches. Lectures can drag on, but for FSHN 120 attendance isn't mandatory, it is actually for extra credit. Easy A if you try and study. Open note tests, tons of EC, and there is basically a 300ish point curve (900 points is an A instead of an A-)

Professor Gist is a very energetic and engaging instructor. She is constantly looking for ways to improve the course structure and involve the whole class during lecture. The class itself is easy so long as you put in the effort in filling out the student workbook.

Professor Gist is amazing at what she does. I took this class online, but I still learned so much. I was actually applying the stuff about food science that we were learning when I was making my meals. The student notes packet is really easy to follow with the lectures. If you do the work on time you'll have no trouble getting a good grade.
Her lectures and the lecture notes provided are extremely helpful and interesting. I learned a lot in this class that I will use for the rest of my life!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Toni is the best!! I took FSHN 120 ONL and had the best experience with her. Her expectations are clear but she makes it known that she is always there to help you. Amazing professor and person! Textbook that she wrote herself was about $50 but that was the only cost

This professor provides lots of materials to make sure her students succeed within her class. Things like lecture videos, notes packets to follow long and so much more. She cares a lot about her students and wants them all to do well, so she also gives a lot of review materials for exams.

As long as you pay attention to lecture or watch the video lectures, you'll do fine on the exams. She has a notes packet made for the class. I would usually fill out the notes packet and then add more notes during lecture. I also watched video lectures as review.

I really enjoyed her summer course! We took 2 modules each week (16 total). 4 exams with the lowest dropped. Final exam is not mandatory if you received the grade you want from the other 3 exams. There are multiple opportunities for extra credit. Toni is not only an instructor, but also a caring mentor! She is always there to help!

Toni is one of the best professors I ever had! She makes this class entertaining and informative. I tried different snacks + got extra credit for just attending! In Spring 2022, exams were online & open-book. Make sure you understand the topic well before taking the exam as it will save you a lot of time. She's always available to help! Take her!

Although I took Gist's class online, her lecture videos and assignments felt very personal. The class is one of the most structured online classes I've ever taken, it was very easy to follow. Assignments were fun! Making YouTube videos, writing papers, and at-home experiments were common. Overall a great course, would recommend highly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Really good at extra credit opportunities and making sure as many people get an A as possible

She is awesome and energetic. for the course though I feel like it could be condensed. It is an easy A, but the lectures had an overwhelming amount of information that I didn't need to know. Overall, good class and fun assignments.

Toni Gist is a world-class Instructor in human nutrition. Toni teaches without surface level interactions. She advocates for all her students, and cares about every single one of them. She is also an incredible mentor. The course is stacked with resources, making it hard not to succeed. Toni is not only a master of her trade, but an inspiration.
The final exam was brutal... it took me 6+ hours to complete online. I only got a B.
Highly recommended
Toni is a very passionate and understanding professor. She makes lecture fun to attend
great professor!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Toni is amazing!!!  I definitely recommend this class and any FSHN class for anyone that is interested in nutrition.
Toni is awesome! This class was very manageable and interesting. There are many extra credit opportunities and you even get extra credit for attending the lectures. Would highly recommend.

Toni was an amazing professor. This was the second class I took with her and her FSHN 120 class my freshman year was what actually made me switch my major to Nutrition & Dietetics. She is very flexible with participation and offers many extra credit opportunities. The class structure is very organized and not hard to handle, and I learned so much.
take all her classes seriously
Prof. Gist is so sweet and understanding! Would definitely choose her over any other professor.
Super easy class! Attendance for the in-person section is extra credit. The exams are difficult, but the class is very informative!

She is a great person and care about her students and her material. She responded to me instantly about a question even on the weekend. There are many assignments but they are quick and easy, just stay on top of your work each week. She even reminds you sometimes if you didn't submit something and it's nearing the deadline. Lots of drops too.

Fantastic! Clear on what's needed for a good grade & final is optional if you have enough points. You can use student notes packet (< than $20 too!) on exams, class is *exactly* like the test. Super low maintenance class (~1-2 hr/wk), for a gen science credit. Super caring, reachable, fast grading, & provides alt. steps for people with ED triggers.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Gist was so encouraging and entertaining! She made nutrition fun and interesting. It was very easy to do well in her class, as she offers many extra credit opportunities and gives helpful reminders for class assignments. I feel like I got a lot out of this class!

She was very reasonable with grading and made sure that everyone would do well within the course. If you ever had any problems she would respond very quickly over email. I took the version that was online but she also offers an in person version of the class, each have unique extra credit opportunities.

Professor Gist has a master's degree in nutrition. I took FSHN 120 completely asynchronous. It was a very straightforward class. The course uses a student workbook along with online lectures. Every week, there is a short video quiz, lecture, assignment, and project step. I would say this course takes up about two to three hours a week.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Toni is super nice, caring, and definitely knows what she is talking about. She is always prepared and prepares us well for the tests. The tests are okay, open note, just really quick, so you have to study beforehand. Just go to class, she gives extra credit and goes over direct materials that are on the tests.

Professor Gist is one of the best Professors I have come across at U of I. I am one of the students taking FSHN 101 this year; her first time teaching it. The class is set up excellently for online students, the course material is very organized, and I would never have guessed this was her first time teaching the class.

I couldn't help but not focus during the lectures. I found the tests to be quite difficult but all the assignments and the 3 long written assignments/papers weren't too bad. She is a nice person and the course is beneficial. Overall I would recommend but be careful.

FSHN120 lectures are interesting, engaging, and representative of the content you will be expected to know for quizzes and exams. There are many opportunities for extra credit, and an A is very achievable by keeping up with lecture material. If you have an interest in food science and human nutrition, FSHN120 with Toni is the best place to start!

Currently am taking 120 with Professor Gist. I would say this class is frustrating. I felt like she just read off her slides while incorporating some personal stories here and there. The midterm project literally triggered my eating disorder. She offers an alternative option but I got triggered midway and didnt feel comfortable communicating this.
Cant say enough good things about Toni and this class! If youre even considering it just take it, great science gen ed if science isnt necessarily your strength.

You need to watch the lectures do well on exams, but the content is usually pretty engaging. There's a ton of extra credit opportunities and some quizzes and homework get dropped, so it's not hard to get an A in this class.
Many extra credit opportunities and no need to study very hard, just follow lecture material and you will be fine pretty much fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I think the class is really fun and engaging. It also offers a lot of extra credit opportunity which I think is really helpful. This class uses a student notes packet which I like because I learn by writing.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Gist is a professor who wants all of her students to succeed! Her lectures were easy to follow, very stimulating, and she offered many extra credit opportunities. She would also take the time to email each student on their progress throughout the semester, and to root them on. I would definitely recommend taking FSHN 120 with Toni Gist!

This class was structured super clearly. The lectures and material were engaging and relevant to life. If you put in the work, its an easy A. So much extra credit and the lowest grades are dropped. Would recommend to everyone to take!
Toni is such an enthusiastic teacher. If anyone needs a gen ed, this subject is a must! Plus, the knowledge is very applicable and useful for our daily life.
Easy A if you keep on top of your work! Shes one of the best professors ive had at UIUC. She teaches well and gives out so much extra credit. If your able to take this do it.

I currently have Professor Gist and think that she is a solid teacher. Attending her lectures which are not mandatory really help and she is very accessible outside the class. Get your homework done and you should be fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Gist is a great professor! She is very caring about her students and goes above and beyond to meet her students needs. She's passionate about her work and cares about what she teaches. I'm not sure why there is a bad rating for her since most of her students don't even have to take the final (I didn't have to) because they did so well.

Professor Gist is the best professor's I have had at UIUC. She is very caring about her students and goes above and beyond to meet her students needs. She is very passionate about her work and does a fantastic job at teaching lectures. If anyone is interested about learning useful things about nutrition and completing a GenEd, take her class.
She is amazing!!!
Great professor. Really passionate about her work and super caring/ understanding.
Decent amount of work, but not hard. If you show up to class and do the homework you should be set to get an A. Lots of opportunity for extra credit too.
really good at story telling and making information stick!
Great Person

Loved the class, super easy gen ed! Very accessible and friendly, lectures are interesting and exams are open note. The only required textbook is the notes packet and you just take notes in there during lectures. You don't even need to study to do well as long as you pay attention and come to lecture!

Toni is a very caring professor and the course is honestly super interesting and helpful if you are interested in nutrition. The class is not "easy", you do have to do some work to get an A, but it is definitely not super difficult. I recommend this class as a science gen ed course.
Great class. Easy A and interesting information.
Toni is such an awesome instructor, she makes you really enjoy her class and makes going to lectures fun.

Professor Gist is awesome and the material discussed in class is very interesting and applicable. She makes learning really fun and provides countless opportunities to ensure students understand everything. Im not a science or math person, but this class is pretty enjoyable. There are also a lot of easy opportunities to get extra credit as well.
Great class with a great teacher :) loved it and learned lots!

There are really only a handful of things you have to worry about in this class. A weekly assignment based on a video/quiz, a weekly quiz based on a prelecture, 3 exams throughout the semester, and pre-exam reflections. All of the assignments are simple and easy to do with little time. Attendance is extra credit and the notes packet is sufficient

This is one of the best classes I have taken at UIUC! I love the professor as she is always willing to help and answer questions. She makes her online lectures very interesting and relates what you are leaning to real life examples. The course content isn't too difficult to understand as long as you complete the lectures and online modules.

This class is honestly one of the favorite ones I've taken at UIUC. Professor Gist is honestly so accessible and helpful. She genuinely wants you to succeed so she gives you so many extra credit opportunities that help you! Do all of the homework, take notes, watch lecture videos, attend tutoring sessions, and you'll succeed in the class!

I loved Toni! She was amazing! This class offers so much extra credit that allows you not to stress about your grade. The lectures are very interactive and keep you engaged! The class is interesting and I would recommend it to anyone who wants to learn a little more about nutrition and their own health.

She is an amazing professor and gives plenty of opportunities to earn extra credit. Lectures are optional, but you earn extra credit for going to them and they do help to learn the material. The exams are open-book, so if you purchase the workbook created by her, this class is an easy A!

This was a pretty easy class as long as you watched the lectures and filled out the notes packet. Professor Gist is a phenomenal instructor and this class is a breeze. It has a ton of extra credit and multiple dropped assignments and exams! I definitely recommend and as long as you do the mock exams the real ones will be much easier
Take this class!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Great teacher and fun class! would recommend

Professor Gist is an amazing lecturer who entices students whether they study nutrition or not. The material is quite applicable to everyday life as she correlates subject material to practical, everyday experiences. Overall, the course material is quite easy to pick up on, but in order to get an A you need to study and be attentive to lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very good professor! Easy Gen Ed. Online course was easy with lots a points to help receive an A.
Take this class!!!
She's clearly very passionate about the subject and her class. The class is very well run and the assignments are easy. Easy gen-ed

This is an amazing class! 10/10 would recommend The workbook is required, but you takes notes in it just like you did in high school. If you are satisfied with your grade at the end of the semester (like an A or B) you can skip the final because she drops one of your exams. I took it online but from the videos I could tell Toni is super caring

If you take this class fully online, like me, you will notice Ms. Gist seems to not care about the success of her online students. The communication I have had with her has often made me feel unheard, dismissed, and criticized. The class is simply harder than it needs to be.

She was my discussion section leader and she is amazing. She helps so much with the transition to college, and really cares about her students. Even though she wasn't my academic advisor, I feel like I asked her more questions than my academic advisor. She is really helpful with all aspects of college and I definitely recommend her as a teacher.

Toni is a great instructor. She is super nice and caring to all her students. If you need help please don't be afraid to ask her during office hours. Additionally, the class is super helpful to take because it is very applicable to everyday life. Toni and the FSHN 120 teaching team are very sweet people! Definitely recommend taking this class.
Amazing Professor!! Organized and obviously cares about her students success!!!
Toni is very passionate about nutrition and is always willing to help. I loved attending lecture. I also liked that she emphasized nutrition is not always about being the skinniest person.

I loved taking FSHN 120 with Toni!! She is seriously one of the best professors at UIUC. She gives clear and thorough lectures, and she genuinely wants her students to succeed! As long as you take your notes in the packet and study what is in there, you will be good. 10/10 would recommend this class to anyone (especially as an asynchronous class).
Toni is an amazing person and super helpful advisor!!

Toni was amazing. She sent out emails regularly to remind of upcoming deadlines, extra credit opportunities, virtual office hours, etc. She responds to emails promptly and truly wants to help her students learn. The class has a weekly video quiz (easy) and a 10 question mini-assignment with information from a video/lecture, and a midterm project.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Toni is by far my favorite professor at UIUC! For her in person course, you got an extra credit point for being in attendance and 0.5 extra credit if you participated. There is a lot of dropped assignments and a dropped exam, so getting an A is very feasible. Lectures are interesting & engaging too! Very clear expectations on the assignments too.

Toni is an amazing professor and anyone who has had her has been literally blessed! Shes funny, caring, and super passionate about what she teaches! Shes always there for you if you need any help! Toni also gave out extra credit for attending class and extra credit for participating. Tests are open student handbook.

Toni is an amazing professor! Her communication is very clear and straightforward and she's always there for students. She is truly one of the nicest and most caring professors I have known. Gives out plenty of extra credit and the lecture material follows the test material so that there are no surprises. Watch the lectures & take notes!

Toni is amazing and one of the best instructors at U of I. She cares about her students so much and would do anything for every single one of them. I am so thankful to have had Toni as an instructor this semester. TAKE THIS CLASS! You wont regret it. She also offers an in person section and I highly recommend it if you are on campus.

100% take this class!! Toni was great and you can tell how much she wants her students to be successful. Tests were open-note, there was plenty of extra credit, and I had enough points that I didnt even have to complete the last unit or take the final to end with an A.

The syllabus is clear, student expectations don't change throughout the semester, and Toni makes it easy and doable for all to succeed in the course given the accessibility of the online format. Great teaching team and instruction delivery!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Honestly one of the best professors I've ever had. I loved going to lecture and was lucky to be in person during the pandemic. She is so sweet and very knowledgeable about nutrition. It's an easy course as long as you do the short quizzes (unlimited tries), assignments, and study for tests. Tons of extra credit opportunities. Very manageable class

As an incoming freshman I was very overwhelmed and Toni made me feel at home instantly. She was always so kind and gave great feedback. Since I was in the online portion, there were no mandatory zooms which I appreciated as the rest of the week was chaotic with other events. She let you utilize notes to take exams and always offered extra credit.

She really just wants people to come out of her class with more nutrition knowledge and makes it really easy to get an A in her class because she wants kids to do well. I am HORRIBLE at science and landed an easy A. Taking it online actually enhanced my class experience rather than weakened it. 100% recommend this class. #ToniStanForLife

Not a bad professor, but this class triggered and literally gave me an eating disorder. Be careful if you struggle with your body image even in the slightest. Counting calories is not healthy when you're doing it for the wrong reasons.
A fantastic professor. One of the professors where you can tell she has passion just by listening to a single lecture. Very knowledgeable, and makes attending class fun.
She is the best person I have met in my entire life.

Toni was very sincere and worked very hard to help me get the best grade I possibly could. She is very fun to listen to and follow along with during lectures. There are a lot of extra credit opportunities and as long as you pay attention to deadlines and instructions, it can be super fun and not very hard! I very highly recommend this class!
Very nice. Class fairly easy, but I learned some stuff about nutrition and food safety. Tests were fair.
One of my favorite classes I've taken!

She's a phenomenal instructor who really values feedback. She's also passionate about what she teaches and, because of that, the course is one of the best offered at UIUC. If you want to learn how to be your own nutrition detective, take this course! The knowledge you gain is life changing. Though, its intro so don't expect workout supplement info.
She is amazing and so supportive!

By far one of the most caring profs Ive ever come across. Gives you plenty of ec opportunities and is clear on what you'll be tested on. Class can be a little tough for online kids if you cant go to her in class lectures for ec, but getting an A is doable as long as youre on top of it. Super helpful and approachable and if you need help ask her!

Toni is an amazing teacher that is always willing to help in any way! She has excellent stories that help you learn the information and presents the content in a very effective way. I would definitely take this class again and the mock exams help me study a lot. The assignments are very helpful and I highly recommend!

Toni was a great professor who truly cares about her students and makes learning the material very easy. FSHN 120 was an easy A, and she gives the opportunity for lots of extra credit and multiple dropped assignments.

Toni offers text reminders, quizlets, two different study guides per exam, mock exams (my personal favorite), a discussion page, generous office hours... On top of all these resources she is extremely charismatic, shares personal stories, has a sense of humor, and is always reminding us to reach out if we need any help. I highly recommend.

I took FSHN120 online and I can honestly say if you listen and take notes from the powerpoints she posts every week with her talking, you shouldn't worry about not getting an "A". There are generous amounts of extra credit given out, which helps make the class easier. Just be sure to stay on top and do the reflection papers before each exam.

Her class was by far my favorite class I've ever taken at UIUC. I took it over the summer, but the time commitment was not that bad. You have to make sure you watch/go to her lectures and do the homework. That's pretty much it. She explains everything really well and is willing to help. Honestly my favorite professor.
She's a very good professor and the class is very easy. I didn't spend much time towards it and learned some useful stuff along the way. Definitely would recommend.

I NEVER write reviews but I had to for Toni. She makes quizlets for the class and even offers her number if we ever need any help. Shes kind and is passionate about the class so its easy to learn from her! Assignments are straight-forward.
The course and professor have met my expectations, even though as a exchange student the topics were easy to understand. Good job, keep it up
Toni has been an amazing professor and super helpful all semester. She is very understanding and respectful of her students.
Loved Toni. She WANTS her students to succeed. I wish I could take this class again. There are few that actually care about their students, but she does. A real gem.

This class is easy, informative, and applicable to real life situations. Attendance is extra credit, so there isn't pressure to attend. I personally rarely went to lecture and got an A in the class (I did have to work a little harder than those who showed up regularly). There are many extra credit opportunities and most don't have to take the final
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: SHE IS ABSOLUTELY AMAZING! please take this class if youre given the option to. Lectures are amazing and she cares and loves all of her students
She's literally amazing

Great professor. Cares a lot about her students. Extra credit for going to class. I didn't have to take the final or go to class the last two weeks because I already had the A. (: PLEASE take this class! I learned so much too!

Great professor for FSHN 120. Extra credit for going to class and a lot of extra credit chances. Easy content. Will be done after exam 3 if you just turn stuff in. Did not have to take final or go to last 2 weeks of class as I already had an A. No difference between A- and A.

She is such a bubbly teacher who cares a lot for our success. She cares for each of the hundreds of students enrolled in her classes and gives you so many opportunities to get an A in the class. She gives you a lot of extra credit opportunities so take advantage of them! She even personally emails students if she notices them struggling on tests!

Toni is the best professor I've had. She is really enthusiastic during her lectures and makes the material interesting to learn. She really cares about her students and is an easy person to talk to. Class is pretty easy, small workload.

Professor Burkhalter is one of the best professors at the university. As a history major, I really loved this class. This class has so many safety nets that the only way you can fail is by never doing anything. Despite the huge size of the class, Toni works really hard to get to know her students and make it work. Take it for an easy Gen Ed!
Toni is a great lecturer and cares about her students. Class is extremely well organized!

To pass basically you just have to do EVERY single homework assignment (three assingments every week) and then go to EVERY single lecture. You don't even have to do well on the tests. I didn't go to her class the last two weeks of school because apparently I'd already gotten an A. Show up and pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Toni was a great professor, but she sometimes treats her class like they are not mature enough to be in college. Besides that, the class was really interesting and an easy A. There is also a doable amount of homework. Toni constantly invites students to meet with her outside of class.

Toni is a great professor! Her lectures are very engaging and she does an amazing job breaking down the concepts so it's easier for students to understand. The tests are super easy if you study the iClicker questions, notes from class, online quizzes, and online mini assignments. I highly recommend taking this class!

Toni is the best! She is so helpful both in and outside of class, and actually makes lecture interesting and go by fast. The workload for this class is light (about 30 min a week), and test questions come straight from her PowerPoint slides. Would totally recommend this class if you need a life science credit, or just an easy class to take.

This class is a great class to take for a science gen-ed. Toni sets you up to do well in the class and she makes sure everyone has the ability to get an A. If you do well on the assignments each week, go to class and actually listen and study for tests you're sure to do well and actually get something out of the class that will impact your health.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I am so glad I I took this class my freshman year. Toni does everything she can to make sure that all of her students succeed- this includes offering a lot of extra credit. If you are looking for a easy and fun life sciences GedEd then this is the perfect option. Just pay attention in lecture and go over some notes and you will be just fine.

If you are looking for an easy class to take this summer/upcoming semesters, I would really recommend taking FSHN 120. It is an interesting class that teaches you about human nutrition. Even if you aren't going into healthcare, it is still a class that is applicable to anyone's life. The workload is not 'too much' at all! Everything i
Her lectures were super interesting and she was very nice. I would take the class again if I could.

I loved Toni. I took FSHN 120 as a blowoff gen-ed, but she made me love nutrition and food sciences. She's an amazing lecturer and the content is actually very interesting. I hope she teaches more in-person classes, so that I can be her student again.

I absolutely loved professor Burkhalter's class. As a humanities major, I found FSHN 120 to be not only incredibly interesting but also very helpful. Professor Burkhalter gives interesting lectures and is beyond fair when it comes to grading. If you are looking to fulfill a gen-ed requirement with a great professor, take FSHN 120.

FSHN 120 is a great class to take! Toni is great at helping students understand content. She is very accessible and uses real-life examples and analogies in class. The work is very manageable and the exams are not hard as long as you study. Toni works hard to create and organize course content on Compass which is great!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I recommend taking this class if you need credit for a life science. Toni, the professor, makes the class enjoyable and lectures are easy to understand. She is very informative on food science and nutrition and a very good professor. If you do the work, it is easy to get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Toni is super passionate and makes learning nutrition enjoyable. Theres a lot of points in the class and you don't have to work too hard to get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I LOVED Toni. The course has 3 assignments every week, they're kinda busy work but they're easy points. She gave out a decent amount of extra credit and she's really fun so lectures were never boring. Exams were moderately difficult, but if you read the book you'll do just fine.

Super hard tests with around a 60% average but curved, tests are not like the study guide and he says you should be prepared for anything and need to compete against AI and other top schools to prove that your better that's why he makes it so hard. Test right after  spring break so not much relaxing if you care about your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great guy with engaging lectures (does story time with students). Lectures are really fast paced so, you need to do your pre-lectures 100%. Midterms are extremely difficult (60-70% class averages & low curve). Definitely attend lectures to get iclicker extra credit. My grade was boosted from a low A- all the way up to an A+ just from the iclicker.

Dr. G is a good guy and pretty funny, with some practical demonstrations during lectures. There is ample extra credit opportunity, which is much needed given the devastating nature of his exams. Dr.G aims to have a test average of 60%, which makes it difficult to have an A. However with good effort a B is easily achievable in this course.

very stressful class imo . go to class, get the extra credit there. the tests are super hard but they're basically very similar to the practice tests so study those very hard and do multiple runs thru them. also u can retake one test for full credit at the end so dont worry too much. and ask ur TA for help!

Students will need to know the ins and outs of general chemistry to succeed. There's always plenty of extra material (practice exams, worksheets, the textbook hmw, etc,.) to use, however, and from my experience he's always communicative through emails. Exams are tough, but manageable with studying. Highly recommend having previous chem. experience.

do NOT take this professor. I would rather mop the ocean than take this class. funny in class but cannot lecture well and just skips through the slides. very condescending when it comes to exam reviews in class. just get ready to be su1c1d@l when you take this class. also his “curve” at the end is BS.

Has a very big ego and believes only a few students deserves an A.
His lectures are fun but not helpful at all and the only reason i attended was for extra credit
his curves do nothing anymore so dont rely on that at all

Is a really easy class in the beginning, super funny teacher and a lot demos in class. But his tests are extremely hard and he does not like to ease them at all. When it is close to test time he can be mean and condescending
terrifying. expects you to commit all your tome to his class only and still yell at you

I really liked Dr. G. Attend the lecture, do all the homework and quizzes, and you should be fine. People complain that Dr. G is a bad professor, but I found him hilarious and fun to listen to. He also gave a bunch of extra credit and curved really well. It is a challenging class, but it's easy to pass with at least a B if you put in some effort.

Dr. G isn't a terrible professor, but he's also not a great professor. His lectures are occasionally hard to follow, but that's not the most challenging part of this class. His tests are really hard, and he spends a lot of time trying to trick you. Tests are less about the course content and more decoding what the curveball is on each question.

Dr. G was a very rough professor. He expected his students to read pre lecture slide, which is reasonable, however his exams are what make you hate his class and him. He gives you a practice exam which sounds great. However, on the actual exams, he adds as many tricks and mind puzzles as he cans which causes his exam averages to be 50%-68%.
The lectures were very fun to attend, but the tests were very difficult and you did not learn from the lectures enough to do well on the exams.
I will say his classes are hard but he is very generous in his curve and if you study for the papers according to the practice exam you should be ok. Also spam organic chem tutor on youtube

If you go to lecture for extra credit iclickers and go to office hours, you will do well in this class. Dr. G gives a huge curve at the end of the semester, so don't worry if you land on a boarderline grade. If you use the study guides to study for exams, the familiarity with exam questions will help make the curveballs on the exam less stressful.

The lecture was okay but the course was test heavy and if you don't study then it would be complicated to pass. Also, the study guides aren't the best study method. You should study the past exams and take notes during lecture.

Honestly, Dr. G hate is so forced. While I 100% agree his class is tough, it's definitely doable if you've taken CHEM101 (if you haven't, you may be cooked). His grading was so generous, and you could earn up to 50 extra credit pts by doing the i-Clicker questions. He isn't a great lecturer but if you're willing to put in the work, you'll be fine.

Okay, I genuinely think he gets a bad wrap... he's not a bad teacher. I think he genuinely enjoys what he does, but I also think he gets a kick out of making questions more difficult than they need to be. For anyone who has him in the future, get help when you don't understand and try to stay ahead of things.

everything you have heard about him is true. he does nothing. going to lecture doesn't do anything in this class. he stands at the front and talks about everything other than what you're learning and then put the world's hardest questions imaginable on the tests. It is common to take his class and get below a C-.

Dr. G's class is fast-paced and challenging. Review his pre-lecture slides or you'll fall behind. Expect weekly quizzes, worksheets, and lots of homework. Exams are tough, timed, and full of tricky details. Stay on top of things or you'll struggle A LOT.

Took the class after having a good background on Chemistry because I took chem 101. Attended office hours once, it is literally just him there so the most you're getting is one question answered. Tests are literally terrible and nothing like the review. His curves do nothing. Would be failing if it was not for my TA or extra credit iclicker points.
Lectures are difficult to follow but he's a funny guy. Prepare for really difficult exams and definitely do a lot of practice problems. Exams are brutal but the curve usually makes up for it.

I loved Dr. G! He was extremely personable and I could tell that he really cared about his students. He was really helpful and always took the time to make sure I understood everything and was happy with how I was doing.
DR G IS MY GOAT. IDK WHY EVERYONE HATIN

He tells you the topic of each question, and the exact order they appear on the practice exam. He then adds as many twists and trick questions as he can. 
It's like being told someone's going to punch you in the gut if you walk through a door, so you cover your stomach as best you can, just to get socked in the jaw. He's funny tho, Solid 2/5.

I feel like if you failed, its because you didn't put the work in. It does require a lot of work and sometimes i feel like students are so lazy and just don't want to do anything. Go to office hours, ask for help and watch youtube videos. Overall, it was a great class and he is so funny and nice. I also never felt dumb whenever i asked a question.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: One of the best lecturers I've had up to this point in the sense that his lectures are well prepared and usually easy to follow along AS LONG AS YOU AT LEAST SKIM THE MATERIAL BEFORE LECTURE. Its easy to get lost in his lectures if you don't already know some of the material. Exams are pretty hard, but he gives good prep in review lecture
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Dr. G has been an amazing professor. I have needed many accomodations and he had helped me with all of them. He has been with me one-on-one for office hours. I think he is a caring profesor and really wants us to succeed. This course is definitely difficult but it is better than I thought it would be. I think that Dr. G is funny as well!

Class is difficult, but Dr.G is always available outside of class to help. You are allowed one retake for each exam and are allowed to "fight" back points on exams. If you took Chem 101 and are a hard worker, you will most likely pass the class. One con is that the past exams given for practice are very old and not accurate at times. Good luck!

Overall, this class was hard. His lectures were sometimes really helpful but other times I would leave more confused than before. His extra credit is really generous, and while lectures aren't mandatory, you get so much extra credit by answering the iclickers that it made no sense skipping. Tests are hard, but he's better than other 102 profs imo

Dr. G was not the best at explaining concepts, I went to office hours and felt like it made my understanding of the material worse. I honestly feel like he makes things hard on purpose, and he wants to be difficult. He made concepts confusing for no reason. Ive revisited concepts from Chem 102 in 104 and understand them way better now.

DO NOT TAKE HIM. I looked at rate my professor, and saw the low rating, and I thought to myself, oh maybe these people weren't putting in the right amount of effort. THEY WERE RIGHT. He doesn't actually TEACH you, you have to teach yourself the material and his class is like a review. (A bare minimum one at that). Just take that early class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: My advice is LOCK IN. Dillydallying won't get you anywhere. Make sure you have a really good TA because you'll probably leave the lectures with lots of questions. Reach out for help: CLC, office hours, study groups etc. He moves fast and enjoys tricking us but he's eager to help and it's not impossible. The curves and misc points help a lot!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Dr. G isn't as bad as portrayed on here. His lectures are fast so prelecture is 100% worth it, but if you ask a question he will ensure you understand. Tests always have D or F averages, but if you pay attention to what he says and the info he gives you, you'll be fine. Take advantage of your TA, lecture slides, and practice tests and u'll get an A
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: My advice for freshmen: do not trust this teacher. learn by yourself.

I thoroughly enjoyed taking his class. Lectures were a bit fast, however he was more than happy to answer any questions. I loved his office hours and would frequently attend. PLEASE go to lecture; get those extra credit i-clicker points. Do tons and tons of practice problems!! Funny guy and would definitely take his class again. You got this!!

I was optimistic going into this class, especially during the first few lectures and exam. Then it all went downhill as his lectures got less and less helpful and exam questions got more ridiculous. He made an introductory chemistry class way harder than necessary for no reason.

Do NOT take this class if you haven't taken chem in a few years, regardless of what the placement test says. This class is very difficult and takes up so much of your time. Dr. G is a funny guy but not a good lecturer. He spends too much time off topic and then rushes through the lecture and it is all so confusing. Pick a different prof if you can.

The tests are the worst part of the course. Dr. G designs the questions in a way to trick you so you have to read everything carefully, but it's still really easy to mess up. My advice is do the I-clicker since the extra 5 percent will save you. 

Get prepared to hear the phrases "zigma bonds" and "are you with me on this one" a lot

Actually a no no. Worked my ass OFFFFF the entire sem and grinded my soul. It is my fault for not doing better on final but having an A+ all sem and then getting a B+ because of a singular bad exam is just not. This man is hateful. If you liked to be played then take it w him. I am a changed man bc of this.
lectures are confusing, a lot of self-learning. Tests will have difficult questions even on topics that were briefly skimmed over in lecture. I recommend take advantage of the iclicker points.

Expect to be drowned in homework every night, get very familiar with the CLC. Continuously taunted the class about how hard the upcoming exam would be with the stated intent of dropping our grade by one letter. Hope you have a TA that is informative. He uses a bell curve that, in my case, barely helped. Only 1 retake between the 3 exams. (2/2)

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS UNLESS YOU ABSOLUTELY HAVE TO AND HAVE PRIOR BACKGROUND. Rushed lectures, thick accent, and poor analogies. His workload is ridiculous. The discussion homework is not always relevant to the exam or the current topic. Looking at the exam was like reading a foreign language, not matching the study guide/ class examples. (1/?)

Dr. G was a very funny teacher, but the lectures moved very fast. Make sure to read prelecture material. Sometimes to pay attention we would count the number of times he said "Are you with me on this, guys?" (183 times throughout the semester). Dr. G was a fun teacher, but he prides himself on the difficulty of the class so be prepared for that.

His lectures move very fast, but he keeps class very entertaining and engaging. Midterms are difficult but bonus iClicker points can help save you a bit. Understanding practice exams very thoroughly and all their concepts are necessary for both the midterms and final.

I feel like Dr. G's class isn't bad when it comes to the tests and overall formatting of the class, but I feel like he is really bad at lecturing. He uses a bunch of analogies which kinda do not help at all. If you didn't take AP chem in HS DO NOT take this class, take chem 101, even if the placement exam says otherwise. (ALSO ICLICKER POINTS HELP)

I thought he spent too much time talking about random things rather than chemistry, considering we only had 50 min for it. Even though I took AP Chem in high school and got a good score on the test, this class still took it out of me. Do the work, read the book (I didn't), and go to office hours (also didn't do this), and you MIGHT be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: pretty good professor, however, midterms and exams were insanely difficult and gave a lot work with worksheets, two types of quizzes, and homework every week.

Very good professor. I really enjoy his lectures. But it is just that the lectures are going pretty fast so you have to frequently review and go to that clc once you got any questions. Extra credits are super important so definitely go to his lectures. Exams are hard but if you do all the practice exams and the review exam you should be ok.

A- SUPER easy. Get all 50 clicker points for bonus EC. 100% every ONLINE quiz and homework. Exercises and sheet homework only take off like .1-2 pts per/1000 total pts so try your best on those. When it comes to midterms, I FAILED 2 of them w/ below average, B- on the final too, still got an A-. I got 850 SAT so not smart, if I can do it you can to

Although Dr. G is super awesome in lecture, I still hate. Where he looses all my respect is the tests. The questions are almost worded as riddles and are meant to trick you. He does not prepare you for the tests at all. He does give you past exams and extra credit with I-Clicker questions, but the exam average is around 50-60% so it dosnt even help

Terrible experience. The content handled in this class is mostly easy in difficulty, yet the professor makes it his life mission to make the test questions as convoluted and unclear as possible. The problem for me was that it was more so logic being tested than the actual material. Also, terrible lecturer.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is poorly managed by UIUC. Dr. G has a very odd way of teaching and writing exam questions, that's all I'll say. No concept in 102 was hard to grasp for me, but I found myself freaking out before every test after realizing that you can understand every aspect of the material and still do poorly on one of his exams. Lectures too fast.

Dr. G is a fun and nice guy, but he lectures so quickly for such difficult content. The exams are often rather convoluted, although he does drop some assignments, offer one exam retake, and curve both the exams and the final.

Dr. G has a nice personality, but his teaching style is iffy. He speeds through the lecture concepts, making them a little difficult to understand. Tests are difficult but the curve is typically good and you're allowed a retake. Requires a lot of studying and fully grasping the material. He's extremely helpful during office hours.

I enjoy Dr. G's personality, he's a really funny guy and I found most of his lecture's amusing, but the content is difficult and I would suggest going to his office hours or your TA as soon as your confused with anything because if you don't understand one concept, the rest will be just as confusing since all the content build's off of one another.

This was my most difficult class of the semester. Dr G is a decent professor but the content itself is also difficult. He lectures incredibly fast for 45 minutes straight and then does a random experiment at the end of class despite everyone being incredibly confused by his lecture. He is a nice enough guy but his class is very hard.

The class is challenging but worthwhile. Leverage all resources, including the free textbook, office hours, and study tools like Unstuckstudy, to stay on top of the material. Attend every lecture - participation points can make a difference.

Dr.G has a very different style of communicating info which made it extremely challenging to learn. The professor designed his exams to be complicated in terms of understanding what he wanted, rather than the actual material itself being challenging. Because of this, the amount you studied didn't exactly equate to the grade you would receive.
Tests were absolute trash and even though I didn't think the content itself was hard nor should have been hard, his exams were so convoluted that I quite literally could not grasp them.
It's difficult but in a good way. Inspiring.

While for most of the semester I defended Dr G for his funny but in depth lectures that actually helped me, all three tests of the course felt like a tough maze and mental gymnastics, asking complicated questions that left me stumped and guessing on several questions each exam.

This class was extremely challenging and I average a C on all the midterms/finals. The final curve brought me up to an A. The tests are difficult and hard to predict, so make sure to use all the practice tests to prepare for the midterms and use all of your attempts on homework/ quizzes to get those points. I clicker points will save your grade.

Dr. G is a good professor who cares about his students, but beware that his class is challenging. The way he writes test questions can be pretty confusing but he provides plenty of resources to prepare. He curves but not that much. Make sure to turn in all discussion worksheets and homework, they will save your grade.

Every time there is a midterm, Dr. G takes an enormous departure from the course material we learn in discussion sections and homework assignments. Multiple times I have went to the CLC to get help studying and TA's have been baffled at his exam questions. If literal chem TA's don't know what you're talking about, how could your students?

I was often confused during and after lectures. However, going to office hours really helped me understand the material. The exams can be difficult, but he gives helpful reviews that shows exactly what will be on the test. Tests are also curved as well as your final grade at the end of the semester. Extra credit iClicker points are also helpful.
After he promised to give a bonus to me, he refused to do it when giving the final grade. He chose to forget what he promised to students and did not care about the feelings of students.

It's a hard class, Dr. G will not hold back on letting you face that difficulty. BUT, he wants students to succeed, and does what he can to make that happen. Practice tests tell you the topics on the test and their order, without being copies of the exams. Curves are the same for all students, but cutoff at the end of semester can turn a D into a C

Defended this man way too much for him to not curve the final(average 60%) and for his very generous class curve to be 5 points. Couldn't do good on his exams no matter how hard I tried, and I did have a good understanding of the material before each exam. He barely curves the midterms despite 2 of the averages being Ds. Keep your expectations low.

id pray on the morning of each exam we took and im not even religious. im glad its over. dr g is a nice guy but the midterms are so difficult and nothing like the reviews he provides. its a tough class but u will definitely have a good understanding of chem by the end of it. dont skip lecture, the iclicker points will save u.

I took chem 102 with Dr. G in spring 2023. His lectures are fast-moving and the work is tough, but it is mostly because the subject is just really challenging. If you took Chem 101 and you put in work outside of class you will be fine. The tests are also very hard but he curves nicely. He knows his stuff and he's a good guy.

Lectures weren't very helpful, He talks about how he's very available outside class and antagonized the class for not getting help, but he doesn't respond to emails in a timely manner. Only reason he's not getting a 1 is because he made it so a lot of people didn't have to retake exams at the end. Not the worst class ever, but take this with Huang.
This is the worst professor ever.
Dr G said he didn't want his rating to be too high because that would mean he isn't doing a good enough job (He is a cool guy). Here you go Dr. G!   (Hi Dr. G, I know you read these!)
He makes the exams way more difficult than anything you do in class. I truly believe that he wants you to fail on the exam. Just save yourself the trouble.

Dr. G is a really nice guy, but I'm rating him 3/5 because I thought his lectures didn't adequately teach us the course material. However, the material easy. The exams are fairly challenging, but there are curves and iClicker points to make up for it. Nothing is waited, so you can earn up to 50 iClicker points that will make up for exam scores.

Dr. G made this class much harder than it needed to be in every way possible. Exams often had typos and did not always correlate with the practice exams provided, especially on the final exam. Had to put countless hours in to get a good grade in this class but he did do a very generous curve at the end. If you can get hummel do it & save yourself

I highly recommend not skipping lecture so that you get the bonus iclicker points. This class is hard but do the practice exams and go to his office hours, he explains a lot and sometimes he drops hints about the exam questions. It was tough but he did a nice curve in the end which helped graded a lot.

Dr. G's class is hard just because the exams are very tricky. Lectures were hard to follow sometimes (because I never read over the prelectures). He is so good in office hours though and he will make himself available to help you if you email him. The textbook is also really helpful. He gets a bad rap but he is actually an amazing teacher.

Dr. G is actually a pretty good lecturer, however it feels as though he designs his exams to make us fail. Questions are purposefully confusing and much harder than any of the content covered in class or on practice exams. A class average of 58% on a midterm tells you all you need to know. When the whole class fails it is the fault of the prof.

Dr. G is a fun professor, he's not relaxed but he has a good sense of humor and is fairly engaging. With that being said, he explains everything in the most complicated way possible. You need to be diligent about learning the topics on your own since he expects you to have a basic understanding before lecture and he loves trick/complex questions.

Lectures are a little iffy, but Dr. G clearly knows how to teach chemistry. If a student asks a question, he has a clear answer and analogies to help explain. Very understanding of students' concerns and holds respect for his students. Gives TONS of practice for the exam, so if you study that, you should have no issue on the exam. Would take again!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: DR. G is very understanding and is always willing to make time to help if needed outside of class. The material is tricky, but it's going to be hard regardless of the professor. I appreicated how he had paper tests which to me is a lot less intimidating than going and doing an online tests. If you study YOU WILL BE FINE!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Personally, I think Dr. G is a very underappreciated professor in the chemistry department. He is a good lecturer in a way that he uses interesting analogies and examples, breaks down questions well, and doesn't hesitate to answer students' questions. His exams can be really tough, though. Beware of trick questions and the fast pace of the class.

Dr. G is clearly a nice guy and means well, but the exams are kind of evil. I get it that he wants it to be "application based" but it ends up reading more like a logic puzzle than an exam. Content isn't too difficult if you put time into studying and doing the work. Also offers lots of bonus credit and curves tests.

Chem 102 is tough and Dr G didn't always explain background, which is why I'm glad I took Chem 101 before. But, he does a good job building on base concepts and does many example problems during class, which is how you get participation points. He likes using metaphors (especially bicycles), which was helpful. Quizzes and homework can be hard.

It is just really hard to get a good grade if you have a hard time taking exams, or god forbid test anxiety. Wish the exams weren't like 80% of your final grade, because it makes it really stressful all semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Chem 102 is a tough class, but I thought that Dr. G did a good job of explaining concepts. iClicker points during lecture are all bonus points which end up really boosting your grade. He also curves exams, so even though the course can be hard, there are a lot of places to get points back. He's a pretty nice and funny guy during lecture.

All of the work you do for this class is for accuracy which makes it very difficult to stay afloat sometimes. I wish there was even just one type of assignment that we regularly have to do that is for completion and the rest stays for accuracy to help cushion the blow of a bad exam somewhat. That is my one big critique of the structure of the class
Gets mad when people don't ask questions but is rude when he thinks it's a stupid question. Rambles on and on without letting anyone actually catch up during his lectures.

CHEM102 is not an easy subject, to say the least, and Dr.G made it somewhat understandable. Sometimes questions were a little too confusing, but overall, if you did the worksheets, and paid attention a decent grade is possible.

I took this class in the fall and the reason I marked it as inspirational is because it truly inspires me how impossible he made this class. I attended every lecture, read all the prelectures, and sought outside help but I still struggled. Truly he has some of the toughest questions that are designed to confuse you and make you get it wrong.

Dr. G is a funny guy and his lectures are very enjoyable. He gives up to 5% extra credit for your final grade which is definitely useful. Sometimes, it's hard to make sense of what he's trying to say and he likes to use analogies that may or may not help you. He also gives you a lot of practice exams with questions similar to the exams.

Avoid at all costs. His lecture questions are convoluted and often trick questions. He is not helpful and is unable to accept any type of criticism. Pre-lectures are disorganized and rarely prepare you for the actual lecture. Exams are extremely difficult and barely curved. Worst professor I have ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Literally had to sell my soul to pass this class. Worst semester of my time here, and it was because of Dr. G.
Literally do not take 102 with him, out of my 4 years of undergrad at UIUC he was the worst professor I ever had.

Pulls out the wildest of trick questions to "make you think" when he just wants you to get the question wrong. I wouldn't recommend him to anyone who values their sanity and doesn't enjoy having to listen to corny jokes to make himself appear as a nice guy. On the first day of class, he claims he is fair but tricks students repeatedly.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. G never stops or slows down when asked, and he speaks so fast you cannot understand let alone write notes. He asks if everyone understands when clearly people are too focused on keeping up to ask any questions. He expects you to read the pre-lecture and even if you do it rarely prepares you for his lectures.

Disrespectful towards students, and overall not a nice or helpful professor. Very heavy workload and unnecessarily fast lectures. Material needed for the completion of assignments is rarely taught in lectures.
Tooooooo difficult,

Dr G is a funny guy. For me, his lectures were really enjoyable, but I know a lot of people didn't feel that way. If you take his class, be ready for the weirdest analogies that may help you or make you more confused. His tests are also a really big part of your grade, so make sure you use all the resources he gives: practice tests. Overall good.
Dr. G is a pretty good professor. Chem 102 is a hard class.

Dr. G is a good professor if you are willing to put in the work. Going to lecture is a must- even though he posts all lecture slides, it's impossible to learn the content without his explanations. His exams are difficult but if you study his previous exams and ask questions then they are predictable.
Good professor but the class was way too hard.

Dr. G is the best.  He's cares about his students and can answer pretty much any question you have.  His analogies are really helpful and should definitely be used across all chemistry teachers.  10/10 recommend Dr.G!

Cares about his students, very much. Tries to follow the rules, and as well as that he also tries to be fair for all. Lucky to have a professor to care very much, but because the tests are hard expect to put extra effort in this class. it is to be expected.

This course is meant to weed out people who aren't prepared to be caught off guard. If you have the time management, organization skills, and will power to learn chemistry, then you will be fine in this class.

Expect to watch a lot of Organic Chemistry Tutor on Youtube to understand almost everything you're going to need to know in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He doesn't teach very clearly and it is a lot of teaching yourself. He does give extra credit and curve but it mainly helps those that already score higher. If you do poorly on tests you're screwed.
