Does not know how to teach whatsoever. Ruined my whole year.
Dr. G is fantastic!!! He gives a lot of extra-credit and lots of practice to prepare for his exams. Highly recommend him over the other Chem 102 professors!

Dr. G is my favorite professor!! He is very helpful in office hours, gives plenty of practice exams, and demonstrates many fun experiments in every lecture. If you actually put forth effort in his class, you should do completely fine. Highly recommend!!!

Great professor, go to office hours if you do not understand concepts, he will also take time to meet with you one on one. He is willing to work with you and give you points back if you make the effort. His class is challenging but what do you expect this is college,don't be lazy and apply yourself.
I like your analogies :)

When comparing Dr. G's class to a high school chemistry course, it is easy to see that the level of difficulty is extremely elevated. However, it is important to complete pre-lectures and really study the mock exams, as Dr. G's exams include a twist on them that can trick you. Overall, this class is not as scary as a lot of people can make it seem.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Exams are tough, timewise. However, Dr. G does his best to teach core concepts in a way students can not only understand but also apply to real life scenarios. His experiments and demonstrations make lectures entertaining as well as informative. Office hours are great for going over tricky questions or reviewing before a midterm, as are past tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Exams are tough, timewise. However, Dr. G does his best to teach the core concepts in a way that students can not only understand but also apply to real life scenarios. His use of experiments and analogies serve to make lectures entertaining as well as informative. Office hours are great for going over tricky questions or reviewing before a test.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: i swear idk what this dude is on...

Might be one of the first professors that tests how well you actually know the content of the course. I feel like I taught myself more than what he taught me, but then again, he made the worksheets. I loved taking his tests. I would love to take him again and speed run the course. Take your time and do what he suggests

He reallyyyyyy likes to use confusing analogies to explain things whenever you ask a question in lectures. Also, the structure of the class is set up to make you fail. Avoid at all costs unless you're a masochist.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Makes very good analogies that explain advanced topics simply. Lectures are entertaining and very informative. Test questions can be tricky; make sure to read them twice and double check units

Dr G. is a nice guy, truly, but the teaching does not prepare you for exams. He teaches in constant analogies and never will answer questions directly. Moves very fast in lecture. He specifically claimed in class that he believes his job is to stump and trick his students. Find another professor pls

He is a very nice guy, and chemistry is a difficult subject. The course load is a lot and you need to do more than show up to class. If you didn't take AP chemistry in high school don't take chem 102 with Dr. G your first semester, start in 101 or find a different professor. He tends to talk fast and you get lost in the lectures.

He's a terrible professor who doesn't respond well to criticism and refuses to correct incorrect answers to online homework. Additionally, he gives very convoluted problems that do not test knowledge, but the ability to see through his terrible writing. I would not recommend this professor to anyone and I would drop this class if I received him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Some of the answers in the homework are wrong, definitely wrong. Exam doesn't match what taught in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Chem 102 is a hard class. Yet, the way Dr. G teaches is great. He distributes the points across homework, quizzes, and tests. The homework genuinely helps you understand what going on. His lectures were not the best, but he went above and beyond to always help in office hours if you were prepared with questions. A very nice man.

Very hard class so you have to be willing to put in the work. From the lectures, Dr. G will seem like a mean guy but if you go to his office hours you will see that he is willing to help you. If you want to succeed on the exams, you HAVE to take/study the practice exams. Also lowkey a funny dude outside of lecture.

Chemistry is already difficult, and sometimes you have to do mental gymnastics to figure out what he is asking for instead of just applying knowledge. Sometimes makes you feel dumb if you don't know something. Way too many assignments and homework is often incorrectly coded. Seems like a nice person, but I was overall unhappy with this experience.
Great lectures where you can study the slides for exams. Exams are not hard at all if you study for them and do ALL the practice exams. The workload is not too bad. Curve a little in the end.
I HATE CHEMISTRY MAKES ME WANT TO RIP MY HAIR OUR OF MY ROOTS.
Just be on top of everything, read the pre-lectures, and go to office hours when you can. You do not want to fall behind in this class.

If you value your sanity do not take this course. Dr G claims that students should have no complaints about him since he makes himself available for help, but accuses them of trying to cheat when they ask questions on assignments. His lectures do not correlate to assignments or exams and he gets frustrated when people ask questions during them

Dr. G is the worst professor I think I have ever had here at UIUC. Sure, he likes to tout that he is expanding your mind, but he doesn't extend any kindness to his students that need the most help. Go with literally any other professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. G is a very knowledgeable professor. I would say, however, this class was extremely challenging for me. I recommend reading the textbook and taking the practice exams. The exams have extremely similar questions but with twists. When emailing him a question, I always received a well-detailed answer. For this class, I would study a bit every day.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dropped a quiz for us and a homework at the end of the year when he didn't have to. He was funny asl too

Dr. G gets a lot of hate because the class itself is pretty challenging and is a lot of work. However, Dr. G was always there to support students. His lectures are informative and he is extremely helpful during office hours. He is also very flexible and if you email him with an issue he will try his best to help you. Do the practice tests!!

I sent him an email about a test question (linking the lecture slide with it) and then he accused me of not reading the lecture and called me a disappointment. I didn't have many hard feelings toward him though, just overall that event sorta made me dislike him. His lectures were really hard to understand, but the tests were very predictable.

Dr. G is one of the best professors I've ever met at UIUC. He is so caring that students can have 30-minute individual office hour meetings with him. His past/practice exams are extremely similar to his real exams. I have even got a full score on a midterm. He also offers curves to boost grades on each midterm exam and even homework/quizzes.

Time and effort is needed to succeed in his class. I was doing fine with an A until the final(got a C) which brought my grade down to a B+. He curves all tests, but the last crap he did had no effect on my grade whatsoever. Lectures are not the best, as he explains crap we do not even need. Get ready to cry and curse Mutha after every test.

I had him for the CHEM 105 Lab and a few of the questions on the prelab assignments were impossible to the point that even the TAs didn't know the answers to some of them. He seemed like a pretty chill guy though, one time my experiment data was wrong because the TA made a mistake and he just gave me full points when I explained what happened.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. G's Chem 102 class is challenging and rigorous, but so is every other section. Chem 102 is a weed-out and overwhelms many students. But Dr. G does his best to help students whenever possible. He is extremely available, provides many practice tests for each exam, gives helpful homework and quizzes, and curves and gives bonus points.

If you have hopes of going into Medicine or Engineering, DO NOT take this professor, take this course later if you can, or take Chem 101 if you need chem. Taking this professor will ruin all your chances, so please look for a different option. I am saving you the trouble now. Or take the class for credit/no credit if you have to with Dr. G.

DO NOT take CHEM102 with Dr. G unless you are ready to learn all of the material on your own time. The grade you get in this class is extremely exam-heavy; you'll spend more time studying his test formats vs. the actual material. Get ready for trick questions and zero good study material. Not even the assigned quizzes help w/ exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: If you value your mental health and sanity, do not take a class with Dr. G. Although his lectures are fine, his exams have much harder questions that are nearly impossible for people who have not taken AP chemistry or an equivalent to that. He also takes pleasure in students getting answers wrong and laughs when he tricks students with his trick qs

He is an excellent professor and his course is organized extremely well. But because he asks questions that require you to really use your head, students believe he is being unfair to them. This kind of attitude not only holds you back from learning but from enjoying this class - and really was enjoyable for me! ALSO he has a beautiful voice.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Hes not a bad person but the only thing that got me a good grade in this class was the fact that I watched Hummels lectures to learn the content from my friends laptop. His lectures are not helpful. He expects you to read the pre-lecture slides but theyre so disorganized. The exams arent hard but the wording sometimes isnt correct English.

He makes things more complicated than it needs to be. There are simple concepts that he makes confusing. But if you practice doing his past exams, it is pretty much the same question outline with different numbers. You can argue for points back. It's a pretty easy class if you have taken AP chem before but otherwise, you need to learn on your own.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I would avoid Dr. G if possible. He is rude to students who ask questions in class (& during lecture with 300+ people, he called out students by first and last name when they got the wrong answer). His exams are not relevant to his practice problems. The only reason that I didn't fail was because I took the merit portion with a great ta.

His class is really test heavy. If your not getting perfect scores on your homework and quizzes go to office hours and tutoring do whatever you can to get help because failing those test will hurt your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Lectures are heavy, you really need to focus to understand the material from him during lecture and thats only possible if you are on Adderall. Exams are really tough so get ready to spend countless hours of self-learning. Personally, I didn't learn much from him it was mainly through book, youtube, TA and worksheet. Avoid him if you can.

Dr. G does care about his students. He is flexible with grading and lets you argue back points. Do this bc midterms are often unfairly tough. He curves but not enough for most. Lectures aren't good. Prepare to teach yourself a lot of material. Take notes, do his practice problems, get a good TA (Daniel Gray), and be prepared to work for your grade
He is available outside of class, but his tests are very difficult and they bring down your grade. His explanations of lectures are very confusing and he uses analogies that don't make sense.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The only reason why I did well in CHEM 102 was because I took AP Chemistry in high school. While I don't think Dr. G is terrible, sometimes his lectures are a bit confusing, and if I didn't already have a strong chemistry background, I would have been very confused.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He is a very knowledgable professor, I'll give him that. However, his ability to explain material is very poor. He did make me laugh during lectures but I felt like I wasn't learning anything. He expects us to know more than what we are taught. His exams are very tough so beware if you get him as a professor. I would not recommend his class.

One of the worst classes I have ever taken. I went to office hours, lectures, did all the homework and still got a grade that was less than acceptable. He claims he is there to help students mature and grow, but I don't see how that's acheived by watching them struggle. If the class averages are consistently low, theres an issue with the professor.

Dr. G is amazing because he really wants students to be successful in his class. He offers up to 25 extra credit points and has lots of office hours and appointment times! Chem 102 is hard no matter the professor. Dr.G just makes you really work for it. Tests are tricky and hurt your grade the most. Lazinessbad grades. Proceed with caution :)
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great and intelligent guy but awful at explaining things. Dont go to lecture without knowing what hes talking about beforehand or just dont go at all. Exams are full of trick questions and not straightforward at all like other chem exams. I got <55% on every single test that semester but curve brought me to a C+ in class so thats good I guess

absolutely terrible! chem 102 is already relatively hard as it is and dr, g does absolutely nothing to help his students succeed. he has no care for if you have other priorities outside of class. as someone who has always been good at class i can honestly say the content isnt that hard but his exam questions are nearly impossible!! do not take!!!
Honestly do all the practice midterms and you should be fine (engineering majors)
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. G is a good professor, regardless of the common word that he makes things difficult. Those who complain are just narrow-minded and can't accept the difficulty. His lectures are very easy to pay attention to, if you read the content before going, as you're supposed to. Most people are used to slacking off, which are the same people who complain.

Dr. G's Chem 102 class was the hardest class of my life. If you can test out of it, do it, otherwise be ready to study like your life depends on it. If you want to succeed on the tests do as many practice tests and past exams as possible, they are the closest to Dr. G's style of questions (which are incredibly complex and confusing at times.)
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This is a hard class, and Dr. G makes it harder. He is harsh and unapproachable. It seems he has no regard for the mental health of his students. His exams are extremely difficult, and the phrasing of the questions makes even relatively simple questions indecipherable. Don't take his class if you don't have to.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Good lectures, basically tells you everything you need to know for the midterms. It's not an easy A, but he will tell you everything you need to know for the test.

Dr. G definitely has a very challenging Chem 102. Although it was incredibly challenging, it was so helpful and made every other chem class so much easier. I learned so much in this class because you actually need to learn the concepts and understand the why. Office hours are a huge help because he takes the time to offer lots of guidance.

This was the hardest class I ever took - and no matter what professor you'll have, it will be hard. Despite what people say about him, I genuinely think Dr. G isn't as bad as everyone thinks he is. Actually make effort for your grade. Going to office hours really does help. He explains better during those times and preps you for the exam so GO!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Chem 102 is a weed out class, but Dr. G is likely to be your best option. Lectures are decent, but you want to go to office hours for an A. Tests frequently are just different enough from the class material to really kick your butt. Good news-I averaged a 60 on midterms and finals, and ended with a C+. It might not be pretty, but you'll pass.

CHEM 102 is not an easy class, regardless of who is teaching it. However, Dr. G is a professor who makes it enjoyable and understandable. His lectures can be very confusing. However, he does have open door office hours and responds to requested office hours very quickly. He often helps you in office hours on what to study for the exam.

This class was pretty hard. What he teaches in lecture seems easier compared to what's on the exam. His analogies are helpful sometimes. He's a pretty new professor, so some exams can be easier than others, but overall they are pretty hard. At the end of the semester, since almost EVERYONE failed the final, he added a bunch of extra credit points.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: He is a very good guy who genuinely cares about his students. The tests are very hard and challenging including the finals but he curved them at the end to adjust low grade. A solid choice over Tina Huang because he teaches well. He makes you suffer in the semester but pulls through in the end. 9/10 would take again.
Nothing to say
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Lectures don't give enough information about the content. The exams are rarely curved and brutal, filled with various twists to the point where it's less about content and more about reading past intentionally poorly worded questions. He does want his students to succeed, but he makes it extremely difficult to do so. Avoid if possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. G is a great professor. Chem 102 is going to be hard regardless but he makes it OK. He tries his best to help all his students and understand the concepts. If you put in the work to understand the topics and do extra practice, you will get an A in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: His lectures were sometimes confusing if you don't read the chapters ahead of time, but he always offered to set up appointments for office hours if anyone needed help. Of course, you have to put in the work to get a good grade. His tests are reasonable as long as you study and read the questions carefully during the test.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors I've ever had. Do everything you can to make sure you do not have him if you want a good grade in chem. Tests are almost impossible and are nothing like the practice exams.

Hes very condescending when students do badly on his difficult tests, even though he admits to making them hard on purpose. Also, aftee a student said he used too many stories/metaphors in his explanations and it was confusing, he started to use even more stories.

He has a genuine desire for his students to succeed. However, the class is very difficult. The quizzes and practice exam questions do not adequately prepare you for the tests. Be prepared to do a lot of additional practice problems and reteaching yourself the concepts.

His tests are very difficult and dont match the content we learn in lecture/discussion at all. Despite students doing very poorly on his tests, he simply tells us that were not thinking hard enough, and looks down on students who do poorly. It seems that the positive reviews are from previous years, when his class was much easier.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Reads off slides which he copies straight from the textbook. But then he puts in useless anecdotes and train-of-thought concepts that make no sense and makes the class nonsensical. His exams are twice as hard as any homework, quiz or online assignment. Bad teaching, worse prep. Also no retakes - not close to as good or lenient as other professors.

His lectures were extremely convoluted and at sometimes seemingly philosophical. He does not explain the material on which he tests on because he expects you to understand the "story" behind the content and assumes that most of his questions can be solved using basic logic.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Don't have anything to say
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He is a genuinely nice person who really cares about his students. HOWEVER, his lectures are the worst. He doesnt explain things well and I always leave lectures more confused than ever. He gives out curves, but I really had to study a lot on my own to get the grade I did. At one point I stopped taking notes because it was impossible.

Dr.G is not a great teacher. His lectures were basically useless and they never helped me in discussion. I often felt like it was my first time seeing test question on the test. His quizzes are near impossible the first time around but he does do retakes. The only reason I'm going to pass the class is bc of all the EC. If you can take Hummel do it.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: He is very caring and wants everyone to do well but his lectures make absolutely no sense. I can't follow his train of thought and most of the time left lectures more confused than when I went in. He has a lot of office hours so use those but beware of his tests that are never even close to the review guides especially the final.

Dr. G is an easy A. If you put in the work, it will directly reflect in the grade you receive! Tons of extra credit, nearly impossible to fail. Also, significantly easier than all the other chem 102 teachers!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Dr. G is an extremely caring fellow  he bent over backwards for his students. I do not regret taking him over Hummel. If you can  take him! Loads of wiggle room to ensure you get a good grade. This does not mean he is easy, though. Watch out for his midterms after the first one! They get tricky...

Dr. G is one of the most caring teachers I have ever had. He always puts his students first and is super nice during office hours. I will say through that his tests are quite difficult and sometimes the material on the exams is not thouroughly covered during class. Overall a solid prof. but get ready to work for the grade
Strongly recommend. Very caring about his students. Has many extra credits. Great review session before final.

Honestly one of the greatest people you can talk to. Cares about all his students even though the course itself is very difficult with a very low average GPA. Lots of people complain about the pace of the course due to it involving a lot of various topics, but honestly very manageable as long as you work hard.
Great lecturer, sometimes goes off on tangents though, tries to be fair in terms of grading, good amount of extra credit opportunities.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: terrible. impossible to understand his trains of thought in lecture, and then half the exam has nothing to do with anything that was covered in lecture or from worksheets- and you're supposed to just know it, OR occasionally mark E on the scantron if it was something he expected you to not know!

Thought i got a 12% on the 2nd exam, and he was extremely helpful.. found out 5 days later i got a 60% instead. He is very lenient extremely professional and he loves helping students. Hes very considerate of your progress in his class. Exams arent impossible, and he is very straight up about exam difficulty. Recommend him highly

Pretty cool guy but the material you end up being tested on is near impossible to fully study whether out of the book or past exams (which there are very few). He rarely curves and just taking notes from his lectures will not prepare you for the exams.

Terrible. Teaches a weed out class, but believes no one should be left behind, then has to increase the pace of the course to twice that of other classes in order to catch up. Puts material you havent learned yet on tests and expects you to answer E) Not enough information. Goes off on random tangents about how he is doing this for your benefit.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I despise chemistry with all my heart but somehow Dr. G makes this class seem interesting and not too bad.
He will make sure everyone fully understand's whatever topic you guys are covering and then he moves on. Class gets a little behind at first.

The class follows a flipped format, so there is not much interaction with the professor outside of office hours / other non-official meetings. However, he is clearly passionate about teaching and has put a lot of time and effort into developing the class materials to be as good as possible. A good class, interesting by virtue of its organization.
The course is incredibly well-organized, and if you follow the materials provided, the tests are very fair and straightforward to prepare for.

The best course I've ever taken. It has a lot of work but it prepares you so well that you don't have to do anything extra for the quizzes/final exam. He gave a lot of extra credit and flexibility to make the class less stressful.

Herman is part of what makes CS@UIUC a top program. He structures the class so that you will learn through struggle. Though the content in the class may seem daunting, after each assignment you will appreciate how much you've learned. Grading is very generous, and it is clear in the way he manages his class that he wants every student to succeed.

Good class and content. However, professor is very academic integrity / FAIR trigger happy. Be prepared to potentially get FAIRED over the absolute stupidest / and pointless things. I believe too FAIR trigger happy for his own good. Just beware.

Prof. Geoffery Herman is a great professor! The course is hard but rewarding. While everything is on Prairielearn, there are very clear instructions and video explanations for harder questions. I attended the in-person section at 9 am, but I am motivated to go to CIF. The TAs and CAs are very helpful and caring.

One of the best CS courses I have took. Herman is just so nice and did really great job on how the course is structured. Even the content and quizs are pretty hard, but as long as you finished all the assignments, you are prepared. And they do provide you practice exam with multiple variants, so dont worry about that. And every quiz have a retake.

my favorite class thus far in the CS curriculum. 233 is extremely well designed compared to other CS classes, although it is a ton of work, be prepared for that (6 assignments/labs a week). even coming in with little knowledge about hardware you'll learn a ton, and the course staff do everything they can to decrease stress as much as possible
Herman was always nice, understanding, and flexible. 233 was my favorite class during my Computer Science undergrad.

Prof Herman is the BEST professor you can ever get. He cares about his student and knows the stuff his teaching.

The class is so well structured and offers very comprehensive lectures and video tutorials for most of the questions plus he walks you through some of those difficult question types before the actual quiz.
Made a boring class fun. Best made course ever, an expert in education.

The course has many moving parts, but Prof Herman keeps everything clear and on track. He has structured the course exceptionally well, building simple concept upon simple concept until you look back and realise how far you've come. There are few professors as caring and accessible as he is, and I would gladly take any course he decides to run.

He cares a lot for students! Every exam has 2nd chance. There are plenty of extra credits, and final weighs only 10%. The course is interesting (cuz I am a great fan of computer architecture, but anyway, you will learn A LOT), and the concepts are generally not too difficult. You should spend more time learning stash, cache, and conscious coding.
Very good professor, really knows his stuff
Amazing professor, he really knows the content in and out and actually cares about the students. Definitely made a boring class fun!

Prof. Herman is a great person and instructor. The course material isn't easy, but he gives you lots of opportunities for extra credit and often grants extensions when the whole class is struggling. You can tell he cares about his students. Plus he knows his material in and out. Overall very well structured and organized class.

The course content could be better taught. Prelecture small clips are super good but under explained sometimes so the GAs and labs can become stressful. Class induces unnecessary stress that can be avoided if the content is taught better. But the class is very very well run otherwise. Other classes at this school should learn from professor Herman

The class is organized and run very well, other professors could learn a thing or two from Professor Herman. The content itself is difficult and the labs + GAs are stressful, but that is true for all all large coding projects. If you can complete every assignment by yourself you will do fine in the course, and there is a lot of EC if you can't.
His presence didn't make a class where I hated every second of the content worse. This is just one of those classes that I had to drag through.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Class structure was very organized and facilitated learning very well, truly one of the best. The lectures were usually solid, and the group work provided great practice for the quizzes. Overall, as long as you do all the work recommended by the prof (which isn't that much) you are guaranteed an A.
The class sucks but I think it's unavoidable given the subject matter. Prof is interesting and smart, and there's a lot of ec and stuff to help your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Really fun professor and a good lecturer. There's a LOT of work in this class but it's clear what you need to study for and TAs and professors are really helpful. The material is really fun and engaging.

If you have never taken a hardware class don't think of skipping the videos he uploads. He goes over things very well but sometimes he just skips over topics to save time which is why I rated a 4/5. This class is a grind so be ready for that

Love his bite-sized condensed lecture videos. He went the extra mile by creating 1-2 hr long quiz review videos and other briefings which were super helpful, and even makes other videos on demand. Tons of practice to understand the material and I learned a great deal. Impeccable course structure, organized, and he was communicative over Campuswire.

Even though I struggled a bit in his class, Professor Herman was a very good professor at UIUC. He gave a lot of practice problems to help understand the concepts and even offers videos to watch to help understand the concepts. In fact, he adjusts accordingly according to feedback and always there for his students to succeed.

This class is the best taught CS class I've taken at this school. Very difficult topics were broken down into digestible 5 min videos followed by questions to reinforce learning. Wish every class was taught like this, can't handle 50 min lectures with unrelated homework anymore. Professor Herman really spoiled us.

Professor Herman is one of the most incredible CS professors I've met so far. He does a great job of teaching content (which by no means is easy content to teach) and does a lot to alleviate student stress. He gives many opportunities for students to get extra points and continuously listens to feedback and adjusts his class accordingly.

Nice and helpful. Active on Piazza. Seemed fair and flexible. Lectures and homeworks prepared you for exams. Assignments were managable. However, for many exams, I felt like I was more drilling specific types of problems than learning concepts.
a good lecturer and overall the class is run very well. he gives you the option to opt out of lecture but i wouldnt, you get ec for participating and it will make it so much easier to learn i promise
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Computer Architecture is not easy. Prof Herman makes it explicit and undertandable. There are pre-flight videos and some conceptional questions before lectures so make use of these , lectures are clear and efficient, hw are well designed definitely do it. If you done the above, exams will be OK. Labs are time consuming, start early.

Professor Herman was the best professor I had the semester I took that class. The class has a steep curve in terms of the material, but he explains everything really well, has review sessions and great exam tips. Also, he kept taking feedback about all sorts of things throughout the semester, and immediately acted on them.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He is a solid lecturer and is someone you would be glad to talk with. The exams are reasonable, but exam 6 is much harder than all the rest. Start the labs early and attend the lectures and discussions for free points. The labs vary in difficulty. The class teaches useful material.

Professor Herman is a good instructor even though occasionally he did not explain the material very clear. Self-teaching matters A LOT in this class, and be careful that this class gets harder before you realize it.
He defines me. Never mind.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: He definitely cares about the students, but sometimes the lectures can be boring
He really cares about students. Responds to emails quickly. Very clear lectures. Great Prof.
Professor Herman is Great. The lectures are clear, and the online mini clips are great. Easy to understand, and the best teaching quality ever.
Awesome teacher, too good to be true. Enthusiastic, happy and knows his material well. Will gladly take another class with the dude
GG always re. Herman best professor NA.

He is super enthusiastic, and will go over everything very clearly. If you have questions however, it is best to go to office hours because if you ask during class he just sort of repeats what he just said. At office hours he is fantastic. Just a super nice guy overall! Hard course though...

Students cant understand full circuit design unless their homeworks deal with more fundamental simple design concepts. Grades are important  so challenging problems on graded homeworks with less time to figure them out debilitate confidence if there is no corresponding support provided in teaching. Can improve course structure greatly.
Great prof. Too bad he is not here all the time.

Professor Herman's version of ECE290 is exciting and educational, giving us the chance to get into team based learning at the sophomore level. He's a very enthusiastic lecturer as well, definitely would recommend taking 290 with him if you can.

Prof. Goldstein is the best ISE professor by far. This class was designed so well and I actually enjoyed and learned so much from it. Prof. Goldstein was accessible and understanding. I wish I could take this class again.

SE101 was a lot of work and took a lot of effort, but Goldstein seemed to care more about how much effort you put in than final product. She is very understanding and great to talk to, as well as she gives great advice and direction. Her lectures are great and she provided more than enough resources to do well in the class.
She was great, very nice and explained the concepts with enthusiasm and well.

She is a good professor and her class is graded on a lot (modelling exam, 4 sketching quizzes, 2 theory tests, weekly assignments, and 35% on the group project). You learn a ton in this class but the sketching quizzes and assignments are brutal and tiny mistakes will be there difference between 40/40 and a 15/40. The group project is a lot of work.

Prof Goldstein is honestly an amazing professor. Lectures can be a bit hard to follow so I recommend looking in the textbook/doing practice outside of class to get a better understanding. The group project is really important so make sure to communicate with your group members and don't procrastinate. Also make sure to study for the midterms.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: professor is nice but the class is ridiculous good luck getting a decent grade if your group isn't good

Goldstein is an excellent professor. She's very kind and understanding, and I would recommend getting to know both the professor and your TA. Be advised that this doesn't mean you can slack off because the class is completely dependent on the semester-long group project, which will take up a lot of your time.

Professor Goldstein is a great person, but her lectures are useless. The TAs do the majority of the teaching - they do a great job. The class itself is very work heavy if you don't have a good group that prioritizes equal workload. The sketching and modeling homework is straightforward. The modeling exam and project determines your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Graded on a ton of things, great opportunity to learn a lot. Group project can become really time consuming at the end, be ready to be in the lab a lot. Clear grading criteria and very rubric-oriented class. Overall a caring prof and class where you can learn a lot, albeit while doing a lot of work.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She made what otherwise should've been a painful class pretty manageable. SE101 is just bad. You have individual midterms AND a large group project to complete. 2 labs a week. 3 credit hour class btw. Goldstein and her TAs/LAs are what make the class manageable. Just hope and pray your group is competent
I didn't take a real class of hers but a very nice professor and super easy going.
professor is good, se101 it self is a horribly designed class......

SE101 is a useful class that will teach you a lot, but it comes with a lot of work. It's easy to feel overwhelmed by this class because of how time-consuming the assignments can be. That being said, Professor Goldstein is great to talk to for advice on any issues you are facing. She's understanding and makes sure grading is fair.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Spent a lot of time in Lab completing the modling project. She was not very helpful and all of her lectures never helped for the project only for exams. Puts a lot of pressure on TA's and most of them do not know a lot as well. Sketching quizzes and modling exams taught by TA's. She seemed to care too much about students rather than teaching.

Goldstein's class is fun, but your group can make or break both your experience and your grade. Her lectures were understandable, but not usually worth going to.Tests were based on the textbook that she wrote. Lots of assignments. I learned more by myself than from her lectures, but she is super helpful to students.

Professor Goldstein is one of the best professors I've had this year. She is very understanding and cares about her students. The class itself was relatively easy, although there is a semester long group project and your group members make or break your experience in this class. The grading policy in this class is also very fair.

Cares a lot about students so introduce yourself and ask questions. A lot of lectures seem kind of pointless while others are good practice. Guidelines and expectations were sometimes unclear or introduced pretty late. Be prepared for a LOT of late nights in the lab, especially the week before the Design Project is due.
Goldstein cares about her students a lot, and is clear and straightforward in her lectures. Would recommend.

Cared very much about all her students. Seemed to remember everyone's names even those of people who never interacted with her (in a class of 300). Definitely lots of work but not unmanageable at all. Overall loved the professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: TAs are horrible. Do not teach us anything and are very harsh graders. Design project is very hard and takes a very long time, be ready to pull all-nighters right before it is due. The lecture sessions were pointless and could have used that time to work on design project. Overall, the class could be organized in a much better way.

Professor Goldstein is awesome. Super nice, really cares what her students have to say. Class is not super hard, but will take up a lot of your time. Expect a 4 to 5 credit hour workload- especially near the end when you are working on the team final.

Professor Goldstein is genuinely caring and nice, and it's really made the course better. The Sketching assignments for the Fall 2020 semester were quite tough, but the quizzes were okay. The modelling assignments were tough for me, but there is ample support hours and the LAs are really helpful. The project takes long, but a good team helps.

Overall a good professor. At the beginning, she couldn't really explain the concepts clearly, but after the first exam she got better at explaining them. Sketching and modeling assignments are a little bit hard. Modeling exams were okay, but written exams are harder than expected. A tough final project. She cares about you and every student.

Prof. Goldstein really cares about her students. The group project is rough, especially when you are assigned groupmates that don't do their work, but the professor is really good about adjusting the project grade accordingly if you let her know. Sketching quizzes/exams are rougher than they should be for a 100 level class.

Her class had A LOT of homework that was sometimes quite difficult, and the lectures and labs don't really relate, but she was very, very caring and considerate when I was in the hospital for 2 weeks and could not get much work done, and gave me chances to get caught up. Overall good class but the final project is rough if you have a bad group

Prof. Goldstein was in her first semester as a professor when I had her, so I expect she will improve, but she was kind of rough as a lecturer. The class is structured fairly well, though the final project can be pretty overkill if you're in a lot of classes. She definitely cares though, she just seems to not really cover material that well.

Professor Goldstein was extremely passionate and a great teacher. Many people do not succeed in the class if they don't put in the work, but she makes it so that if you want an A, you can get an A. Tests weren't easy, but weren't unfair either. Final project is rough.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Not the best professor, but very passionate about what he does. He wants all students to succeed.
Awesome professor, cares a lot about his students and wants them to suceed.

Great professor with great lectures that bring in outside experience and examples. Very useful content as well for future internships. TA's were not the most helpful but professor was always willing to chat outside of OH and help out.
Awesome lectures! Great balance of theory and practice. Highly recommend this course to everyone.

Mani loves teaching and is really passionate about the industry. He runs a company and tries to inspire students to get involved in starting up their own and keeping up with the rapidly developing construction industry. He lays out goals before every class and will personally sit down with whoever does below average on a test to help them along.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Prof. Mani is not the greatest lecturer, but the man straight up wants you to succeed in this course and in cm in general. Very well organized course, and if you review online quiz and clicker questions, you will be fine come exam time. Also, go to his office hours he is a great person to connect with if you want to continue in Construction Mgmt.
Very well organized course. Good presentation and very interesting research topics included. However, speed of lecturing is too fast and hard to grasp. A lot of the material is relied on self-study.
Inspirational lectures and great guy. Learned a lot of real world skills in the class
Has extremely unfair attendance policy, and no attempt to make it fair. Show up everyday except that one random day he takes attendance? you lose.

Best professor I've had in college. I actually changed my secondary field of study to Environmental Engineering after taking this class with Guest. Inspirational, makes the material easy to understand, and very reasonable with the gradings. Do not miss the chance to take a class with him!

One of my favorite professors I've had, just because he is very personable and his lectures are extremely clear. Environmental engineering isn't the most interesting topic, so lectures can be boring at times, but I have no doubt I would not have done as well as I did with a different teacher. Attendance is taken daily and all work is done in groups

Really passionate teacher which makes the hour and a half lecture not as painful. Willing to meet all the time, including coming in early and setting up extra study sessions. Constant group work though which can be a pain.

Great lecturer and super nice. Interesting material + lots of practice problems during lecture to keep you engaged. He comes around to help students once he's given the class stuff to work on, so you never feel lost in the crowd. There's a final group project that isn't too hard. I learned a lot and really enjoyed this class overall.

Prof Guest is a very nice guy and he gives great lectures. I wasn't particularly interested in enviro, but I really enjoyed learning from him. You'll be working in the same group for weekly homeworks and a final project & presentation, and he has good policies in place to make sure your members pull their weight. Overall very enjoyable class!

Amazing class. No exams, with grades based on very easy projects and reading assignments. It is at most 2 hours a week outside of class. His lectures are very informative and he uses lots of real world connections.
Easiest class ever, pretty much just watched ads every class. Didn't learn too much, but Prof. Hall was funny and chill

Professor Hall is great! Lectures are quite engaging, and he provides a lot of examples. The grading scheme is extremely generous, and the lectures are attendance optional. This is truly a class you take not just for a grade, but for actually learning the material. If you show up to class and are engaged, you can learn a lot. 100% would recommend.

Super duper easy, you really don't even need to attend the lectures. However, if you're looking to actually learn something, don't take this. He mostly just shows commercials and talks about random concepts. The discussions are small projects that you complete with a few others, just follow the instructions and you'll be good.
Every program puts one of its best lecturers on the intro course. Steve is wonderful. Get ready for a lot of examples of food across the United States that Steve has a liking for.

This is an easy class to take and I think the professor makes this class as enjoyable as it can be. Discussions require work but it's never hard and grading is beyond lenient. Take this class for a GPA boost.

Even though I have not been at this school for very long, I can already tell you that Professor Hall will be one of the best professors I have ever had. Even though his lectures aren't mandatory, I still went to them all because he is such a fun guy to listen to.

This guy's cool. I've been considering adding a minor in Advertising and Prof Hall convinced me. The lectures are easy to follow and assignments are straightforward. I usually have a hard time following lectures, but this class is actually engaging!

doesn't matter if you skip lecture, go to discussion and TA's summarize lecture. 
easy 100%

You have to try to fail this class. All the homework and discussion projects are easy. I don't remember the last time I showed up to lecture and still have an A+. If you need the credit and don't want to put barley any work in, I recommend this class.

professor hall's class is interesting and fun way to get into advertising! there is little homework thats from your discussion section and the book  but the assignments are quite fun! there is the textbook and hw from mcgraw but it is easy and as long as you do your assignments youll get an A. also there are no tests or quizzes!
Great Professor! The class is super interesting and the few homework and projects take very little time to complete.
Attendance not required, and not needed to get an A, but I found myself going purely out of interest. Some of the best lectures I've sat in at the University
Steve is an extremely likable person who clearly showed his love for advertising through his lectures. His students really got to know him through his lectures as he talked about his interests a lot.
best prof ever, class is really really good

Loved this class. I'm currently undeclared, but Prof Hall definitely sparked my interest in advertising. The lectures contain a lot of examples (ads and pics) so it's really easy to follow along. The HW load also isn't heavy. You are basically guaranteed an A (as long as you do the work).
His class is pretty fun and interesting. I took ADV 150 with him, it was super easy, easy A, highly recommended.
Sensational

Lectures are bad. You can use the textbook to learn or yt vids. Lots of extra credit though, and not a lot of heavy work. They also offer curve, even with your final grade. If you can self learn you'll be fine, if not than STAT 400 might be better.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment:
Lectures were sub-par. Recitation from TAs is far more valuable in this course, as the lectures are overly complicated and hard to follow.

TAs were instructed to write vague answers for practice tests so problems could be used on future tests.  Very difficult to learn problem solving without attending office hours since almost 0 feedback is given.

Professor Liu is not a great teacher. The course itself should not be difficult, but the lectures are structured very poorly making even simple concepts very difficult to understand. If you have the choice to take STAT 400 or CS 361, 100% take STAT 400. You'll learn so much more.

This professor is undoubtedly very kind and sweet, but her lectures were hard to follow. There is an incredible amount of extra credit. What I found weird was that there were so many websites used for this one class (PrairieLearn, Gradescope, etc.) and the different exam modes (CBTF, in-person, etc.). She wanted us to try everything, I suppose.

This course shouldn't be difficult, but the professor doesn't help. While I think she's very nice, her lectures are very hard to follow. That being said, I think the course is still quite doable especially with the metric ton of extra credit that's given. Honestly, the 45 minutes of TA discussion taught me more than the 2.5 hours of lectures.

Easily the worst class I've taken here. I think it's clear that Professor Liu is a kind professor and is knowledgable about the material, but she does not know how to effectively teach the course. Lectures are useless and only serve to overcomplicate the course concepts. Exams are also overly difficult. Avoid taking this course if possible.
WORST course ever. No reply to a lot of ed posts including questions of pl autograder.

She is the sweetest professor ever and she makes the tests very easy to prepare for. I barely had to study at all outside of going to lecture. She is seriously very sweet and nice and knows what's she's talking about. Don't listen to these other reviews, if you have an issue with Hongye Liu you're gonna have to get through me first.
A lot of homework, quizzes, and exams. But, most importantly, the notation use and notes are really hard to understand.

- lectures: below average, don't really learn stuff, better off with YouTube videos and textbook
- homework: tough grading
- extra credit: yes
- exams: A LOT HARDER THAN THE PRACTICE OR PAST EXAMS, they can be CBTF or IN PERSON

overall, don't take this if you don't have to, save yourself from headaches.

Horrible lectures, I stopped going to class after the first few weeks and relied only on the textbook to teach me.  Tests are difficult and keep changing in format, from paper to CBTF to in-class PrarieLearn.  Office hours are mainly over zoom and not great.  Content is made hard by the bad teaching.

reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The professor is very nice and the course content, while challenging at first, gets easier if you pay attention to the lectures. There is a lot of extra credit.

The class felt badly put together and very chaotic most of the time, the lectures tend to not make sense and got confusing and the textbook is pretty dry. However, if you talk to the professor in office hours she can help explain things that you were confused on. Expect a lot of self-studying on your own and using online resources like statquest.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Can't use proper grammar in her questions, slides and lectures are completely useless, not sure why she's allowed to teach a core required class.

Probably worst core cs class. The lectures are useless as it is just slide readings. Everything is poorly organized. The homework are useless busy work that is graded long after its relevance. 

Only good thing is the curve on all the exams, which is just an indicator of poor teaching.
AWFULL!!! Almost nothing prepares you for the exams. Complete nightmare!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Liu is very thoughtful and caring about her students.

This class was a nightmare. Not only is the content inherently hard, the professor makes no effort to help students learn it. Everyone fails the exams but there's an often 20%+ curve and extra credit. Homework is pointlessly difficult and does not prepare you for exam content. Lectures are unorganized and esoteric. I learned more from the textbook.

She responds to emails about once a week, usually on Wednesdays from 10am to 11am. Office hours are always packed. Lectures are useless. Take STAT 400 instead if you really want to learn statistics for CS.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Exams were super hard especially when they switched to an online format. Homework graders are bad and you have to literally submit a regrade and discuss the question with the graders. Also, fake extra credit. The TAs were good though
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One star for being a very nice person. Keep in mind this is a stat class for most of the parts, so be prepared. Very challenging and lost for students who have not taken any statistics courses before.

Just awful.The grading is so bad, so many issues with how class is structured, unclear extra credit, exam material hard to prepare for, home works is on your own no examples, lectures are read from slides, can go on forever. What's so annoying is that department hasn't done anything about it, wish they actually cared about how students felt.

Make sure to partake in all EC opportunities, especially the weekly HW. Get a good group for discussion, to minimize points lost. Survey EC was unclear, and missing a single requirement resulted in zero EC, read them carefully.

I lost the majority of points in Midterm 2 & Final. gl
The only difference between a nightmare and this class is that a nightmare goes away when you wake up.

There are no examples for the homework and concepts taught in the class and much of the content is taught in on your time. And also the professor lacks in ability to take grip from the students. Exam and Homework is something you never saw in your life and it will give you biggest failure of your life.
She is very nice and offers lots of extra credit opportunities, however she structures this class in a way that makes it way harder than it needs to be.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She is very nice and always makes time for student in office hours, but her lectures and the readings she assigns are so incredibly boring and uninformative. Half of the content that she goes over in the class is irrelevant to the homework and exams. Also, midterm 2 is notorious for students doing terrible on.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Caring person, lots of extra credit
I mean, "hilarious", literally. Full of meaningless staff such as group discussion.

As previous responses have detailed, she is sympathetic and caring but lacks empathy. This lack of empathy is her biggest weakness as an educator and precludes her from expressing concepts clearly or responding to student inquiries unambiguously. I say this matter-of-factly, not contentiously, and with sincere hope that this course improves.

This course is indispensable to data science/quant finance careers but is unfortunately poorly taught. Please read Forsyth's "Probability and Stats for CS" and Ross's "A First Course in Probability" and test your understanding with the chapter exercises instead of going to lectures/disc. You will learn more and be better prepared for the industry.
she's very engaging
She's a sweet person but a very poor math teacher. I would look for a different class or professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Take a different class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Avoid her at all costs. Lectures are impossible to understand as she cannot speak English well. She moved tests online so that partial credit is not given, making them way more difficult. I can think of no reason to do this other than she doesn't want to do grading. Tests often don't reflect what was covered lectures. Poorly structured. 0/10.
Could not teach to save her life. She's a sweet person, but an awful teacher.

The atrocious management of this class compelled me to even make an account. The lectures are drawn out and overly general, and the homework and exams require students to spend extra time relearning poorly explained lecture concepts. It feels like the course overcompensates for its complete lack of direction by giving large amounts of extra credit.

In my opinion, the course would greatly improve if there were pre-lecture videos to explain the theory and a lecture with real-time solving of problems similar to the homework so students can also see an application of the theory already taught. I learned the theory in this course by doing homework problems, when it should be the other way around.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: CS 361 is difficult, but Prof. Liu has helped me really understand the concepts. Her lectures are can be confusing sometimes, but if you go to office hours, she is more than willing to re-explain things. She’s also one of the most understanding and kind professors I’ve had, and she really emphasizes the importance of having a growth mindset :)

The lectures are really hard to follow and confusing. It is a pain for those who have no background in statistics/probabilities to follow the lectures. I know the prof is knowledgeable and has tried hard to explain the concepts, but she cannot effectively present in lectures. This makes easy concepts (most 361 concepts) hard to understand.
class taught very poorly

The lectures were not helpful at all. You can't do things like answer questions for credit that would give you feedback and check whether you got things understood altogether, and the homeworks can often be quite unclear in the instructions. Not a great experience, don't want to do it ever again.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pretty good professor, very caring and is very understanding of how students struggle. Class was fairly graded and expectations were clear. Good prof but lectures can sometimes go over time.

She's a very caring professor, but math is not her forte. Her lectures cover the basics with lots of examples, but go overtime. Homeworks are doable (but time demanding), and the exams are not too difficult (difficulty level is the same as or lower than homework's). The project instructions were confusing, but piazza/discussions were helpful.

She's really nice and clearly cares about her students. Her lectures were solid and everything that is needed to do well in the course is in the lecture slides. Homeworks were challenging but not too hard and exams were fair. Overall, I learned a great deal about stats and probability from her and had a pleasant experience taking this course.
She is a real nice and responsible professor!!

Took her 361 class spring 2020. She is by far the best CS prof Ive had; shes super caring about her students (regularly extends her office hours to make sure everyone has their questions answered), while lecture can be a bit slow at times, she goes through everything super clearly and theres never any surprises on exams or homework. Great prof!

This course created lots of meaningless jobs, e.g. mandatory discussions. It is really a waste of time to force all students to go to such discussion with everything understood. Besides, the course is disorganized and the delivery is unclear. I didn't learn much from the course and I think it should be improved.

Overall a poor teacher. Lectures are confusing and hard to understand, even with prior statistics background. Homeworks are not well designed and clarifications and alterations are scattered throughout Piazza. The exams aren't too bad, but the project was kind of a mess. She seems to care about the students, but isn't good at teaching.

Bad lecturer as she mumbles about topics and presents elementary stuff as facts (no proof). I'm a CS+Math major and feel that Math is not her forte, she gave wrong solution(s) to project. Her grading is a joke; she curved the final by adding 10% points to everyone so that the median becomes an A, tries to makeup for teaching by hilarious grading.

The lectures were okay. I took 361 in her second semester teaching, and there was improvement along the way, I honestly didn't have the best experience at the start, but she genuinely wants to run a course where students can learn and engage and get help, which is probably the best trait a professor can have. I hope the poor reviews start to fade
You can definitely tell it's her first time teaching -- confusing lectures, not very good course arrangement.

She really cares a lot about the material and especially the students and is generally a very good teacher. I have never seen a professor as active as her on Piazza and its just really nice to have the professor directly answering student questions.

It's her first time teaching at UIUC, I think she did a great job putting all the important contents in to lectures. Everything on the tests you are able to find on the lecture slides. Homework and projects are sometimes hard/ time demanding. But prof was super available (like you can email her questions and go to her office directly).

her lectures are super confusing. she doesnt help with the coding but just expects you to teach yourself everything. this class has been an exercise in watching youtube and teaching myself. she will yell at you for going to the bathroom in a 200 person lecture. she literally asked people to ask permission to use the bathroom. do not take her class
She is a great teacher. However, the class is quite boring which made lectures somewhat hard to get through. However, she does have a lot of passion for what she is teaching and it shows.
I love her she's amazing and wants you to succeed

She's organized during lectures, explains it well, presents the topics clearly, and does examples along with the material. As long as you pay attention during lecture it's pretty easy to get an A in the class. The quizzes are just like the homework, but the retry's get harder the later in the class you get so try to get a good score the first time.

The lectures were okay but definitely not enough to be ready for the test. You are expected to use hw problems for exam prep which I often found not enough. Second exam was diabolical, do textbook problems.The course structure is amazing tho. She is not accessible outside of class like at all so only TA help. Biggest advice: come to office hours!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Halloran cares about each one of her students; I had accommodations which I needed to be fulfilled and she was always on top of things by periodically checking with me to make sure she is doing what she needs to. I knew fusion 360 before the class, but I could tell that fusion is taught pretty well in the labs, so don't worry too much about it.

Halloran is the best for TAM 210/211. She is very clear and knowledgeable and has plenty of exam/quiz level examples during each lecture. Show up to lecture to get the i-clicker points. I went to office hours for help on any HW problem I couldn't do, and that prepared me for the quizzes and final. TAM can be tough, but Halloran is great for it.

Halloran is a very good prof. ME170 lecture content can be boring but she explains it well. She gave good feedback on the presentation for the final project and clearly answered engineering career and Fusion360-related questions. The lectures matter a lot less than your group for the group project did. As long as you work on the project, it's easy.

She's great. The lectures are a bit boring at times, but it's mostly due to the material and not because of Prof. Halloran. She's quite active during office hours and is easily accessible by outlook, which is helpful. However, this class in general is made to be quite easy. The only annoying thing is that you may have to settle for a 8am lecture.

ME 170 is a great class, but the fact that it is only taught at 8 AM does make it a bit annoying. Lectures can get boring sometimes, but you do learn a lot during the labs and the team project. Overall, its a pretty easy and chill class and you get a lot of hands on experience with Fusion 360.

Great lecturer. Good at explaining hard TAM concepts, does plenty of practice questions, and answers questions clearly. Also wrote reference pages and frequently uses them in class. TAM sucks but Halloran at least teaches it well.

Professor Halloran was an amazing instructor. Her lectures were clear and easy to follow, and her recorded videos honestly carried me through the class. Grading was fair and on the lenient side, which made the course way less stressful. Overall, a great prof—definitely recommend taking her class!

Great professor with clear notes and lectures. Loves to answer questions during lecture. The course is designed very neatly and doing the homework is plenty enough for the exams. (If you're stuck on homework, office hours are extremely useful as well!) Overall, I learned a lot from this class and I would highly recommend learning from her.

This class is taught well but man it could so be two lectures a week. Half of each lecture would be review and sometimes the lectures lowkey felt like purgatory. Its good that Dr. Halloran is a nice, respectable, and personable lecturer who teaches the content in a way that is easy to understand though.

Prof. Halloran is great. She has great lecture slides and as long as you pay attention and do the homeworks you're set for the class. Just don't space out or else you might miss something. The TA's are really helpful during office hours, although the discussion sections are kind of useless imo
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great prof, cannot recommend enough. Her teaching style is very intuitive, albeit the subject matter itself is pretty easy as well. On top of that, she actually cares about the class and is enthusiastic every lecture so the mandatory attendance isn't a chore. One of my most enjoyable classes.
Really nice professor but a kind of boring lecture, not too hard of a class tho

This class suffers from the poor structure, and the fact that it is at 8am. Otherwise, the class is easy and gives you a good foundation in Fusion 360. The Cadlabs were really frustrating, and the graders graded pretty strictly. Dr. Halloran is a good lecturer/teacher, but the lectures at 8am are extremely hard to pay attention to.

Professor Halloran is the goat. Her lectures were super clear and organized, she emphasized being a visual learner which is very helpful for TAM and answered questions very clearly. HW was easy, quizzes and final were slightly harder but doable and it was easy to get an A, especially with retakes.

I found the lectures quite boring at times. The fact that they are at 8 a.m. does make them a pain. The labs are good, and it's pretty easy to do well there. Some lectures are pretty useless or just a very small part of what is being tested in this class. CAD is not taught in lectures at all and it's only taught in labs.

Dr. Halloran is great. This class is a pretty easy one as long as you follow the rules in the pdf assignments, and the rubric outlines exactly what the graders are looking for. She and all of the TA's want you to succeed so I highly recommend going to office hours. Imo lectures were a bit boring but maybe it's because they're only offered at 8 AM.
i would love to have profesor huang again she is a lovely teachjer.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Yiju is an excellent teacher. She is intelligent kind and fair. I love being in her class and I hope to take her other classes. The thing about learning languages in college is that it is necessary to spend A LOT of time studying them. Absence will affect your grade. It is so much more effective to come to her class in order to learn the language.

Great Teacher and makes Chinese enjoyable. Although I don?t know too much about the other CHIN 201 teachers, I would recommend that you take Yiju?s class ? She is especially effective in teaching Grammar and I honestly believe you will be better off learning Chinese from her. During class, my advice would be to Always be respectful and courteous.
I love her class!!!  She rocks ^_^!
very smart and courteous teacher. gradiing is strict but fair. does not show favoritism to students, but does appreciate genuine interest and hard work.

Awesome teacher and a really fun class--you do a lot of talking, skits, games, etc.  She calls on you even if you aren't raising your hand.  Chinese is hard but Huang laoshi (teacher) makes it probably as easy as it can be.

First of all she is crazy beautiful as well as damn cute. Her teaching style is very organized and very effective. Exams are easy, as they completely reflect lectures and lectures completely come out of the book. Attendance is the only thing that might hurt your grade. She gives extra help at her office hours. Skits are surprisingly fun as hell.
Best professor ever

So hes goated, i got an A- in the class and emailed him to bump it up and he did it with no questions asked to an A. Besides that hes a really solid guy, maybe not the most interesting but his lecture are super solid and he explains clearly with tons of examples. Open not quizzes and homework with extra credit. Really good guy for a tough class.
He's genuinely a good professor.  Would go to lectures, they help.  Quizzes are easy and useful to study, midterm and final are chill.

Really good course policies for ME200. The biweekly quizzes are genuinely a breeze. His class is open note for all tests including the final. You dont have to attend lecture but I'd recommend it because I think it personally helps you understand the content better. The homework is somewhat tedious but there's room for extra credit there.

He is great for thermo. Lectures are filled with exam/quiz level examples that you need to know for the course. The HWs can be difficult and time consuming but I found that doing all of the problems including the extra credit ones always prepared me for the quizzes and exams. The final only covers the cycles and psychrometrics so focus on that.
This instructor is best for this, what he is teaching.

This man is a robot, but a good one. His class runs at 8am, but you should go. Open-note quizzes and exams so take notes on paper. Very informative class on the basics of thermodynamics with 6 20-minute quizzes and 1 midterm. One drop for the quizzes. Final exam is heavily weighted and mainly covers later content such as cycles and psychrometrics.

The professor is very lenient and generous. Homework is graded on effort, homework has the same weight as the final (with extra credit attached), and all tests and quizzes are open-note on paper. It truly helps in offsetting the high stress of course material. If taking class for credit, pick him over other professors that you aren't sure about.

The class was generally pretty straight forward as long as you did the discussion and homework material. Out of the other 102 profs she's definitely the best and the only one who gives out little bits of extra credits and offers a retake on all of the exams. One thing to note is that her class has exams every three weeks.
Was an amazing professor and I enjoyed her lectures. Tests are a bit difficult but should be fine with enough preparation.

She excels at teaching chemistry. Participation does matter because of iClickers, but her lectures were consistently interesting and engaging. There was a lot of homework, especially in the worksheets that were multiple pages long. The exams were difficult but fair; most conceptual questions were hard, while calculation problems were easier.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class was one of the hardest I've ever taken. The exams are extremely hard. My advice would be to read the book before lecture, go to office hours, and do every extra credit you can. I failed every exam even after studying for hours and still passed with the curve and ec, so DON'T GIVE UP!!!!

I know a lot of people complain that she's hard and stuff, but just dont use chatgpt for hw and discussion questions. If you do, use it to teach you, not get answers. Review lectures, and redo worksheets (esp that), and you will be fine. 
She's very generous with retakes and drops 1 quiz 1 exam, but if you try your best you won't need them.

I love Professor Huang! The course is very learnable. It is very easy to meet with her, and there are lots of recourses. She is not intimidating, and actually really funny. She cares about her students, and the grading is generous. Her exams can be humbling, and the material can be challenging. My favorite professor here so far!

Professor Huang is a really good person and cares a lot about her students, but in my opinion, she is not a good teacher. Lectures were brisk summaries on topics covered in the textbook, and the tests were exponentially harder than the material discussed. Though I am going to do a grade replacement.

I LOVED HER. SHE SAVED ME. TAKE HER SHES SO AMAZING. this class is insanley difficult but honestly if ur pre med take her. she will save you like she saved me by sitting with you in office hours and making sure you understand. take her all the others suck.
Best chem teacher in all of the 102 dept. dr g, Hummel, miles worse. very good grading policy for midterms n quizzes (not enough to save my a- though).

Dr. Huang's lectures were very hard to follow if you did not do a pre-lecture review. I referenced the syllabus a lot for an overview of what would be learned and it was super helpful. Also, use the homework and sample exams as review material for tests and quizzes. Know the concepts AND how to solve the problems.

The lectures teach the content well, but you have to really understand the homework to do well on the exams. If you put in the effort, doing well isn't that hard. Lectures are really fun, and worth attending. The discussions are not as useful, but you can leave early if you finish early. The TAs are also a great resource.

Her exams are extremely difficult as they are more complex than material that is covered. Even if you are fairly confident that you comprehend the information and know how to solve the problems, certain CBTF exam questions can be written like a riddle. I was only able to pull off a B- due to her generous extra credit.

The lectures are quite fast-paced, but still really good, especially with her humor, but she uses iClickers. Discussions (Quiz) sections are straightforward but relatively meaningless.
