She has a test schedule which goes a quiz, an exam, and a retake. The retakes are very similar to the exams, which are similar to the quizzes and practice exams.

She's fs the best lecturer out of all 102 profs. Basically the best out of the worst. She's honestly very fun in class. But her CBTF exams were very hard. No partial credit.
You also had smth to do every week like an exam every 3rd week & a quiz the previous week. Final exam was pretty difficult. The extra credit saved me from getting a B+ though.
Her class is really hard, and her exams through CBTF were nothing like the hw, quizzes, and practice exams. She also never replies to emails regarding grade confusion or general questions.

I really liked Dr. Huang. Her lectures were engaging, funny, and easy to follow. Office hours were incredibly helpful—often better than lectures—and showed how much she genuinely cares about her students. CHEM 102 was difficult overall, but Dr. Huang is the best option and I highly recommend taking her course (and going to office hours!).

do the readings from the textbook if you have not taken chem in a while, her lectures are more a mini summary rather than a complete lecture. the CBTF format for tests is not good, and with a class with this much calculations, students should be able to get partial credit for knowing every step rather than losing 15 points on one problem.

Huang was a good person but not so great teacher. Her tests were tough, nothing like the homework, worksheets, or quizzes that would be assigned though. She had few extra credit opportunities. My TA didn't help much either. Honestly though, she had a decent amount of recourses if you went out of your way to find them. It was ok I guess

Material in the class is easy. Final was hella hard. Make sure you have a high grade before the final, otherwise your cooked. Final is worth 22%, it makes or breaks ur grade and expect it to break it. Did well all year to get cooked by a final. Hoping for a curve. Very funny teacher in class, connected to GenZ lingo. Having a good TA was clutch.
She is a really tough grader and the exams are really difficult to score in since they are taken on cbtf

Nice professor. She gave me lots of useful instructions during office hours, and her lectures are interesting. The test scores were usually out of my expectations(personally I don't like cbtf tests), but it has nothing to do with professor's teaching. Best 4 exam scores out of 5 already saved my life lol, though the retake is not that much useful.

She grades really well (like 4 out 5 retakes and one lowest test drop). In addition, she is really helpful with quizzes and tests. I love her lectures because she is straightforward on what will be on the test. She is genuinely so funny and chill. #lovetina

I don't know how the ratings for Prof. Huang or so low tbh... Her class was a very fair gen chem class that was not hard to achieve an A as long as you study for the class. She gave a ton of resources such as lecture notes, discussion worksheets, answer keys, quizzes, discussion sections, practice exams, and so much more for each and every test.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: THE BEST OF CHEMISTRY AT UIUC

Do not listen to anyone else about Huang. She teaches a hard weed out class that many people do not do good in. However, the way she designs her class and her syllabus could not be better. She allows for drops on quizzes, tests, homework, so you dont have to stress about having one bad grade. She teaches well, but you still have to put in the time.
Very accessible and was very friendly. Exams can be a little tough but this is expected for a Gen Chem course. Highly recommend her over other professors.

Lectures were 10-12 minutes. Answers for lecture participation questions were in the book not the lecture. There were only 2 hws, #3 never got posted. It had content for the final. Course grades were sent without publication. Final exam and hw 2 grades are unknown. I found my grade in my degree audit. "Review” topics from ochem are new concepts.

Dr Huang is very overhyped. took her class Spring 2025, after hearing her great reputation. She is a very nice, but exams are so difficult. She gives few practice tests, rarely answered emails, limited office hours. TAs were not helpful. This class was my downfall last semester. Got a C+ after the curve even tho I studied 24/7

I lost count of how many times I cried because even if I feel I've mastered the content, the exams are 10 times harder and you are guaranteed to tank. I feel like she tries not to have any As with her students. Study and when you think you're done, keep going. I only got a B+ after her massive curve since everyone else tanked as well. Good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Huang is NOT a professor I would recommend taking. She is a bad lecturer, does not explain difficult content well, and is a lazy grader who does not grade material by the deadlines that she herself says. I haven't seen an exam yet where someone gets 100%, and she seems completely disinterested in being available to assist her students.
Everything is straight forward.  Exams are a bit tough, but she allows you to have a cheat sheet.  She expects you to know your gen chem stuff and there is a lot of it in this class.

She refuses to be accommodating to students. Participation is required thru iclicker and she uses something called "focus points" so make sure you have multiple devices to keep on 24/7 and don't switch tabs or else you get points off even if you're just taking notes. Exams are absolutely ridiculous and she basically takes points off for funsies.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Dr. Huang is the best 102 teacher. Read the textbook! With that, the class is not that hard. I had no prior chemistry knowledge, if you put in the time, she makes it so you get the grade you want. Has a ton of office hours, everyone says she grades late but there is nothing for her to grade so I am confused? you see your test scores immediately.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The only 102 professor to use CBTF. Testing schedule had something every week (do the retakes). Discussions were graded by correctness (diabolical). She assumes prior knowledge during lectures, but is accessible at office hours. Grades very very late. Saving grace is the generous drop policy with lowest scores. Make a grade calculator by yourself.

Her lectures were not bad, but constant iclicker questions and she was a very late grader, exams very tricky wording, allows retakes. No grades put in at all during the whole semester hard to tell what you actually had, put final in last minute and never released actually grade for final.

Doesn't explain much in lecture, have to use many additional resources to try to understand the material. Test questions are worded awfully just to throw you off and make you wrong. She never gave us final test scores, and most of the final was low key incomprehensible. 

Managed to get an A but only because I put my life and soul into this class.

Takes SO LONG to grade.Didn't release final grades until Christmas Eve morning 2024.(btw nobody got their final test score back) Just evil cause that final was awful,Idk anyone who was even able to finish it. Unfortunately,She might be the best of the worst, so good luck! You're not crazy, and you will survive:) my advice READ THE TEXTBOOK!

I think that Dr. Huang is a great CHEM 102 professor overall! I would highly recommend doing the discussion worksheets from scratch when studying for exams and attending her office hours helped me become exposed to the same topics on several different instances.
Terrible at lecturing.
She was amazinv=g really enjoyed with her she made nitrogen ice-cream

Moved incredibly quick in lectures, some topics on homework and exams were never taught. Discussion classes and my TA were helpful and the grading curve helps a lot but overall a very challenging course.

The class is doable but be ready to study a lot. If you did not take chemistry in high school you will most likely struggle. I highly recommend reading some info before each lecture or you might be confused on what's going on. Professor is a nice person but the curriculum is very packed and intense. Midterms were alright but final was hell.
Best 102 professor

She just doesn't explain properly. when we learned about quantum numbers, i walked out of the lecture confused, i relooked at the slides (No lecture vids) but they don't help since they don't have word for explanation. i looked at one YouTube video and it was so easy. I don't know why but she has a habit of making the easiest topic hard.

Textbook isn't needed to understand course content, her lectures pretty much cover everything on the tests and you will do fine just going to lectures and studying the lecture slides. Do not skip lectures though, as you will learn lots there, and iclicker questions are a thing. She allows retakes and averages out the exam scores if you do better.

class is a lot of work, but she's a great professor. i've never understood chemistry before, but in huangs lectures, things made sense. the ta's are really good and huang drops a lot of your lowest scores

She's very funny, allows retakes on all tests except the final, quizzes are online but usually timed, and she drops your lowest grades for almost all categories. The tests can be difficult, can go quick during lecture, gives lots of practice material, homework every week, and worksheets every week. Frequent office hours and TAs are very helpful.
Very engaging, always helpful , exams were fair
Was hard to follow in lecture, had to teach myself, 5 exams and can retake all, TA saved me.
Prof. Huang is funny and engaging. Attend lectures, use Unstuck study for test prep, and you'll do well despite the challenging final exam. The workload is manageable with the right approach.
She is the Goat of Gen Chem

Not a good teacher - goes too fast, doesn't explain concepts thoroughly, overcomplicates topics. Be prepared to teach yourself the information. Tests are 5/10 difficulty, throws in distractor numbers and tricky questions. Saving grace - allows test retakes. She's not a good professor, only the best out of the other horrible CHEM102 professors.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Huang was a great prof for such a difficult course

She is a funny and outgoing lecturer, makes class interesting. Material is somewhat interesting, but exams are gen chem heavy rather than actual environmental chem, so must remember lots of gen chem. Exams felt difficult bc of heavy gen chem focus, but there is some extra credit. Lectures aren't very detailed, so must read the textbook thoroughly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Huang is very understanding and kind, she understands that the material is challenging for many and works around that. You will succeed if you do the work. The final is much different from the tests and is much more difficult, so be ready for it!

Knowledgeable, funny lecturer and was very entertaining with her demos. There were a lot of exams and quizzes but were similar problems to discussion and homework. Plenty of extra credit opportunities

Dr. Huang was a great professor. Definitely go to lectures and take advantage of office hours, as she also takes the time to learn and remember your name. She is funny and makes lectures enjoyable. Practice variants of practice problems from all given sources for the tests. Tests can be tough, but her grading is fair.

Dr. Huang was a good professor. She covered everything in lectures, made discussions productive, and gave extra credit. As long as you showed up to lectures and discussions, learning the material was straightforward. The weekly worksheets are essential for studying for tests! She made an effort to make the final low pressure. Overall, great class!

Dr. Huang cares about her student's success. Definitely gives students the ability to get an A with a lot of mistakes. She could be hard to reach through email at times. However, she was funny and helpful during lectures and often used analogies to explain concepts. There's a lot of homework and practice problems available to study.

If you're a good note taker 102 with Huang won't be too bad. She's not the best lecturer, sometimes her classes are a bit boring, but if you understand her review material before the yests you'll do well on them.

Dr. Huang is an overall great professor, really funny and does lots of in class demos. My only complaint was the exams at the CBTF every 3 weeks which included one the week after Thanksgiving break. The exams weren't particularly difficult, just exhausting since they were so frequent.
Compared to everyone else in the General Chemistry 1 department she is much better. Very friendly. Exams are fair. Beware of the final though as it can be tough.
Hard class. Doable. Best out of the chem 102 teachers, but still not great at all.
best professor for chem 102. offers retakes on almost every exam and the final is very doable.

PROS: Tests are doable if you study textbook problems/understand the concepts, homework relates to class, retakes every test and she will average them/drop low scores. CONS: lectures were fun but you still will need to teach some things completely to yourself to be able to do well. Overall, Dr. Huang is a fun lady and is a good choice for 102.
Her exams were very fair compared to other professors, she was hilarious, and taught effectively.
Literal Goat of General Chemistry
Reading the textbook is not necessary, but I think it helps. She is funny during lectures, and she is a fair grader.

If you don't have a solid understanding of chemistry, Huang will be a very difficult professor to follow. She doesn't go in depth, exams are nothing like the problems done in class, and there is no homework or practice problems. There is some lenience because of exam retakes, but besides that, I got an A because of my prior knowledge of chemistry.

I liked Dr. Huang's style of teaching.  Yes, I was annoyed in the beginning, she will not tell me answers to problems when I went to officee hours.  But I understand why she did that, cus I learned how each steps works.

I do NOT recommend Dr. Huang. Her exams are nothing like her lectures and she's a tough grader. She doesn't really assign homework either so it's up to you to do practice problems. Her lectures aren't really good either, and I often have to teach myself almost everything. She also takes forever to get things graded.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Huang is a goat she is hilarious. Lecture mandatory because of iClicker. You get a notecard for the tests, but the tests were pretty insufferable because she asks very specific/hard questions. This class was basically gen chem but with reactions in the environment so be prepared. I did not like gen chem so I did not like this class that much.
Exams are like every other week (5 exams and 5 quizzes) thats worth majority of your grade and not curved. WOULD NOT recommend Huang unless you took AP chem in high school.

Over 70% of your grade is based off of your score on exams and the final. Exams every other week with hard questions. The class isn't curved throughout the semester, only at the end (not sure how much). She's a nice lady but her lectures are not the best and she doesn't explain things well. Would only recommend if you like teaching yourself lol.

She doesn't explain things very clearly and often doesn't answer questions well. I learned more from discussion than any lecture. There are quizzes and exams nearly every week. Grade is curved at the end, retakes are offered, and some scores are dropped. Study and do the practice problems if you want to pass, especially before the final.

Dr. Huang was one of the best professors I think you could get for a weed-out class like CHEM102. Her lectures are pretty clear, and overall were fine. To note: She does have a no-technology policy, I know many people were annoyed with that. Otherwise, expect every week to be a test/retake/quiz. Hard class, but she made it bare-able for sure.
Lots of office hours and was overall helpful during office hours, class is very exam heavy,  you can get an A if you put in the work.

Lectures aren't great, you learn mostly in discussions but even then it's not enough. If you want to do well you have to put in effort outside class. She has 5 tests with retakes on 4 of them, 5 online quizzes and she'll drop 1 of each. No curve because of the retakes but at the end of the semester she drops a lot of stuff.
Basically had to teach myself the course...
Not the best lecturer, but the textbook is really helpful for her tests. She also makes the best dippin' dots !!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: One dropped quiz and exam. Exam retakes to average with your original score. Nothing curved, even with an F class average on some exams. Many lectures failed to convey the material, leaving students confused. Take this class if you like to learn from a textbook AFTER attending a mandatory lecture. Otherwise, I recommend Hummel or Dr. G instead.
Huang gives generally straightforward lectures, provides many opportunities for practice, offers retakes on exams, and was pretty funny. Would recommend.

Lectures were very vague and she kind of expects you to know how to do problems instead of doing examples before having you try on your own. The worksheets during discussion only cover the "easy" questions on the tests, so the ones that are harder and worth more points aren't even practiced. Would not recommend.

I wish I could give a 4.5 She makes chemistry lectures fun, with plenty of real demonstrations of physics and chemistry during lecture. It's impossible not to like her as a person. CHEM 102 is hard, though, so be prepared to do outside research and spend a lot of time practicing.

There are a lot of tests in this class so be prepared to do a lot of work. Lectures were not that helpful and didn't learn a lot from it, but discussion sections really helped a lot with understanding material. Doesn't really respond to questions during lecture either. She seems like a great person, but her teaching skills could use some work.

Expectations is high for the students, need to remember lots of Gen. Chem  and orgo stuff.  Read the textbook and do the problems in the chapters, and you should be fine.  I hear she does curve the final grade.

Dr. Huang was a pretty good professor. I found it really helpful to read the book and DO PRACTICE PROBLEMS. Sometimes she wouldn't explain things at a level that was easy to understand if students were struggling. There are retakes on quizzes though, which help you learn and grow. And she seemed to be the fairest grader of the chem 102 professors.

She gives us objectives, but she doesnt do very well explaining concepts and assumes you know how to do everything. Lectures are essentially pointless but mandatory. You also have to read the textbook because she asks you iClicker questions and discussion questions based on what you read.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: As long as you beat the curve for the exams you should be locked in for a B in the class. Make sure you're active during discussion section (recommend the merit option) and you should be able to absorb a majority of the content from lecture. There's no homework, but I recommend doing the discussion worksheets in full and review them before the exam

Very caring professor, but her lectures aren't very good. I didn't put enough effort into the class, but I managed a B+ because I read the textbook and went to discussion sections. Do this and you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: She never gives actual homework, only suggested practice problems that we should do. Also, she lets us drop our lowest test and quiz grades. Everything on her tests comes from the lecture slides, her test reviews, and the discussion worksheets.

Love Dr. Huang for giving retakes on all exams and one test and quiz drop. It's fine if you don't listen during lectures, but always review the slides after. To get an A, get at least the avg score on the first exam and a 100% on the retake (which are exact copies). The content isn't difficult, it's just the grading scale that's hard (12 pt test).
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Took this class FA20, Prof Huang is pretty good as chem profs go - lectures aren't always completely clear, but extensive office hours are helpful, and the optional retakes + 1 dropped exam are nice (even if exams are relatively difficult). Definitely go to lecture, take textbook notes, and do tons of practice, even if you took AP Chem before.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Her lectures are pointless. She doesn't show all of the work and she will skip over complex concepts that she just thinks everyone already knows. She will tell you to ask questions in lecture, but when you do you are ridiculed and made to feel like you are stupid. One person called her Ms. Huang and she said "It's Dr. to you" and refused to answer.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Easy class, rushed pace.

Overall she is a good instructor, but the grading scale is really tough, at least for the careless people like me. There is literally no HW grade for this semester, that's the main point, so all the grades are from things like quizzes and exam. The class is actually easy, it is just the grading scale is hard.
Saw my life flash before my eyes when I realized I missed an exam due to me thinking it was an hour later. Emailed the professor about it and she came in clutch and let me retake it. She is a saint.

Dr Huang has a lot of office hours, so its very easy to reach her. Her lectures are very clear and she takes questions throughout them. The textbook was free and very easy to understand. The exams are challenging, but if you study, you'll do fine. She offers retakes and averages the scores. A exam is also dropped at the end. She is a solid prof!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Very helpful in answering my questions about exams and quizzes. Don't like the online exams. She does allow retakes and will drop one exam at the end.

I took her class 2 years ago, I was a new freshman and STRUGGLED in her class. I had taken only basic chemistry in HS. The kids that excelled in her class had all taken AP Chem and had prior knowledge. 10000% go to Office Hours every chance you get. If your fundamentals for chemistry are not strong, definitely take Chem 101 first.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Nothing in particular bad, just lots of work to keep up. I need this for pre-med, so I put in a lot of time into it. The topics weren't particularly hard, but just lots of different things to know. Dr. Huang was helpful during office hours.
Chem 205 is notorious for the Cobalt lab, Dr. Huang is helpful if you have questions about the lab report. She is also reasonable about extensions.
The main thing to this class is to keep up with vast amount of work. The retake exams are a grade saver, you can go to her office hours and she will help you with the hard exam questions.
Dr. Huang doesn't teach most material in class. I learned more from my TA and ALEKS.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Dr. Huang's class is something I found quite challenging. However, her office hours are something I really took advantage of to get further clarification outside of lecture. Her exams are really tricky, and there is no partial credit given, but points can be gained because she offers retakes for every exam. You cannot be complacent in her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: ALEKS homeworks are tricky in the beginning, once I get use to it, it wouldn't take long. You get retakes on the quizams, and she averages the two. I get quite the grade bump from these. She will drop a few of the discusion quizzes and one of the quizam. Not a fan of CBTF, and I have two classes that uses them. She is nice in office hours.

Lectures are useless. She fails to show sufficient work or explain concepts. Grades are determined by quizzams, filled with trick questions and choose all of the following which are true mc questions with no partial credit. Assigns online homework which never lines up with lectures and takes a long time to do. She does not care about your success.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: If you use all the resources available like go to her office hours, go to discussion, get help from the TAs, then you should be OK. Definitely read the textbook, the concept questions on her exams can be tricky, and are generally just straight out of the textbook. Get into the Merit section if you can, the extra two hours every week is helpful.

Like everyone else that takes Gen. Chem, it's a lot of work. Dr. Huang was OK, she is helpful if you go to office hours prepared like showing that you tried. I bombed the first exam, but did the retake and was able to recover from that. Lecture can be a hit or miss at times.

Huang's lectures are pretty useless, she just reads off the slides and does little explaining. Her tests are fairly difficult, however she does allow you to re-take them online for a 10% or so boost. If you have her, be prepared to teach a lot of the material to yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Terrible. Even if you attend her office hours, she is extremely degrading. Attending office hours should not scare you especially when you are making the effort to get help. She makes her exams awful with context she has never provided.
Dr. Huang has high expectations for her students, so prepare to work hard. Get help early, studying for this class is very different than high school.

Dr. Huang doesn't explain concepts very well in class. She goes over concepts and then expects us to learn in-depth on our own. Her exams were very difficult, but she had online re-takes that could get you around 10% back. There's an average amount of homework. One thing that's good is that she has office hours for people to come and ask questions.
Overall good, I didn't find the text easy or hard, it's what I expected. I just did the homework problems and go to lecture.

Huang was decent in lecture but left out lots of important info. I went to her office hours to get help on book problems and she didn't know how to solve them. She also made fun of and was condescending of people when they asked questions. Her tests had lots of trick questions (i.e a change in wording, not really testing the material). Thumbs down.

CHEM 102 is notorious for being the weed out class. Dr. Huang's tests are, for the most part, pretty straightforward however she likes to add A LOT of trick questions that don't even really "test" the concepts you study for. I never found lectures to be helpful. I would take advantage of TAs and the CLC for learning concepts and asking questions.

Her lectures aren't great but her tests are straightforward. In other words, get ready to do a lot of learning on your own if you want to do well in the class. The other professors are most likely better lecturers with more difficult exams. So take that how you want, personally I'd rather learn the material than deal with her lectures.

Her tests are exponentially harder than any of the homework or quizzes given, but she's a really reasonable professor. I missed my last midterm and she helped me figure out how to still do well in the class. She just over complicates things during lectures and then makes the tests really hard without a curve.
She was the best of the worst Chemistry teachers at uiuc. I didn't like her but try your luck.

Tina Huang is a good professor. Her lectures are pretty fun because of her demonstrations and humorous personal anecdotes. You will definitely have a strong foundation in general chemistry after taking her class. It is definitely an easy A as long as you study appropriately.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Her tests, like all chem 102 teachers, are VERY DIFFICULT. Prepare yourself with the practice test, and be ready for trick questions. We used a free online textbook - which nobody read anyways. And lecture is mandatory, along with a no-technology policy during lecture. She assumes that you already know a lot of what she's talking about.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Tina Huang did a great job with explaining the various concepts. Her lectures were always fun to attend because of her: experiments, sense of humor, and I-clicker points which is extra credit. Her downfalls are the exams may have material that weren't touched on in lecture and her practice exams don't reflect what is on the actual exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Tina Huang is worse than most any of the chem professors. If you are reading this, take anyone BUT her. This is your sign. I went to her office hours and she called me "extremely challenged" and that I shouldn't be at U of I. Im not even lying. Just dont do it, Take tricky Tom before her
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: One of the toughest class I had my first semester in college. But Dr. Huang was helpful when I went to her with questions. I had AP chem in high school and I still struggled. The final exam really helped with my final grade.

Tina is hilarious. Chem 102 is hard with any professor so don't expect it to be easy. The way she explains things is straight forward and easy to understand (as easy as Chem 102 can be). Would definitely recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Workload is a lot. Tests are all multiple choice and can be very unfair as one small mistake can cause a wrong answer, even if you understand the content. Definitely do not rely on the practice tests being anything like the real exam as usually the real exam is way harder and specific. Offers "exam retakes" but it barely gives you any points back.

Dr. Huang is great, she helped me through the course when I got sick and got a little behind. Go to office hours and ask questions, she will take the time to make sure you understand the concept. The CLC is also good and you can get the answer key to the problems.
Dr. Huang is available to help. When CLC gets busy, I just go up and see if she is available. Exams are not hard if you do the problems.

She makes a lot of academic mistakes during lecture and makes things hard to understand. I would not recommend this professor as you're gonna have to learn on your own to do well in the class. She also talks about her personal life a lot instead of going through the lecture, wasting everyone's time. She is one of, if not the worst professor Ive had
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Tina Huang is the worst professor I've had in over 2 years at UIUC. She is terrible at explaining things and I always left lecture more confused than when I came in. She also frequently goes off on tangents and talks about things completely unrelated to the course. I had to teach myself the material to do well on the exams and succeed in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: hard test, likely to fail, no curve
Hard class but not too bad if you don't fall behind. Large lectures, but the discussion is smaller in size, the worksheets were somewhat helpful.
Honestly a great professor, always responded to emails within minutes. Class not hard at all if you have any chem experience.

She may seem intimidating in lecture but is very nice and helpful if you go to office hours. She sets a lot of different times for office hours, so she can be reached easily. Teaching wise, she's a great lecturer as long as you pay attention and exert at least 75% effort.
This is a hard class, it seems every Chem 102 class is just as hard at Illinois. Go to class and read the textbook, she uses the chapter problems for her exam questions.
Ok teacher, but terribly structured class!!! One of the worst weed-out classes i've ever taken. Don't feel bad if you don't do too well in this class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Midterms are so much harder than practice exams, sometimes even irrelevant to lectures materials. I think she did it on purpose. DO NOT take her classes.
Great professor that truly cares about her students. She is available anytime you catch her in her office (even during lunch breaks).
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: Tina is great, but she really makes you work for it. If you want to do well, go to her office hours
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Exams are hard, but there is a curve for the class.  Unlike the previous comment, I find the lectures quite specific, she goes over specific problems in class, I find that if I don't do the homework problems or read the chapters before lecture I have a hard time following what we do in class.  Office hours and review sessions were somewhat helpful.

She's was an absolutely horrible professor. During lecture she was very vague about all concepts even though on tests she is super specific. She also words questions horribly on exams so that you don't even really know what she's asking you to do. Unfortunately she is the only professor that teaches 204 and it is required for my major
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Expect to work for this class.  The content isn't hard, just a lot of stuff to learn compare to high school.  Exams were doable if you study ahead and do the book problems.  Lots of easy points to get from homework and going to discussion.  She is helpful if you go to office hours, but you'll need to show your work.

Huang is a great professor, but the lectures went very fast and were sometimes a bit scattered. Definitely read the textbook, and study along all semester NOT just before exams. The finals a bit of a grade saver.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She's awesome, if u take her class seriously. A lot of people didn't do well but most of them slacked. Exams are hard, don't rely on practice exams. Makes TAs do a lot of her work, but thats ok. Go to class, you'll need the Clicker points. Do practice problems. Show her respect, she HATES phones in class! Shes pretty funny lol

Dr. Huang is good at explaining things and is very helpful during office hours. However, her exams are very tough. She says to read the textbook, but I felt that I wasted my time reading. Just use the book for practice problems and look over her lecture notes. Attend lecture because you get extra credit, and don't rely on the practice exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Huang was a great professor! She provides numerous opportunities to improve your grade and is very helpful. Her lectures are interesting and she clearly enjoys lecturing.
If you want a decent grade, you'll need to go to all the lectures and discussions and do the book problems.  Go to the review sessions, go to office hours, we went over my exams in detail.
Make several academic mistakes and sometimes misleads students
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She's a really funny and awesome professor!! if you have taken AP Chem in high school this class should be a breeze for you for the most part, but that doesn't mean you shouldnt try! Go to lecture and her office hours to clear stuff up! The first test I didn't do this and I did bad even though i thought the material was extremely easy!

Dr. Huang is strict about certain things. However, she is very caring and will help each student individually. Most classes come with a considerable amount of work, so expect to spend some time on it. But overall she is great, and she is hilarious in the lectures.
A lot of work for a 3 credit class.  Dr. Haung is funny and helpful.  If you can get a hold of the old exams questions, these are helpful for studying, but definitely do those chapter problems.

The course was fine, she was organized in lecture and available to answer questions.  Office hours can get busy around exam time, wished she had sign up times to meet rather than waiting for your turn.

In hindsight, I shoudn't have taken this class in the summer, it goes so fast.  I am able to keep up, but can't slack off too much.  Dr. Huang is very helpful is you go to office hours.  Go to the exam review sessions, you can ask a lot of questions and she'll answer them.

The class overall was really tough, but as long as you work for the grade you should be okay.  It is definitely necessary to attend every class because the information covered in lecture really help to understand how to do problems.  The tests were really hard but the lectures were very interesting.

I struggled in this class, so I went to office hours a lot, she is very patient and helpful even when I asked really basic questions.   She also gives additional review sessions every week.  Even though she said she doesn't curve the exams, she will scale the overall grade.  I thought I was getting a C, but ended up with a B.
Honestly the best chemistry teacher I've ever had. It's very hard to fall asleep in class because lectures are so interesting. The exams were very hard for me, but the final saved my grade.
Good, albeit strict professor. Optional textbook readings and problems. Lectures were well run and entertaining.
she's a good professor, is very friendly during office hours, and she's pretty funny. Tests were kind of hard though.
Tests are hard, definitely do the exam retakes to get the extra points.  Final was easier than the nornal exams.  A lot of work for an intro class.  The demos were kind of interesting.

Nothing really special or bad about the class.  It wasn't a very hard class, just have to do the work, like all my other classes.  She was helpful and flexible when I got sick during the semester.  Office hours can be crowded sometimes, so I generally go to the extra review sessions where she would work out the hard problems in detail.

Geez, hardest dang class I took. I studied so much. Her tests are doable, but the questions just love making sure there's no cracks in your armor. Heard Chem104 is easier than this. Other than my bitterness of not getting an A after spending countless hours studying chem, Tina is AMAZING! She's the best one to take Chem102 with. Wish it's easier.

She was pretty helpful in answering my questions.  Makes you show your work though  if you ask her about homework problems.  Pretty organized, provided lectures notes for studying.  Her tests are not that easy (not for me, anyways), but not impossible.

Compared to all the other Chem 102 teachers, she definitely has the easiest tests!! However, her lectures are not very helpful. I found the class pretty easy because I remembered most of the material from high school. Even though the lectures aren't very great, you can definitely get an A if you put in the time.

Professor Huang had challenging exams and her lectures were fast paced. She was a good professor though, she was available during office hours and kindly answered any questions I had. She explains things really quickly so you have to keep up. Overall, she was a good professor and I've heard she's better than a lot of other chem102 professors.

Her lectures are fascinating but you have to read a lot before class in order to follow her steps. She only talk about the most "important" part in lecture and expect you to know the elementary things through reading by yourself. Anyway, you can get a really good score (I mean, an A+)  in her class as long as you work hard.
Her lectures are interesting, and she keeps me entertained and awake with demonstrations and awesome questions.
Very clear, not much patience if you don't put in your own effort
This class is a lot of work, more than I expected.  Lectures were pretty organized, but we jumped around the textbook, had to keep checking the syllabus to see which chapter we are doing.
Not a bad class if you go to lectures and the discussions.  Exams can be a bit long, sometimes feel rushed.  She is friendly at office hours.

You MUST do the assigned reading before lecture or you will have no idea what is happening. The first exam was alright but the second & third ones were insanely difficult. Even Huang admitted she made them too hard. The TAs for this class and the professor NEVER seemed to be on the same page and the discussions and lectures often did not match up.

Dr. Tina expects you to do the reading before you come to the class, or else you will not understand a single thing unless you have good high school chem. Her lectures are not that interesting and her explanation is not very clear sometimes. Exams are extremely hard. You gotta put in lots of effort in order to catch up with the class.

Don't wait until before the exam to do the problems, she based some exam question on the chapter problems.  Quizzes are somewhat easy, doesn't curve the exams anymore, I heard she did before.  But you get to do exam retakes that will give you a higher score.  Overall good.

Tough class for a freshmen if you don't have good high school chem, which I didn't.  So a lot of catching to do where my friends thought it was easy.  Do the homework problems and go to her for help.  I probably should have taken the remedial chem course first, but it worked out OK.

The tests are doable, I just made a lot of stupid mistakes at first but nothing due to the professor's bad teaching or the sort. If you ask for help by arranging a meeting or going to office hours, you will get it. It's really enjoyable when she does demos. Make sure to do the textbook problems for studying. She is a pretty down-to-earth person.

Yeah, she does expect you to read the textbook before coming to lecture, so if you don't, you will get confused.  But if you do, the lectures makes sense, she also expects us to do a lot of practice problems.  I find her office hours very helpful, and she will answer the online homework problems if you show her your work.
The lecture is so confusing. She assumes that you have read and understood all the concepts. Really learn by myself ...

I  had her last semester, she is friendly and helpful.  Her lectures vary from interesting to a bit boring when she is just doing calculations.  Exams are hard if you don't do the  problems, go to class even if you don't feel like it.   It's a hard class whether you take Hummel, Marville, or her.  My friend and I got same grades,  she had Hummel.
Probably the least clear lecturer, expects her students to be able to grasp hard concepts without actually lecturing on them. STAY AWAY if youre Taking 202204

Very helpful and patient if you are confused about something, but do go to her right away, don't wait till the last minute to ask exam questions, her office hours can be packed during exam week.  Grading was straight forward, no curving on exams, but 25% of the grade is based on homeowork, quizzes and partipation, which was not that difficult.

I was told by someone who took this class that it will be hard, so I was prepared.  Go to lecture and pay attention, do the problems.  Lots of things to keep track, homework, quizzes.  She does drop a couple of the lowest quizzes, and some extra credit.  Does not curve the final, but you get 2nd chances to get extra points for your other exams.

This class was HARD-tests were not curved. Overall, didn't think she was a good professor-unclear, not very funny but was nice. My main issue were the tests: very very hard. Chemistry overall was a good subject for me but I found her class very difficult.

One of the better classes I had  my first semester.  I go and ask her questions about homework, exams and she is willing to answer all my questions.  She gives extra credit if you show up and answer clickers questions  during lecture.   I did learn a lot in this class even though it's just a requirement for my engineering major.

I liked Tina when she was giving lecture, and that's it. Her tests are very tough, makes students to memorize lecture slides.. NOTE FOR THOSE WHO ARE TAKING CHEM 102, THERE'S NO CURVE FOR THE EXAMS IF YOU TAKE TINA HUANG'S CLASS.

Be prepared to read the book and ask questions to your TA because she usually doesn't finish her powerpoint slides on time by the end of the lecture. However, she's generous with bonus points, test retakes, and removing your worst quiz grades, so there is time to improve your grade!

Some topics are harder than other in this course.  Some are covered more in-depth than others.  Need to be prepared for lecture, I get more out of it when I read ahead and do a couple of problems before going to class.  The final exam is easier than the regular exams.  Overall good.

Lots of work in this class, there are weekly quizzes and homework, plus problems that she assigns.  I really dislike the online homework and quizzes.  She is helpful if you go to office hours, but sometimes it  gets crowded and have to wait your turn.  Like the other said, do the exam retake, that's what saved my grade.

Agreeable professor. Exams are easy. A is easy only if you go over assigned pages of textbook and do exercises in textbook. Online homework and quiz are not tough. The largest advantage of her class is retake exam. Good stuff.

Her tests were pretty straightforward and she could help clear things if you come to her office hours. But sometimes during lecture she leaves some things unclear and moves on to the next slide without really explaining it in detail. Remember to revisit her slides before the exams.
She has the occasional jokes and is serious at times, if you manage to stay awake in class you will be good to go.

I don't know how I made it through this class without Dr. Huang's help.  I was ready to drop the class after I failed the first exam, but she gave me a pep talk and gave me some advice about how to study. Definitely need to do a lot of work outside class or it's impossible to pass the class.

Chemistry is a hard class with any teacher, but Huang made it a little easier. I would suggest her over all the other chemistry teachers. Just make sure you study the test reviews and go in for help for topics you don't understand. You'll thank yourself later.
She was a great teacher. She wanted to keep her students involved and she was really easy to talk to during office hours.
Good overall. It's chemistry, so it's not easy.  The pace is a lot faster than than high school .  Can't wait till the last minute to study, I found out the hard way.

She is decent. I took her class with barely any chemistry knowledge. She is okay with presenting the material but tends to only give easy examples of problems and such you will be facing. She is tough but if you taken AP Chem in high school you will be fine. For those who do not study hard. Use your book and if you got a good TA you are in luck.

I had a borderline grade, she bumped me up because I did well on the last exam and the final, and I went to all lectures.  Dr. Huang was helpful in answering my questions, but don't wait till the right before the exam to go to office hours, I had to wait to ask my questions since there were many people. I would recommend her.
She is very helpful and smart!
Dr. Tina Huang is the easiest chem 102 professor. She explains the material fairly well and is very warm and helpful.
Her exams isn't too bad if you just do the problems similar to her practice tests. She gives these clicker points for credit, so just show up to class.

I also don't understand the negative ratings. Dr. Huang makes every effort to help her students. I didn't mind her explanations because it is clear and simple. Sometimes she takes too long to answer questions.You do need to be prepare yourself to do a lot of outside work. Like others said, do the book problems and go see her during office hours.

Tina is probably the best prof for this class but she doesn't really take the time to make sure you know they material.  She goes over it once, not very clearly, and then moves on.  Obviously the material builds on itself so it gets tough and her exams and usually confusing and tough.

I am a health profession major, and was afraid of chemistry.  But Dr. Huang was very good in helping me understand.  I didn't have problems understanding her lectures.  I would recommend that you go see her office  hours regularly, so if you need a letter recommendation, she can provide one for you.
Pretty mediocre and it didn't help that we just watched her lectures live-streamed.

Almost every week, Huang makes mistakes. She is a bit clueless when it comes to teaching. She was also proud to say that the class had a D average on an exam. Her office hours were not helpful at all.
She is not very helpful and her ability to explain question is not very good. I won't take her class any more

Dr. Huang is amazing and undoubtedly my favorite professor. The class is pretty hard but that's expected from a nationally ranked chem program that's filled with so many chem/ biological engineers. She always makes time for her students and gives weekly planners with suggested hw problems alongside the chapter readings. I definitely recommend her.

I just took her final exam, and while it was really really hard, but since it will be curved, I think I will be OK.  DO go to her office hours and ask for help, she is willing to help me improve my exam scores, but you need to do a lot of work outside class.  Sometimes she gets sidetracked with her stories, but good overall.
Dr. Huang does not explain concepts very well. Her exams are fairly difficult and she makes them like that on purpose. If you know some Chemistry going into the class you should do ok.

Dr. Huang is amazing! I don't know why her rating is so low! She truly cares about students! Go to office hours!She is fantastic in office hours! She will spend 60 minutes on one question if that is what it takes for you to understand it! Plus she is very personable! I go in there to just talk ab out life sometimes. Wonderful professor!

This is the second time I am taking the class, I had to drop it last semester because I had Jamieson and I didn't understand a lot of the materials covered in his lectures.  I took her section this time because my friend had her last year.  She is very helpful if you go see her during her office hours.
really cool and funny. the exams are exactly like the practice ones. in class she's sometimes difficult to understand, but she will clarify extremely well if you go to office hours

great professor! she's so funny, lots of demos, makes early lectures fun. friendly, answers questions well during office hrs, but not well during lectures. she teaches the same general topics from high school, but newer (specific) material. WARNING: exams are VERY SIMILAR to practice exams. study those like crazy.

It is a harder class than my high school chem for sure. I personally find her easy to understand, some of my friends thought she should've done more problems in class, I just go to her office hours to get the help.  She pretty much tells you what you need to know to do well, so I have no complaints.

While she is very knowledgeable and knows what she's talking about, she cannot teach to save her life. Very unclear and spends less time on hard concepts than on easy ones. Also has some very annoying habits. Would not recommend and will not be taking another one of her classes!!!

Some people might think she does a lot demos in the class.but so what? That is useless for the exam. Also, in her class, she just mentions surperfacial knowledge about the topic. And her explanations are keeping saying"right?" without solving it. I won't sellect her class again.

She keeps lectures interesting with experiment examples and is a very likeable person.  Keeping up with the assigned textbook reading and problems can really help when it comes to exam time. And make sure to do the practice exams because they are very similar to the actual tests.
She makes an effort to keep classes interesting and she teaches very well. Go to all the lectures, discussions and office hours. They can save you from the slightly trickier questions on exams.
She really knows the material she teaches. She also has a lot of charisma and is a pretty funny Asian.

She is a very likeable professor and keeps lectures interesting. She assigns online homework and a quiz once a week that is either in discussion or online. Test questions can be tricky so you will need to fully understand the topics. She is extremely helpful in office hours, so definitely go and she will walk you through the questions you have.

Although Huang is new, she seems to be the best general chemistry teacher at UIUC to have. Going to both lecture and discussion is very helpful. The class covers a lot of material and there are some trick questions on exams, so understanding all the examples presented in lecture & in worksheets is important.

She is a very meticulous and clear lecturer, though her handwriting is sometimes hard to read. It's a naturally hard subject and you have to read the lecture notes and Taylor on your own to get the full picture. Homework load is not too big, and exams are very fairly designed and timed. Office hours are a blessing. If you have her you'll be fine.
Hard lectures
Great professor, great lecturer, very nice and caring towards her students, and always happy to talk.
Material was difficult, but her lectures were well paced and very clear. I really felt like I learned a lot in this class.

By far the worst physics class I have taken, tried rewatching lectures for notes and studying but the recording is facing away from the board. Also sometimes hard to understand and very blackboard heavy; derivations would be online though. Needs some time to figure out the class structure

I took General Relativity with her ( a 500 level course, she is an expert in this). I highly recommend her. The lecture notes were helpful, lectures were clear. A lot of homework but she is available during office hours. She's passionate and caring . Ignore the 101 reviews, professors do not choose the rules in these basic courses

This course was taught almost entirely by lecture but she was clear and easy to understand. The exams are easy if you do and understand the homework, and two of them offered bonus points. I would have liked more rigor with some of the derivations, it was easy to get a bit confused at times.

If you have the option to take a high level PHYS class w/ Prof Witek, I highly recommend her. The lecture notes were extremely helpful, lectures were clear, and tests sometimes had extra credit problems. The class is homework heavy but she is available with office hours. She's a passionate and caring professor, disregard the low level PHYS reviews.

She is a very good professor and constantly stays mindful of those who have questions or need clarification in her class. This class specifically is very difficult in terms of content. It is hard to say if it was difficult because of her teaching or content itself. Her office hours were very helpful. She gave a lot of opportunity for extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Didn't give good lectures for such a difficult class

terrible professor  terrible class. if you're into failing this is the class for you. not an easy class to drop either because it's 5 credits. it will ruin your GPA. every week kids from my lab and discussion groups drop out. the class is an absolute mess. exams are graded unfairly as well. if you miss 1 question, the grade can drop to a B.

It is her first year here but to be honest, no one cares. Gen Ed or not, we want to be able to succeed in the classroom and did not do a very good job of that. Her TA's were better than her. Lecture size decreased from 200+ to almost 10 by the end of the year. She also lies about the Exam Scores, probably cause she doesn't want to expose herself.
Awful professor, genuinely did not watch one lecture because they confused everyone even more. 7 people would show up to her 200+ person lecture. Self-taught myself all material.

This was Prof. Witek first year at the university, which I'd like to give her lenience for, but college is hard enough, especially physics, and is why I can't rank her higher. I believe she's made the class harder than it should be. Lectures were pretty useless and being self taught is the only way to go for this course. Tests also try to trick you
The WORST. Doesn't even know her own material.

The lectures are useless, the exams are almost weekly with additional weekly quizzes, students aren't given a break from discussion on exam weeks, some discussion "review" sections are completely worthless because they take place after the exam, and the grading scale is shifted to make getting a good grade even more difficult. 0/5 horrible class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This woman is incompetent and a horrible teacher. Do not take Phys 101 with her unless you want to fail. The lectures do not help at all and you learn more in the prelectures than you do in actual lectures. The tests are online in the CBTF and are quite unfairly weighted. If you have to take it with her good luck.

I had him as an advisor. He wasn't great help. I almost didn't graduate on time because of him. I had to go to a different random counselor to put me on course. He's a bit unmotivated to help a student out.

Learned so much but grading criteria is super subjective and unclear. Lots of essays and writing. feedback on these did not make sense. Honestly offended that my letter grades on essay exams averaged to C+. I ended up getting an A- on my official grade so I guess his grading system (based on accumulation of points) was clutch.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I sincerely did not enjoy this course. grading procedures are very unclear. No rubrics he archrive the class before all grades were posted.  very rude and condescending in class. I truly wish I didn't have to take another course by him again. His lecture sides are very confusing and not formatted well at all. and he assigns five 50 page readings/wk
I've never had this professor, but he was rude to me on an email, and I did not appreciate it.

Tough class, but worth it if you engage with the content. Lectures can drag, but the professor is knowledgeable. Consider using Unstuckstudy alongside the free textbook to prepare - it helped me stay on top of things.

I had him as an advisor, and not a professor, but I imagine his advisement carries over to his teaching. I had a terrible experience working with him. He would force me into classes I did not want to take, he was very rude would mock me for my questions, and would straight up tell me false information about classes. Talk to someone else.
if you have tripophobia do NOT take this class!

If you expect a hard, but straightforward class this is not it. Joseph leaves all of the grading to his ta and is too lazy to make sure his ta even grades it. His ta decided not to grade the midterm or second paper, just submitted them as zeros expecting me not to pay attention, but hey neither did joe.

If you're going through these reviews and thinking that you'll be able to handle his class, good luck Charlie. I had that confidence too. There's supposed to be 8 modules and we had 5. He expected us to complete 2 essays and a presentation without putting up the remaining modules, so we contacted someone above him. Instead, final's 45% of our grade
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Incredibly condescending and demeaning.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Lacks empathy.

The nicest professor that I have ever had. However, his tangents and overuse of unnecessary words made the class drag on. When this course switched to online only (COVID-19), he was very accommodating. He is very knowledgeable about what he is teaching, but teaches as if he expects his students to be at his level of expertise, for better or worse.
The lectures are extremely heavy and he refuses to share his ppt. Brutal towards grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Odd mix, but overall a good one. Because there is no disc. sect for PS 318, many of the questions on reading happen in lecture, and can get awkwardly silent when no one can answer them. Tests essay based, but he gives you the selections ahead of time to prepare. Genuinely caring, promotes a good understanding of topics, but weird lecture structure

He's an animated professor, but comes off as condescending imo. He seems to really care and encourages students to visit him during office hours. Tons of readings (100+ pages per week), lectures are disorganized (no powerpoints; he goes on tangents), grading is fair (2 midterms + final w/ cheat sheets, paper, attendance). Material is really dry
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Requires lots of reading: different articles/books every week. Wrote about 4 short essays and one term paper. No quizzes and only one test (final). He is a nice guy who is open to suggestions and helping you if you ask. Overall he is not a tough grader and this class is not hard to pass if you do put in some work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS

reviews.comment: Very practical- if you need an extension because you were lazy, he will grant that. If you need extra help, he is willing to help. However, his grader (at least for PS 321 Fall '16) was not as lenient. Hinchliffe's personality as a professor puts you at ease and then you receive your first homework  grade. Don't play yourself- put in enough work!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I'd like to keep it short.. As much as i like his personality and kindness, he is the worst instructor i've ever had in my entire life.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Such a boring teacher. Asks questions that have no answer and if they do, you have to be a mind reader. Soooo much homework for a 1-credit class. Wouldnt recommend this class to my worst enemy. He is an eccentric teacher but will talk on and on about vague and abstract ideas which dont help you at all for the papers he assigns.

PS 220 wasn't an entertaining or fun class. He droned on and on and just laughed at his own jokes. However, he's also an adviser who tries to get everyone to not go into law. I don't listen to him when he tells me to give up on law school, but I wish he would help students rather than making them feel dumb and incapable of succeeding.
Lectures were incredibly boring and you have to work to get a good grade in this class. He uses powerpoints for every lecture and it was difficult to stay interested.

Hinchliffe said that he has not taught this class in awhile so I'm assuming it can only get better. The class lectures can be long, the readings are pretty interesting. Overall, you should do fine as long as you're somewhat interested in Public Policy.

I took his 101 class my freshman year and had him as an advisor. He told me to change my career path my first semester of freshman year. I did not listen to him and I changed advisors. I now have an internship at a law firm where the partners have agreed to hire me in three years once I pass the BAR. Point is DO NOT LET THIS MAN KILL YOUR DREAMS.

The class content itself is very interesting which is why I chose to take the class. If you're looking for a blow-off class, this is not for you. Long 120 minute lectures plus reading assignments that can be up to 120 pages + is not my idea of a blow-off. It isn't impossible to pass though as long as you go to class and do the readings.

So I'm a freshman who took his PS 101 class. READ EVERYTHING HE GIVES AND SHOW UP TO LECTURE. That is what I learned this past semester. This class is serious, but incredibly interesting. I say read because his slides give  brief overview over what you had to read. Political Science is no joke and I think that's what he's trying to show.
Worst instructor ever! He is rude, selfish and arrogant!
I don't know why everyone here hates Hinchliffe so much. I think he's a super nice and helpful guy. I enjoyed 321, and I think he's a great advisor.

Not at all surprised by the other comments- this guys likes to smile while actually being rude to students.  Not a pleasant experience working with him, not just for his attitude but also his ability to effectively do his job as counselor to the students who pay his wage.

How did this man become the director of undergrad polisci studies? Inconsiderate, patronizing, so pretentious, and a lazy advisor. The other two advisors are way more interested and personable. They should stage a coup.

Ok for all the PS101 posters and rookie PS majors: PS is a major that has LOTS of reading as a Junior I have 600 pages a week typically. (I don't read all of it) So the level of reading in PS101 is typical. PS-322 was an amazing class, not hard, although I know of some people who struggled CAUSE THEY DONT READ ESSAY PROMPTS lol. Hinchlife is awsome
Just do the work and study and you'll get an A. Everyone is exaggerating. However, you do have to put a little more effort than you would expect to get an A.

Professor Hinchliffe is a great professor with high exoextations! Although this class is difficult, I've gained allot from the experience. This class is here to get rid of all the Poli Sci major students who don't really have a passion for Political Science. Just do the readings+practice time management. I'm continuing my with my major!

I was very interested in politics before this arrogant man ruined it. Want to ruin your gpa? Take his class. I am now changing my major thanks to this pathetic man. Oh, and as an advisor he is useless.

Absolutely horrible class, demanding 3 hours A DAY for this class, assigning articles with 8 point font and with 20 pages, and expecting it to be done by the next day for another article in preparation for his lectures and the test. I came hoping to do well in PoliSci as I love it, but this professor and class turned me away from it.

I understood this professor was challenging. I was eager for the opportunity to prove myself and learn a lot. Looking back, I regret it. The class was a headache and uninteresting. Yes, there are challenging courses that can be quite gratifying, this is not one of them. Unrealistic reading assignments, annoyingly over-animated powerpoints.
He really isnt as bad as everyone says. If you do the readings (there are a lot) you will have no problems on tests. For papers just make you sure you follow instructions. You should be fine.

Absolutely horrid. My first polysci class, 101 in fact, and the course work equals about a 300-level class. Lucky to be even getting a C, seriously. Essays along with 80-100 pages of reading back to back. Multiple choice tests with nothing covered during class. STAY AWAY from this class, unless it's mandatory...
If you want to keep your GPA high, avoid this professor's classes. you will be screwed for sure

This class turned me away from Political Science. It is by far the most demanding class and it will kill your GPA. He is a nice guy, but not too helpful. If you don't have at least 2hrs a night to spend on this class THEN GET OUT WHILE YOU CAN! Good luck everyone, pray for that 70%.

NO, DON'T,RUNAWAY! I am praying on my hands and knees for a 70.00 in this class, all of the other students pretty much feel this same way. I have never in my life never understood a word the teacher was saying, and I went in for help many times. THIS WILL KILL YOUR GPA. Please listen, I wish I had. He never said anything constructive and definite.
Hinchliffe is a cool guy as a friend, just like to chill... but as a professor, he sucks. You pretty much have to do all the studying by yourslef, so BUY and READ the book.

Professor Hinchliffe expects a lot out of his students. His lectures can be confusing, and he uses material on the exam that was not covered in class. However, he challenges his students, and if you do not mind having to work hard, then his class should benefit you in some way.

As an advisor, simply not helpful at all. Outwardly discourages students from pursuing competitive careers and is incapable of accommodating students needs if it requires he step outside his contrived thought process. If you plan on going into law or a Ph.D. program another advisor will be a thousand times more accommodating, helpful and insightful
essay questions very wordy; uses questions at 200 level. Does not necessarily use information discussed in class on tests.

A difficult teacher and not one recommended for froshoman to take, unless you want an difficult 101 class change sections. That being said, he is very knowledgeable of the material and can usually be pursuaded into giving aid. He makes the class a lot harder than other sections, but in the end the extra practice prepares you for harder classes.

Unit 1 is tough but it gets easier, I promise! He is VERY passionate about politics and it shows in his lectures, very energetic and wants his students to love politics. Going to lecture is very important and so is doing the reading. Tough but manageable and interesting.
Does Not teach well; does not use material on tests talked about in lecture

Very kind man who is extremely intelligent and helpful if you see  him outside of class. Unfortunately, lectures are incredibly dry and the tests are Very difficult- half M.C. half essay... essays usually are fine( broad topics) but the multiple choice is very tricky you need to read the book with a comb to get all of those correct.
Dr Hinchcliffe has a great passion for Political Science. He expects too much on written essays though. If you put in a ton of work into the class you shall do ok.

He's really not as bad as everyone says he is, but I still wouldn't recommend the class. The readings are heavy, boring, difficult, and unfortunately essential. If you study hard, are dedicated, and know your stuff you'll probably end up with a B+ or an A-. There are other PS 101 classes that are easier and more enjoyable though...

At the beginning of the semester I wanted to drop this class.  But as long as you do the textbook reading, study for the possible essay questions, and go to lecture, you can get a decent grade.  I got an A-, which is proof that he does give As.

I'm not sure why he gets such a bad rap. He's accessible and he challenges his students but wants them to succeed. It's true that his lectures are sometimes boring and there is a ridiculous amount of reading with which we did nothing, but overall a good introduction to American politics. For the most part, it's a pretty easy class.
i would advise taking another political science class. i learned a lot, but it takes up too much time.
I was an idiot for taking this stupid class. I could have chosen so many better PS classes to take that would have been easier and more worthwhile. My suggestions: do the online readings.
he is a nice professor and offers a lot of extra credit. it is a lot of work and it is important to keep up but worthwhile. Focus on the online readings he gives, they are key to the midterm

It is true that he teaches this class as if it were a 200 level, but just do what is asked and you'll do well.  He gives you three tries on quizzes and gives about ten extra credit chances, so getting an A is not hard.  He's friendly and approachable and gives you every opportunity to succeed.
Hincliffe has a twin..and his name is Satan.

I took this class as a Gen-Ed. Bad mistake.  As an engineering student i have enough on my plate than to have to read 2 hours each night for this class not to mention that his classes don't prepare you at all.  If you like learning based on reading, you might this class. Other than that, good luck.

Wow this class is horrible. I am not exaggerating but like seriously you might want to try another professor because this guy makes this class almost impossible. It is supposed to be a P.S. class but it seems like a 200 course. I just freaking took U.S. Gov (H) last year and i did very well in it. This guy makes me depressed.
P.S. the syllabus is 18 pages long!!! AND HE COPY-RIGHTED IT!!!

This guy is my adviser and after 3 tries, including a shouting match with the professor, he still doesn't know my name! I have zero idea what the hell he is talking about. He seems to be very biased. Only he would be pretentious enough to record himself explaining how to read a 18th century document and proceed to play it in front of the class.
The most compelling questions this man is capable of raising is how he was hired and why hasn't he been fired.

He likes to give you way more work than necessary, like having to prepare for 16 essays for the final exam.  This class takes a lot of work, but can be interesting when you are reading by yourself...not in class though.  Go to the TA's..they're the best source of help and the only way to understand what is going on.

Ill make this short. Horrible. The man is incapable of listening to anybody but himself.  Completely narrow minded and useless for help.  What's really disturbing is the fact that a professor with only 3 positive reviews of nearly 60 could not only avoid termination, but rise to become the director of undergrad polysci studies.
I actually liked him a lot.  He was really friendly and helpful if you wanted the help.  If you work semi-hard this class is a breeze.
On the first day of class he shook everyone's hand except mine. It made me feel uneasy about him and the class
Don't take this class! If this is not a required course, don't take it.  You have to give this class 100% of your time if you want an A or B. Not worth it.  Take another gen-ed or Prof.

Possibly the worst teacher ever. Useless in lectures. Useless in office hours. He's not a professor, just a staff member (undergrad adviser), and now I know why. The worst professor is better than him. Plus, he is not a great adviser either (but that is a different topic)! Take someone else. Anybody else is better.

You guys are all right. This class is unneccesarily difficult. How could any 100 level course be so hard? His class was a real pain in my neck. And when writing papers he has so many expectations and demands. Its his way or the highway. I STRONGY suggest taking someone else's PS 101 course.
Took this freshman year. The man is hard to understand, tests are bogus. If you plan on slacking off this really is not the course for you.
This class was much harder than the major specific PS 101 class that my friend took.  Joe expects a lot out of the students and doesn't feel that anyone should get an A.

I hardly need to add to what other people have said... but its all true. This class is nuts. He lectures you like a high school student... and then tests you like a grad student. Advice: Make sure that you understand the ridiculous amount of online reading before the exams...or, even better, take someone else's PS 101 class.

The professor is helpful--he expects more out of the students than just a memorization of the topics. The class is not difficult if effort is put into it~ just take it as it is. I had it my freshmen year and it basically served as a wake up call that college is not going to be a repeat of highschool. Go to office hours, TA's are useless.

Joseph Hinchliffe smacks with such blatant arrogance that he believes that, should someone (god forbid) get an A in his class, that it is too easy.  Maybe that is why answers on tests deviate by the most minute measure, and his lectures jump from topic to unrelated topic.  The workload is akin to law school, and even doing it won't assure you an A.

He's a nice person, but there is a lot of work for his class.  The examples he's shown us of A papers and D papers look almost the same to me.  I've always gotten straight A's and I can't figure out what he wants.  If you ask for help, he'll talk to you, but you still never really know what he wants.  He says his class is the most important 1 here.

Makes this class harder than it needs to be. Not very helpful when I went in to office hours. Definitely should not have gotten the grade I did for how hard I studied. I put a lot of time and effort into this class and still couldn't pull an A.  Sometimes he was just totally unfair.
Worst.  Professor.  Ever.
Never take that class.
Joe Hinchliffe sucks. I'm a straight A students and had to work my ass off to get a B. Stay away from this man. He is Satan.
The class is rediculous.  Understandable that there is a lot of information to cover in a short amount of time, but it is crazy the way that Joe teaches.  Very unreachable and the T.A.'s are NO help.

This class is hard because the essays/ papers are graded by different TAs.  They all grade differently, so there is no way of knowing what they want.  My friend had her bro. who is in law school here help with her papers- and she still got C's!!  He is not of much help even if you go in to ask for it, and it is hard for a 100 level
worst class ever.  DO NOT TAKE PS101 WITH PROF. HINCHLIFFE. YOU WILL REGRET IT!
He would've got 0's all across the board had they been offered.

What in the heck is the deal with the class.  We take online quizzes that count for 10% of our grade but we have to read one hundred pages of material to take it and he asks the most detailed questions.  This is introductory and he doesn't test over stuff he lectures on in class.  Stay away it at all possible.
Dont take any of his classes, total Pric!

The difficulty of the class is extremly high. I always thought 100 level courses were of reasonable difficuly. He proved me wrong. Do not under any circumstances enroll in this class unless you are opposed to a 4.0 GPA, because I guarantee you will not get it.
