He does have a thick accent but the lectures and slides are pretty easy to comprehend. He did offer extra credit when midterms came around and is a nice guy overall.
can't understand him

Worst professor I have ever had, should not even be a professor, MR. IVANOV, would make the DRES exam harder than other normal exams, and have the thickest accent that makes u think he is teaching in full Russian, torturing both TA and STUDENTS.

Although Professor Ivanov does have a thick accent and is hard to understand, his notes are very clear and full of good information. His exams on the other hand aren't the most difficult so if you study well you'll do better than your friends with other professors. Another good thing is the extra credit there is lots of it! Overall not the worst.

As it has been said by many others already, Professor Ivanov has a very thick accent that can make him difficult to understand at times, especially when his microphone cuts out and no one decides to mention it to him. However, his notes were very clear and helpful for completing homework and studying for exams and he gave a lot of extra credit.

Professor Ivanov was an incredible professor. He was a little difficult to understand in lectures, but his notes were immaculate, and his reviews reflected the exams. He makes Calc 3 intuitive and gives a lot of EC. 10/10

He copies all his notes from a different professor; none of it is his. He is very passive-aggressive and honestly doesn't like being wrong. I find it unforgiving that he makes the DRES exams way harder than the regular ones just because they have more time to take the test, but that beats the whole purpose of getting the accommodations.

First time teaching MATH 241 I believe or in a long time, HAS A THICK ACCENT which makes it hard to understand, however the lecture notes he posts are super informative. However what I do it is watch professor Leonard then then review the lecture notes. GO to office hours, it helps, also you maybe get extra points for it.

Math 241 broke me. 
I got an A+ in Math 231, but I cried a lot because of this class.  The way it's taught doesn't help at all, and honestly, it left me traumatized. I'd say it's the worst class I've taken at UIUC.
Basically just reads off lecture notes, not necessary to attend class. Can be a little passive aggressive with asking questions. Tests are very similar to reviews.
Good class

Had him during covid. Lecture was online, just read off lecture slides, which was exactly the same as the video lectures he posted on the course webpage. I stopped going to his lectures, so did most of the class. Study on your own for this class. Also, he is unforgiving of deadlines, no matter what the circumstance.

Worst professor I've ever had. His thick accent makes his lectures very difficult to follow. He just reads from the lecture notes and hardly even looks up at the students or does example problems. Exams are pretty straightforward but going to lectures alone won't help. I honestly stopped showing up to them midway through the semester. Avoid!

Math416 is supposed to be a proof based class while he made it more like Math415 which only has some short easy proof. He gives extra credits if you present reviews questions on the blackboard and all exams will be based on the review problems he gives us. So if you want to get easy A then go for him, if you want learn strong proofs then avoid him.

I can't understand his English! And he is not willingly to help you with the homework questions. Basically learn it by yourself. ] He even told his students this is a hard class and if you can't do so just drop it.

I really liked professor Ivanov.  His sense of humor was...weird, but he taught the material decent enough.  The homework was fair and the tests were easy.  Before each test, he would give out ~25 problems and guarantee that the test would be made out of 6 of those only changed slightly. Overall, I learned a lot.  Review sessions are useless though

Did not learn much by going to lecture. Pretty much taught myself through the book. Overall, was not horrible. The homeworks were difficult and so were the tests; however, the final has some problems straight from prior tests and homeworks.
Got easier after alternating groups.

His homework can be pretty tough due to the many proofs, but those are seldom graded. He has quizzes every week, but they can be cancelled at times. Gives bonus points during review sessions, and had a Thanksgiving quiz. Exams are pretty simple. They are pretty similar to the questions he emphasizes during the review and the quizzes
Way too much homework, I spend more time in this class combined than Calc 3 and Physics 211 combined!

She is very mean and snarky, so don't bother asking questions or getting help from her. The homework assignments are worth 55% of your grade and are graded very harshly (1-5 points off for each simple mistake). Homeworks will not be graded if not named correctly during submission. The final project will take over 30 hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: she is just extremely mean with how she answers questions for no reason.
This course is the most frustrating course during your 4 year college experience. Trust.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Enough HW to last a lifetime and horrendous curriculum. Going to Dagestan would be more productive and more fun.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Lectures are fast and direct and require prior familiarity with 3D modeling and computer-based design. I had to spend extra time outside of class to keep up with the CAD material. The workload is high for a 2-credit course (about 6–8 hours per week), and the textbook is required for assignments.

Often comes off as passive agressive when asked questions. The class isn't super difficult, but it's very demotivating especially when your hard work isn't graded due to your homework files being slightly different from naming conventions. Work with other students and this course will be much easier. Final project is difficult, so start early.

For 2 credit hours, this was the class that took most of my time. HWs are graded harshly, and lectures are not helpful. She is kind during office hours, but most of my questions weren't answered well. Most of my CAD understanding came from HW, the final project, and asking TAs for help.
it does not have to be bad, but it is
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Yes I got an A+ in her class despite despising doing every morsel of work Laystrom-Woodard assigned. I don't think I've met a teacher (she's not a professor, even though she addresses herself as such) who has the ego that one would expect from an investment banker. Pray that her and her graders don't hate you and you will (somehow) survive.

god forbid you ask a question in this class. She works as an instructor part time, and could clearly not care less about her students. This is a 2 credit hour class but the workload is the same as a 4 hour class, and just as challenging. for ppl taking her class: pray. If you're not religious, pick a religion and pray.

Course with the least credit hours im taking this semester yet the one I spend the most time doing homework for. Lectures often aren't that helpful and a majority of the homework is out of a textbook you must buy (yet sometimes they post the instructions on PDF's so idk why we must by the textbook). Homework is graded way to harshly
not a great teacher
horrible, please skip if u can, save yourself

For a college professor, her knowledge of CAD is subpar at best. She's a little passive-aggressive but not rude. The HW and final project are ridiculously time-intensive, especially for a 2 credit hr class, but aren't difficult. TA's grade HW, not her, but her grading guidelines are fairly harsh. Easy midterm quiz. Sometimes gives candy and snacks.
Awful no feedback completely disregarded part of the final project in terms of grade. Super slow grading and will not help at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lowest credit hour class, most time consuming. Homework is graded very hard weeks later so you can't fix mistakes until it's too late. She clearly dosnet want to teach and treats us as such. Rude when you ask questions. Shaky knowledge on the software used. Final project does not develop actual skills. Most widely despised professor I know of.

She's a very tough grader, files are not graded if they're slightly off from the naming convention. You can see her expression sour when a student asks a question. The homework and the instructions can have errors but if the file name is off, your hard work is not even considered. I dreaded going to the class and was afraid to ask a question.

Do not ask questions to her, she will eat you alive in front of the whole class. Might be the worst professor/teacher I've had in my life. Makes no exceptions for students that are sick. Single handedly made a fun class miserable. Most people in the aerospace department have these thoughts on her too. It speaks volumes when everyone thinks this.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Gives out too much homework, and grades the homework too harshly. Doesn't know the software she teaches, and is annoyed at anyone who can't follow along or has a question.
Extremely incompetent as a teacher. Doesn't know the content that she's teaching. Whenever a student asked a question, she would give a very vague answer while being extremely passive aggressive.

Easily provoked and very passive-aggressive. She's rude to anyone with a question, especially if its a simple one, and is constantly on a power trip. She isn't as bad a people say at teaching CAD, although definitely not good. The class is unnecessarily difficult, and final project sucks, pick something easy to CAD. Character limit saves this rant.

Very rude professor. Go to office hours if you want to be disrespected. Homework is unnecessarily difficult and graded weeks later so feedback is useless. Final project is way harder than what she teaches in class, and she isn't good at teaching the material anyways. If you're in aerospace, you must take this class with her anyways, so good luck.

One of the most disrespectful, boring instructors I've ever had. Don't ask her questions or else she will give you a passive-aggressive, dull response. Office hours are completely useless. Homework makes up a large portion of the grade so ask the course assistants for help. Final project is torture and the course doesn't prepare you well for it.

Shows zero respect for her students, so I have zero respect for her. She is very rude and dismissive. Unless you are put on earth with flawless CAD skills you'll never get 100% on homework, partially because she grades so harshly and partially because she doesn't teach it (and frankly doesn't know it either).

She's the only professor so don't have many options. She's not bad at lecturing but is often passive aggressive when asked questions. The homeworks are graded weeks later so feedback doesn't help and basically impossible to get a 100. Did I learn CAD, yes. Did she also refuse to answer questions because she doesn't teach it in the course, also yes.

This class was way harder than it needed to be. The homework would take me 6+ hours. She also was not willing to help. For instance, someone would ask "If I deleted this zip file would it delete all my files" and her response would be "You're an engineer, figure it out." And the homework was graded SO HARSHLY it felt impossible to ever get a 100.

Lectures are slow and painful. Do not ask questions unless you want to be told off. Do not submit homework late. Homework is unnecessarily long, due every week, and makes up a large portion of the final grade. Material isn't hard, ask other students for help and you'll pass. (AE140)
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures are excruciatingly slow, she doesn't know a lot of stuff beyond what she teaches, not really knowledgeable when answering questions either.  But she is (to my knowledge) the only one who teaches the CAD class, so it is what it is.  She doesn't get as angry as other people have suggested.

Very rude to students in class, berates students for asking questions and getting them incorrect, homework and project deadlines aren't flexible, she isn't up to date with most recent NX softwares and criticizes students for not knowing her methods
She is not approachable, and will make you feel bad for asking questions. The lectures go extremely slowly and does not prepare you for the final project.

She clearly outlines criteria and was very accessible outside of class. I like that she does not simply give you answers on homework but helps you change your perspective which can help a lot. Just be sure to stay on top of homework and you will learn CAD in no time.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the worst prof I've ever had, seems to be learning the content as she teaches despite having taught the same course for several years. Generally very strict, unaccessible, and goes on power trips especially if you question any of her decisions.
Make sure to double check homework if you want a good grade. Very accessible and open outside of class, just go to office hours. As long as you followed guidelines, getting an A is pretty easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very Very tough grader combined with a hesitancy to help students makes the class difficult. Whenever I asked a question she would respond harshly and treat me as if I wasn't listening instead of actually helping me.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She's caring and nice, but is also kind of strict. I didn't think the class was too hard, but I speak as someone who already knew CAD prior; this class may not be an easy A if you don't know much about CAD. Homework and exams are graded harshly, but the final project is generally much more lenient. Just don't procrastinate!
The very worst prof ive ever witnessed at UIUC.

It wasn't how she strutted across the class questioning the authority of the ethnicities 
of each student, nor how she made our TA quit and cry whilst filming her, nor especially how fundamentally arbitrary her grading guidelines were, but it was how she completely shifted my perspective on what good and bad “teachers” look like. I pity her.
The course is really interesting, though topics are kind of sorrowful. I didn't realize how the native Americans' lives was, but now I have a clear thought.

She has a very hard time staying focused during her lectures. She will often go on tangents during lectures, resulting in a major loss of class time. Her grading is horrible and she feels that she gives you the materials for success, but she doesn't. Do not take her classes, and if you have to.... good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: wouldn't recommend

Too many weekly essays that started to feel like busy work rather than actual learning. Because there was always one due, I often completed them just to finish. The study guide did not fully reflect the exam, which made studying difficult given how much content there was. Even my engineering classes prepared students more clearly for exams.
Gives alot of busy work not related to test or lecture material. Wouldve ben a great class if the assignmnets actually related to anything on the exams.

I took PS304 with James (only let me put 303). Don't listen to the negative feedback, I almost dropped PS304 bc these reviews stressed me out, but it turned out to be one of my favorite PS classes. James is understanding, a fair grader, and very knowledgeable. His lectures were very interesting, he's kind to his students, and wasn't hard to get A.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I wouldn't recommend this class. There's way too much work, he tries to cover too much content, and the exams are a nightmare that are barely related to the lectures. Also didn't help that he got rude and condescending anytime I asked a question or for help on an assignment. Do yourself a favor, take a different class with a better instructor.

This review is for PS 101. Fairly easy class with relatively easy exams and papers, but requires a LOT of reading. Minutiae from readings WILL pop up on exams, so read and take notes! Papers are interesting and fun to complete. Lectures more-or-less summarize the reading (but still do the readings!). Responsive to feedback and forgiving.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Caring

reviews.comment: OK I am not going to stand for the Steur hate. He gave us all a free 5% on our exam because he knew we were all very anxious and he is already a tough grader so this was WILD. Steur is a friend to his students. Everyone give this guy a chance his homework is purposeful and lectures are good and full of content. ACTUALLY READ THE TEXTBOOK NEXT TIME!

I do all the homework, put my best effort in, go to class, take really good notes, am organized with my work, participate, basically everything you should do for a class and still am beating my head a against a wall.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: I didn't think that Professor Steur's class was as bad as many people have been rating it. As expected for every class, as long as you do the assigned readings, show up to class, and complete the assignments with reasonable effort, the class wasn't overly difficult. Boring at times, but he seemed like he cared about feedback and improving his class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: If you do the readings and engage in lecture you should be totally fine. I could do with less papers but they aren't bad. Steur is good and grades the writing assignments pretty fairly. The exams can be hard but if you have a decent understanding of the readings and practice your AP testing skills from high school you will be more than fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor Steur is really caring and supportive of his students. There's a lot of assignments that were not worth a lot, so there's less pressure on everything. The material was very boring to me but he's great at showing videos, making jokes, and fostering discussion to make class more interesting. Not a super challenging class if you do the work.

super accommodating with deadlines and values supporting his students. a LOT of small assignments that stack up and is busy work. class is kind of theory/memorization heavy but if you study textbook you will get an a
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--So many papers--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This is a content heavy class with numerous papers and difficult exams. Nonetheless, he always asks for feedback and prioritizes student needs. He has given us many different study materials and is always lenient with extensions. Anybody willing to work hard who has a genuine interest in the class should take the other reviews with a grain of salt.

I took PS303 this semester and although it is a bit heavy in terms of amount of assignments, Professor Steur is very caring about his students and very accommodating. Lectures were always well organized and interesting. Exams are a bit difficult but if you go to class and read the textbook and study a bit, you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: he needs to change this class completely. I am regretting this as my first 300 level PS class because he leaves a bad impression on the department. He assigns way to much without consideration that people have more than just his class and he seems to not like his students and it is so apparent that his students don't like him
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Do not take this class. James has made us take so many surveys on how this class can be improved, and he basically is taking our feedback as a joke. Given, he is funny, his tests are no joke. He expects us to know EVERYTHING from the textbook and lectures, and then proceeds to make the essay questions on the most niche concepts that aren't helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Some of the assignments and lectures were beneficial to learning, however, much of it did not match test content and made exams difficult. There is also a final exam and project due within days of one another, with the project being a large amount of work that shouldn't have been assigned to be due so shortly after other papers.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class is interesting and the assignments throughout the course will be useful if pursuing a career in politics, but the final project paired with the final exam is an EXORBITANT amount of work. While the rest of the class may be useful the final project makes this class a burden and a waste of time. Steur doesn't care about his students at all

the study guides do not help at all. all of the exams were extremely difficult and felt like we covered nothing on them.  every single review session was during a time when most people have class making it even more unaccessible.  the assignments were long and tedious and I felt like I did not gain anything from them.

Worst lecturer in the world. Never puts exam-related content in his lectures and there is a huge paper due every few weeks. The content of the papers are never talked about in lecture and you basically have to teach yourself these concepts. Nice guy but condescending when you try and ask a question.

Super kind and cares a ton about his students and their success. Course content can feel a little bit repetitive from other PS courses but manages to stay interesting. The exams are a little difficult but read the textbook and don't miss class and you'll be fine

Good guy, lots of clear grading criteria. Class involves a group presentation, personal presentation and a midterm/final combo MC/Essay test. Textbook can be found online but is required. Read it and the lectures get repetitive but attendance is required. I enjoyed the class because it was a deep dive into the material and stayed interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: This was the first time Professor Steur taught this course so it was a bit of a trial run, but I think it went really well and will only get better next semester.

Heavy in chapter readings, answer questions for both the reading and quizzes, due Monday mornings. Doctors note when absent, no exceptions. 4 assignments over spring break, 2 don't open until Monday, can't do them early. Only post work by the week, so can't get work done early. Profs are nice, but kinda unorganized. Lectures usually run over time.
The class was structured very well and the teacher was exceptionally kind. I had some trouble with sickness and she was very accommodating and helped me catch up! Great and passionate teacher.

Amazing class. Dr. Jelinek is passionate and cares about what she is teaching and her students. The final project was fun and allowed for a large amount of creativity. All readings were provided and while there was group work, it was clear that you were being graded on what you did rather than what your group did as a whole.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Pretty mid, the material was pretty interesting. There was a lot of group projects.

The class material itself was exceptionally easy and pretty interesting. However, the class seemed always mildly disorganized and was filled with boring group projects. The group projects were particularly frustrating as they relied heavily on getting good group members pulling their weight. She was pretty reasonable when it came to group issues.
ANTH246 was an online course so there wasn't much direct teaching involved, but the class was still fun regardless. No complaints.
This professor cares about your education and does it in a unique style. Prepare to work hard but it will pay off!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor was easy to contact and always asked if any material needed to be clarified. She always encourage students to ask questions and to see her outside of class if they needed extensions or were interested in any topics that were discussed. There are a couple of group projects, however, there is minimal public involved.

If you even remotely like anth this is a very cool class. She genuinely cares about students and teaching the material, and our TA was great. There were some group projects and you definitely need to study for the tests, but it was worth it in my opinion. Pay attention, take notes, and study at least a little bit and you're good. Highly recommend!

Wonderful teacher if you like anth! She's so kind and really cares about students and hopes they do well. Quizzes can be a bit tough, definitely need to study. Requires a textbook but I only read from it once. Two group projects which were kind of annoying, and weekly discussion posts. But lectures are interesting and engaging.

Jelinek was not an active participant in her classes, I did not speak to her or even see her face this entire class, that being said it was online and I did receive an A with minimal effort, so whatever she's doing she must be doing it right!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Partial online and lecture. Taught by TAs. Attendance is not mandatory but there can be surprise extra credits during the discussion section that is only earned if you show up that day. 1 or 2 assignments per week. Easy A.

If the class is taken online, go to the discussion solely for extra credit(which is recommend). The TAs grade your PBLS as if you were majoring in anthropology which isn't fair to a student solely taking the class as a gen ed. The quizzes are very hard, you must read all the reading its not just general information that you can find on the internet
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: simply put, she would not be a prof if this was the average education she received
Not worth taking this class. Quizzes are very difficult and she just reads off slides.

Most boring lectures at UIUC and genuinely does not do anything beyond reading the slides. Lecture quizzes are right after lecture so good luck if you didn't understand a concept or need time to actually digest topics. Don't take this class, esp with this prof

Largely unengaging due to dull lectures and ineffective teaching. After every class, there are Canvas quizzes based directly on the lecture, which can be frustrating since they are strictly right-or-wrong and require immediate mastery of the material. Feels tedious and workload-heavy, and it doesn't meaningfully support learning at the grad level.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The professor does not teach. The course is constructed by student presentations. The assignment instructions are confusing and milestones are impossible to fully complete. Does not learn anything.
The BEST CW professor on campus. Her CW100 put me on the path to declaring a CW minor. Super funny & discussions are never boring.

The one thing about this class and Julie is that participation is key. She is the funniest person I have ever met, while also being caring and giving amazing feedback on your writing. I wish I could have her for every creative writing class I ever take here!

Professor Price is certainly a creative writer and a good one at that. She is very poetic, witty and overall incredibly caring. She gives great feedback and in a very respectful way. I would say that my writing technique has improved after taking this course, 5 stars.
Julie is one of the best professors I have had so far. She is funny and knows how to give you good feedback. After two weeks in CW 100, I declared a CW minor.

Professor Price has a lot of humor and is so understanding of personal circumstances. She allows for revisions up until the end of the semester and gives great and clear feedback on assignments. She passes out paper assignments around 3 weeks before the assignment is due. Gives great examples of topics through other short readings.

Not even done with the semester yet and I already love her. She creates such a good, open, and comfortable environment for the class. Her expectations are fair and you'll do well in her class if you meet them. She's also really funny and approachable.

The best writing professor I've ever had or heard of. Take her nonfiction and trauma writing workshops if you want your life/work to change forever in the best possible way. I couldn't love this woman more.
Take her class. You won't regret it. Get ready to be challenged but leave changed.

If you can manage to find a spot in one of her classes, take it! You won't regret it. She pushes you to write and improve. The energy in her class is amazing and I always looked forward to seeing her. Her feedback is great and she is always willing to hear you out outside of class.

I love her more than life itself. She is so funny and cool and laidback. I really really respect her and look forward to seeing her. I'm definitely not a kissass so this is a big deal coming from me. She is definitely my favorite professor so far. I am gonna try to take as many classes with her as possible because I think she has a lot to teach me.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Julie is and will always be my favorite professor. Her readings, lectures and discussions are wild and interesting, and her feedback is helpful and kind. She really helped improve my writing as a whole, and motivate me to keep going. She's just an amazing person-- I strongly recommend every class she's ever taught!

One of the best professors I've ever had and an even better person. Gives fantastic feedback and really inspires you to want to continue writing. Her class was incredibly therapeutic with how laid-back it was and was something I really looked forward to going to. I'll always be mad that I won't be able to take more of her classes.

ulie Price is the most amazing person on this campus. She is not just a professor. She is a mentor, and an amazing human being. She will not only walk you through every step of writing pieces in narrative fiction, poetry, and creative non-fiction, she also gives you the option to earn back ALL points lost in revision. TAKE CW 100!!

Julie Price is the most wonderful and influential professor I've ever had. She really cares about all of her students and, I believe, would do absolutely anything to see them succeed. She gives wonderful feedback and makes sure you leave her class with better skills than you came in with. She's also hysterical and makes class a lot of fun.

Julie is the only professor at UofI who I really connect with. She is hilarious, dark, a spunky hardass, and more compassionate than any prof I've met. She creates a community vibe in class where people get serious about their writing while still having a lot of fun. She makes you work, but why else should you be at University? Highly recommended.

She's worth taking a class with. You will write many poems, but it's up to you to revise as she doesn't (or didn't in this class) require a portfolio or grade revisions. Strongest rapport I've probably ever felt in a class because she is able to tie everyone together well. Good experience.
Julie is just amazing! She is so chill and insightful! she was a hippie and still is at heart! she is the whole reason i continued my cw major 3

I took both 106 and 206 Julie and she's a real star. Wise and understanding of what students need to hear in workshop. A blast to work with. Please note: Show up to class on time. Turn on assignments on time.
She is the most entertaining and passionate professor I have ever met. She makes writing fun.

Julie is an absolute gem. Her class is fast-paced, witty, fun, and genuinely useful for writers of all calibers. If you are one of those people who can't seem to make it to class on time, however, be warned that she has absolutely abhors tardiness, and will deduct points for it without mercy.

I also took her CW 106 class. And for both classes, she made me a better poet and gave me a lot of poetic motivations to go on with my own voice and style. I will greatly miss going to her class as they were the highlights of my two semesters with Julie.
Probably the best professor I ever had.  She taught and inspired me a lot.  Just love her!!

Possibly the most passionate, funny, and interesting professor I have had at U of I so far. Her poetic knowledge was amazing and her ability to even make your worst poems look best always works to your advantage. Though sometimes I wondered about the sincerity of her compliments. But she is a good professor overall and I'm glad I took her class.

I hate writing essays but I love her class. She is very nice and helpful. I hate writing but I got mostly A's on my papers. I believe as long as you put effort into writing, she will def give you a good grade. also, only four papers for the semester. all with interesting topics. take rhet 105 with her!!
Does anyone know where she went to?
Julie wont be returning as a teacher here  : (   She was fabulous!
She is the definition of a hippie poetry teacher.  She is hilarious and a fair teacher.  I would recommend taking any class she offers.

She was a great ass teacher. She was funny and she made the work easy. We were only given four papers for the semester and  we were allowed to return them all in for full credit after corrections. It's sad that Spring '07 is her last semester here but if you can find one of her classes available, register for it.

Terrible class. I had to give her a "good" rating because she was very easy. But go ahead and ask me how much I learned: NOTHING. Her writing instruction is utterly useless! If you want an easy A - take her! If you want to LEARN something... search elsewhere.
Easiest class i've ever had. Revisions for papers for new grades, funny teacher. Easy class.. just go to it and you will get an A.

One of the best, if not THE best teacher I have ever had. Julie is extremely willing to help as much as the student wants. She is Very approachable. Grading is fair. Very tactful with criticism, and gives A LOT of praise where it is deserved.
She totally rocks!
A great, fun, helpful instructor who makes me love going to class.  My writing has taken on a whole new attitude.  Julie is the best teacher I have EVER had!!!
She is so awesome! She really makes you work feel meaningful.
A highly superior instructor, as well as a human being. She not only made me a better writer, she changed my life. I love this woman.
His exams are based on random examples he uses in class, and some of the questions make no sense at all. Avoid at all costs.

Professor Wirtz's class is definetly tough, but I think many of these reviews are harsh. The lectures do have rambling and sometimes jump around, but if you can take good notes and spen time studying it's very possible to earn an A!

Honestly, the worst professor I have had at U of I. He is inappropriate with his examples in class, calls students out in disrespectful ways, and laughs when you ask questions. Stickler about technology. Everyone in this class looks at each other when he goes on his unfiltered tangents. I think he has tenure, so the university won't get rid of him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Awful professor. He is a nice guy, but does not do much to help students succeed. Rushes through slides, does not share the slides, and rambles on about subjects, sometimes without finishing his thought. It is very hard to get a good understanding of the content and material, given the way he conducts the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Wirtz is, by far, the worst professor I have ever had. Not only are his exams quizzes of random examples that he used in class, but nearly 65% of the grade was due within the last two weeks of class. I paid attention, took notes, and attended every lecture, yet barely earned an A. Stay away from Wirtz if you care about your GPA or well-being.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I went to every lecture. I paid attention to everything he was saying. I asked questions. And I did not learn a single thing this whole semester. He rambles about anything and jumps around on the slides making it impossible to retain a single piece of information displayed on them. He is on tenure so he clearly does not gaf. Good luck.

Slides are impossible to follow and in addition to a final exam he assigned two final strategy applications; the first involves watching 8 hours of videos to get a certificate and the second is over 10 pages of short analysis instructions alone. Said it was in place of a final and then had a final anyways. Quizzes are specific and not enough time.

I am actually so confused on how he has 5- and 4-star ratings. Wirtz cares about his students but is a horrible professor. I am not sure what changed, maybe it was him getting tenure or his medical issues, but his lectures, slides, and tests make absolutely no sense. When he teaches, he jumps all over the place and I have no idea what's happening.

I so badly wanted to finish the public relations minor. However, after a few weeks of this course, I realized that is not possible without an entire semester of anxiety both in and outside of class. The quizzes are incredibly specific and impossible with confusing slides that are difficult to follow, especially with his speed.

he is a sweet and understanding person and is accommodating, but I have no clue in the slightest what has been going on in this class. lectures are really fast paced and his lecture slides make no sense. i don't hate him, i hate this course format. the "case study" quizzes are also short on time for the amount of questions that are given.

Wirtz cares about these students and has tried to accommodate them when necessary. However, his lectures are incredibly confusing and fast, especially when he tries to explain concepts that others are all over the place. I'm often confused in that class.

Quizzes are extremely difficult and once you choose an answer, you unable to go back and review your work before submitting. Wirtz has had temper tantrums and has angerly cursed at our class of mostly women rather than calmy expressing what he needs to say.

John has a lot of passion for PR and he is not the type of professor who tolerates students who cut corners. He wants you to learn. Very strict no phone policy in class. While the quizzes and exams were online and open-note, they were challenging. Be sure to take good notes (and especially note examples he uses in class). I would take him again.

He's actually so nice. Lectures are kind of confusing. Open note quizzes and tests but they are still a bit difficult. He is a good guy and wants you to learn. Although one of my more difficult courses, I would take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Test heavy

reviews.comment: His class isn't for those who aren't willing to try. If you actively go to lectures and keep up with the material, it isn't difficult to get a higher grade. The material is actually useful and interesting, plus very relevant to those interested in a future in PR.

There was a previous rating from someone who took his summer class that stated Prof. Wirtz had technology issues. It's safe to say that he ironed out these issues in the fall. He kind of delegated email response to his TA so that it is possible to get a hold of someone if you have any questions. The class is straightforward and easy if you keep up.

I love Professor Wirtz. He's a funny guy and fair grader. His lectures are very useful to someone who wants to go into PR. You will actually learn things that apply to real life. There are quizzes after every lecture but they are easy if you do the readings. I learned so much from this class, it was my favorite this semester.

Professor Wirtz is a really kind and fun professor. He looks out for his students and is willing to make adjustments for us. I just finished taking his summer course for ADV 310 and unfortunately, he doesn't work with technology well. I have had issues with accessing videos and quizzes. Worst part about Wirtz is he NEVER responds to your emails!

Wirtz's class definitely isn't for people who aren't going to try their best to get a good grade in the class. He's tough but only because he really wants you to do well and he is more than willing to help you if you reach out to him or go to his office hours. If you're interested in the PR field then you should definitely take his class
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: If you go to class and pay attention it's easy (and enjoyable). Wirtz is a nice guy who wants you to learn and succeed in his class. If you don't go to class then don't get expect to get great grades. The material is easy to follow and understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Wirtz is nice but should stick to research, not teaching. Monotonous voice makes students fall asleep. Pointlessly drones on for an hour on things that should take 5 minutes. I have had 400 level courses that are much easier. Hardest class I've ever taken. Quizzes are impossible as questions are vague. He expects too much out of a 300 level class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Surprised at the 2.3/5 rating for Wirtz. You do actually have to do work (bummer ik). But if you actually enjoy PR/Media Careers in general, Wirtz will be one of the few people at UIUC that will guide you down the right path. And make it enjoyable. By no means am I a good student, but I still got an A and looked forward to this class every week.

Honestly, a lot of the reviews here make it sound worse than it is. Wirtz is a good guy who will help you if you need it. You HAVE to go to class, there's not any graded attendance but his tests are completely dependent on lecture materials and examples from class. If you go to class, take good notes, and read the assigned reading you will do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This is an intro to PR class but Wirtz makes it as difficult as possible. He says that he doesn't want his students to do poorly, but the format of his tests begs to differ. He's THAT professor that has exams that have the options "A, B, C, A and B, B and C, All of the above, None of the above." They are also extremely difficult to study for.

He's really not as bad as these reviews say. If you go to class and to his office hours, he's helpful and will tell you what you need to do to succeed. But you HAVE to go to lecture. He posts outlines of slides online but the majority of exam material is things he says in lecture. He also gives 3 analysis assignments that make you apply concepts.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed this class. His tests are not super easy. He provides an exam outline and highlights important terms on the slides. The short answers are pretty obvious. GO to class he puts examples on the slides and they will appear on the exams. Don't miss attendance points, and do the extra credit and you'll do fine. He's a good professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: horrible, horrible, horrible. I would never take Wirtz again. His lectures are impossible to pay attention to and the exams are close to impossible. Avoid his class.

I didn't think he was as bad as everyone made him out to be. His class is interesting since he ties in current events to help explain things. It's easy to expect what he wants because he highlights important notes in his slides. Only downside are his test formats like everyone said. Also does a few in-class participation assignments so show up.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: John Wirtz is really a character, let me tell you. His boring lectures pretty much put me to sleep and it is a struggle to go. Unusual personality but a dry sense of humor. He invented the term "media snacking" and asked us all to tweet about it to get his word trending. Tests are so hard it hurts and every question has almost every letter in it

Content is relevant if you are into public relations/journalism, but often does not make content compelling. Also, kind of rude. Often will call out people for looking on cellphones even though everyone has a computer; kind of hypocritical. Teaching style is lecture heavy with decent amount of reading.

Wirtz has gotten a few bad reviews but honestly he's not that bad of a guy. His lectures are interesting and he makes it clear what he wants you to know. The only thing I don't like are his exams. He has options A,B,C- Only A & B- etc. and it makes you second guess yourself. In order to pass the exams you HAVE to study at least 3 days in advance.

Wirtz is one of the most paranoid professors I have ever come across. He will call out students during class for taking pictures of the slides and gets smart when students ask him to slow down. His cynical and condescending attitude is really off putting. How he has ever had any job involving interactions with other humans is beyond me.

Hated him. Very difficult exams. His questions were always a, b, c, a&b, all of the above type. Asks stupid questions on exams like what did wirtz say about... Random participation 8 times throughout the semester, doesn't post notes online, lots of work in the last 3 weeks of class. Very overwhelming

A bit full of himself and a weird teaching style, but he's fair. All of the information on the tests is covered in lecture (important stuff highlighted). He has so much knowledge to offer if you can get past him being a little preachy. I am certain that I've benefited from taking this class because of the content, and the rest is fine if you try.

What a self-righteous junky egotistical piece of mashed potatoes. Never wrote a book but routinely asks "what did wirtz say about...". Tests are WAY too specific, asks a, b, c, a and b, a b c and d. Most BS professor I've ever had in all of professorship. Doesn't matter how prepared you are, it matters how much he likes your particular class.
Is always accessible for help with the course and you can tell she cares about her students. Material is a bit boring, but enjoyed the class overall
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Honestly, it was one of the most boring classes I took. We spend 3 hours each week in class learning nothing, yet attendance is mandatory. If you just watch the pre-lecture videos, you'll get an A on tests. The group project was very easy, just pay attention to the rubric. Overall, the content is similar, if not easier, than an AP Stats course.
Lost all my interest in the topic of the class after taking this class. Self study is what kept me going. Lectures are just a waste of time
Nice and caring person. Lectures are not very interesting and a lot of workload.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Honestly a good professor. She cares a lot about her students, and the class is pretty good. One of the few professors I've never heard a complaint about in college which should tell you a lot
She really cares about her students, is always easy to access, and answers questions thoroughly. She sets up review sessions and rewards good attendance, though it is not mandatory.
You can tell she really cares about her students and always tries to be accessible outside of class
Amazing professor who really cares about students and dedicates a lot of time to the class!
Improves over time and worth it

Mr. Terry is the literal best!! He made TE 200 such a joy and my favorite class. If I could take this course again I would in a heartbeat just to be in his class again. He is a fantastic teacher and such a remarkable, remarkable human being. I will forever be grateful to have had him as my teacher.
Project based class. TA's do the grading but he'll change ur grade if u ask nicely enough, great dude. Follow the rubric on assignments.

I had Professor Kasdan for TE 200 and he is the man! He always made class super fun and engaging! You will be working towards an idea for the IDEA Fair as a cumulative project and he is extremely helpful in giving you the guidance you need! I did not really consider myself an entrepreneur before the class, but I have changed my mind thanks to him!

I took TE 200 this semester. Professor Kasdan was always engaging and taught well. It always felt like he was very prepared for class and shared helpful and relevant information. If something came up he was also flexible about due dates when he could be. I recommend taking him.
I think Terry is a great professor. He also has a good amount of knowledge in entrepreneurship and teaches well.

Professor Kasdan allowed us as students to explore innovative, creative, and technical opportunities. I loved how group oriented this course was, and how willing Professor Kasdan was to share his experience, his expertise, and his mentorship.

Terry Kasdan is easily one of the best professors I've had at UIUC. I just finished taking ADV409 and I honestly can't recommend it enough. Professor Kasdan really cares about his students and makes an effort to get to know everyone. He's super encouraging and gives great feedback. A lot of 409 is focused on group work so try to get a good group!
I took TE200 because it's required for the innovation llc. The material is pretty easy but Professor Kasdan made it interesting. Like others say he's very caring and wants to see students succeed.

This course overall is pretty easy and Kasdan is a great if not inspired lecturer. He makes students feel at ease and creates a great environment but can't really inspire students (though this may be due to the dullness of the material). Overall though, he was very kind and genuinely interested in students which helped to make the course enjoyable.
Good professor

Professor Kasdan is one of the best instructors I have encountered at UIUC. He genuinely cares about his students and is very helpful and thorough in class discussions. This class is great due to its unique design and I believe it to be a phenomenal course that teaches entrepreneurship because we act as though we are entrepreneurs in the class.
This was one of my favorite classes at UIUC. This class is how every elective should go. Terry is a great professor and genuinely cares about students.
Professor Kasdan really cares about this class and his students and that shows each week in class.
This was an amazing class. Real life business experience taught by a successful entrepreneur. Also very engaging with students and wants to see us succeed. Id take this class again if I could!
Always open to helping students, very thorough with lectures, and gives enough time in class for the semester-long group project. Overall a great person and teacher!

The material isn't even hard, it's her absurd questions that she throws in on the exams that she never talked abt! And its only 15 questions, no curve!!! I would study so much in this class and genuinely understand the concept, but on the exams it's just a guess, honestly, like you'd be better off calculating probability.
She spends so much time on simple concepts and completely glosses over things that need clarification. Very condescending to questions in my experience.

Marville's class has graded iClicker questions during the lectures that are often harder than what was previously presented and considered "trick questions" on previous exams. Her class only has 3 multiple choice midterms, while other 102 classes have 5 exams with multiple attempts per question. She explains concepts in the hardest way possible.

She does not care about helping students learn, she purposely explain concepts in the most difficult way possible. She doesn't seem to care about teaching, but rather proving her own intelligence. Little of the material is remotely similar to the exam. On the bright side, I met some lovely TA's at the CLC. Switch out of her section asap.

Definitely get 100s on all of the homework/assignments. You will need it as a "safety cushion" for exams. As of fall 2025, exams are out of 15 points (excluding the final) then weighted, so not much room for error. Exams are also tricky. One word or exception can change the entire problem. Get the 24 bonus points, they're worth 3 exam questions.

Exams were on ALEX, which was bad for me. I had no experience with Chem before this, so it was rough. My friend, who took AP Chem, enjoyed it, but I didn't understand the lecture. She moves the fastest compared to the other professors and does an unnecessary unit at the end. If CHEM is just a required for engineering, then do not take her.

I took AP Chem, got a 5, and had A's both semesters in HS but nothing could've prepared me for this. The lectures and discussions give you false confidence and when you take the midterms you realize 75% of the exam (which are 15 total questions) are problems you've never seen before. Steer clear of this course. Should've listened to the warnings.

Dr. Marville is not as evil as some of the older reviews say, but the class is tough no matter what. The best piece of advice I have is to do ALL the worksheet pages that correspond to the lesson and to review the lecture slides before lecture. Also, set reminders for homework that way you don't miss any. Godspeed, everyone.
She gives lots of practice problems to get you ready for exams, and the exams are not hard at all if you're prepared.

I admit that it is 80% of me for the score. But there are few things to say about CHEM 102.
She skims main stuffs real fast.
The way she elaborates on the materials is hard to follow.
Pop-up iClickers are hard.
She is inspirational for her few last words before the final. So you might not learn chemistry but you might learn other lessons to mature.

Do not take this class. The average midterm scores are around 50-60, and she doesn't curve. Though, there is a retake after each test but it averages with your previous score, which makes it not as helpful. This class is not worth your time and you will be better off taking other sections, just look at the GPA distributions for each respective one.

The midterms are very difficult and so was the final and she does not curve ANYHTING. Her lectures are not great and she provides close to no study material. If you take this course be prepared to self teach

Gang she's reformed from her academic probation days, I will say though her exams are still very difficult. Make sure to get perfect scores on hw and extra credit and you'll survive. She does not give enough study material for exams, and her questions can be tricky, the final is not as bad as the midterms imo.
I wouldnt take her if I were premed

After scoring 40% on the first midterm, I had to self-study the entire course. Lectures focused on inessential info while skipping key topics, and discussions were useless. However, her mock tests, prep exams and worksheet booklets were lifesavers that carried me throughout. She will test your test taking skills more than learned topics.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's not as bad as she's made out to be. Her tests seemed fair, especially if you do the homework and take advantage of the extra credit mock exams, and she gives retakes for all 3 midterms. She's also pretty good at communicating important concepts in lecture. Biggest downside is that she often spends lectures repeating very similar questions.
This class made me reconsider my entire major. Take any other chem 102 section and save yourself (and your gpa).

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. I really do not understand how she is still a professor. She is not welcoming AT ALL. If you have doubts, just ask Youtube or chatgpt. She is extremely unhelpful. Whatever she teaches does not come in the test. She purposely makes the exams so hard and confusing. A really bad nature. Please take any other professor available

If you can, switch teachers ASAP. I didn't find the material hard, however this course certainly was. She is a fast paced lecturer, lots of homework that felt all over the place because they were on different websites. Her discussion can be helpful, she goes over old exam problems, but she doesn't explain the concepts to how she solved them.

Dr. Kelly Marville is doing too much but never enough. She doesn't curve any tests and allows retakes. However, these retakes are an average of the original and the retake score. She also does in-class clicker questions that are GRADED, which will mess you up BIG TIME! She skims the material and expects you to get them right.
Awful professor. Incredibly disrespectful, refuses to admit any fault.

While the lectures themselves were coherent and well done, none of it showed up on the exams. She specifically said she tries to make the questions confusing to "make sure we know the material", but will put material we haven't learned yet on major exams. She also is rude when students ask questions, and condescending.

Please, please, PLEASE do not take Marville if you want to maintain a good GPA. She's an eloquent and fantastic lecturer, but everything you're taught in class might as well be forgotten because literally nothing on the homework, iClicker questions, iClicker assignments, pretests, discussion packets, OR practice tests is on the exam. 15 ?s Btw.

she is incredibly rude. she laughs at the students when they ask questions, making you feel extremely dumb for being confused. the test averages are always like a 50%, and there are retakes but no curving. if you can avoid her, do it, because even the ta's talk bad about her.

Dont take her, actual exams are nothing like hw or prep exams, averages are 7/15 on exams and does absolutely nothing for curve even tho everyone fails. Theres other professors teaching chem so take them not Marville

I would never in a MILLION years take this professor. DROP the class immediately. She doesn't care about the students. You can't understand her at all. The exams are the worst part of this professor. You can study all you want, but the questions are never like the ones on the mock hour exam or the practice exam she gives.
Taught nothing in class, rely only on learning via online resources. If you want to learn anything you have to teach yourself.

FIND ANOTHER PROFESSOR IF POSSIBLE!

Marville is the toughest teacher I've had at UIUC, and the class is a total weed-out. Her lectures move fast, and there's a lot of homework. The average on the exams were always around a 50% and she doesn't curve the grades. If you go to get help at the CLC, the TAs will literally badmouth her.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Was on academic probation for a reason.

When I first signed up for Marville in FA24 I thought I was low key cooked. Her reviews RMP were awful and… they were right. She's an extremely fast lecturer, who lacks the basic ability to teach humbly. However, she's not unfair. She's very nice with midterms, which are practically identical to the practice exams.

do not take her. save yourself. i took her twice. dropped the first time, took her online class next se, and regretted it. she doesn't teach, just talks insanely fast and doesn't show you what's on the tests. study guides and practices are nothing like tests. if you take her, good luck and prayers, may God be with you because this teacher is satan.

Dr. Marville's class is difficult, but she gives you all the resources you need to succeed, and it's the student's job to take advantage of them. Going to discussion section was SUPER helpful even though points aren't awarded for going. There are also a lot of assignments other than exams that can help boost your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Very rude professor
Good luck.

Doctor Marville is great at explaining difficult concepts. She has a well-structured class and gave us a lot of practices to prepare for exams. Definitely an amzing professor for introductory chemistry.

The lectures are alright, but sometimes they're pretty confusing to understand. Lots of opportunity to practice on the content, and she offers a lot of ways you can get good grades in the class (retakes, dropping quizzes and homework), so for me I felt like I was given a lot of leniency. Tests are do-able, especially if you take advantage of retake
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I was afraid of taking her class because of the other ratings, but honestly the professor wasn't that terrible. I would absolutely recommend taking notes before lecture and staying on top of assignments, but she is overall nice when you come to office hours.

I think her class is pretty good, lectures are clear most of the time and resources are easy to attain. She does go through lectures fast sometimes, but slides are also posted online. Tests are pretty easy if you come prepared, final wasn't bad either.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Marville is clearly knowledgable about chemistry, but unfortunately she's not as skilled at explaining topics and concepts to students, especially for a general chemistry course. Also, beware of her grading policies-she told our class she would be curving the final, and she didn't curve at all.

Lots of studying. Lectures are very fast so notes need to be taken before lecture. Tests only have a few questions on many topics which make studying very important. Use your TA and Office hours early on so you don't fall behind. Lots of practice questions and study material given. Very nice teacher but difficult content.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I think Dr. Marville gets a little too much hate. It's true she produces the lowest GPA average for her class out of all CHEM102 profs, but I think she is really inspirational and provides a LOT of resources for help. Don't forget to look through the printable practice exams in ALEKS resources. Put in the work and you will do well. Good luck!

Dr. Marville is definitely enthusiastic to teach chemistry and wants her students to do well, but the way that she presents the material is hard to follow. She goes through slides quickly and just reads from them, rarely elaborating. Tests are extremely hard and often have questions that were barely covered in class.

Would rate a 0 if I could. I would rather have a middle school chemistry teacher try and lecture me on this course. Her pacing is way too fast, she never explains any concepts, and is a really harsh grader as averages on all her tests are failing grades. Not to mention she is very condescending and gets upset when a student asks her a question.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Not a terrible professor as people think, her slides are very organized and there are prerecorded lectures online. The scary part is that her grading scale is extremely unforgiving. The average for every midterm exam is a failing grade and exams are NOT curved. I recommend attending EVERY discussion to consolidate knowledge after each lecture.

Here's advise: the iclicker assignments are almost always questions we did in discussion. To get a 12/15 or more in mock exams do the practice exam that's not for points a day before, they are almost identical.For hour exams go over the overview of questions and try to match the hints with problems you did in discussion, prep exams, and wrksts.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
I should've read the reviews before taking this class. All she does during lecture are practice problems and she never takes the time to explain any concept in detail. Despite studying really hard and attending every lecture I would still do bad on the hour exams. 
And the class average itself is not great.

Aleks grading system is HORRIBLE in the way that you'd jump from a 100 --> 66% on a 3-question prep exam because of a tiny rounding error etc. The class was fine bc I learned it all from AP Chem. Her exams are fair/predictable, she emails the topic of every single question in order and you can find another version of that topic on her prep exams.
Take this class with another professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment:
Ap Class didn't help. She didn't either...
You know what helps though?
Choosing another professor!!!
Save yourself and your sanity, or if you wanna hate your life, your family, your job, and your roommate, choose this class. This class has been so frustrating, I had to break off a 6-year-old relationship with my girlfriend... I miss her :(

If you never took AP Chem try to avoid her section, but if you have to settle for her, the online section is better. Her class relies on you coming into class already having some understanding about the content so keep that in mind. I-Clickers are annoying but manageable, exams are only 15 questions so that can screw you over if you don't prepare.

I made the mistake of choosing this class and didn't check her rate my professor beforehand. I don't find her teaching style very helpful, I would teach myself most of the time, the notes and worksheet took 2 hours on average, the in class iclickers were terrible because they are very stressful especially for a topic we just learned.

Just finished the final exam. Just know these ratings you're seeing are generous compared to what she actually is. She is not understanding, helpful, nor does she effectively teach her class. Her way of teaching is rambling off words that r on the slide and does not explain. She was put on social probation for a reason. DO NOT TAKE THIS SECTION.
Good luck going to med school with Marville killing your GPA!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Kelly Marville is a brilliant professor with much experience, but her grading procedure makes her class so hard. She refuses to curve (even with failing averages) while giving the most work and hardest exams of the 102 professors. She offers retakes which is the only reason people pass. You'll have a letter grade higher with any other teacher.

I took online Chem102 with Dr. Marville this semester and while she's not that bad of a teacher, her grading procedures are terrible. There are 3 'hour exams' that consist of 15 multiple choice questions in 50 mins and are extremely difficult. There were over 100 kids in my section and only 7 got a perfect score on one of them.
Probably the toughest Gen Chem I teacher out there, but her lectures are well made and the grading criteria is well-lined out. If you put in the work, you'll be fine.

I was nervous about Marville going into the course because I read this page, but honestly I don't feel like she is as bad of a professor as people say she is. Tests are pretty easy, though punishing if you make some mistakes, and her lectures are pretty good, even if she goes fast sometimes.
rip if you have Marville

not terrible for a weeder class. doing the practice problems on the discussion worksheets helps a lot because they're pretty similar to the exam questions. i usually have to review the slides myself after lecture because she tends to rush through them. ridiculous amount of weekly homework.

YALL. DO NOT MAKE THIS MISTAKE. pick another teacher. She was on social probation because she failed too many students a couple years back and she hasn't changed. Her tests are not curved even though the averages are around 55%. The wording of the questions on the exams do not make sense. DONT TAKE THIS TEACHER!!
guys......

Dr Marville rush through her slides, she expects the students to have read/written down what was on the slide in 5 seconds and moves on. We can't ask questions during lecture because it takes up too much time during class. Her explanations for difficult concepts don't make sense, and I end up teaching myself the lecture using YouTube videos.

Professor Marville's class is challenging, but the lectures and homework are very helpful in learning the material. Utilize all the online resources, including unstuckstudy, to prepare for the exams. Attend every class and stay engaged, as the iClicker questions can impact your grade.

Posted the homework in random places, overload, and she would not like you to ask questions IN THE DISCUSSION. She would only want to review what she wanted to go over. She also did not go over definitions well, she cared only about memorizing each thing.
She is not flexible, she punishes students who are sick/mentally unwell, and she refuses to change her ways. 0/10. Avoid her at all costs if you want to be happy.

I studied so much for her tests and it got me nowhere. She gives us mock exams which are suppose to mimic real exams but none of those questions are anywhere similar to the test. Literally choose anyone else. You will be doing yourself a favor. The average grade disparity for her section is so much lower than other sections. PICK ANYONE ELSE. PLS.

The lectures, homework, etc. were incredibly helpful in learning the material and preparing for the test. The tests can be hard, but the extra credit and the retakes can cushion your grade. She goes through material really fast and does graded iClickers, so it's recommended to go over the content before the lecture.
Rushes through everything and is horrible at explaining the material during lectures.
Use her online resources how she tells you to and you'll get an A with some work. Doctor Marville is pretty intimidating but wants the best for you, I would definitely take her class again.
do yourself a favor and switch to another chem 102 professor.

She ruined my semester! Her class is way too difficult. The material she teaches in class is not on the exams. I study 20+ hours per week on this class (sometimes more) and still fail the exams. Take this class with anyone else!!  Beware of her sassy attitude!

RUN.  iClicker questions are graded on correctness.  It makes zero sense to basically quiz students on topics that they literally JUST LEARNED.  Exams are NOT curved, even though every single one of her averages is failing.  She also only tells you HOW to do things and not why, so without outside help you're blindly doing math without knowing why.
test heavy
require tons of effort
difficult but easy
good
EZ
Do not take her class!!

Her classes have several times more to do than other professors, and her lectures are not really helpful for exams.she is busy proving that she is smart and doesn't care whether her students understand or not.
You will struggle with this class. The review for the exam is nothing like the exam.

Never take Marville. She seems nice but she's a bad teacher. She gives out these "mock" exams for our actual exams but those questions are nothing related to the actual exams. Her tests are timed but its never enough time because you only get 3 minutes to do each question. Take any other teacher than her.

her class is crazy, she teaches things that are far beyond what intro chem should be. If you have friends in other chem 102 classes, you'll notice you're doing triple the work and learning different (much much harder) topics.
Really bad grading system. Lectures are not helpful.

She has been failing kids for years now. I would never recommend taking her course. The class average on the last exam was a 53%, with no curve! The questions on the exams barely represent anything that was discussed in lecture. Same with the practice exams that she swears by, almost none of those questions help for the exams.

Dr. Marville has been failing kids for years now, yet she still uses the unhelpful lecture videos instead of actually teaching in in-person lecture. Her exam averages are extremely low, however instead of taking responsibility and curving it, she blames the student. She is very unfit to be a college chemistry professor.

My fav professor at UIUC! She responds to email very quickly. Once I sent her a question on the Bohr model and she replied to my email within 10 mins. Her way of teaching helps me retain information a lot more effectively. Her class is tough, but she makes sure that students understand.
She is very rude to students who try to ask for help in front of everyone in the online lectures. Her lectures also don't really help with understanding the concepts that appear on the exams.

Marville wasn't involved in the lab section of this class at all and only came in on occasion to pick at dress code violations. The TA did everything. Having a good TA and rewatching the pre-lab videos a lot is going to be very important if you want to pass the class.

Would definitely not take it again under any circumstances. She seems like a nice person and actually a decent lecturer but only in person. Online was and is an absolute no for her. Rarely any curves on the tests. On one test I took with her, the average was a low D and there was no curve whatsoever.

Do yourself a favor and don't take Marville's class. Her exams and quizzes are not reflective of her lectures. She does not curve exams and quizzes like most other Chem 102 professors. I also heard she was on academic probation for failing too many students.

Yells in the lab and is overall rude. When you ask for help she makes you feel stupid and requires you to submit everything by the end of lab or you are screwed. Does not make any accommodations or exceptions and is horribly rude about it. Sat in my car and sobbed after lab one day. Ruined my week.

I have huge respect for this professor. It was very fast pace and the lectures and iclickers honestly did not help me at all. doing the worksheets and hw was the best way to practice. the key is to practice a lot instead of relying on the prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The class is hellish at times but I got an A in the class with zero chem experience (not even in high school). Marville states in the syllabus that she expects her students to put in a lot of effort which frankly is the reality of what a college class should be like. I really wish she curved grades though.

WHATEVER YOU DO DO NOT TAKE MARVILLE! Not sure how true this is, but I have heard from everyone around campus that she is allegedly on parole with the university for failing too many kids. After taking her class I can 100% see why. The point distribution of the class isn't even that bad but the way she teaches her class is the worst I've ever had.

Dr. Marville makes chemistry unnecessarily hard and has even degraded people for asking questions in class. She is by far one of the lowest ranked chem 102 teachers by gpa disparity. Definitely do not recommend unless you wanna hate chemistry.

Being completely honest, this is the worst professor I've ever had. She was rarely available for office hours, explained the absolute minimum. I switched my major after taking this class. Also good luck if you're an athlete. She wouldn't let me miss class even if I was in the middle of a race. Very difficult to deal with.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Shes uncaring and generally terrible at teaching the material. Dodge this class if you can and try for another prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She ruined my semester and I never want to see her or take a class from her again. She's very smart but can't teach.

If you messed up by accident during the actual lab and went over the time, good luck. Because once you missed your deadline to enter your data, you would NOT gain access to your post lab and learn from it. It's a ridiculous way to get punished for mistakes that may made by humans and not able to learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Lectures are very straightforward, neat, and detailed, and work through a lot of example calculation problems. Polite and responds to emails very quickly. A minor gripe is that on the exams, you can't go back and change your answers once you have submitted once. The exams are hard but also only 45% of the grade which is very generous.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Just don't take her class. NEVER.

Marville's exams were completely different than the material she covered in class. I would have gotten an F had I not dropped before the final exam. The iclickers are way more difficult than helpful, and she degrades students or completely ignores them when they ask a question. She is the reason i am not in pre-med anymore, and I had to retake :(
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The I-Clicker problems are much more stressful than positive and deterred my grade. Thank god for test retakes because her exam questions are oddly unrelated to the questions she goes over in lecture and homework. She cherry picks questions from "recommended, but ungraded" worksheets and is absolutely terrible at explaining anything. Good luck.

The two midterm exams during the summer class were very difficult to get good grades on. However, the rest of the class is not hard if you pay attention. Marville is a really good lecturer and if you put in the work you will do well in this class. Unlike many other reviewers, I actually thought she was very polite and respectful.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Marville has good lectures and office hours but her tests are nearly impossible.
