The level of difficulty she exemplifies in lecture is no where close to how hard the tests are. Be prepared to put a lot of work and effort into this class. Gonna warn, most people drop the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Worst experience of my life. Marville is the worst professor on this campus and if you ever need to take 102 DO NOT TAKE IT WITH HER. She doesn't teach class she just calls you stupid or calls you out for your stupid question. I never felt dumber in any class or as helpless. I had a grasp on chem 101 but then marville makes you feel like an idiot

Please just take this class with a different professor. Marville is the toughest grader and does extremely poorly explaining the content and preparing students for exams. She is also very unresponsive and she does not allow you to unmute during class. She usually never checks the chat. Worst class of my life.
Please if you can do anything do not take her, any other teacher would be far better.

She prides herself on getting significantly lower exam averages (In the 50% range) than all of her other colleagues. I have seen her make future doctors and engineers cry and be forced to choose a different career path.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The class is so poorly structured. She intentionally makes the class hard and grading unfair so that people fail.

I took it in Fall 2020. Professor Marville along with TAs are very helpful answering students questions. We mostly meet TA, and TAs clearly explain the lectures. The video lectures post challenges due to only one chance. However, they give extra time working on postlab and answers students' questions very patiently. Be aware of reef questions too.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She knows how to teach and teaches well; however, her section is significantly more difficult than other sections. She has a very condescending attitude when you ask her any question. The usual response entails "why are you asking me this when you can find the info on the website you know I teach over 1000 students" f u Marville

You have to watch lectures before a synchronous lesson review and the notes take easily 2 hours to take every week and you have a discussion where you have to complete a worksheet and on top of that homework problems each week and extra practice problems and a quiz each week. No curves on tests she just wants to see us fail.

For online you might as well drop now or pray you can switch to an open class, the explanations in her lectures are very confusing and are little to no help. Her quizzes and tests have a very short time limit, 3.3 minutes per question which is not enough time to do each question in my opinion. If you have her good luck
Class is hard. Youll get a C if youre lucky.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Thorny professor, great lecturer. When I missed a homework deadline at week 5 through what I will admit was my own fault, I sent what I thought was a polite email. I asked for extra credit/how much it impacted my grade as it was a large assignment. The reply was not only unhelpful but discouraging, feeling more like a rant than a reply.

Chem 102 is a hard class, but Marville spells out exactly what you need to do to get an A, offers lots of extra credit, responses to emails fast, and gives great lectures. Do all the readings, do all the suggested problems, do all the worksheets, and you will do well in her class, anyone saying otherwise did not put in the necessary effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The exams are difficult and only have 15 questions, making it harder to do well. I had a C average on the midterms, but I did well on the final and got an A+. The homework is basically free points. Unlike many of the people below me, I didn't find her rude at all and I appreciated that she replied very quickly to my emails and was very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Very unprofessional and rude in emails. Her teaching ability is not very great.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Her grading strategies were unforgiving, especially for a summer class that required learning a lot in a short period of time. Additionally, the concepts are taught in a strange and confusing order and there are important vocab terms used but never defined. She was also very rude and curt in her emails.

I took the online Chem 102 summer course and there was a lot of material to be covered within such a short period. Even though there was a lot of homework and the tests were really hard, if you put in enough time to understand each and every piece of information she gives you, you will do fine. The bonuses she gives makes up for low grades you get

Tests only have fifteen questions and missing just a few destroys your grade. Average exam grades are usually around 60% and many questions are presented in a confusing way. The class is curved only very slightly, and although there are retakes, they barely improve the score at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Energized, fun lectures. Outside of that, if you have something negative to say, is impossible to approach, and doesn't take criticism to heart, very unempathetic. Petty in the way she talks, very demeaning. When your average GPA is that low, there's a certain point where you cannot blame your students anymore. no curve, retake gives low points(bad
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Had her as the lab professor, emailed her about something I missed. Instead of telling me gently that I was unable to make it up, she sent me a long email basically saying 'too bad so sad', and how I lied to her (I didn't, she misread my email and missed something I said). She is intimidating and hurtful and I could not approach her after that.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I hated this class as well as the teacher. She treated the students with no respect and did not care that COVID-19 played a huge role in people not understanding the material. She was not helpful at all!

Great professor! Has a ton of experience from industry that helps make the course applicable. Explains content well. Never felt unprepared for an exam because she tells you the exact types of questions that are going to be on the exam, and spends the whole day before the test walking you through the types of problems she will be asking.

As a lecturer she does well. She gives you all of the information you need o succeed in class and the extra credit is good practice and can boost your grade. Her exams are reflective of the material and are fair. Where she falls short is her personality. I have never had a worse relationship with a prof. She didn't like me and made it very clear.

She's tougher in 102 than 104. All her students from 102 received higher overall grades in 104. Her lecture notes are detailed and direct. Lots of work, you have to study and go to lecture. She's very honest and direct, she will help you however she can if you put in the work and effort. Take her if you're planning to take higher level chem.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Will not curve grades. Exam are like reading foreign language for the first time. Rude especially when asking for help. She made me feel like the stupidest human alive.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Her tests are ridiculously hard. I wonder if she does it on purpose, giving out misleading practice exams so that all the students fail the real ones? The practice exams have completely different material than what is covered on the tests. In addition, there is so much homework that there is little time to focus on other classes.

Mrs. Marville is very rude to students during online lectures, whenever a question is asked, she blatantly ignores the intention and basically makes the student feel bad for asking the question. The TAs were great and kind, yet she was so rude. She would dodge questions by telling students to look at the text. Why even have Q&A lectures then?

Listen she isnt an easy A, but then again neither is any Chem 102 teacher. Marville has decreased her difficulty since her academic probation and the class is much more accessible. DO THE ALES EXTRA CREDIT. Btw I got an A in the class with 82% Test average (with retakes) and I basically got a 95-100 in other categories.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I'll be honest, CHEM 102 was not difficult, but Marville's exams were really difficult. There was not enough time, and she comes off as condescending at times. However, if you put in the work, you will succeed in this class. She's also an awesome lecturer Give her a try then drop the class if you don't like it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Marville's TAs are better human beings than she is. She is condescending and tries to intimidate you for no reason. The exams are nothing like ELMCs, unlike the person below me says. You are better off with a different professor if you don't want your GPA tanked. She can explain things well, but there's no point if exams are curveballs.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Shes tough and the class is difficult. If you try and put in effort you will be fine. Go to office hours right away if you dont understand something , she works with you until you get it but you need to prove youre putting in the time. Awesome and detailed notes. Hard tests but similar to ELMC qs. Prepares you for higher level courses.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

reviews.comment: if you care about your grade and sanity, dont take her. her exams are impossible and unpredictable, the tests are nothing like the homework/practice exams/things we do in class. she dumbs it down for us in class but when for the exam, the questions are near impossible. she assigns way too much homework and we moved twice as fast as other classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: First day, said she has a photographic memory and will remember your face forever and tries to scare you immediately. Tried to go to office hours to get help and she told me I should already know this and didn't try to help me. Practice exams are not even remotely like the test. Also I think 3 kids total actually finished the exam in the time given
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS. Her exams are virtually impossible, even if you know what you're doing. She only gives an hour and fifteen minutes for 11 page exams that are all math. In other words, you will not finish. Also, she puts things we've never done or seen on the exams. There is also SO MUCH HOMEWORK. Spare your sanity and find another teacher.
She seems like a really cool professor, but like the level of difficulty of her tests is out of this world. Some of the things in her test, are not even mentioned in class. Would not recommend.

It's true that Marville's classes are challenging, but that's because chem is difficult and you need to put in the work. Her grading criteria is strict but consistent, and if you go to office hours, you will see that she really does care about the students. If you're prepared to work hard, you will succeed and learn a lot in this course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I love her. One of my favorite and would love to get to know her better. She calmed me down from the first class with her bright enthusiasm. Yes, she can be a bit harsh and cutthroat, and her class is difficult -- I love Chem, it's basically my major, and I'm good at it; kept a solid A and barely kept it with the final. But I learned so much <3

Marville is the most condescending teacher I've ever taken a class with. She builds up the students who do well and shames the students who don't. Her TAs are also much better chemistry teachers (and people) than she is. If I could go back and take CHEM 104 with a different professor, I would no doubt.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: To anyone taking Chem 102, I would recommend doing the retakes and any extra credit, as both helped gradewise. Additionally, despite what everyone says about Marville, if you go to her office hours, she is pretty nice. The exams might be hard but over time you get used to the exam format. As long as you put in the work, you should be fine.
WORST PROF EVER. She is absolutely horrible and heart less. I wouldn't recommend her as a teacher or a friend. Truly the most nasty woman I have ever encountered.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Dr. Marville is extremely tough, and generally doesn't care if you're failing. Her lectures are very dense and she has a tendency to start 2-3 minutes earlier than the posted time and finish 15 minutes early. If you have the option to, I would recommend taking another professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Yes, Dr. Marville is tough. But she is a great professor. She posts lecture handouts and solutions online so it is easy to follow along in lecture. Tests are difficult, but if you do the homework, quizzes, and study, an A is achieveable. Also, take the worksheets and discussion quizzes seriously. Theyre just like test questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Good lectures, a decent person overall, compassionate about chemistry, but she lacks empathy and compassion for the students. She gives LOTS of homework but her tests are not very reflective of the work that she makes you do building up. Its the same topics but in completely different forms. Would not recommend if youre not a Chem or Bio major.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Dr. Marville makes a nice, easy subject terrifying and unbearable. Students who want to pursue chemistry, DO NOT take this class as it will demoralise you.

Dr. Marville, in my opinion, is not a good choice for a Chem 102 class. The course and topic, in general, are easy. Chem 102 isn't a hard class by any means, but Marville makes it as hard as possible. Every weekend you will have online homework, Exam level multiple choice questions, and a quiz. Then every week you have a roughly 20-page worksheet too.

She is not an easy teacher, and you really have to understand EACH lecture in order to succeed. Her tests are very similar to the hardest questions on her worksheets  and quizzes. Lectures are also very useful to attend in order to understand the basics. If you don't understand something, I recommend going to CLC, it was a lifesaver. GOOD LUCK!!

She's definitely not easy that's for sure, but an A is possible. And she can be a tad scary at times  but she warms up when you get to know her at her office hours. Attending lectures is definitely a must, reading the book is not necessary but the problems at the end of the chapters are good practice for her exams. STUDY STUDY STUDY!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Marville is an exceptional professor who is very professional and expects a lot out of her students. Despite what many think, she truly does want her students to succeed and will help you do so if she sees that you're determined. Exams are fair and straightforward. Just study the PowerPoint slides and worksheets and you'll be fine.

She over complicates everything and is very unclear. For example, when she was talking about z (number of protons) in class she would say zed, which confused the crap out of me for a while until I figured out what she meant. There are so many tips and tricks she neglected to teach that I learned on my own that would have made my life so much easier

She had some odd desire to make students fear her which isn't all too conducive in a class of this difficulty. At the same time, she always made it a point to come and get help from her at office hours but I've heard both horror stories and grand revelations. Personally found her lectures to be useless. Read the power points and do practice exams.

Do not recommend her. The exams are not representative of what we learn- they're way way harder. I've completed all extra credit and assignments but I currently still have a D+ in the class. Angry that I wasted my time. I spent more of my semester frustrated and worried rather than enjoying the class and learning.

She really wants her students to succeed, but she makes it nearly impossible. There is NO curve in the class, although she claims to curve your grade at the end, which is only 1-3 percent. Her class is very exam heavy and the exams are very difficult to conquer. She takes the easiest concepts, and explains them in the most complicated manner ever.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I am annoyed that I didn't check ratemyprofessor before taking this class. Marville gives the hardest tests of any of the Chem 102 teachers, and her grading scale is extremely test heavy. Doing bad on one test sets you up for a semester full of stress. In my opinion, if you have the option to take Chem 102 with a different professor, do it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: To do well in this class you have to read the textbook and make sure you understand the overalls concepts because you will be taught mostly the math aspect of it all during lecture videos. Also the exams are only 25 questions of MC and she makes them extremely difficult compared to the material you were actually taught. Exam averages are very low.

CHEM 101 really does help with this class. It's great to go to lecture because as she goes through the lectures, she literally tells you what will be on the exam. She is in no way trying to trick you; yes, the material can be difficult at times but if you're willing to put in work and understand what you don't get, then you will do fine on exams!

She seems very intimidating at first but she knows her stuff! She is really helpful if you go to office hours and wants her students to do well. Instead of having a curve, she lets you do exam retakes which is honestly better than a curve because then how your raw exam is curved is based on you. You definitely get the grade that you deserve.

Marville can be very intimidating. That said, she is a very blunt and honest person who values the honesty of her students. Going to office hours is a must if you want success in this class. Do not be afraid to get extra help. She truly does care about her students.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: It was a tough class. Felt like it was too much for a first semester chemistry course. Probably best to try other Professors

I highly disliked her as a person. Her class was also very tough, without reason, as I learned that the great majority of the other Chem 102 classes were much easier. I would not recommend her as a teacher for Chem 102.
Definitely take this class. If you go to lectures and discussions and keep up with the class, you'll basically be ready for the tests and exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Arguably the most difficult professor for chem 102. Avoid if possible
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is very hard. She teaches fast and the questions she explains during class are easy, but absolutely nothing like the questions on the exams. I came out of the reviews feeling very confident about the tests, and I never did well them. I studied for hours preparing of these tests, went into office hours, and got help from another student.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: A hard professor to be sure, she'll readily admit that her class is the hardest one. Doing the practice problems is a grade saver to be sure. Try to go into office hours or introduce yourself and she'll remember you. She wants everyone to succeed but she'll make you earn it. Quite an amazing person/professor, wish I could have her for more classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: She may seem very intimidating at first but once you go in to office hours she genuinely cares for you to do well. Basically try and you'll do well. Chem 102 and 104 are hard but I guarantee if the effort is there, Marville will put effort in you. She doesn't put up with bs and those in the comments that didn't give full effort know who they are.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Honestly the worst professor I've ever met. She would consistently not help me when I came in for extra help. She was constantly hostile in emails and in person. It's her job to work along with her students but she refuses to help you. Would highly recommend Andino or Ray over this worthless professor.
She does not care about her students, she puts things on the test that we have not gone over. She's just overall not a nice person.

She comes off as a mean lady, but she means well if you get to know her. She's tough because she had to persevere for her love of knowledge; she's very passionate about learning and takes it very seriously. She's very professional as well.

Extremely hostile in emails and just an overall aggressive and unfriendly person. I don't understand why she hates everybody she encounters. Sorry I didn't spend 3 hours reading the fine print in every single syllabus/policy/frequently asked questions/etc. Get over it, you're a teacher, you're here to help students and answer our questions, grow up
Great teacher very nice if you get to know her she loves helping people.

Seriously the hardest professor I have ever taken. Without a doubt the trickiest and most difficult tests to date. I studied for hours on end for these exams, taking practice and past exams, but that didn't help because I still ended up with a D.
Tests are completely unfair, much harder than anything covered in class or the mock exam. Take with another professor if you want a fair chance at a decent grade.
I asked her for an extension cause I was ill and didn't start class till 3 weeks later than everyone. She didn't care and said I couldn't make up any work.

Her exams will be the lectures, so just know the lectures and you will be good. The homework, the discussions, and the practice tests are great practice. Definitely do one practice exam. If you go to her office hours you can get close to her, but know that she will expect a lot from you if you do that. The chem help center is a great resource.

Marville is the best in the chem uiuc department and will definitely help you get into med school with high chemistry grades. If you are looking into premed or anything like that take Marville. Instant GPA booster.

This was by far the easiest class I've taken. Before this year, I had no chemistry at all (my high school was focused around english) so it was something new and shockingly, it was a cakewalk with Marville.
She is alot better in the spring for chem 104 because she knows that people who take general chemistry ii are not whiney engineers who are "forced" to take chemistry and she is alot better.
I loved this class because it was the bomb

Dr. Marville is a excellent professor and is always willing to help her students. I would strongly suggest attending office hours and asking for help when you need it, she will respect that and will be more willing to give you a good letter of recommendation in the future. She is a great professor and mentor to entering freshman.

Marville is always ready to help you, as long as you show that you have put in the work and attempted to understand the material. She really wants her students to succeed. Do the book problems and practice tests. This makes the class so much easier.

Lectures are good for explaining material, I didn't even buy the book. I suggest looking through the PowerPoints before class so they make more sense and so you can ask questions/go to office hours as soon as possible. Speaking of office hours, they're really important if you ever want to be liked by her. Quizzes however are unnecessarily hard.
Very cut throat hard professor, clear concise lectures. Expects the best from her students

Amazing lectures. Concise. fridays were fast-paced,useful clicker days.. I received an A- in this class after the very very very brutal final (Study hard for it guys!) Chem discussions are pointless to go to unless you have a good TA because she posts the worksheet keys online. Do not read the textbook, go off the powerpoint, hw worksheet problems.

She is an incredibly smart woman and gives great lectures. However, she can be very intense when lecturing and she expects you to completely understand the material at the level she does for exams. Loved her as a professor but this class is very difficult. Be prepared to put a lot of time in.

Dr. Marville is a very intelligent woman. However, here intelligence makes it difficult for her to understand that even in a general chemistry class that some topics may be difficult for students to master or even understand. she embarrasses people for not understanding material in lecture, she makes her class extra difficult. I would not recommend

She speeds through lectures and expects you to be prepared beforehand with the knowledge she will be covering in class. Her tests are pretty difficult. But if you really put in the effort, you should manage to do ok.

She is very intelligent, but she expects her students to understand the material as well as she does by the exam, which is a very hard standard. My biggest criticism is that she gives you fairly simple problems to solve in lecture and discussion, then makes them much more complex on the exam without you having practice at that difficulty level.

Dr. Marville is an extremely intelligent woman but she speeds through lectures (50 minute classes and she was done in 25-30 minutes almost every time) and expects you to understand what she's saying immediately. DO NOT TAKE CHEMISTRY WITH HER if you want a good grade. Never struggled with a science class more than this, and I'm a senior now w a 3.7

Marville is a very organized and efficient lecturer. Lectures are easy to follow. She doesn't like stupid questions, which scares a lot of people away. A lot of people on here have said that her tests are unfair, but they are very doable if you understand the material and are a decent problem solver.
She is tough, but a good teacher. Avoid if possible, her tests are actually the worst thing ever.
Lectures are fast-paced. Tests are difficult. She expects you to have some understanding of the material before she teaches it.

If you want to have a good GPA by the time you graduate I strongly suggest avoiding Marville. She constantly mentioned the engineers in class and purposefully made the material harder for their sake. Tests were significantly harder than every other chem class's. Lectures were unclear and very confusing. Biggest regret taking her class.
Amazing teacher, tough tests.

Please! Please! Please! DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH MARVILLE! She was condescending, rude, and degraded her students; even to the students who worked hard. If you do not want to be verbally abused then do not take her class!
She's tough but you feel like you understand the concepts after lectures.

If you can, avoid taking this class with Marville. Condescending doesn't even begin to describe her during office hours. Looking back, the material was not hard at all, but her explanations were often confusing; she made basic algebra seem like rocket science. Would not recommend her class at all.
This class is taught as if you have already taken AP chemistry. Don't ask questions, you will receive a condescending response.

Marville is an extremely serious and strict professor. She doesn't sugar coat anything and her explanations are often unclear and end up confusing you more. Tests are really really hard, but she gives you the opportunity to do better on the re-take. Online homework can take long, especially because you only get 10 tries.

She is very rude in office hours and acts like it is a hassle to help you. She makes this entry level course ridiculously difficult. Where other professors assign problems from the book she simply says "do them all". DO NOT HAVE HER
hard

She's a very difficult teacher. I never went to office hours for her because everyone else told me she was super rude to them. Her tests are insanely hard and she speeds through lectures. Try not to take her if you can.

She means business. Don't take this class lightly and definitely don't mess around. She can be intimidating at first, but if you genuinely care and want to do well... then this class should be no problem. And never have your phone ring in class, she WILL kick you out!

I took 17 hours my first semester at UIUC, all technical courses as I plan to go into engineering.  This professor told us we had to memorize the periodic table, and we did not need to.  Her tests put Hummel's to shame.  This teacher makes Chem 102 the hardest entry level course in this school.  Get Tina for your teacher if you value your grades.

Very good lecturer, and clear about what will be on the tests. Tests are hard but fair. She is extremely intelligent and explains things very well if you go in and ask. She can be intimidating but she's actually very nice and happy to help. I you're willing to work hard and put in the effort, she will prepare you best for higher level chemistry
She was not very good at lecturing. Her level of explanation wasn't up to par and I had a really hard time learning the material in her class. Would not recommend.

She teached the lectures very well.. Because they were so simple. The tests on the other hand were unnecessarily difficult. I dont know why she made the tests so difficult when the overall concepts were portrayed as rather simple

Marville definitely knows what she is talking about, and I can see how a lot of students think she is tough. Even though the exams are pretty difficult,there is nothing in them that she doesn't go over. Just visit a TA instead of her office hours-- she can be intimidating. Overall, a fair teacher if you're willing to put in real effort in her class

she definitely knows what she is talking about.. but i think she makes her exams a lot harder than they need to be. the material is already pretty difficult as it is, and she makes them even harder. also, the only way you can get points back on the exam is by "retaking" the exam online, but you only get 2 tries per question and its still hard.
Just get a different professor. Please

People are right, Marville's exams are difficult but not rediculously so. Everything on the exams is covered at one point or another in lecture so as long as you go, study hard, and put in the time to do the make-up exams you should be able to get at least an A-.I enjoyed this class

Marville knew what she was talking about. However, she had the expectation that you would automatically understand what she was saying and had prior knowledge of everything. Her lectures were very simple however her tests and online quizes were impossible. She was also very very mean during office hours. Save yourself and get a different professor
Very smart lady, but she does not know how to give lectures. She gives gives worksheets that are only half as hard the exam questions. She is very difficult, even with having a 3 on the Chem AP exam.

Her lectures are just her reading her ppts way too fast to understand. Exams are unnecessarily difficult and I truly regret taking her course. Extremely arrogant and only tests you on little caveats & exceptions. Horrible, professor overall who doesn't care if the students learn or not. She's just mean for the sake of being mean. Take Huang.

She is a great teacher. She knows the information she is teaching and she knows how to teach it. Many people say that she is very hard and I would agree. If you study you will do fine. She is a great person. I had an hour conversation with her just about random topics. Great person, great teacher, difficult teacher. I recommend her by far.
Very Sweet lady! Class was very difficult

Marville is a fantastic teacher who knows her stuff and also knows how to logically teach it. She has funny stories and brings the energy in her lectures. Her class is a really humbling experience for all the engineers that think they know everything, so study if you want to do well! I would highly recommend her if you want a quality chem class.

Exams are very hard! Retake exams are harder than the exams themselves. Discussion didn't really help that much. Be sure to do the worksheets because most likely they are exam questions. If you want a better grade for chemistry, go with a different professor.

really hard on exams and quizzes. Although there is a retake after every mid-term, the questions are even harder than those in the exams. The average scaled score for the second mid-term is only 67, if you want an easy A on this course, better choose another professor.

Dr. Marville clearly loves chemistry, but she is not good at teaching it.  Her's is my least favorite class because the material she covers is not at all like what she puts on her tests.  The tests are much harder.  Not to mention she is very condescending to students during office hours and when asking general questions. I would avoid her classes
She's a decent lecturer. It's a bit annoying that she starts a few minutes before the class period starts. Other than that she makes things pretty clear in class.

Dr. Marville is a very smart woman, but I think she lets this get to her head. She is not a nice person. She acts superior to her students, and it is very annoying. Try to avoid having her as a professor if you can!!

Dr. Marville is a very engaging lecturer and obviously knows her stuff. However, in office hours she is still amped up so be prepared for the same sort of dynamic personality if you go in. Her tests are very difficult.  If you want an A you most know every piece of the material and understand it.  The final was easier than expected.
CHEM 102 is pretty hard, but I think Marville does a good job of preparing people for the exams and lecturing effectively. She curves the final grade by half a letter or so.

Dr. Marville knows the chemistry very well, and usually does a great job at conveying her knowledge to students. She is, however, VERY condescending to students in office hours. The class is moderately difficult, and if you need help I recommend going to a TA- she will just make you feel worse about yourself.

Dr. Marville is a great professor in that she is very clear as to what you need to know. She'll tell you what mistakes to avoid. She does show a little bit of bipolarity in her personality, so try not to be intimidated--especially during office hours. She chews everyone out! Keep going though! I promise it'll pay off and get you a better grade.

Completely swagged owttt! She's awesome! Her powerpoints tell you everything you need to know. Make sure you do the weekly worksheets and study hard, because exams are difficult and you only get 10 minutes for each quiz. She's clear about what her expectations are and what she wants you to know.

She was very organized which was nice. She puts all her powerpoints online for easy acess. Do not underestimate her tests. People say chemistry is hard at UIUC for a reason. She makes the class hard but is a responsible teacher. After talking to my friends She is defaintly the best choice out of the three 102 teachers

I got used to the accent, but I never got used to the frantic Iclicker quizzes in lecture. Tests were extremely difficult, and she makes the tests to set you up to run out of time if you don't have the material down solid. Weekly online quizzes are timed also but homework gets you a lot of points. Overall stressful but she's extremely knowledgeable

Marville clearly knows her field. However, taking her for 102 was intimidating. She is intense in lecture to keep everyone's attention and forgets to turn in down in a one-on-one office hours situation. I would highly recommend her IF you don't think you will need to go to office hours. Otherwise, Hummel is the way to go for 102.

Marville is a magnificient teacher. The way she constructs her quizzes and iClicker questions prepare her students for exams. Attending every lecture will benefit anyone who takes her class. Any problem we do, she goes over and explains. She always gives tips for how to take exams. Considering I had Jamieson last semester, she's a great switch.

The class itself was stressful to say the least. Iclicker questions were extremely challenging and given without prior warning. She would teach the information, then immediately quiz on it. Accent took time to get used to, and speeds through the material. Gives a fantastic curve at the end of the course so half the class wouldn't fail.

Very knowledgeable in her field. Provides many real world examples to clarify content. Extremely difficult exams, but manageable if you prepare correctly. In the end, the class is curved. However, don't let yourself take that for granted.

Her quizzes are pretty tough, but the homework and quizzes are  easier. Just don't miss much on those and you should be fine. She is kind of tough and moves fast, but she really does want you to do well. Not to mention she has an awesome accent, so at least that will make you pay attention in class.

She has an awesome accent that takes a while to get used to. She moves very fast in class, however has the powerpoints available online. Her quizzes and homeworks were fairly easy.Marville and my TA thought I would do well on the exams, but I failed all of them. They are very tricky and hard. My advice is do well on ALL the homework and quizzes.

Good lecturer who provides you with all the study materials you need.  HOWEVER, her exams are very difficult.  practice exams may be easier than actual exams, but the quizzes are very close to the difficulty of the exams.
she is really nice and funny.  the ppts which you can also downland on the lon- capa are really clear and useful.  I missed my final exam but she still gave me another chance. she is awesome.
While her class and exams were difficult, if you study and know how all of the theory works it is very doable.
She uses practice exams that are easy and the actual exams are impossible

Unless you are a whiz at chem before entering the class, I would go with another professor. Speeds through material that could take weeks to learn in just 50 min. Her tests are pretty near impossible & look nothing like the material she gives you to prepare with.  Chem102 is a challenging enough class; taking it with Marville makes it much harder.

CHEM 102 is a challenging class with any professor at U of I. Dr. Marville's exams can be difficult, but she does give you plenty of materials to prepare you for them. The key to this class is to practice. Do the weekly packet in discussion (q's similar to test/quiz q's). I like that her quizzes were online, because they were essentially open book.
exam's and the final was hard, but she curved our overall class in the end!

I honestly wish I would have looked into other professors before choosing her class. It was so difficult. The exams looked nothing like what she taught in lecture, the homework, or even quizzes. Rarely curves. Yelled at our class because people did so badly because it was so HARD. She is the worst professor for chem 102.
Calls students ungrateful, rarely curves, gets angry at scores below average, knows her subject well but that's her downfall.  She is not a teacher, she is better off doing instead of showing.

She doesnt teach right and yells at us for getting a bad average midterm score. Curved our exam 3 from a 50% to a 60% and called us ungrateful. Horrible at teaching and going over material. She didnt care if we failed her class either, saying "see you next semester!" Do yourself a favor and take someone else for chem 102..

She has a fantastic Jamaican accent and knows everything about chemistry. Unfortunately, she zips through material really fast and I often left lecture feeling lost. Luckily I had a great TA and didn't have any problems with the class overall. I never used office hours but I heard hers were great, she'd sit with students for hours until they got it

She seems very knowledgeable about chemistry but she is definitely not geared towards being helpful to others in the classroom. She has a confusing way of doing stoich, moves too fast to take notes and comprehend them in lecture, and she makes easy concepts seem difficult. I would not recommend this professor to ANYONE. She also isn't approachable.
She's a nice woman, very knowledgeable, but she belongs somewhere other than the classroom.

This class is a breeze if you took Chem 101 and took it seriously. IF you didn't take 101 then prepared to study like crazy for the first exam. Marville explains everything you need to succeed in the exam but almost to the point that she makes it seems getting an A on the exam is better than actually understanding the concepts.
Extremely hard class, but she is fair overall.

I think Marville was probably one of the best Chem 102 professors available all you have to do is put in the effort. My advice to you is go to class, she basically tells you what topics and types of questions will be on the exam. Do the mock exams, the worksheets, the homework. You can get an A if you just put in the effort.
She has a tough class but gives you all the materials you need to be successful.  Just do the practice exams PLUS the extra worksheets and you will be fine.

Marville is a decent teacher, but manages to make fairly easy material seem confusing.  She has a confusing way of doing stoich, but her tests are straight forward.  Go to class, take notes (don't just print off the powerpoint), study the notes the night before the test, and you'll be fine.
Only reads off powerpoints, explains everything in a confusing manner, doesn't clarify anything

She's a good chemist, but a bad teacher and unfit to instruct anyone about anything. She has ZERO desire to help struggling students and gets angry if you don't understand her nebulous, disorganized way of explaining concepts during her office hour. But the whole point of office hour is to learn what you don't know. So it defeats the purpose.

best teacher i ever had!! if you go to her office hour,you will know she's very patient at explaining,she'll make sure you truly understand the materials. She had spent over half an hour with me for just one question and make sure i understand, very approachable, you'll know if you talk to her...i just love the way she teaches!!!
She's got an awesome accent. But she doesn't explain things in a way that makes sense. Also, her tests are pretty hard if you're not used to working for your As

There are better chemistry professors out there. Her tests are relatively easy...the problem is she really doesn't teach in class. All she does is repeat the power points, but attendance is mandatory so you have to go to class anyways. So, yeah....if you are already good in chem or you are good at learning things on your own, then go for it.

I loved her! She takes the time to get to know her students, and she spent extra time with me in office hours before the exams, even when she was only supposed to spend 20 minutes with each student. She actually cares about how her students are doing, and her exams are easy if you know you're stuff. Plus, her accent's cute. WAY BETTER THAN TOM H!!

This class is definitely challenging. Dr. Marville is a great chemist, her skills as a professor are lacking sometimes. Study every bit of material for exams and do practice worksheets. This class is not an easy A, but it is doable.

I would say her class isn't easy. I also would say that if you TRY a little harder, you will be fine. If you struggle, just go ask professor, because she is more than willing to help you. She is very nice and encouraging. I like that she always takes time to help the students first... no matter how long it might take.

She is the best professor that I have been met. Her office hour is infinite.Once, I stayed in her office for 3 hours asking questions, and she always smiling to help students answering questions. Exams are easy first time, and then gets harder. But she curve a lot. but she has accent, everytime I have to go back to read ppt again after lectures.

Very hard to understand her. Her lectures are also pointless because she talks super fast and just reads off the powerpoint. But the lectures are mandatory because she gives iclicker points. Her exams are extremely difficult and confusing. I recommend you go talk to her once a week about the material. Her powerpoints arent helpful too.

I would recommend her to anyone.  She legitimately cares about her students, which is sometimes a hard thing to find on this campus.  Exams are pretty difficult but her office is always open and wants to help. The accent is a little much at first but you get used to it.

She is extremely open and helpful. She talks really fast in lecture but if you ask her to slow down she does. Her tests are difficult, but she curves and having a good TA will help tremendously. Probably not as good as Hummel, but definately 2nd best

Might be the worst class I have ever taken during my time at college. Most ridiculous grading contract I have seen. Almost impossible to get an A given that you start at a B. Only thing this class does is bring down your GPA.
Do not take this class. One bad assignment and your gpa is cooked. Also heavily group project based
I would rather take an 8am class than have this teacher. DONT TAKE HER FOR ANY CLASS

Take caution when registering. You start the class with a B and you have to go way above and beyond to get anything higher than that. One mishap or late assignment revision on her "grading contract" penalizes your grade by a whole letter. Grading is too subjective and way too critical, would not recommend.

This professor's teaching style made the course unnecessarily stressful. Instead of helping students improve, the feedback often felt harsh and discouraging. I did not feel supported in developing my writing skills throughout this class.

Where do I even begin with this class? It claims to use a "mostly labor based" grading contract to reduce anxiety but in practice grades feel subjective as can be her standards shift pushing students to argue for grades. Lectures are filled with low value activities that rarely help the actual portfolio and mostly restate what she wants to see 0/10
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Her rigged grading system gives her way too much power over your GPA. Because she's such a strict grader, missing a single rubric point on one assignment can tank your final grade by an entire letter. It got to the point where I was in genuine fear of submitting assignments, knowing she could instantly kill my grade for the semester.

Easily the worst teacher I had throughout my whole life. Her grading criteria does not make sense and will mark you down a whole letter grade if you forget to do something that isn't in the directions. Would rather write a 10000 word essay every day for a semester than take a class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: By far the worst teacher I have ever had. If you mess up an assignment in the slightest way, you move down a whole letter grade. Your grade by default is a B and you can only increase your grade by doing extra things outside of class that are not reflective of your work. She is very knit picky and harsh when it comes to her grading.

Course has a unique structure that adds several unnecessary components. Instructions for assignments are unclear and graded very harshly. The course is designed so that you start with a B and if you mess up on an assignment, rather than it receiving a poor grade, it is just marked incomplete so if you mess up more than once your grade drops to a C.
Dont take her class please!
Terrible professor. Do not take her class. She has a bunch of extra criteria in her grading that are not very specified in assignment rubrics. Very difficult to please.

She has a different grading system that has so many unnecessary extra work to get an A unlike the other Rhet professors. You have to do extra peer reviews and group presentations to get an A whereas in other Rhet classes, you just turn in your papers. Other Rhet classes also gave more freedom in the topics you can choose for your research paper.

Oscar is a great guy and very funny. Very difficult class. Exams are impossible but partial credit is given so it's not bad if you know what you're supposed to be doing. Homework is only doable with the help of the TA. Unfortunately, the class is not applicable at all to real-world engineering, other than some research.

He teaches the conceptually tough classes with patience and passion. I think he can be a part-time comedian with how many jokes he drops, making the content bearable. He's easy to talk to and can relate to students. He makes the work tough but grades with grace. And he is very intelligent. One of my favorites for sure.

Professor Lopez-Pamies was one of the most arrogant professors I've ever met. He uses a textbook that is just a pdf of his lecture notes, with no clear explanation of concepts. The homework does not reflect the content taught in class and without attending office hours there is no chance of following along. The exams are impossible as well.

I took CEE 598: Computational Fracture Mech with Prof. Lopez in Spring 2025. It was an amazing experience; his lectures were perfectly structured and crystal clear, with every topic presented precisely and in depth. His passion for the subject was obvious, and while the course is not easy, you'll truly enjoy it if you're curious about the topic.

Professor Oscar is a brilliant man. If you're sincere in class and study every day and actively ask questions, you will get good grades. He has a very friendly aura about him and genuinely cares about his students academics. And very knowledgeable. Makes class interesting and very interactive.
He is one of the best teachers I have ever had. Explains things very well.
Toughest course ever.

Yes, the course is tough but if you put in the work you can really understand it. The math required is higher than what is usually expected for engineers, so if you are taking the course to directly apply mechanics, this might not be the right class. I enjoyed the course because it teaches us the basics of the of structural mechanics very well.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Horrible professor, do not take his class if you don't have to. Mechanics and FEM are taught in different departments as well, take those instead of this professor.

Prof. L. is unable to emphasize key points, explain problems from A to Z, and most importantly, re-iterate key concepts. He lectures far too fast and his writing is sloppy and hard to differentiate between variables. He curves heavily, but I wish he taught better so there would not be a need. Brilliant man, strange class, poor pedagogue.

He presents material in such a way that he expects the class to all be geniuses. The homework problems expect you to already be experts in the material, rather than building on concepts to ensure understanding. The exams are nearly impossible. If there was ever a "weed-out" class, this is it. I feel I learned nothing.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: A very clear professor, a very though course, expect really difficult homeworks and exams. In the end you understand mechanics. Not friendly at class.
She is very insightful and truly cares about her students.

Very condescending, discouraging, and often made racist and elitist remarks that went unchecked. She overloaded us with 500+ readings, gave contradictory directions, and created a hostile, unsafe learning environment. Completely unfit to teach future social workers. Hazing dressed up as “rigor”. Get ready to do phd level reading/assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Tara made me not want to be a social work major. If I had not taken this course at the same time that I was in other Social Work classes I would have transferred out of the college. She is incredibly rude, unclear, and should not be working with students.

She loves to talk about how she wants her students to not feel stressed but she does the opposite. She locks our grades all semester so we don't stress over them but not seeing my grades made it hard to figure out where I needed to improve in the class. She also grades in a confusing way and I could never seem to figure out what she expected.

3 long papers, the first two are 6-8 pages each, and the last one is 8-12 pages long. Extremely picky grader. Is overall disorganized and you do not know your final grade until the end of the course. You are uncertain where you stand. Also, she is very heavy on participation.

Dr. Tel was sort of a difficult person to communicate with. During class, she would encourage students to speak with her for any additional support. However, when I would ask a question, it became "a personal problem." Overall, it was a decent course but lacked good communication.

Dr. Tel is a decent professor, but she's not very organized and can often be all over the place during class which makes it difficult to succeed. She also has a tendency to play favorites so if you participate a lot in the discussions then you're more likely to do well because she likes those kind of students.
She takes the time to try to get to know her students and genuinely cares and is passionate about what she is teaching.
She is amazing. If you are looking to get into social work she is the perfect intro.
Great professor. Love her and she definitely is always there for students. Big end of the year project but other than that easy class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Tara really cares about her students understanding the material over simply memorizing it. Most classes were discussion based and I felt like I heard a lot of interesting stories from fellow students. Not too difficult, but not a blow off either.
She is the best professor I had and she gets the class to be interactive rather than sitting all the time. She lectures in a way that is active but yet informative.
Good lecturer; Cares a lot about class understanding

She doesn't know how to teach, she just reads ppts. And attendance is mandatory, you need to waste your time every week. I also took ASTR310 last semester, which is also taught by her. I have to say NO DIFFERENCES, except no attendance and I am good at py so I got A+ at that course.
no practice exams, no meaningful lecture
I have never had a professor that gave such poor instructions on assignments.

I personally liked her lectures! She explains the intuition for the equations shown on the slides in-depth and has good body language. The only con: the lectures are very dense, and half of the equations/general knowledge they cover won't even show up on the homework or the tests. Definitely use the textbook for the homework.
Pretty good lectures, a lot of reading and homework if you want a good grade
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Attendance is 5% of your grade but after the first two weeks I stopped going. She just reads from the ppt and your time is better spent working on your own. She creates the HW herself, which I respect and they are tough which is good for learning course material. Exams are pretty tough and no practice exams are given. Good class but bad prof.
She doesnt teach, she just reads the notes. She never gave any exam practice or numerical questions to prepare for the exam. But she was sweet i guess

Professor Young was very kind but a bit of a dry lecturer. However, Astro 210 is by far my favorite course I've ever taken, and the homework was challenging but so much fun. I believe Professor Young wrote the questions and they were relevant, interesting, and appropriately difficult. You had to work for your grade but it was entirely worth it.

Fun course! While Prof. Young isn't an exceptional lecturer, the class structure is great for actually learning the material. There's homework due every week with optional extra credit and all free-response problems. The graders have a fantastic partial credit policy.

Both exams are similar to the homework. Not hard to get an A if you do the work.

This class was boring. If you want an easy A where almost no effort is required, this is a good course for you. I tried to follow the material at first, but his lectures had little engagement. He is also hard to hear. All the slides are posted on canvas, and all homework is open note.

Dr. K was really enjoyable to have as a professor. His expectations were very clear and easy to follow, and was always super easy to talk to and ask questions through email or in person. The projects, homework, and exams were pretty much exactly what he told us they were going to be from lecture and graded very generously. :)
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Although the class is quite rigorous and requires much time to do well on the tests, I enjoyed the class a lot. On lab days, the TA and Dr. K come around and answer any questions you have that can help you on the exam. Hard class, but totally worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: His exams literally make no sense. He can never elaborate on the slides, if you ask questions, he can't seem to answer any. I need this course for my major but it was such a waste to take it.

He marked multiple people absent while they were in class sitting in front of him, and this was daily. Also, he's weird to talk to, he'd avoid eye contact and mumble. Never really knew how to answer questions about the stuff we'd talk about. Hard to contact via email as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Cannot teach
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. K is an ineffective professor. I was disappointed to take his course because he's incapable of teaching. He mumbles, reads off of slides that make no sense, goes on tangents for way too long, and cannot answer basic questions about the material he's going over. Overall his exams are a nightmare because you barely understand the classwork.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's not as bad as people make him out to be imo but cs128 has many flaws and it is MUCH more difficult compared to cs124. The biggest issue involved the quizzes, which often are one question and all or nothing where a small mistake can get you a 0. I do think he does a good job explaining things in video lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The MPs felt long and more complicated than expected for an introductory C++ class. The quizzes are worth 60% of the grade, and it one small mistakes could result in a 0. Things were going okay at first, but it became harder to stay engaged when the course switched from readings to longer video lessons.
Worst class I've taken

Content consisted of recorded online lectures. Daily questions, some MCQ and some coding, for practice, as well as several substantial MPs. The lectures were very average, mildly boring but contentful.

if possible, take the proficiency test and avoid this class. the quizzes are extremely difficult. nothing is taught in lectures and no practice quizzes. recitation sessions are not helpful at all. the MPs are bad. all he does is yapp in the lessons instead of giving something actually useful. only 1 quiz drop. cant accept his own fault. ego ego.
Not the best teacher.

I'd suggest they should've continued the written lessons. He doesn't know how to teach, just reading off the slides. The MP's were okay, quizzes were okay until 3B, where one question counts for 100% of ur grade. They need an English prof to help them write their problems because they are unnecessarily long.

The quiz where more than half the people got 0s, he revised it. My score goes from 0 to 0. What's the point?
It was going well till week 6, after that, there were videos lessons. He makes 6 videos to explain a simple concept and still doesn't explain it properly. Quizzes are unreasonably hard sometimes. Instead, he can give 3 smaller problems.

I don't understand why UIUC, especially for CS, would hire a professor like this for an intro class, it just makes no sense. We are ranked top 10 for CS, and we have profs like this teaching our foundational courses, which is very alarming.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: This class is challenging but the coding  questions for the homework and MP's have really helped my problem solving skills.

While this class is challenging there are many resources to succeed. Office hours are great and the professor is very helpful through email. While the quizzes are challenging, they are not unreasonable. They test applications of lesson content. Also catchup grading and 1 quiz drop is there so there is a decent margin for error

Okay I understand many people are frustrated with the quizzes, but I don't think they are unreasonable because he doesn't ask anything we aren't expected to know. Also, the 128 office hours are helpful and the professor is quite helpful through email.
You will basically teach yourself all the content and be cooked in the quizzes.

i would tell you to run and go take the course with a different professor but guess what. there are no other professors.

reviews.comment: Quiz is so hard. Over half of the students got a 0 on the quiz. Run!

The daily lessons are hard to follow and the professor does not explain things clearly enough to understand considering this is an introductory level course. Quizzes (making up 60% of the grade) are very confusing and structured to set you up for failure. Worst class I ever took.

Grading for quizzes rose from 40% to 60%. He tested toughly on the MP's on almost all of the "b-quiz". He basically had students re-code the MP in another form and context on the "b-quiz", which was merely impossible to do. This is simply because of the 50min timing, you had to interpret the entire question and code for it, which could result in 0.

Professor Nowak's handwriting is REALLY difficult to read. MPs are challenging!

reviews.comment: Quizzes make 60% of the grade. Every two weeks, we need to take 2 quizzes. The quizzes are hard and unreasonable; one coding problem takes 100% of the quiz grade. If you get stuck in or encounter bugs, you may get 0, though it is still hard. This course is just a nightmare... Just run, kids.
Bad bad bad.

The organization of the course website is complicated. 12 weekly quizzes make 60% of the course grade, which is too much. The quizzes are usually hard and long. It's unreasonable to put so much weight on quizzes that are difficult to finish.
Totally nightmare and makes no sense.
I agree with all previous reviews.

if i could rate a 0 i would
Just reads of ppts he uses during lecture videos. He does not know how to teach.
dosen't know how to write or teach

Man should go back to Texas! oh wait, another prof told me that prof Nowak actually lives there and is barely seen in Champaign!
Professor is mid

reviews.comment: Lectures are pre-recorded youtube videos without any engagement or effort put into them. Quizzes are insane, accounting for almost half the grade wihout any practice available to study for them. MP's were engaging but often took too much time to complete for a 3 credit hour class.

I had no prior knowledge of C++, but I learned a lot during the course. While the videos are online, I was able to grasp the concepts well. However, Professor Nowak's handwriting is quite difficult to read.

MPs are challenging, but TAs are always there to help. If you want an A, consider this course to be 4 credit hours.

Terrible course, even worse professor. Made the quizzes unreasonably difficult: few coding questions worth half the points on the quiz, 50 minutes is nowhere enough to code and debug and pass all test cases. Videos are long and doesn't get to the point, most of the time reading off the slides and the things. Avoid unless they make the class better.

reviews.comment: Howdy!! yeah did not like this man

I just finished my last MP, and my final grade is about 99

I don't recommend this professor. 

The lessons are taught by videos. Bad. Hardly any help.
Biweekly quiz: 1 coding question can be 50% of the grade, and failing it means only 50% for that quiz. 
MP(i.e. coding hw):Even worse. Wordy background. Restrictions even on the length of your code
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
Pros: Nice intro to C++

Cons: Lessons are long, low-quality, and time-consuming, he just reads off the slides. It was fine the first few weeks until they switched to video format. Quizzes are a large portion of the grade yet there is almost no clear practice for them. Instead you must spend a HUGE portion of time reviewing those terrible lessons.

This class is a living nightmare. His outdated videos from the pandemic suck at teaching you new content, and the recitations are incredibly useless. The quizzes are atrocious and are the worst part of the class. One question accounts for about 50-70% of a quiz, and it won't matter if you study or not. Take this class only if you enjoy suffering.

online lectures everyday, with low-quality teaching videos. And there's no textbook or outline, so you have to watch the videos if you want to make sure you master all the knowledge needed.

Machine Problems are assigned biweekly. It's not that hard, but the problem description is just so long and unnecessary (about 3000-4000 words)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
This class isn't hard. If you don't procrastinate, it's fine. But the quizzes are awful.

Prepare for 50-70% of the quiz to be one question. If you mess up one line of code, you're screwed. Pretty much automatic 30-50~ on the quiz. 

Unclear instructions & hard to debug. Real programming involves googling, debugging, and time. Quizzes are useless.

Dr. Nowak is inconsiderate when it comes to the use of the testing software, making CS128 a nightmare. I was putting in efforts to do well, finding the MPs and projects enjoyable, but there is no late policy for lessons, just an automatic 0. I lost almost all of the points over 1 line of code in 1 question in a quiz, and tanked my grade.

(Bi)weekly MPs take at least 10 hours; daily lessons re-use videos from the pandemic with overly long explanations; discussion sessions are unhelpful; biweekly tests are hard to debug and contain tough 50-point questions that test skills irrevalent to the focus of the daily lessons; DO NOT RECOMMEND FOR ANYONE WITHOUT PRIOR C++ EXPERIENCE

reviews.comment: i actually have no idea whats going on in the class ://

reviews.comment: Lectures are comprised of low quality youtube videos that don't get the point across. Would've been better to just provide the links to popular coding youtubers.

His videos may be difficult for some people. But if you go over the videos, understand the videos, you will be fine with the quizzes. Also, there are not that many twists in the quizzes, so if you study, there are not that many things to worry about.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Class isnt that hard but the MPs take a while. Professor looks like a twink ngl and his videos are pretty useless hes terrible at explaining. 124 was set up mcuh better

This class could have been a series of youtube videos and I think I would've learnt about as much. That's about all I need to say, it's not the worst and it's not too hard but it's such an unengaging class.

this class was a lot of work tbh, be prepared for that. it's a significant workload increase from cs 124 imo.  be careful planning your schedule during this semester if you're not used to a higher workload as this class may take up much time. nowak himself is just "ok", typical college professor. not the best one but not the worst you'll have.

It's hard to learn anything from the course because he just throws videos at you and doesn't care to summarize what you've learned or answer questions. I once posted a brief forum question on why the answer to a question was correct, and he said, "Just go to office hours." (Yea, like I would actually waste my time to ask such a brief question)

It seems daunting at first, but it's honestly not too bad. Daily lessons are pretty intuitive and easy, just don't save them for the end of the week. MPs are difficult but rewarding. Quizzes are a bit stressful but aren't worth much of your grade. If you suffer in this as a CS major, you should probably switch majors.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Professor Nowak's handwriting is just terrible... that wouldn't be so bad if he didn't rely on it so much to teach. Reading Nowak-scratch is a necessary skill. That said, his lectures are clear and mostly to the point. He does an alright job: he's no Geoff Challen, but if you can decode his runes he can give a clear and informative lecture.

This class assigns plenty of daily and weekly HW. People complain because it takes several hours or more a week, but I think it really helps improve your programming ability. By the end you will have improved and learned a lot.

reviews.comment: The course overall is fine I think. The MP is just struggling.
