I don't know why this class even did so well. Should scrap
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Nowak was awful. I ended up dropping the class in the first few weeks after taking a quiz that did not work in the CBTF and he rudely told me, "you got the grade you deserved". Just inconsiderate. The limited time I had in the course was also bad; his handwriting is atrocious and his videos are way old. I know some have to take it but be aware.
Very bad course, outdated videos, machine projects that are more difficult than the level of the course. Since it's a required class and you don't have any choice, you're forced to take it.
There's a reason why comments and thumbs downs are disabled on his youtube channel

I could not understand what he wrote sometimes, it seemed like chicken scratch. Additionally, this class seemed pretty difficult as the MPs were tough, but rewarding. The concepts are not very hard to understand but the notes he use makes it much more difficult than it should have been. Btw, the notes he uses are video lectures from the pandemic.
I thought the lecture videos explained concepts very well, and the coding hw is sufficiently difficult. MPs are very tough, but rewarding if done with effort.

I had a major family emergency where one of my parents was in the hospital and I had to miss ten days of school, but Nowak did nothing to accommodate this, despite offering to give proof of the hospital stay. This, combined with his terrible teaching style made me fall too far behind to continue in the course. Congrats on weeding me out.

Wish I had this guy's job. Record a few videos 5 years ago and sit and do nothing while TAs try to make sense of the MPs that are way beyond the average level of skill of students. Outdated and terrible quality lecture videos, MPs are unsolvable without lots of help, and terrible experience overall.

There's a scene in every Spiderman movie where he has to choose between his hero-ing and his personal life, and for the low, low price of taking this course, you too can recreate this experience (Only, with less noble justification and even more sleepless nights).
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lecture videos are outdated and do not explain concepts properly, lecture activities do not supplement videos in any meaningful way, and CBTF quizzes and MPs are such a massive stes up in difficulty from lectures that I am convinced they are designed so that students cannot do them.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: it's WAY more than 3 credit hours. lecture videos are outdated, boring and don't teach how to apply logic. graded hw lessons have 1 opportunity to earn full marks. MPs are a huge jump from lesson hws. the TAs are carrying this class while nowak uploaded his videos years ago with unreadable handwriting. the worst class, huge letdown from 124
Awful video, Awful writing, Awful Professor

Prof Nowak is terrible in explaining concepts. I know it crucial in cs to learn concepts by searching stuff online, but tbh the rate of learning from Prof Nowak is surprisingly low compared to that of cs124.

The course website crashed, and he firstly only agreed to postpone that day's lesson by a mere day, instead of extending the deadline for the assignments due that day. The teaching is completely illogical, far inferior to the professors of course 124.

His handwriting is like worms. GPT explains a lot better than his teaching. MPs are very hard with tons of instructions. Discussion might be useful if your TA is experienced since it's a good opportunity to ask questions on MP. The difficulty level of this course matches a 300-level course.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The class has improved a lot after the changes this semester. Workload is very manageable, lecture videos are solid, and they provide you with plenty of support to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The videos are from years ago and the class structure is the opposite of engaging.

DO NOT be intimidated by the harsh reviews for Prof. Nowak and CS128. The changes he and Prof. Schlatz made to the course's pacing make it far more manageable than past semesters. If you watch the videos, do the lesson activities, and give yourself time on the MPs, you will succeed in this course and become a better programmer. Highly recommend.

The Structure of 128 suffers from many of CS124 issues, however with even less support from Nowak comparatively. Material is understandably difficult given the setting at a top CS university. However, the majority of the course being taught by 2 year old covid videos is disappointing for such a pivotal class. Makes the material a massive bore.
Why is this 100 level class?
use GPT or google as your teacher instead of him,

Prof. Nowak made a PRODUCT "CS128" and quit his interest to teach (and still gets paid). Outdated lectures (2 years old) and its purely ran by TAs. Other profs covid online lectures BUT still have live lectures as they're PASSIONATE about teaching and supporting a thriving learning environment. Sad a pivotal course is awfully structured/taught.

This is definitely a 4 credit hour class in case of workload, so manage your schedule wisely before taking that. Even Professor Nowak cannot understand what he wrote in the lecture sometimes, so treat Google/ Stack Overflow, etc. as your professor instead of him. Don't be expected to even get into office hours if an MP deadline is approaching!
I think he's more bearable when I watch him on 2x speed, otherwise he sounds depressed to me and is very unbearable.
Howdy Welcome Back!
nothing to say
Nowak was confusing at times, but I think he generally explained everything well. If you put the work into them and use your resources, the MPs are super rewarding.

Michael Nowak is not good teacher. I am not exaggerating when I say that ChatGPT explains basic computer science better than him. Quote me on that and put it in a headline, because it's true. I wish I could explain more but I have a character limit, but trust me. There were times were his teaching was so off I genuinely thought he was trolling.
Too much homework with confusing lecture, double confusing. The teacher is confusing, i am confusing and the entire class is just confusing.

CS 128 is a lot of work but is very straightforward in what you have to do to succeed. The weekly machine projects suck out your life force but it was always a reward to watch the video lessons with Nowak afterwards and do the coding HW. It's obvious how Nowak cares about the subject and he's grown on me some.

Nowak's CS 128 course is bad. Each week's MP cover much more than what the lectures cover, which would be fine if the office hours (which are the only source of help allowed) did not have a one hour queue at best. CS 128 is understaffed and asks too much of students without giving them the proper means to succeed.

As someone who got a 105% in every MP and 100% on very homework (hence not lazy), I hated this course. Content for what should be taught is genuinely interesting, but Nowak can't teach at all. Course consists of videos from 2 years ago which are very confusing. Nowak is more obsessed with Texas A&M then wanting his students to succeed.
CS 124 was a lot of fun for me, CS 128 was not. It is not easy AT ALL. This class is what made me stop pursuing a CS minor.

professor doesn't exist, help site is horrible and half the time i attended office hours the ta would tell me to just look it up and then leave immediately after i waited 30 mins to 2 hours to talk to someone

unfortunate that this class is a prereq to take other better classes
Don't trust someone complaining about CS128. They are just lazy and their brain is not big enough.

Prof Nowak is very amiable but not a good teacher. He reads from the slides and then draws in low quality and messy writing on them.
He assigns lectures/homework 6 days a week and then an MP each week which takes 8+ hours. There is little guidance on getting help from these MPs, and office hour queues are 1+ hour long.
Thank u for awesome hw, though it brings me huge pain, which is even better

The professor's handwriting is terrible. It's better to self-study all the stuff of C++ elsewhere rather than watch his video. MP is a little bit tricky and time-consuming. The course content is important but you should expect to learn most it while doing your MP.
Beware of this class. Only if you have an aptitude for CS, take it. Prof. Nowak is very demanding, his lectures contain his doctor-like handwriting and the MP's are a task.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: very bad experience
A really bad lecturer and bad hand writing:) Don't expect to learn anything from his lecture:)

He isn't that bad at first. Then you're crying at 11:59 because you've been stuck on 9/10 test cases for hours. He gives you the most convoluted homework question to explain how classes work, when there is a MUCH easier way to solve the problem. You have 6 DAYS of lectures, and an MP every week. Not to mention, he keeps bringing up Texas A&M. Why??

If you put in the work and do all the assignments it's trivial to get a good grade. High amount of programming practice that forces you to improve. The lectures are terribly boring videos from 2 years ago and I never bothered to watch them. Make sure to start the MPs asap. OK class, biggest plus is it makes you a much better programmer.

Most complaints about Prof. Nowak is essentially complaints about CS 128. I have no prior experience with C++ but I find the homework and coding projects pretty easy. I only spend 5-6 hours on the MP per week. If you can't do the MPs, maybe you're simply not good enough at coding.

They got rid of a ton of the course from last semester and somehow the class is still incredibly demanding. It requires well over 20-30 hours per week to complete the weekly MPs. It would be enjoyable if not for the constant stress caused by this class. At least the content is interesting...
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Be ready to put on time for few weeks. But the richness of the course is awesome, it prepares and build logic thoroughly. Loved his lectures, only thing that he is very specific about few things which can frustate you.
too hard for entry-level, 40hr work for people not with strong cs background. prof. is too arrogant,

Howdy, welcome back!
I would give a 5/5 for Mr.Howdy if he improves his handwriting...
C++ is not hard, but just his handwriting...

Sadly, I think this has the potential to be a really good class. It's just too much work. Prepare to spend a full week hacking through an MP, and just when you finish, get handed another one. On top of 40ish min lectures (with homework qs) 6 days a week. I'm growing as a programmer but prepare for WAY more than a 3 credit workload.
not the best teacher
Lots of people view the course as difficult, but if you're interested in the material, it's quite enjoyable. He's a great professor, I've had some good conversations with him.
Could've taught the class so much nicer & better. Absolute moron.

CS128 is a bonkers grind that requires a lot of reading and practice. Online lecture videos are 30-45 minutes every day, and MPs are honestly quite brutal. Be prepared to learn and practice every day. It definitely feels like more than a 3 credit hour class. If you understood the concepts in CS 124 well enough, then 128 should be okay for you.

reviews.comment: I do not recommend taking this class with this professor over the summer. The lectures are long and monotone so you can't focus during them. The instructions for the MPs are so confusing and I have had to go to office hours almost every single day to fully understand what is expected of us. He doesn't explain well and is very confusing.

reviews.comment: His lectures are very monotone which forced me to watch them multiple times to actually retain anything. Also, the instructions for the MPs are basically useless and I had to go to office hours almost every day to fully understand them. I definitely do not recommend especially over the summer.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: tooooo many stutters to understand. The logic is also awful. sometimes find it difficult to recognize the accent. too much workload to make. even worse than finding a free online class.

Pros:
1. Awesome help site + website (similar to CS 124)
2. Offer 2 late penalties waiver for MP
3. 3 lab drop + 3 quiz drop + some daily HW drop


Cons:
1. Weekly MP, Lab, Quiz, and daily HW (15 hours minimum per week)
2. MPs are thiccccc bois
3. Lecture is tedious
4. HARDCORE C++ class (will be easier if you took CS 124)
Sure a lot of work but no different than other core courses in CS and gives many drops
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: There is a crap ton of work but most of it is based on completion. Lectures can be long but they all have slides that you can easily reread. I found this class to be very fulfilling tbh. We get multiple drops for HW/Quizzes/Labs. Definitely recommend it as long as your schedule isn't already heavy.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Really rigorous course but worth it in the end. Feel like there were times when I was frustrated but in the end, the course did end up making me a better programmer. However, if you're not a cs major I do feel like there should be an alternate course for a minor or something as managing this class with another technical class can be hard.

The number of hours you put into this class alone will be roughly equivalent to a part-time job. The workload is downright insane. That being said, Nowak himself is a fun professor and I enjoyed his lectures. It really is a shame that the MPs were such a crippling amount of work, because otherwise I think it'd be a pretty great class.

For a 3 credit hour class, Nowak assigns at least 45 minutes of lectures each day with a hw problem along with it that can take a long time sometimes. Labs are like a mini MP with a lot of work. The MP can vary in difficulty with some taking a couple days and some can be finished in a couple hours. 

TLDR: Too much work for a 3 hour class

I'm sure he's a great professor -- he clearly gets excited in his videos from last semester. Is he good at constructing a class? No, the load is absurd. Is he caring for his students? Well, kind of. He answers questions in a forum, and you can email him regarding private manners. Does he hold office hours? No, other undergraduate students do that.
His handwriting is so badddd

He's usually a pretty good professor but his lectures just need to better reflect the content of labs and mps. I learned more from one online blog post about smart pointers than I did the entire week of 4 lectures.
Howdy and welcome back!
Terrible. Probably not even a day where I didn't have a panic attack doing the MPs. He has no consideration for students' mental health, and the amount of work he gives is absurd. DON'T TAKE.
omg it's my first time learning bfs and dfs, and I have to write a huge MP during my midterm week...

The problem is not him but the course. He is good teacher that always helping students and his lectures are great. The problem is the workload is just too too much. Lab will take you 2-3 hr alone, then MP will take you many days, then 4 lecture with long activity every week. Also 1 short quiz 1h. Too much work.
He thinks this is our only class of the semester. Besides that he is an amazing professor
seriously way too much work its worse than 400 level CS courses
He is kind and helpful but the machine problems are too too difficult compared to what we learn in lecture. i have no time to do other classwork
Those negative comments about workload is true, but also it would really improve your coding ability a lot if you go through this tough process.

All lectures are pre-recorded and has made no attempt to optimize them. Quite monotonous in tone and relies on CAs to do the actual teaching. Having had lots of prior coding experience (but no C++), this class was unnecessarily difficult. 

Get Ready for: 1 Hour Daily Lectures, Daily Homework Problem, Weekly Quiz, Lab, and MPs

Don't let the influx of negative ratings skew your perception of Nowak's CS 128. Although the class is a lot of work, you will gain a thorough understanding of C++. I do, however, suggest watching the lectures in 1.5x speed as Nowak talks slowly. If you are an above-average student with previous coding experience, you won't struggle much in CS 128.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
no amount of "Howdy"s will distract us from the fact that your lecture videos are overly long and unhelpful and that you assign way too much work for a 3 credit hour course. 

and go do something about your hair.
Way too much work given just for an introductory course. The content is mundane and makes me lose my interest in the subject.

Bruh too much workload and overly hard for beginners of C++. Not everyone is a pro here and he expects people to know so much about computer science and his explanation is just terrible and unhelpful and I am just not sure what I am listening to everyday.
It must be so easy to have your class be all prerecorded lectures and CAs teaching the actual content while you do nothing all day

Huge workload for a 3 credit hour intro class. Average 1 hour of lecture video a day. Daily homework lessons, weekly labs, weekly projects that take 10+ hours average. On top of that a weekly quiz. Far too much work for a 3 credit hour intro class.
poor explanation. lots of workload

This course is very useful as it teaches C++ comprehensively. However lectures are incredibly long (1 hour every day), and the workload of this class is also huge, one MP, one lab and one quiz for every week. This is definitely a 4 hour class but not 3.
Lecture every day & 4+hrs Lab & 10+hrs MP & 1hrs Quiz = 3 hrs credit
Completely outdated 90's teaching style. Unhelpful TAs.

This class is just way too hard for a C++ beginner... Lectures are long and pretty boring, MPs (and some Labs) are hard for beginners and really time-consuming. 
Prof. Nowak is a good professor - he encouraged me when I reached out for help on forum.
I know you(and I) are required to take this course as a CS(+X) major, so good luck people.

He teaches okay for the most part, but his lectures are way too long and a lot of the daily lessons could be much shorter if he valued the student’s time. Also, be prepared to do a lot of work even though it is only a 3 credit course. You will be spending roughly up to 20 or more hours weekly on this class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: For an intro to computer science two class the workload is insane. It's crazy to think the current iteration of the class is a reduced workload version of the pilot offering last year. The time sink this three credit hour class has become has been mind boggling.

The lecture system is abysmal and hard to follow many times. Course work is also way higher than typical classes and the assignments are often confusing or redundant. This class is basically impossible for beginner cs students.
One of the worst teachers I've had in 3 years. Needlessly difficult. Incredible load for no reason. If he ever gets a class outside of CS128 -- AVOID. Sorry if you're stuck with him.
This class has a far too intense workload for a 3 credit hours.

Avoid at all costs. Run. I mean it, run. If you can't, make sure you're already a C++ expert by the time the course starts. Otherwise, MPs will take up all day every day, TAs are both hard to access and a lot of them are as confused as you are, and the professor's only advice is to watch his videos, which are super long, and unhelpful. Good luck

The class is structured so atrociously shit. He chooses to make students lives a pain in the ass by not including make files and starter code. The lessons are at times poorly worded. There’s literally no sense of community in the class. MPs are unnecessarily long and vague. It's many aspects of this class that make it absolutely dreadful.

This class is an absolute waste of time, and I am not sure how he was able to get his job. There are too many lectures, the MP's are straight up ridiculous and interfere in my time to do many other things. For a CS major, I am worried for my career and my future due to this absolute nonsense of a class.

I would say avoid him at all cost but you likely won't have a choice. It's a required class and you basically have to teach yourself everything through the videos. Don't expect to get help since at least half the time the queue is frozen as ice and on top of that there are rules on when you can and can't go to office hours

Good luck with his 5 - 9 lessons (each lesson with 30 - 60 minutes videos and with multiple choice questions or coding homework), 1 quiz (varies from 20 to 40 lines of code, and maybe out of contents), 1 lab(at least 30 lines of code) and 1 MP(varies from 100 lines to 400 lines of code) every week!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Came mid semester to comment that the lectures are always longer than they need to be because he fails to get to the important teaching points effectively. The lecture questions are too often slightly irrelevant to what he's directly talking about or worded very strangely.The lectures are just straight up boring.

The class was just so boring in so many ways. There are so many ways he chooses certain aspects of the lab and MPs to be unnecessarily hard and hard to understand and it's such a waste of time. It's so frustrating and annoying I've never experienced this in a class before. For Intro II CS class, it sure did not ignite my passion for CS.
Terrible professor

Absurd amount of lecture time, especially compared to later CS courses. Not a good understanding of his students level - overexplains certain things and leaves you confused in others. Asisgmnets are hastily made and often rely on you having watched 50 minutes/day of specific material that you won't use again.

Very difficult to get from a TA/CA. Prof cannot give much useful feedback if you are stuck with MP, he would tell u to review his lesson again(which, btw, onlined version and I wonder if he would update them each semester). So you are basically on your own for this class and shall not anticipate too much help from course staff.

A really good lecturer. Really no problems with him as a teacher, but more as a course organizer: having warm-up exercises along with each lecture would prove really helpful since most coders learn better by trying and failing at applying new concepts rather than watching lectures.

Nowak is one of the most passionate professors I have met. He is always super quick to respond on the discussion forum, and his online lectures are as good as they get. Unfortunately, they are phasing out TRAs, which were probably the nicest kind of assignments since they involved fewer but smarter lines of code.

If you are comfortable with basic programming before this class, it isn't too hard. I found the lectures very useful, but they aren't necessary and you can just read the slides he posts. The class is a solid amount of work: an MP, TRA, and lab weekly (about 10hrs total/wk) + 200 minutes of lecture. If you put in the work, it's a fairly easy A.

I think all of the reviews here are slightly harsh considering FA21 is the pilot semester for CS128. That being said, Nowak's lectures aren't exactly fun or innovative. He isn't inspirational and seems to have a problem answering your questions on the forum. Nothing exceptional. The class is a lot of work but it definitely teaches you a lot.

His class, CS 128 is run abysmally. No support whatsoever. Office hours (for later MPs) are a 3-4 hour wait and often the queue is just closed. Forum answers are helpful at times, but is a slow and clumsy way to communicate, like email. The professor, from my experience, is old fashioned in his teaching approach and not willing to change.

Heavy MPs, incompetent professor. He changed the deadline early 4 hours of "checkpoint" of MPs without any form of notification just because his student asked him why the deadline of the checkpoint is different from normal assignments. His lectures are also boring, feeling lucky that I know most of the course materials and not watching his lectures

Lectures are fine but all prerecorded, and honestly professor is not understanding at all of student responsibilities and life. As someone who has already taken 225, the material/mps are a breeze but it still irks me how poorly run this course is and the professor seems a bit narrowminded in his approach towards teaching and interacting w students

He's not the worst lecturer on the planet but he has no idea how to run a class. Office hours are constantly backed up and he provides almost no help to students on the forum, telling them to just re watch the lessons even though the solutions aren't there. He also scheduled the most difficult MP of the semester during exam week.

the absolute goat. so accommodating, hilarious, really makes it clear that she's a support outlet and lets you take initiative of how you want to take her class. take this class take this class take this class take this class

Mary is amazing. She knew all her class materials and gives  excellent lectures. The exams are not very easy, but as long as you pay effort, you will receive a fair grade. She's inspirational and energetic, take her class!

Dr. Ramey is my favorite professor I had in my entire time at the U if I. She is funny, caring, and makes lectures interesting and applicable to students' lives. I took both classes she teaches and she is just the best! You get to decide how much your assignments/exam/participation is weighted via a grading contract, which sets you up for success.

Dr. Ramey was the best for family comm! She is funny and makes the class fun for lectures. There are some weekly readings and short response to them but they are not difficult. You can get away with not buying the text book! You can also choose what weight the assignments have on your grade so you can really do well in her class. 100% recommend!

Dr.Ramey is one of THE BEST comm professors at UIUC. I took her CMN 368 class and CMN 336 and both were awesome! CMN lecture was in person as well as the discussion but all the exams were online as well as lecture reflection videos on flip. She is very informative and a great professor overall. Attendance is mandatory for notes.

Spring 2022. Overall, good professor with lots of knowledge. This class is actually exactly like its name, and you learn a lot about sexual communication from gender, cultural, sexuality, race, and etc. standpoints. Lecture is online discussion was in person. There are exams, participation, video response, and papers in this course.
This class was amazing and the lectures were always super funny and informational. This class WILL help your personal and romantic relationships, 10/10 highly recommended!!!

Dr. Ramey is an amazing lecturer and she genuinely cares about her students. There are weekly assignments, so be sure to keep up with those, but they are for credit so if you do them, you'll get an A. Notes aren't available online so attendance is mandatory to get the notes. Overall would take this class again if I could, I loved it!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Don't expect any response or communications through email. Throughout the course of my 2020 semester, she responded very few times to my emails.

Dr. Ramey is awesome and funny and approaches the topic of sexual communication in a down to earth way. Her lectures are very thought provoking. However, she did make one comment about bisexuality that seemed uninformed.
I love this woman with my whole heart. Her class was my favorite all semester. I looked forward to watching her lectures and even doing the work. Cant say enough good things about her.

Dr. Ramey is probably my favorite professor at UIUC. The material and the way she teaches is genuinely interesting. She wants it to be interesting and she wants you to learn; rather than just shoving information down your throat like other profs. She's very understanding and never overwhelms you with work. Take any class with her that you can!!!

Yeah, she is great at teaching but it takes her ages to respond to her emails. She doesn't seem to exactly try to connect with each of her students probably because it's a big class. She seems very distant from the student's point of view.

One of the best professors I've had at uiuc! The lectures are interesting and she's a really funny and engaging person. There's a grading contract which means we got to pick what percentage of our grade tests/papers/participation were worth. I got a 100% on a test and she emailed me and it was just really sweet. Take her classes!!
As long as you show up and do the homework you basically get an A. The course and material itself are so easy. Ramey is HILARIOUS and so sweet.

Funny relatable professor, I thorouly enjoyed taking her class and would take any class she teaches again. In addition, I advise that you pick her discussion class bc she superrrrr chill in discussion too :)))

Absolutely take this class for your advanced hours or for your major. She's makes the 9am bearable. The content for 368 is very straightforward and easy to do well on the exams. She lets you decide how much everything is worth for your grade. Attendance is 5% of your grade minimum. Discussions aren't mandatory if you do a super easy paper. LOVE her

She is AMAZING. I didn't like interpersonal communication classes, but her lecture was different. She has tons of examples to illustrate the course material and she is very funny as well. I laughed at least once in her class. Exams are very straight-forward and there are many papers to write, but the content is interesting.

Dr. Ramey is the best professor I have had in all of my time at UIUC. Aside from the fact that she is hilarious, kind, and just wonderful overall, she is also an amazing instructor who knows how to make her lectures interesting and engaging. If you pay attention in class and take good notes, you should be set to get an A.

I LOVE Professor Ramey! The examples she uses throughout class are relatable & it makes it easy to remember the material. She is a fair grader and hilarious. The course consists of 10 reaction papers, 3 exams, 1 research paper, and a self reflection. Make sure you participate (it counts)!! I would take this course again if I could, 10/10 RECOMMEND

Dr. Ramey is my favorite professor of all time!  She is smart, knows her material extremely well, and is absolutely hilarious.  She's well respected and is so much fun!  Highly recommend both of her classes!

Dr. Ramey loves teaching her classes and shows lots of expertise without being boring. She lets you break down your grade via percentages in the beginning of the course. The lecture is only twice a week and discussion is once a week. Overall I enjoyed the class, great choice if you need an elective for the semester. The material is useless as well.

Professor Ramey is that type of professor that makes you pay attention. She presents the material in a way that is engaging, though-provoking, and interesting. I wish I could take all the classes she teaches bc she is just that good at her job. The class isn't an easy A but it's difficult to get less than a B-. Show up + Get involved  Pass class.

She is a great professor. I am really happy I took her class, she gives you note outlines and lets you pick how much your assignments are worth. 3 exams, test aren't too bad if you take notes and attend class. You can get away with not doing the readings, no book for this class either.

Awesome course and Professor. 3 exams and she lets each student weight their grade. 1 paper due at the end of the semester. You can choose if you want your attendance in discussion to count or if you'd rather write 2 short essays. Interesting info and pretty easy for an advance level course.

This class was extremely interesting!! I loved it. It is easily relatable to your everyday life. She is one of my favorite professors. Very funny and makes lecture interesting. She lets you use your notes on tests which is awesome. Be ready to do a TON of writing in this class though! No textbook. Take her sexual cmn class too, it's awesome!

She is an awesome professor! Very knowledgeable at this subject and makes class very fun. You get to pick what percent of your grade you want each test and assignment to be so that calls for a good grade. This class was so interesting and I learned a lot. I highly recommend taking this! Don't need a text book, you just use fill in the blank notes.

Professor Ramey is the best professor I've had in my 4 years at UIUC. Her course material is very clear and interesting. She is a very fair grader and in most cases as long as you have understanding of the readings and take notes you're fine. Exams for either class are not hard as long as you actually pay attention in class.

There's no textbook; she posts all the readings online. Most of your grade depends on how easy your t.a. grades. Class is pretty easy overall. I stopped going to class and got an 89 on the final. 5-7 page paper due towards the end of the semester. I would recommend the class.
Easy grader and interesting professor. Overall, it is a easy A course that you should take

This class is a joke. It's way too easy and basically all the material is common sense. Take this class if you want an easy A without really learning much. One mandatory 7+ page paper, 3 exams. You create your own grading contract.

Just attend the class, take notes, attend discussion section for additional notes and comments.  You have to decisions to make your own grading policy; percentage of assignment and exams.  You have to take all exams and submit at least one assignment.
Excellent teacher. Fun, energetic, and makes material very relevant. Good sense of humor. This is not a hard class if you just do the readings and participate every once in a while.

Awesome teacher. She will surely bring a smile to your face even at 8 am on a dark and gloomy day. Though, she may be leaving soon to obtain a higher paying job elsewhere so take 101 with her as soon as possible. Very nice, very kind, and very understanding. Also, did I mention she has a great sense of humor? ;-)
No Comments
Extremely clear and understandable.  She has an interesting sense of humor, but she's so nice and helpful.  Class was fun with her.  Definitely one of my easiest classes!
Title IX needs to look into him.

The worst Game Theory class ever. Hard to understand what he is trying to convey. He spend two hours talking about Nash Equilibrium and I still could not understand it. However, I just spend 5 minutes read the book and everything is much more clear. He talks in a plain tone, so many of us fall asleep easily in the lecture.
Good lectures, homework is hard but few.

Great professor! However, lectures can wander off topic quite often. Doesnt give too much work, and nice curve. Homeworks can be difficult be are very interesting as he challenges you to come up with sometimes odd solutions to tricky problems.

Bro had no original thoughts, the entire class was written by AI. He just pasted his prompts into a google doc for lectures and didn't even have the decency to put it in a slideshow. The grading was also super slow, but at least the class was easy.

This class is a joke. Every assignment, announcement, and post is generated completely by AI. I'm not even sure Michael proof-reads any of the assignments he posts, as there are countless mistakes. I do not know why this guy is getting paid 90k a year to be a ChatGPT wrapper.

Puts very little effort into the class and over utilizes AI to the extent that I'm not sure he actually knows what he's assigning (because he can't answer questions about them). Poorly organized class and lecture is blabber. He's not malicious but definitely impressively lazey, so he won't put effort to help you fix his mistakes.
Lectures prove you don't need to be an llm to produce ai slop

This is a really easy course (at least for me given our TA). The professor doesn't really matter for this course, it's just whoever your group mentor is. The prof is pretty funny in lecture even if you barely ever end up listening to the lecture.

This professor rly didn't do anything-good or bad. The course itself was always so unorganized. The deadlines kept shifting as well as expectations. They weren't that responsive on campuswire either. Honestly, it was just a semester long group project with some lecture quizzes that weren't that relevant. Didn't walk away with a whole lot.

The bulk of the course grade is from the group project's weekly updates, that is graded by a TA. This professor's lectures are fine: not super fun, but I appreciate the content. The quizzes can be done by just referring to the lecture slides. I think the professor is cool, and my group was lucky to have a chill and understanding TA for the project.

Lectures are really useless and taught completely by TA. Quizzes are super easy and can be answered from a quick glance at the slides. Knock out your project early so you don't have to worry about it during finals :)
He's a really nice professor but his class is not very useful.
There is no help in this class. The class is not structured properly and unnecessarily stressful. Learned absolutely nothing from this class.

Professor Woodley is great at communicating if you reach out to him, but the structure of CS 126 has been frustrating. The grading rubric is incredibly subjective and the only solution is to email your code moderator with every step. There is no time between projects and we aren't taught the material necessary to complete them.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Lectures are merely a quick recap of the readings, he don't go into detail at all. The lectures themselves are not particularly useful.

Very hands off, delegating way too much responsibility to the course staff, who were generally unhelpful and did an awful job designing the assignments. His lectures are fine and the class isn't too difficult if you put in the required time, but the way that he runs the course vastly overcomplicates what should be an incredibly easy class.

Lectures were barely helpful during the first month of class. I stopped attending after they continued to be that way. Didn't bother to watch any of them later nor did I need to. I ended up having to use a ton of outside sources to work on my weekly projects. After 6 weeks, we switched from Java to C++. Pretty much expected to learn C++ overnight.

He never responds to emails and solely trusts senior staff and your grader to deal with your issues. He was often distracted during lectures and many times came with broken code. This made lecture even more confusing than it needed to be. Most people only came for iClicker points...

terrible prof, doesnt do anything in lectures, the TA's do all the work and theyre very bad at communication. dont teach you how to code at all, documentation for MP's are often wrong/not helpful. office hours havent helped at all. feel bad for any future students since unfortunately cs 126 is mandatory for cs majors.

The class would function better without the professor. The projects were created by the former instructor. Woodley's lectures are rambling nonsense and are mainly about himself - you have to learn everything on your own. If you are assigned a tough code mod, you will get the same grade as someone who put in much less work but had a lenient grader.

While people are talking about grading, it's his lectures that are his weakest. It's very unorganized and he would just talk for half an hour about random stories, which are rarely helpful. This semester, he realized that nobody cares about his lectures so he had to start doing iClicker to force students to come in.
Great lectures!
Best professor in UIUC!

Extremely unfair grading. What grade you get totally depends on if your section TA likes you or not. You might do much more than other students in your section but get a lower grade than them. It's a joke they say you can argue about that, because you know if you ever argue with the TA he/she will be more picky about you next time.
I loved the lectures. The assignments were also very practical and taught me a lot. Well-planned and organized course. I highly recommend this to any CS major that wants to enter industry.
He is an amazing lecturer. Sad that this is the only class that he teaches. I hope he teaches more classes.
My friend in another discussion section got the same grade as I did (did all requirements) for just showing up in the discussion. :))))))))))))
Fake extra credit, students beware.

Lectures are a joke, he reads off the outdated course website and tells anecdotes. Grading and Piazza are 100% handled by the TAs. Your only actual teacher in this class is StackOverflow, since even the documentation the class provides in insufficient. Professor is a complete non-factor in this class, be ready to learn a lot of code on your own.

There's no actual grade normalization. This means that you get whatever grade your moderator gives you, which means certain moderators will definitely give you a higher grade and you don't get to pick.
He didn't keep up with what was previously mentioned. Way too much subjectivity and no actual normalization.
Keeps lectures short and realizes if he doesn't have anything else to say. Casual attitude in class which was enjoyable.
Class is hard, but really approachable since professor is mainly looking for your attitude towards this class. Strongly recommanded
Awesome prof. Comes very well prepared. Strongly recommend taking AE 353 with Prof. Ornik

Awesome professor. Awesome TA. Awesome projects. Very practical and at the same time strikes a very good balance between theory and implementation. The favorite part about the course were the projects.

I took the course in FA23 and it has to be one of the best, most organized, well taught courses. Enjoyed every bit of it from the professor's teaching to TA's project explanations to professor's feedbacks. There was plenty of opportunities to learn and better ourselves.

Really great lectures, is well prepared and explains things clearly with the use of example problems. The projects in this class are very fun, and he gives plenty of opportunity to earn extra credit on both projects and tests.

Prof Ornik explains concepts well and if you are looking to learn about optimization, without lot of math rigor, I recommend taking AE504. He is pretty focused on whether students are learning the material & he makes lot of effort to ensure you are not wasting time by attending his lectures.
It's was alright
In all my years as an undergraduate, I never felt I understood concepts to such details! He's dope.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Man knows his stuff

doesn't do anything except nothingburger "conversations" with students. doesn't teach anything, doesn't try to work on actual designing, no organization, doesn't post grades EVER, doesn't give clear instructions, has no interest in doing anything besides working on her own personal stuff during class time

She is inconsistent, gives approval for project ideas only to tell you to change the entire idea the next class, this happens often. It's a waste of time. She also wastes our time by being late to every meeting, usually by almost 10 minutes. She gets away with teaching literally zero skills by saying that what actually matters is networking.
Disorganized class, no communication, the class focus is not about learning, and even if it does teach you something, it has no value. There is no gain from taking her courses in general.
Super easy to engage with in class not boring at all catered to student needs and gives good feedback
Half of the reviews are lies she's a free A class of you just do your stuff

She never communicated with her students. How do I take class with you while you never reply any email?? She gives no care about her students. The design of curriculum is not clear and hard to follow. I don't know how good her art is but she sucks as a professor.

I don't know why she never reply the Email. Even if I asked when can we talk about my grade and other course related questions inperson, she said she will reply my email later after class, but there is still nothing in my inbox. Also the course setting is awful, as a designer, she even don't tell us how to design, but tell us to training AI.

Extremely disorganized class timeline. Does not respond to emails. The assignments kept changing, requirements and expectations were not fully communicated, or she would contradict herself. Has frequently cancelled class only hour before, or wasted an hour of class time to take care of things that should be dealt with outside of class.

It's incredibly frustrating to be in a class where nothing is explained clearly, leaving students feeling lost and overwhelmed. To make matters worse, with only three days left until the semester ends, no grades have been posted. The combination of poor communication, disorganization, and a lack of transparency feels disrespectful students.
has no syllabus. I feel like all her classes use the same PowerPoint and barely organized class structure. She's nice tho

Amazing. Put 20pt extra credit on midterm 3 which boosted my grade above 100%. There was no homework so it's up to you to participate and make sure you understand. Significantly lessens the busy-work burden on strong self learners but punishes slackers who need to be forced to do practice to succeed.

When I took ECE374B, there was no homework and grades were based on quizzes and exams. The content was challenging, but the professor explained concepts clearly and worked through examples in lecture. Quizzes and exams were tough, but fair. Great professor, difficult course.

Class structure was not good. Lectures are not mandatory. Lab is not mandatory. We have 2 quizzes a week in lab. 3 midterms and no final. Quizzes are relatively simple, but it's hard to figure out what exactly you should study for. Exams are relatively difficult, but are curved. Our curves were 6 pts, 16 pts, and 1 pt respectively.

the content in this class is very challenging, which is expected, but kani does a great job teaching it. lectures and labs were helpful, and the practice material made things clearer. the course felt organized and fair, and it wasn't as overwhelming as it first seemed

Literally one of the best professors I've ever had. Extremely clear in his lecturing style. Also very patient with answering questions. He used to curve exams to have average 70, which means even if you don't do well, if everyone else scored low you don't have to worry. He's also just a very kind person in general

he spends 20-30 minutes going over the prelecture question, so the last 30 minutes of lecture are super rushed. he frequently mentions content before we actually cover it, which makes it hard to understand current content. skips thru lost of slides. 75% exams and 25% quizzes with the retake replacing even if its lower made this class awful. 👎👎👎

With every year, his tests get longer. Two years ago, his exams were 4-5 questions long. This semester, each test is 7-8 questions long. That is, with the same duration. I don't understand how he expects us to finish the test and do well. Not to mention, exams and quizzes make up our whole grade.

Your whole grade is based on midterms and quizzes. I think it's insane how the grade you get in the class is based off three 75 minute exams and a bunch of 5 min quizzes. Though, he does provide you with a cheatsheet created by him for the midterms. Also, the actual midterms aren't REMOTELY close to the previous/practice exams he provides us with.

One of the best professors in the ECE department. Clearly cares about his students and explains all his policies thoughtfully. Good lectures and very well-run course website. Content is tough but he makes it manageable.
Used lots of examples in his lectures which I liked.
Please finish asynchronous videos.
If you attend lectures and discussions, getting a A is pretty easy. HWs are tough but I just used drops on tougher problems and it was fine.
Great lecturer and all the content is available online. Lots of practice exams and recordings so its not essential to go to lecture, but it is pretty easy to fall behind if you don't.

Good lecturer. Terrible course backend. TAs freely cancel office hours without finding someone to substitute. Kani needs to get control over his staff. The homework questions are okay, but the solution keys are terrible. TAs will blindly cite the key, even though much of the time the key barely answers the question (it would barely get half credit)
Made a very hard class manageable for the students. Criteria was clear, curve was generous. The best lecturer for ECE 374, makes confusing concepts easier with his great example problems.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I enjoyed his midterm drop policy.
I wish he teaches CS473 or CS475.

He is the GOAT. Course policies are very lenient and fair but be warned, he will probably not make exceptions. Lot of students get turned off because of his rigidity but I'd rather have 4 exams with one drop than 3 exams with conflicts.
Great lectures, reasonable amount of work. Learned a lot and went back through his notes for my leetcode prep.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I don't think he is a great lecturer since he just mostly reads off of the lecture slides and often assumes that we are already familiar with certain topics that he never covered, some of which I don't think we were expected to know. He is relatively lenient on grading though, but I think properly teaching us the topics is more important.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's one of the nicest professor out there.
Care about students, very clear explanation for algorithms, getting an A- and above would be easier than section A.

He definitely cares about the students and the stuff he is teaching, in fast, he tries all he can to make the course more meaningful (I think he is still trying, there is a long tough way to go). Even though the course content is absolutely hard, I personally still learned a lot from spending time and attending lectures. Kani is a great professor.

Kani just really cares about his students and the content. You can tell how much he wants to change the course for the better, too. In general, the exams are hard, but the homework and discussions do prepare you for them. I struggled sometimes, but I really learned and enjoyed the content.

Overall, I would take him again 100%.

Although this is a hard class he has been(t an amazing professor throughout. He is truly someone who cares about his students and makes a very difficult class fair. I was struggling a lot towards the middle of the class and reached out and he really took the extra time to help me succeed.
A nice guy. Could be a better lecturer especially closer to the end of the semester, but far from bad. Truly does care about students, which is more than I can say about most professors.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: (finally) I personally didn't come close to an 100+, yet I can certainly say I learned a lot from Kani's section. I can also say I probably understand algorithms better than many A section students (including the emotional student behind that most likely bullshit review). And content-wise, this IS a pretty-very challenging class.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: (pt 3) Despite Kani's generous curve (which seemed to have piss off some A students-but like, if you care so much about your class grade, you should've taken his section instead of complaining profusely in the A section LUL), it is very difficult to get above 100: no one just "gets it easily."

(continued) And to get above a 100, you *definitely* need to understand the material very well & learn a lot (I personally know some of the special 16 students who got above 100). So I can rule them out as not the salty keyboard warrior behind that review. Additionally, no one "got over 100% easily,"

The review below complaining Kani's section being "so easy I got above 100" is 100% bullshit-I'm sure it's coming from a comically salty A section student, who isn't even in the B section. How do I know? It's very difficult to get above 100 raw score-only 16 students out of hundreds of B section students were able to.
He is a patient, caring student, and generous professor.
Difficult class as expected. Extremely flexible and lenient grading policies.

I don't know why he has such a low score here. Overall good prof, caring students, and taught me a lot, turning a hard algorithm class into a fun class (although it is still hard and you should put all your effort into the class). He gives a good grade to everyone. He put tremendous effort into the course, and I would say his hardworking paid off.
Has no caring for the student. Doesn't care about students's mental health at all. Also, I don't think he can score more than 60 in the 374 exams. Doesn't know the materials. Bad professor

This class has a reputation for reusing old homeworks and tests and I think most people took it with that in mind. After the first midterm I realized old materials wouldn't help me and just made sure I knew the coreconcepts presented lectures which helped a lot. Tests were definitely hard but the final grade was very generous I think....

I am not really sure why students are filled with hate. I think the course was absolutely fine, same goes for the professor. Most students wanted to work on HW and exams that are borrowed from previous years, which Prof. did not do. if you want to apply yourself and work hard, take this course and prof.
He made our final with problems and concepts we had never seen before to challenge us to see who deserves to pass. All because students complained that they couldnt read his handwriting lol

Personally, I think the LING 100 professor is very helpful — she will carefully answer your questions during office hours and outside of class。 However, since exams make up 60% of the grade and the midterm and final have very few questions, it means you need to get almost everything right to earn an A. But overall, LING 100 is a very easy course.

Professor Newkirk is clearly knowledgeable, but her explanations were often hard to grasp for students new to linguistics. The single midterm and final made up most of the grade. I know people including myself who studied hard for the final and found it more complicated than what we had done in class.

She is a good professor teaching LING100: Intro to Language Science. Her handouts are very useful. But maybe because of the restraint of time and intro-level nature of the course, some explanation of materials is not sufficient to Linguistics students. Overall, very good, recommended for students not majoring in LING to take for GenEds

I disliked this teacher very much. He did not explain the material well at all and sped through the powerpoints. unreasonably tough in expectations and demeanor. He seems unhappy in his role as a teacher.

The class wasnt organized at all. He posted the review for the final exam on Friday, when many people were taking it on Monday. He groups the in person and online class together when they should not be. They are two separate classes and should not be according to one another. I would not recommend his online class to anyone.
He broadline ignores his online students and grades almost purely off tests. If you're a bad test taker, I would recommend looking elsewhere
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: TA for Astronomy classes. He is okay just a little boring and forces group projects and discussion
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: This professor's lecture is not clear. Taking photos is not allowed in class, even if there is reflection.

This teacher does not allow cell phone photos in class. Students are not allowed to take pictures of the board to record the board. Even if you sit in the back and can't see, you are not allowed to take pictures. It's really mean.

I really enjoyed this class but is was definitely difficult. He is a nice person and I think he is a great lecturer and is very passionate about the subject. Although, the homework is due at 1pm on Wednesdays and is very exam heavy.

This class was definitely difficult but manageable. He gave many examples during class, many which were close to the questions on the actual exam. Homework is due in the middle of the day, and are done during exam weeks as well.

Grading was way too harsh. Literally no partial credit. I normally get A+ in math courses with ease, so the A- was quite disappointing. Spent 10+ hrs/week, solutions in the homework were super long and arduous, and this doesn't match up with the 441 experience of students from other sections at all. I'd recommend you take a different section.
Passionate lecturer. Exams cover basic concepts but grading is a little harsh. Homework takes a while. Overall a fun guy.
80% test scores on average one question in exam = 10% in total grade. Not easy exams and massive homeworks. Unless spend 15+ hours per week or no A.

Professor Tzirakis is definitely one of the most passionate and caring professors I have had. That being said, the course is made to feel a lot more difficult than it needs to be. I felt that concepts that could have been explained in a simple manner were overly complicated. The homework are difficult and the course is a lot of work.
VERY GOOD!
nice

If this is your first experience with differential equations, avoid professor Tzirakis. Infact, avoid this professor either way. Egotistical and hard to believe he gives any second thought to his student's success and the quality of his teachings. You will find yourself completely reliant on the textbook and other material found on the internet.

Honestly one of the worst professor that has taught a class I have taken. Maybe it was because it was the first semester we went online but idk. Very rude and combative in his response to questions. To be fair, I stopped going to lecture a couple of weeks in, but even when I went I thought they were fairly useless. Got by studying the textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Some of the least well prepared or helpful lectures I've had, attacks students who ask questions, and combative when questioned. I never actually asked him a question after seeing his response to others, and quickly found it best to just read the book instead.
He is a clear lecturer. But sometimes, he does not motivate topics too well or goes overtime in lecturing. Some early homework assignments were too long.

Material is extremely difficult to follow. Don't take this class with him if it is your first diffeq class. He expects you already have experience. Extremely harsh grader on exams. I failed both exams even though I only got half a question wrong on each of them. Gets angry and defensive during lecture if you ask any questions (especially "dumb" one

Goes through lectures extremely quickly while leaving students confused and lost. You NEED to go to office hours and study the book a lot. Teacher expects you to understand the basics rather than teaching the basics. Was told this class was a blow-off class, but it is definitely not. Requires lots of work inside and outside of class. Don't reccome
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Engineering student for Math553 PDE here please, 1st, are you familiar with lebesgue convergence theorem, n-dimension sphere surface area and many other concepts in functional analysis?  if not, forget about this course. 2nd, you care about why the solution to a PDE exists and why it's unique rather than how to solve it?  if not, forget about it.

Dr. Tzirakis is an amazing professor who loves the material he teaches. It is very difficult to make ordinary differential equations interesting, but his lectures and applications make the course (while more abstract than MATH 285) much more useful. Take the course with him.
He is a great Professor, knows the material very well, approachable, nice person!! A lot of HWs!! good grading , very hard

Friendly personality. Not arrogant like some higher level professors. Likes to lecture without looking too closely at notes so he'll make a few mistakes, but takes student corrections well. Has an accent but easy to understand after a few classes. Explained concepts well. Tests were very straightforward and similar to homework and study guide.
Good professor. His questions come straight from homework. His grading was fair, and he tried to help the student out frequently.

Pretty easy class. Exams are only a few problems so make sure you know how to do everything on the study guide as well as covered in the homework. Not the best partial credit either. He doesn't try to mess you up. He's just making sure you know the material that he taught. Many 100's were achieved on exams in my class.
He's a nice guy. His exams are on study guides and homework. Make sure you study all problems on your study guide and homework, then you can get 100/100
DO NOT TAKE A CLASS WITH TIM, RUN FOR THE HILLS.

Given this was a sophmore level course, the reading was light and easy and I genuinely enjoyed going through modernist lit and poetry with him. I will also say that he puts up guardrails around the topic matter than ensure students stay in the moment and topic of the poems being analyzed, instead of going awry in their analysis of the pieces.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: His grading albeit tough, has a clear explanation and allows students to improve their writing and comprehension provided they go to office hours and go over their papers with him. I would recommend paying the utmost attention to his lectures which practically give you the tools to write your paper and the different topics you can engage with.

He gives a lot of unsolicited advice but frames it in a way where you can't really say no to it. I enjoyed that he was outspoken about his political beliefs and makes sure that he's respectful of pronouns, etc. But his language is a bit outdated and comes across as racist, or ignorant at the very least. His handwriting is impossible to read.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The teacher is very strict! As a person with social anxiety, I did not like talking in front of the class. No participation accommodations were offered. Essays are heavily graded and you cannot read his handwriting at all. Be prepared to be called out in class if he assumes you are not paying attention (very humiliating). Attendance is strict too.

Literature is my passion, but I stopped attending classes halfway through the semester bc the prof was kinda racist, makes weird comments, singles you out all semester if you stand up for yourself. I was one of two Black people in the class and he called me by the other Black person's name. Loved writing the essays, but prof ruined the experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took ENGL 241. Professor Newcomb is easily one of the worst professors in the English department at UIUC. He's awful. He hardly teaches and refused to accommodate students with disabilities. If you try to get Disability involved, he will take his frustrations out when grading your papers and will make snide comments. I hated his class.
Made snide comments and class was boring/not well structured
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Overall, fascinating class, I learned a great deal about film theory. I wanted to say however that he did not use trigger warnings before films, and there were a few with loud noise, shocking scenes, and uncomfortable topics that made me very uneasy. If you have anxiety or are disturbed by movies with heavy topics, think twice before taking it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--SO MANY PAPERS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed having this professor. He is clearly passionate about film, literature, and the arts in general. He has a great sense of humor, but he does not tolerate cellphone use/repeated tardiness in his class. He's pretty quirky, but an overall kind and efficient professor. We only had a handful of minor assignments and a few 4 page papers.

Coen brothers film class. He was very enthusiastic about the subject matter and fairly laid back and pleasant in contrast to the other reviews I'm seeing on here. He was more interested in having fruitful discussions than he was in being a punitive instructor or administering pop quizzes and lots of homework/readings. Flexible deadlines.

Yeah Professor Newcomb is definitely uppity and can be pretty mean at times. The prompts for his papers can be really convoluded and he's honestly only looking for a certain thesis an pretty specific supporting arguments. Overall there are multiple crude names that would be perfectly fitting for the dude.

Spends weeks on one topic, repeating lectures and material but spends one quarter of one class session on topics that make him uncomfortable or he does not find interesting. He is uppity, has low expectations of students and is passive aggressive, manifesting his anger/disappointment in assignments and offhand comments.

Horrible class- boring and repetitive.  Room was always freezing cold and the movies weren't interesting at all except JFK.  Teacher wasn't really willing to help.  I wouldn't recommend this class or teacher.

Great film class. Three papers, one final and attendance/participation. Not difficult since every other class was just a movie screening. Seems uppity at times, but just wants to see effort and participation in discussions.
Flaming ball of fire

I was a bit bored.  He did not have a good questioning style that created class participation.  We mostly sat and stared at each other.  I even started asking questions (of the class) one day because I couldn't stand just sitting there.   The texts were interesting, just not the way he presented them.  I did however like his use of Dr. Strangelove.
showed lots of fun clips in class
not bad class.  he teaches it well.  he makes it sound a lot harder than it is.   i didn't buy any of the books, and still got an A
pretty funny
Interesting class, he presents the material well.
No Comments
No Comments
Slowest lecture I have ever taken at UIUC. His teaching style needs to move into the 21st century. Only prof for 537. He also makes this sound effect super loud. Student engagement is non-existent.

He has no clue about what he is doing. He would post 70-min lecture on Saturday 8pm with hard some concepts. And he asks us to finish an exercise before Sunday 8pm, and the exercise has nothing to do with his lecture. During regular class time, he just talks about homework or exercise, he never mentions his lecture.

He is so mean and he is always playing word tricks in his exam. For example, there are both 3 and 9 in the option. We always calculate variance when practice and he asked for standard deviation in the exam:) I don't understand why except just being mean

FIN567 covers billions of topics. He does not use a textbook and his slides are not useful. He is knowledgeable, but he rants for hours and jumps all over the place (like his slides) when teaching. He has lack of teaching skills. The material should become more realistic. Data-driven approach is missing. 40% of exams does not reflect his material.

Although his examples are useful and funny, he spends time talking about useless stuff. The content is hard, but he does not explain them well in depth. He's been teaching this class for years, but there are still tons of problems in the homework and he don't even do the homework before assign it, which caused tons of troubles for us. Exams're hard
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Great professor with nice skill and he is really kind with his students. Thanks a lot
One of the best professor I've ever had. He gives a lot of homework, but he is really helpful. He tried to explain a lot of stuff and gave practical use of what he taught.

While this is one of the harder finance courses, Prof. Pearson is one of the more helpful profs and his class is very well taught. His real-world examples are great too. The slides are actually useful, unlike other classes out there. Take this class: but beware the final exam because you will need to review hard.

Knows accounting very well and shows enthusiasm in lectures. However, his accent is very thick and difficult to understand. In Coursera videos I can replay a certain part or use the slides to know what he's talking about, but in live sessions I'm often lost. Seems to have a good sense of humor and gives extra credit for singing. Mixed reviews.

He is incredibly helpful in facilitating a smooth transition from the phd program to a tuition-free master's degree if he is your advisor. This makes the process both efficient and beneficial while offering a great learning experience.
If you're considering a Ph.D. in accounting and are looking for a tuition-free master's along the way, he's the ideal person to guide you.
Favorite
Best professor ever!! He is fair in workload and offers plenty of help to pass with an A.
Great professor overall. The material is challenging, so get ready to do work.
I took his introductory accounting class as a part of iMBA program. He knows what he is talking about, approachable, funny, and always available to answer questions.
Oktay is a fantastic professor. He teaches basics of accounting in an intuitive way and brings the topics alive with real life examples.

Oktay is an excellent professor, I took Financial Accounting with him, and it was just an excellent, excellent class. Professor Oktay has a passion for teaching, he does a great job producing a very well organized class from the beginning to the end. Very efficient communicating with students. I will look forward to take another of his classes.
Great professor, great experience
Nice professor very patient at explaining questions if you go to his office hour. super chill
Toughest class I have taken at UIUC. Oktay does an excellent job explaining the material. Exams are hard but there are no surprise questions. Generous grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I wouldn't recommend this teacher ! Whenever you ask for help , he start talking about private tutoring. Why should I pay for privacy I don't know if I attend class and just want some support. Plus teacher speaks with strong accent which is hard to understand .

Horrible teacher , when I was face to face with him he was creating eye contact and locking the door to key and asking stupid questions takin off his glasses . In public yes he seems sweet and nice , but his face change once you one by one . I left his class and moved to another teacher
Easily the best accounting professor I've had at UIUC. Learned a ton in this class and Oktay made it an enjoyable learning experience
Great teacher! So funny and smart!
Great guy, seems to enjoy teaching. Wants to see students do well.

Passionate and very knowledgable about Latin American art history to the point where I became more interested in the topic outside of class even. Very sweet and understanding, willing to provide extensions. The yellow outlines in the slideshows should be taken note of!!! Tests are writing based + little memorization. Pay attention to the class = A

This professor doesn't grade anything until like after the finals which is very dumb since you dont know what grade you are aiming for to get the grade you want. Participation is 5% of your grade and it is very hard to get the points. Lectures are extremely boring and papers/exams are dependent on if your TA is in a good mood...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Vazquez was really great. For ARTH 260 he provided TONS of online notes that made it easy to study for exams and writing assignments, would recommend that class BTW. He explains things well and has a passion for his work. He sometimes goes a little fast on the lecture slides BUT they are posted afterwards. Don't believe the low rating!!!

Professor is a really kind person. Though he grades harshly, you learn a great deal in this class. Tests are hard and have essay questions, you have papers too but they are fun to write. I think he cares about his students.

Lectures are packed with information and Professor Vazquez doesn't leave enough time to write down important information on slides, but slides are posted to canvas later on. Discussion class was boring, but if you have a good TA, you should overall be fine. Essays and exams are not that bad, the instructions and grading criteria are clear.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Long readings, you can't get a good sense of what's going on throughout the class. 10 weeks into the semester and hasn't graded a single assignment so far. Readings and lectures are disengaging with his monotone voice. He seems lost and tries to make everything sophisticated more than it actually is.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures are boring and dreadful. He still uses iclickers so you have to pay for a subscription. There is a lot of reading that gets talked over during section. There are a few lectures that are interesting but most are boring and also confusing what he even is talking about.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is a very nice guy but terribly disorganized. Without good TAs the class would be difficult. He lectures like he is speaking from a textbook but that is just his vibe. He gets a bad rep but he is very passionate and nice but boring!! He is overall not a bad teacher.

Very boring in lecture, it was hard to stay awake. He rarely responded to my emails, and grades were never posted in a timely manner. It's the end of the semester and my first assignment still isn't graded. Also didn't post the grades for the first exam before the second exam opened.

Lectures were dry and as others have mentioned, he tried to use iClickers, but could never figure out how to make them work. Exams use material taken directly from powerpoint slides, but graded really nit-picky. Same with essays: no rubric/vague rubric given and takes points off just because he can. This class was a huge waste of time.

I'm an art major so I like him he is a woke king but he is also a boring king if you don't like art. Honestly, you can skip class because he doesn't know how to use the iclickers. Just be super chill with the TAs and you'll get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: doesnt know how to get iclickers to work half the time, lectures drag on from the slides themselves, hard to pay attention to monotone voice. nice professor though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Reads directly from his slides in a slow rambling voice. Graffiti and Murals was way more Murals than graffiti, and many of his slides repeated information. He wanted iclickers but they never worked and we eventually stopped having physical classes, and he was already uploading his PowerPoints. Tests are almost word for word from his slides.

Don't take a class with him if you want to have a clue about the subject. He's long winded and rambling, and in the class I was in we never got our papers back promptly (a class of 5 had over a month with no papers back despite continually asking) and never received any sort of prompts. It was impossible to tell how to get an A in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I've taken a few classes with Dr. Vasquez.  He has always been helpful and funny.  I would highly recommend his collection class or his public art class. He's a sweetie and a cutie.

He speaks soooo slowly, drags out and complicates his sentences, which causes a lot of confusion. Other than that he's actually a really nice guy, very willing to help students. You will never pronounce "origin" the same after this class ("or-IDGE-in"). His assignments are useful and we learn a lot with them.
Lots of information on his slides so it's hard to write everything down but he posted all the powerpoints online. A lot of memorization, but a fun art history class.

Vazquez really isn't too bad. The class certainly isn't a blow off, it requires a lot of studying and memorization, and essay portions of exams are difficult to score well on.  Lectures can get boring and vazquez seems kinda out there. But super nice guy and def knows his stuff!! Great class to take all things aside
Has a needlessly bad rap. Very nice guy and very intelligent. Has his own style of lecturing, but interesting once you've gotten used to it. Tests come largely from study guides.

This class is useless. You get graded on attendance, tests, and papers. I have watched The Office on my laptop through every class, and I still have enough knowledge to get myself through the class on my own. It's not easy, but if you study hard for the exams, you never need to step foot in the classroom. Oscar is useless, dry, and boring.
Unless you're extremely interested in the material already, this professor won't do it for you. You have to really focus in on what he's saying to get anything out of class. =(

Agree completely with the other 2 '06 comments: very disorganized, lacked coherence in most lectures because of his tangents, did not go chronological which throws most of the course off, and made a pleasant topic monotonous and extremely cumbersome. Very frustrating!

Smart guy but loses his 112 class when he goes off on wacko tangets. Super-disorganized, had to leave class to go get a presentation on CD that he left at home.
