Often late, hates the concept of "chronological order". Material is interesting enough to save your grade. TAs are a godsend in deciphering his antics.
can not operate powepoint

I took more than one class with this professor. He was extremely helpful. He asked us to write thesis proposals for research projects of our choice, something that is actually practical.  The other ratings are written by fine art majors who think school is hard.
No Comments

this is worst class ever. who the hell can follow what the professor is saying? He is impossible to understand in terms of his word choice. This course could be interesting, but the profesor makes it BORING
Lectures and topics are good and engaging. Dr. Oh docks points in various grading categories for unknown reason. She is a tough commenter and responder so be sure to communicate about expectations.
She was very nice, but could have been more clear on her expectations for the final paper.

Prof Oh is awesome! I took ENGL 200 recently, and she's amazing. She's very passionate about her work, and is willing to help you!. Feedback on close readings is very VERY important, as it will help prepare you for the final essay. As long as you participate in class (even if it's once/week) and stay on top of reading/deadlines, you'll be good!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She did not provide a rubric/instructions for the final essay, and docked students points for unknown reasons.

Dr. Oh is definitely passionate about her work and this class was so interesting !!  She explains her lectures in heavy detail with lots of examples - I also loved the novels she picked out for our genre units... helped me relate to my culture in a PWI school with a teacher who isn't even the same ethnicity as I am. She was able to get engagement!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Very kind, knowledgable about postcolonial and environmental studies and compiles fantastic (though copious) reading lists. However, her classes are so disorganized and her lectures are all over the place (she also talks at a breakneck speed). We barely got through the copious assigned readings.

ENGL285. Prof Oh is nice & the material is interesting but the format of the class was a mess. Usually she'd lecture for a bit & then we'd do think-pair-share, but there are other small assignments (communal note taking, mandatory paper workshops) that are easy to forget about. Tough grader with papers; it's hard to deduce what she's looking for.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I enjoyed the course overall and class discussions, but Professor Oh has high standards for papers. It becomes difficult to figure out what she is expecting. Papers are also what make up most of the grade in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: In the middle of my second class with Professor Oh, also had her for ENGL 285. I love that she gives good, honest feedback, but still is generous with the grading. I always know how to improve for my next paper, but don't have to worry about my GPA. She picks super interesting material to read that I had never even heard of before her classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Gives good feedback--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Loved taking his ANTH 279 class and it really challenged my perspective. He and the TA are very accessible during office hours and provide thoughtful feedback while genuinely supporting students' success. Despite the substantial reading load (~100p per week), the course content is engaging and the syllabus is well organized. Highly recommend!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He is very knowledgeable and well-spoken, but it is very theory-heavy and can go over one's head. Looks for a lot of participation, but can be a bit condescending if it's not the answer expected. One group presentation and some essays. A lot of reading (100+ pg/week). Grading is about as tough as the material. Gives days off to work on essays.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: He is a great professor and is very insightful. ANTH279 specifically has a LOT of required reading that builds on the other. Overall, if you don't like reading or have the time to do ~80-100 pages weekly on dense material, take a different class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Everyone says "ANTH 103 is a breeze"... but what they FORGET to mention is that online version is easy and the in-person class is not. If you think you're just going to get past this course without having to attend lecture, do the reading, or study... good luck! Avoid taking this class with Orta at all costs unless you're okay with taking a B.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: interesting class, but way too much reading. you can skip them and go to lecture, which seemed to workout fine. theres very few graded assignments ( basically only two midterms and like 4 discussion posts) but if you make a quizlet out of the list of words he gives for each midterm, you'll be fine
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: so much reading and complicated content for a gen ed class, probably find another one to fulfill, the work isn't worth it and the terms are super hard to understand, difficult exams
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I do not recommend this class. The professor is alright himself but the class itself is very demanding just for a gen ed class. It's very heavy on reading that is impossible when you have other classes. Not many assignments to help your grade. Exams are pretty difficult. For a gen ed class it is too much work, online class for this seems easier.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: For context, I'm an anthropology minor, but I really enjoyed this class! I really appreciated how he posts exam review terms, they're very helpful. This is a very reading heavy course, but you can get away with skimming the readings if you go to lectures. Really king guy. Discussions are mandatory.
Bad...... really different from what I learned in Utah
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Fine teacher I guess
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Fine I guess
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: As an international student whose English is not very strong, I would say he has shown great tolerance and kindness.
As a native of Illinois, I can only say that the course content often conflicts with my common sense......
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would strongly suggest you take this class online. The lectures were so boring and I could hardly stay awake during them. There are a lot of assigned readings that you have to reference in the midterm and final. The only semi-good thing was the discussions but if you missed more than 2 your grade will drop a letter grade every time.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Avoid if possible - really boring & dry lectures, 100+ pages of textbook reading every week (which is impossible to do), and no extra credit. Giving him a 2/5 because he knows how to hire good TAs - my TA was awesome and heard good things about the other ones too. Tests aren't too difficult if you spend at least a week prior studying for them.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He says attendance is mandatory but I've never seen him take it. Be careful either way. The lectures are great but understanding the class is very reliant on reading, sometimes an unrealistic amount for how much other coursework I had at times. TAs are great. Don't be late and don't let the door close too hard because he'll glare at you.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: se vira

I was not a fan of ANTH 279 as a class. I was not particularly fond of the material which made discussing it and writing papers on it challenging. However, Prof. Orta is great at facilitating discussions, makes everyone feel heard, and is a very fair grader.

If you have to take an ANTH class, take it with Prof. Orta
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Orta is the best! He is open to discussion during lectures, very engaging and fun to listen to if you're passionate about the subject. The class is very reading heavy, but the main books are interesting. I skimmed a lot of the other readings and still did good. Pretty much have to go to lectures as he doesn't post them online.
He is very nice. I learn a lot from this course.

He's a fine professor I guess, but the class structure was focused only on reading and weekly quizzes and discussion posts. He never actually "taught" because there were never any lectures. I only learned from the readings.

A pretty average professor if I'm being honest. He was super well-organized for it being an online class and he responded to his emails quickly. But the class is a LOT of work. It usually consisted of an activity or two, two chapters of reading, a discussion post, and a quiz. The material is easy to understand, but make sure to keep up.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He seems like a nice guy, but I had to drop the class because I couldn't deal with reading 100+ (usually very dry) pages a week. And then having to discuss them in detail for 3 hours straight. My ADHD just could not keep up lol.

Genuinely nice and caring guy, but awful teacher in my opinion. The class isn't necessarily hard at all but it has four group papers which are never fun in a gen ed. He also revolves lectures and exams around his own personal experience rather than more general anthropology.

Very poor lecturer, was very boring, and didn't learn too much. I found the easiest way to succeed was to just memorize his exam study sheets like a mad man. The test were just regurgitating info back. As far as electives go, this was terrible, group projects were dumb, and graded in an almost nonsensical way.

This class was the worst one Ive taken at this school so far. Youre only graded from two tests, 4 projects, and your final. The group projects were very boring and it was also hard to stay awake during lecture. He reads from topics from a sheet and makes the content seem more complicated than it is. He also does not explain the content well.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Definitely did not like this class. Every time I went to it I almost fell asleep. He drones on and on and you don't know what to write down for the exams. The papers were graded overly hard and the books and readings were intense.

Try to avoid him! He didn't do much for any students. Lectures were hard to follow, exams fairly difficult, too much arbitrary grading. Received a grade that ended in -9.96, and he refused to round. Thinks he's a big shot, really didn't enjoy his attitude toward students. Overall, makes life difficult for a gen ed course.
Professor Orta is fairly good at his job. Lectures are not listed as mandatory but for a good grade they are. I was more disappointed with his TA selection for discussion sections than anything else

Though lectures aren't mandatory you must go or else you will fail tests. Take notes verbatim from his slides and pay attention in discussion. The topic isn't too hard but he is very specific on tests!

Orta's lectures are a must: try not to miss any, as the exams are heavily based on them and if you don't want to be cramming last minute to figure out all the material, just GO TO THE CLASS! His lectures are a little hard to follow because he kind of reads from his sheets but I have learned a lot from his class. 3 five page writing assts & 2 exams.

This professor is terrible! The test are very hard. There are a few multiple choice (worth a lot of points), fill in the blank, and 2 essays which you have to do in 40 minutes because he hands out the test late. He is boring, old school, and confusing. He doesn't give out busy work, or any assignments for that matter. 3 test and a term paper.

I'm not sure what the other reviewers are talking about. His exams are super straightforward. He gives a "word list" prior to each exam, and if you know all the definitions, you'll get a 100%. Readings are all discussed in class, so you don't have to do them. Exams only require 5/6 questions to be answered per page, too, making them extra easy.

Professor Orta is a very old-school professor who relies on slides and lectures during his classes. There is a lot of reading but a lot of it is interesting so it is not hard to get through. He provides studyguides before every exam and there are only 3 exams.

Do not take this class with this professor. He is absolutely terrible. You have 3 test and a term paper and that is it. He does not offer any extra credit and the test are very hard even if you study. He is a terrible teacher, he gives out no study guides and does not try to help his students pass the class.
Lots of readings, but if you go to class you can do well without them. Class is interesting, tests mainly focus on general themes. DO NOT take pictures of his slides.

Teaching style reminds me of a high school teacher. He knows a lot about his subject, but expects students to write down what's on his power point slides while listening to what he is saying. One of the most ineffective ways of presenting material. Just don't take this class with him. Choose another Anth103 prof. 103 is supposed to be really easy.

This guy doesn't want us to succeed in his class at all. Too many readings, doesn't go through all of them, useless slide outlines posted online (which are actually just the slides without the content and only the titles) and we can't even take pictures of his slides even though he talks much more than what's on the slides. Avoid him for this class

Terrible class. He is a bad lecturer and when you ask a question he never gives a direct answer, so you usually end up more confused than you were before. His tests are very hard and there's no good prep for it. Requires too many readings-3 books at once. I would avoid a class with him.
I never really go to class, and got As on the tests. Studying in needed and his tests are kind of weirdly structured.. Interesting class though.

I took prof. orta's anth 430 last spring. You will learn/read so many important authors in this class. Hes great for advice/consulting on papers. One word of advice though: the readings are really intense.. but if you can work through it, the class isnt over-bearingly hard. its heavily relient on discussion though.

I took Prof Orta's class last year and he is great. Sure, he may be "dull." but he is very smart and a great lecturer. Honestly, he is probably one of my favorite professors at this institution and deserves more recognition

I'm an Anth major and this class was by far the WORST Anth class I've ever taken in my 3 years here. Professor Orta is boring and his lectures are dull. Could not stand this class. Still got a B+ but it was a struggle since I just didn't care and he made it harder than it needed to be. Tests are hard, so study.
Classes can be boring at times. Some reading are important but some others can be skipped.He is very interested in his subjects and makes some funny comments sometimes.
Amazing professor.  He is super intelligent and it shows.  Class is extremely interesting if you are already into Latin America.
Lectures are pretty boring, but the class is not the hard. You have to go to discussions, but the readings are somewhat opitional because the tests are mostly just key terms.
a. orta rocks my heart
made some wierd comments on race, wierd guy
Studying was needed, I often slept during class, but he taught fairly well.
keeps class relatively interesting and engaging
No Comments

Cool professor, really learned a ton from her class!
It's nice to engage in the final show.
Professor Khometa was very understanding and enthusiastic in the material that she taught. Would definitely take her class again.

Olsen is awesome-clearly knows the content of the course very well, and also is very accessible outside of class. Awesome teacher, but my critiques are that his lectures go to fast, and the problems assigned for the course are not too similar for the exam questions. Otherwise, if he is teaching your class, I can guarantee that you will learn lots!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The material was difficult but he takes questions well and is always willing to re-explain things. The class is linalg heavy and there's little to no review, but that's sort of expected at this level. Would've preferred more exams each weighted less though, the exams covered too much and each question ended up being worth 0.7% of the final grade.

Great professor! He knows the course content very well. Very approachable and patient. Takes time to clarify doubts. Doesn't curve the class or drop a few worst hw grades (and there is a lot of hw), so hard to get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I'm taking this classs now. This is CS450, which I feel too smilar to CS357. Honestly, I would say Prof Silva in CS357 is a better teacher than Olson. Olson is fine, though. But he is somewhat boring and I don't feel he really masters at these numerical computing things.

Took this class with no prerequisites.  He was always very approachable and helpful.  Provided basic codes for homework/projects that definitely took work to modify, but it was doable.  Tried to cover a little too much theory in lectures so it didn't always make sense, but I did learn a lot!
Great Professor, great course, lots of things to learn, minimal examination pressure
Great professor! Very approachable and knowledgeable.  He is one of the nicest professors I've ever had.  Get him if you can.

Lectures are hit-or-miss. Sometimes they're really clear and organized, other times they're all over the place. Sometimes assumes we know things from pre-requisites that we don't (mainly linear algebra). Not too big of a problem considering the exams are multiple choice. Very nice guy though.
Teaches very well..But tends to go very fast over the slides..
He is a good teacher and you learn a lot.
Very passionate, very kind, and amazing at teaching!
Lectures were dry, but a really nice professor. A lot of reading off the slides. Exams were not too bad, but definitely need to study and memorize some of the slides/diagrams.

I really enjoyed O'Dwyer's teaching. IB 203 (Ecology) is a core class for IB Majors. While it may not be the most "exciting" class, O'Dwy really made it interesting. The class is adv comp, so you will have to write a bit (its one 15pg manuscript but you work on it for 2 months+ one project). The class is very relevant and covers climate change.

Professor O'Dwyer really cares about his students and the material he teaches. I went into his class thinking that I would hate ecology. I ended up liking it a lot more than I expected. He makes himself very accesible to students and is a great lecturer to listen to.

Fantastic professor! He cared a lot about us understanding and enjoying the material, and never tried to trick us. He spoke clearly and encouraged us to ask questions and seek extra help at office hours.

Exams are open book, note, Moodle, homework, etc, just no Internet. Math can be difficult to understand at first but ask questions and you'll understand in no time. Had one midterm, one final, three homeworks, and one project (paper+presentation). Super interesting class, and a great prof. Don't skip class, you need the notes! Highly recommend.

She is a great instructor and if you follow along in lectures it isn't too difficult to stay on track. Math 285 itself is a well structured course and it does a good job helping students learn fairly difficult content. However, the auto-graded exams can be frustrating, as you may get no credit for showing your work if you made one small mistake.
Prof. Yu was an absolute gem. Her lectures were so clear, and it was very apparent that she cared about her students. Attend lecture and you will do well!

The professor was very enthusiastic about teaching and helped us learn through examples, which is the best way to learn this subject. I felt confident about the midterms and the final. She is also accessible through flexible office hours, during which she thoroughly covers each topic. Overall, she is the best math professor I've had at UIUC!

Dr. Polly Yu is amazing!!! Her lectures are so detailed and have a lot of practice problems, and she is very good at explaining the content. Attendance isn't mandatory, but it is worth it to go. She also gives hints about what type of questions are going to be on the midterms and final during class. She is definitely my fave prof. so far at uiuc!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super nice professor and very clear in his lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor McNamara is an amazing professor, by far my favorite. He truly cares about how his students are doing in class and cares about their success outside of class. His Chocolate Economics class is veryyyy lecture heavy, but that doesn't mean it's hard or that it wasn't fun! If you ever have an opportunity to learn under him, take it.
Great professor!

Textbook was a must for this class, as we read 1-2 chapters each week with a quiz every few chapters.  Fun professor/class, however he's really busy and was gone for maybe 6 lectures over the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. McNamara was always so helpful, and really took interest in his students. He is an excellent teacher and explains things so well, although he does expect you to perform. He really invests in his class and is really good at what he does!

Dr. Pu was amazing! Her class was very intriguing and it was easy for her to keep students interested.  There is a pretty big group projects but everything is done at a pretty slow pace and feedback is given for everything!  Her TA's are also very sweet and easy to work with.

I really liked Dr. Pu. She's really funny, her lectures are pretty engaging, and the class directions and objectives are great too. I do love her test policy too. There aren't really many or any general EC opportunities I can think of, but in all honesty, do the readings and participate, and you will not need it.

Attendance was done with iClicker, but she often forgot to open it during class, leaving many students with absences when they were at the lecture. Lectures aren't related to discussion sections (although that may have been my TA). The group skit was awful, especially since we had a final exam anyway.

She is extremely funny and is good at explaining the course material. She is also very understanding when it comes to absences or extensions. I don't really like the course's content but she still manages to make the class fun.
Yujie is super approachable and really cares about her students. The class activities are awesome and make learning fun.

I took Ms. Yujie for Hist 220, she is a caring teacher and very accessible if you ever have any concerns about classwork. I found her grading fair and reasonable, and the readings were never too long. I recommend her to anyone looking for a 200-level history to take!
She is very enthusiastic and passionate in her teaching. She is also very kind and reasonable.
She is helpful and good at teachingI really enjoyed her lessons!
Test heavy + boring lecturer. Take any other teacher if you can but stay away. Even if it delays your graduation to not take
would not rec
His class was hard, but I think I'm better for it. Really made students learn the material.
He gives you more than enough materials to prepare for exams and quizzes. He explained concepts very well and set clear expectations. I learned a lot from him and am glad I took him for FIN 300.

Communication was a major issue, despite multiple attempts to ask questions, responses were either delayed or unhelpful. The most frustrating part was the sudden grade change at the last minute without any explanation or opportunity for discussion. It's better to take other professor's class.

Super smart man.  As long as you read the book, pay attention in lecture, and practice what you learn-  you will succeed.  He gives you all the resources you need.  Before each quiz and test he gives you practice problems that will help you so much, you just have to practice.  Take good notes from his examples in lectures- I wouldn't skip class.

I absolutely loved both classes I took with Dr. Peters. He gives students all the resources to succeed but has high expectations for students with difficult but reasonable exams and quizzes. I learned way more from his classes than other finance classes I took due to his standards and lecture ability.

I took FIN 300 over summer online, I found the class to be a bit rushed for weekly material but thats my fault for taking an 8 week class. Other than that the professor provided all relevant formulas in his notes and his problem sets were great as they would be the same on projects and exams. Class is hard, overall great curve to make up for it
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: He is very smart and always willing to help the students, he gives a very clear rules of the course and gives us specific guidelines. There are a lot of homework but if you take it seriously, it will really helps you to understand the materials well and it is a good preparation for the exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Some many homework! Even though this is summer session, there is 8 assignments in the first 2 weeks, each takes 30 minutes to over 2 hrs!
Lecturer only read PPT, calculate the numbers by himself; No elaborations on concepts or tricky questions, boring class
Makes no attempt to teach but presents material as if students have a phd in finance. Unrealistic expectations for online modules, generally abrasive and difficult to talk to.

I really enjoyed Peter's teaching style. He gave us many resources to help us do well on quizzes and exams and made guided notes and problem sets for each section. This is a class where you need to show up, pay attention, take notes, and practice the questions outside of class for at least a couple of hours each week to do well on quizzes.

I did not like his teaching style. There are no PowerPoints, no slide decks, no online presence so if you suck at taking notes good luck. there was way to much group work and overall I was unclear what was asked in the class. It often felt disorganized. He is a really interesting and nice guy but I hated his teaching style.

I've taken this professor twice now and would take another class with him if given the chance. The other poor ratings seem to be coming from students who have a difficult time taking notes and going to class. No online material so notes are essential. You could get an A without opening the book. Overall: relevant, knowledgable, and applicable.

Funky grading method and curve, but go to class and study a reasonable amount and you'll do fine. He takes a bit of warming up to, but in the end Professor Peters provided really useful & practical knowledge about business and Finance. His class is simple (but not easy) and straightforward. Class discussions were enjoyable. Overall good professor!

As I look back on college, Peters was definitely one of my favorite instructors. If you participate in class and study the material you'll be fine with the curve. Think a little outside of the box. The material is super interesting and pretty useful in the financial world. Don't take him if you want to sit in the corner and avoid learning.
Easy grading scale, Cool guy. Interesting in class. Not sure what these other kids are talking about. Probably not business majors and don't know basics.

Of the handful of Finance classes I've had to take towards my Actuarial Science major, this one is by far my favorite because of Professor Peters.  He is a fantastic professor who pushes his students towards critical thinking beyond the textbook.  The class wasn't "easy", but the knowledge I'll for sure use later on is well worth a B in the class.
Avoid him if you can.

Do not be deceived...this man is not a good teacher..he tries to act like he knows what he is talking about and is above everything but really is just rambling a bunch of nonsense about banks. I don't even bother with class anymore b/c full-credit is impossible to obtain with his exams and the grading approach he uses or however the hell he grades.
Seriously? He seems to think that he is extraordinarily smart, and you should easily know what he is talking about. My recommendation: don't take him.
No Comments

Considers himself smarter than the textbook, tests are essay/short answer(with partial credit- arguing for more points will do you NO good).  Finance majors might do alright in his classes, but everyone else should steer clear.
Prof. Quick was an excellent professor. Very understanding of any outside of class situations.

Professor Quick has been my favorite professor in my 3 years here on campus. He always brings the energy to lectures, always has a fun or interesting story to tell, and actually cares about your success and interests.

Prof. Quick is amazing, and his lectures are very high quality. There's a lot of information, but he breaks it down into very understandable chunks. TA's are also amazing. His lectures are very engaging, and he's very funny. As long as you show up and complete the course packet (notes) and study, you'll do great! There's also extra credit!
I am more used to stern and serious profs but I can see why people like him. I personally don't because I like someone who is more information dense. He is very enthusiastic.
All I can say is that Prof. Quick made this class absolutely fascinating. I took this class last semester and I still think about many things quick taught all the time.

Loved his class, reason I decided to take a minor in CMN. Honestly, I just learned sm from his lectures and enjoyed going. Super engaging and always has a fun story or experience to share. fav thing about this class is just how welcoming it always felt. the class itself is pretty easy, youll ace the exams as long as your taking notes and review!
he makes the course interesting and knows how to make students pay attention. he is hard to reach via email but overall super easy and informative course.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: For 465, this class was like 102 with the notes fill in book and test structure. There is a major group project that is research based but is not very difficult! The grading rubric is a little vague for some of the project parts but you can always ask. He has a lot of energy and is always passionate about the material he teaches. Def recommend!

He is a good and positive teacher for sure. I enjoyed his stories. Class is pretty much mandatory because you do the notes inside the book packet, but I like it that way. Overall, I liked his style and the tests were not bad.

He really brought the energy and passion for the subject, which I appreciated. For my class, we were discouraged from tech usage + all used paper notes which actually helped me focus a ton. The expectations for the class were clear from the start. It was very attendance focused though, and if you missed lecture/discussion, you would lose points.

Professor Quick is such a great lecturer! The only thing is that you can't really miss lecture or you'll lose points in the class. The grade isn't based on many projects- in class assignments and exams. I really recommend this class, Quick seems like he's really interested in what he is teaching.

This was such a fun class to take and Quick made it such an engaging and easy class. The lectures were very note-heavy but they were easy to follow as note packets were provided. There are only three exams throughout the semester and you don't have to take the final if you are satisfied with your grade. Overall Quick is such a fun and easy teacher!

Prof.Quick was an excellent professor. His lectures were super engaging, even when discussing already well-known topics. Additionally, there was a workbook that included pictures of all the slides with space next to each for notes. This made lectures extremely easy to follow along and to study for exams.

Professor Quick is a great lecturer! Although the class can be very lecture-heavy, professor Quick makes the lectures very interesting and engaging. The midterms are fairly easy if you look over your notes. The TAs in the class are also amazing in preparing you for the midterms, so attending the discussion sessions will be extremely useful.

Prof Quick is amazing! His lectures are interesting and engaging. There is however a LOT of material that he covers which may be a little difficult when studying for his exams, but the material itself isn't necessarily difficult. So as long as you study and pay attention in the discussion section you should be good!

Professor Quick teaches excellently, but it seems there are too many things to learn for such a basic class. Maybe he should decrease the quantity. The overall classes are not difficult, but it's just too many.
He is a great guy

Prof Quick was an outstanding teacher. He genuinely cares about his students and is passionate about each and every topic. So much energy. His lectures kept me awake and looking forward to the next class. Buy the course packet, study the notes, show up for class, and you will be fine. I recommend this class. Prof Quick is amazing!

The class is not too bad, if you study and pay attention you will be fine. Professor Quick tells too many stories that are too personal and it is a bit unprofessional at time. Plus, he teaches with his opinion too often.

Lectures aren't mandatory but you need the notes so I recommend going. Super fun class that covers a lot of interesting material you wouldn't normally see in other courses. It's 4 credit hours which is nice because it's definitely the work of only 3. Exams can be tough but pay attention in lecture/lightly study and you will be fine

Professor Quick is genuinely the best instructor that I have had in college. He is hilarious and his lectures are so engaging. He was also extremely fair with grading and always made sure that we knew he was available and willing to talk with us about anything. I would definitely recommend his class!

Prof Quick is an amazing professor. He is funny, knowledgeable, and I love how he ties social marketing principles to the real world. After this class, I learned a substantial amount about the field and I now know I want to apply more social marketing principles to my future research projects. Take this class! You won't be disappointed.
I took the online version one. Show up to discussion cause it is not recorded and some questions will come from discussion sessions. But Prof Quick is amazing and super easy to understand.
PROF QUICK IS AWESOME!! he brought his dog to class !!

Prof Quick does a great job of making communications interesting. I would love to take another class with him in the future and I would recommend CMN 102 to anyone who is trying to decide what to do or wants an easy gen-ed. I took it for my cmn minor and I was worried it would be boring, but it's a really useful class and Quick is a great teacher.

Professor Quick is a very kind, personable guy. He is lively and his lectures are engaging. In COMM102, you get course packets so it is very easy to take notes. If you miss class you may miss some important examples that he gives that aren't found in the course packet, and they may show up on the tests. So I suggest you don't skip.
Very passionate about his lectures and had more energy than most people could dream of. He clearly cared a lot about his lectures and research and seemed like a great professor.

Prof. Quick is such an inspiring professor and made me actually want to come to class. His lectures are fun and engaging and I learn so much in them. I really recommend trying to get him as a teacher for a CMN course cause he's the best. Sad to be done with his class.

Former grad student - B L QUICK world renowned... Look up psychological reactance.. organ donation campaigns. This persuasion seminar... 1. MA and PhD take together. If you're MA you will be overwhelmed, stressed, and feel dumb at times. But Brian is very nice. grading consists of a midterm paper, final paper, how well you lead discussion 1x etc

Prof Quick was my favorite teacher this semester! He's pretty laid back and there's literally only 1 homework assignment a week. He does a good job of making lecture's interesting. An overall awesome and genuine guy!

Prof Quick is awesome. He makes his classes easy- for sure an easy A. There's no textbook- just a workbook with all the notes that was $25. There are three exams and one final- lowest score is dropped. For most, people keep their first three scores and don't take the final. He's so passionate and easy to listen to- take his class!!

Brian is the most inspirational professor I have had while at Illinois. He is a masterful researcher and his lectures are fun and very engaging. He makes no qualms about meeting outside his classroom for help, career opportunities or even just to talk. Teaching the first class that CMN majors must take, he puts to rest any doubts that you may have.

Brian is very passionate about his research and is a very nice person. Great lecturer. Discussion sections are easy and almost always get out early. Exams are basically all of your grade and you only have to take 3 of the 4, or you can take all 4 and drop your lowest grade. A lot of theories and application to remember, but other than that decent.
Great class and awesome teacher. Everything you need is in one fill in workbook.

Prof Quick's lectures were fairly easy to be engaged in because he is funny and relates his personal life to what we're learning to make it easier to understand. I definitely had to study and attend lecture to do well but even studying minimally usually got me an A or B on the exams.
He's really funny, shows good examples in class, and makes class interesting. I wish I could take another class with him, but he only teaches one other one in Health Campaigns. Overall, fun class!

Prof Quick is great.  He makes his lectures pretty interesting, and he can be funny sometimes.  Barely any homework but you do have to take decent notes at lecture (its guided notes) then study for like 4 or 5 hours for each exam.  Four exams total (including the final) and you can drop the lowest one.  A few extra credit opportunities as well.
Really great professor. Loved his lectures!

Professor Quick is great. He knows what he's talking about and he's good at what he does. I feel bad for using my phone in class, but he's never called me out. He makes the learning environment in lecture like lecture/discussion because it's so interactive. I learned a lot.
Professor Quick is the best professor that I have ever had. His lectures were interesting and he always tries to make the class fun.

He is very passionate about his teaching and he is very good at it. Very interesting lectures. The information is not very difficult as long as you pay attention and take good notes. Definitely recommend this class!
Awesome
He's the best professor in this university!I promise you won't regret taking his class!Lectures are amazing and inspirational!I wish I can take it every semester!

Prof Quick is awesome! He's so enthusiastic and cares about the students. Great TAs as well. Discussions are mandatory to recieve participation. Only 4 exams and you get to drop 1. At least 5 opportunities for extra credit each semester.

Prof. Quick is a cool cat and a great teacher. He explains concepts well and is easy on the eyes. Most importantly, he is one of those teachers that CARE about their students. He isn't on a power trip. I HIGHLY recommend taking a course with him! Tip: You need to take notes in class though!
notes from lecture are necessary, but no participation grade. you can skip all sections and write a term paper, or go to all but one and complete an easy weekly worksheet. very easy class

Listen up: this is the best professor in the CMN department and debatably the entire university. It is literally unreal how great of a Professor he is. I am now a senior and I have never had an experience quite like his class. He dumbs down the material so much! If you are a CMN major, do not even THINK about having someone else teach this to you!
Brian is a really nice guy and i really find this class useful and practical.

Not only was he engaging, hilarious, and nice to look at, but he was a great teacher. Always made the content clear and relatable with a lot of examples. Discussions also really helped clarify concepts. Tests were easy as long as you reviewed your notes. No book.
Pretty fun class, required for my major. Interesting, and interactive lectures.
Funny and interesting, tests can be tough though. If you spend 4-5 hours in total studying for each exam you should be good though.
Interesting lectures, very accessible professor.

I wouldn't recommend taking this class unless you have to. There was a lot of material. Tests were tough, but the assignments were easy.  Tests were worth 75% and assignments were worth 25% of our grade. They only give you notes to study from because there's no book. Professor Quick is a nice guy, but I still would fall asleep in class.

Nice and charming. Gives you great examples and the lectures are mostly interesting and fun. A lot of people show up even if there is no attendance taken. Course materials are fully covered, while the best thing is it's not hard to get a good grade!
Best Prof of my freshman year. Great Prof and made me feel better about big lecture halls
Everything you could want in a professor, and then some. You can tell he really loves what he does.

He is a great professor! I was kind of nervous about taking this class because I am normally not good with understanding and memorizing theories but he is very good at explaining them and he made class a lot of fun. He is very outgoing and is a pretty funny guy. You will definitely enjoy taking this class with him.

Class is easy as long as you do the hw assignments (based on completeness and effort) and fill in the blanks in the notes section of your book and attend discussion sections to get extra info that's on the exam. Also, the 10 points extra credit is a big help since the class is only out of 400 points

Brian is one of the best professors I have had so far at this school. He really cares about his students. There are 3 non-cumulative exams and 1 final and you can drop 1 of them.  There are also weekly assignments that are very easy and short due in discussion every week. I highly recommend this class! It persuaded me to major in communications!
Great professor! He kept the class engaged and he is very passionate about what he teaches. Yet, sometimes you might think you understand the material but think again because the exam is very tricky.

The best professor i had in the CMN department. The class has two group project, one classroom presentation, grades a lot base on eye contact and final paper on a health campaign. Two exams total,the exam were more towards concept applying in a scenario, not memorization of the definition. He grades fairly as long as he see you try and the ideas.

Brian is a nice guy but I hate the way he lectures. He shares way too much personal info about his life and calls the class "gang" which is kind of belittling. The exams seemed disorganized and disproportionately harder to the way the material was taught.
Prof Quick is super nice. He's passionate about what he teaches and makes each lecture engaging. He loves his students and wants them all to do well.

Quick loves what he does and teaches the material effectively. CMN theory is pretty dry but he tries to make it more interesting. Don't be afraid to get to know him and use the resources he offers you to your advantage. Go to class as much as you can and you should be alright.

The class itself is very interesting and informational, you actually get to learn a lot of practice stuff. It is consist of 3 exams and a final project. Overall the exams are easy, it is divided into 80% lecture notes and 20% reading material. There are three types of exams format u get to pick which kind you want to take, such as multiple choice.

There are 3 exams and a cumulative final, but you get the drop the lowest exam grade of the 4 exams. This allows some people to not take the final, but if you do take it, it is the same exam questions as the previous 3 exams but they don't allow you to see what questions you got wrong on the first 3 exams so I feel like I made the same mistakes.

Professor Quick is a fun professor, he tries to make the class very interesting possible. The class itself was not hard, as long as you takes notes and do the course pack readings. There are 3 exams and one final. However, only 3 exams counts, so final was optional. However, what i don't like about this class, is that final was cumulative.

he makes lectures interesting and gives great examples. the exams are kind of tough, but you get to drop one and there is a TON of extra credit. overall, good class, great guy. oh, and we got out of class early almost every day.

Great Prof. he will love to teach stuff and explain things very well. Also like making fun and make the class more interesting. I did learn l lot form him. In addition, care about his students, give reasonable extra credit

Prof Quick is awesome. He makes lectures fun and interesting. Make sure that you print off or have the powerpoint slides because sometimes he will lecture off of those and not the work book. I would also suggest going to office hours. He's a cool guy to talk to one-on-one with and very helpful.
Prof Quick was super cool. The class was interesting and he kept it fun. He was very helpful in answering questions at his office hours.

He's a cool guy who is obviously interested in the subject and does a good job explaining the information. Make sure to pay attention to the power points because that's what the exams are based off of.

Prof Quick is a super nice guy and his class is pretty easy.  There is no attendance in lecture.  Attendance is taken in discussion by turning in 9 very easy homework assignments.  The tests were harder than I thought judging off lectures.
This class is easy and fun. Prof. Quick makes lectures interesting and he's pretty lenient about the tests.
Very enthusiastic!  He tends to go a little too in depth to some examples, but he's very straightforward and approachable.

Prof Quick is a a lot of fun and obviously cares about his subject. He jokes around and really encourages class participation even in a huge lecture. Class is not very hard and both Prof Quick and the TAs are very helpful when there are questions.

Worst professor. Absent for 1/4 of the term with only 2 HWs total. For 3-credit students, the final is a massive 45%, yet grades were never shown and no curve was applied. Extremely disorganized; you have no idea where you stand before the end.
Worst Professor, worst grader. no exact rubrics. doesn't curve at all.

does not have a clear grading rubric, not good lecture notes clarity, TA's are very bad graders, homework is easy but does not help for exam. Professor does not have a clear grading rubric either. Three exams 30% each, homework 10%. TA's are not helpful at all.

Exam 1/2/3: 30/30/40%. No mandatory HW or attendance. 
His class is very challenging and goes very deep - not recommended if you want an easy A without heavy work. But his lectures are excellent, clear, and he's always open to questions. Highly recommended for motivated grad students. I learned a lot that directly supports my research.

The student professor is very conscientious and responsible and tries to teach a lot of things. The professor is very responsible and is always willing to answer questions. After each exam, he/she will send emails to provide personalized feedback to each student. Students need to study it hardly and not try to get a grade without putting effort.

Professor Brewster is one of the best professors I've had at UIUC. ME 404 is tough but deeply rewarding. He explains complex ideas step by step, answers questions with patience, and truly wants students to learn. I gained a real understanding of thermodynamics that still helps me in grad school today. I truly respect him.
Avoid this professor. He is incredibly difficult to get a hold of outside of class, and provides little useful feedback on exams/projects. Lectures are very difficult to follow.

The worst professor in the mechanical engineering department without a doubt. 

We had an A before the *completely discretionary* faculty advisor adjustment grade, where he subtracted 15% off of our grades one day before grades were released. 

Do not take a project with him!!!

The worst professor in the mechanical engineering department. If you take senior design with him, get ready for unreasonable expectations (such as a full design/prototype on 48-hour notice) as well as a hugely negative grade adjustment at the end of the semester. 

AVOID AT ALL COSTS!

If you have the option to take his class, don't. He makes you feel stupid for not understanding and expects everyone to understand things immediately. This class covers 1.5 textbooks and you need intensive knowledge from chemistry and quantum mechanics to understand it. The worst professor I've had on this campus.

The class is interesting, especially if you already are intrigued by the topic. Brewster is very smart, and I think he thinks his students to be just as smart, so his tests were very difficult and very unlike the homeworks, which were very straightforward. Overall, I did well in the class, but probably because everyone else did so poorly.
Great professor, homework is reasonably straight forward but an assignment is due every single class. Tests are very focused on derivations and can be brutal. Overall would recommend.
I learn a lot, for sure, and I never regret.
I would say he is extremely friendly, and you better pray that he is your professor in this department.

Most useless class I have ever taken. 20 minutes of class dedicated to nothing that we should be learning in class. The curriculum was based on no actually important skills or knowledge for planning. Biggest waste of time being taught by a man who likes to hear himself talk.
One of the best professors I've ever had
Makes class enjoyable, enthusiastic, and funny, but delivers a good lecture.
He is an awesome teacher and a very engaging speaker! Long lectures though so drink coffee I sometimes get drowsy

Professor Qiu's lecture is very thorough and detail oriented, always followed by concrete examples. The homework and exams are in consistent format, with sufficient guidance provided for studying. Overall, very nice professor.
Amazing and very organized lectures if you come to every class and do your work. If you don't do your part of the job, you may find the classes difficult to follow and the exams very hard.
probably the worst professor ever.

You will not pass this course if you don't attend lectures. She won't post any notes to compass. This is the most stupid course that I have EVER attended in U of I. You just need to memorize everything. You won't use what you learned from this course in real life. Basically, this course sucks. The grader is the toughest grader I have EVER seen.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The worst professor in ECON major, I would rather choose Petry than her even Petry is in prison RN. Required 3 hr final exam and she only gives two hrs and saying "another one hour is for TA collect exam papers" too many calculations needed in an 80 min midterms.
She gives amazing lectures, very organized and crystal clear. Her teaching is very thorough. Probably the best econ class you can get in UIUC.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: best prof and best class ever!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Attendence is important. Extremely easy class if you take notes. The exams and homework are the same as exercises in class.
Great professor and great class!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Only 4 Homework assignments that count basically as quizzes. 3 Midterms and Cumulative final. Alot of formulas to memorize and little room for error since one mistake can determine a whole letter grade. Attending class is more vital than the slides since she only gives and solves practice problems in class.
5 homeworks and 3 exams for the whole sememster, you will find them pretty easy as long as you go to every class
Best of them all
Obviously just another crummy grad TA working on an English degree at UIUC. He's very into himself (figures for someone in fine arts), and the class was nothing more than boring/typical.

Quentin was so intelligent, kind and helpful! The work was pretty hard but I went to class and did well. I think the other student just got a bad grade, probably because s/he never went to class and never did any work.

This class is unnecessarily hard, lecture slides are confusing and not helpful at all, exams and HWs are hard to complete since they don't line up well with the slides. Very harsh grader as well, would not recommend
Notes and lectures are extremely insufficient, little to no practice problems, exam problems are much more difficult then HW and in-class examples, brutal
Dr. Yu is an amazing professor who truly cares about her students. Her lectures are clear, engaging, and easy to follow. I learned a lot in Stat 426 and really appreciated her dedication!

Dr. Yu is the best professor I've had at UIUC. Her teaching style is clear and incredibly effective. I learned so much from her—not just about statistics, but also how to think critically and apply concepts meaningfully. She truly cares about her students' success, and it shows in everything she does. I'm grateful to have taken Stat 426 with her!
Worst stats class I have ever taken.

The worst course i've taken in the stat core sequence. Most of it is due to the prof. My biggest issue was trying to work with her with disability accommodations as she would constantly make it difficult to have my needs met. Otherwise I thought the content was okay, pretty typical stat modeling stuff.

One of the worst professors I've ever come across. She cares more about failing her students and giving out penalties than actually evaluating students based on their understanding of her material. I submitted an assignment five minutes late and she tried taking off 10% of my overall grade and retroactively failing my old homework assignments.
Her 207 Indian Film class requires you about 1hr of effort every week for an A. If you are looking for an easy gen ed, she's one of the ideal profs.

In way of teaching, she is somewhat of a yapper and my lecture notes consist mostly of 500+ word notees for the day (up to 900), but if you like film and/or history I think you'll find them interesting enough. It's a pretty light workload, I only did homework in like 5 instances across the whole semester. The grading is lax, 90+% is an A.

Her lectures are yapathons. There isn't too much work, so that is good. If you put in the effort, you will do well. But she doesn't consider her audience at all. The busy work is insane in this class. Some of the activities are so boring. Take it if you are interested, otherwise eh.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Rini Mehta is probably the worst professor I've faced at UIUC. Her grading scheme and plagiarism check systems make no sense. She is inconsistent with her classes and makes absolutely no effort to make the class close to interesting. I would strongly advise students to not take this class.

Attendance was inconsistently. She accused a student being too chatty and putting her headphones on during the last class before final, and asked for her iCard(make a report, I assume) and ended the class rather abruptly. Project was disorganized. The team grades slowly, can be hard to predict a score before the semester ends.

Rini Mehta is a woman who is passionate about what she teaches, but not who she teaches. She treats adult students like they are in the 5th grade, stopping lecture to ridicule those who have a side conversation for 0.5 seconds in front of over 100 people. If she claims she does not want distractions in her class, she should follow her own advice.
Easy class but was disorganized. Id say I spent maybe 10-15 hours on the class the entire semester

This is a class you can ignore except for the weeks where there are projects/exams. Rini seems nice and is flexible as long as you communicate. Like others said, Project 1 is tedious (took me 4 hrs of data entry for 2 hr movie). To do well on writing exams you definitely need her book. Grading is really relaxed and MC exams are straightforward.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This class is really easy. However, theres just so much annoying busy work. What sucks the most is that the busy work revolves around the book she wrote. Its a terribly written book. You can tell she took normal words and replaced them with a rarely used synonym from a thesaurus.

Lectures super boring, tests are online and pretty easy. My main problem with her is we had an assignment that was *loosely* related to the class and involved literally manually entering data into a spreadsheet. It. Took. 30. Hours. Just find a different gen-ed, the easy A isn't worth it.
No tests. It's 6 assignments that are 500 word essays pretty much super easy. Do not worry about this class!

She's not a great lecturer; she sometimes doesn't express herself well and there are a lot of pauses while she's talking. Otherwise, the class is quite fun and super easy. ResponsesExams aren't hard and there's a good amount of extra credit to make up for lost points. Going to class is a good idea even though lectures are boring.

Super easy class, especially if you're familiar with Bollywood. On the downside - be prepared to read some real ridiculous feminist drivel, because in the humanities, opinions = facts. Who needs evidence and proof when you have *feminist theory*.

While the class was interesting, she expects you to be familiar with Indian directors, film stars, and Indian history. The tests reflect 0% of the class content but weekly responses are easy. She's a nice person but the class was boring if yo u aren't familiar with the topics she discusses which often don't relate to the films at all. 4/10 tops

She was really nice and new the material really well. The in class discussions could get heated at times so that made it a lot more interesting. There was hardly any homework and the weekly in class assignments were a breeze. Despite that, I did feel like I learned a lot. Good course, good instructor.

The most boring class I've ever participated in. Although lectures and discussions are mandatory, I have been to less than half of the classes because I'd rather risk losing points than sitting through that class. Discussion TA is horrible, has no idea what he's talking about. Avoid, there are much better classes that fulfill gen eds.
This is undeniably the stupidest class I've taken so far. The worst part is the 8-10 page term paper on that stuff that we didn't learn/she didn't teach us.

I hate this class. She is absolutely useless when it comes to lecturing, and extremely boring on top of that. She lectures about random things that have nothing to do with the stories we read, and then quizzes us on things that are neither in the stories, nor in her lectures. She's also a horrible procrastinator.

Buy the lecture notes and the book and don't bother going to class. Rini spends the whole time reading straight from her notes. The only thing that you might need to go to class for are for the pictures that are going to be on the tests. Honestly, you'll be fine as long as you read the book. The tests are very straight forward.
Memorize your workbook and do the readings. Just attend class to take pictures of the slides or get a friend to do it. Very attainable A and no +-.

Easy A.  Don't have to go to her lectures, just read the study guide and textbook.  If you decide to go to her lectures, then you don't have to read the textbook, just copy down the lecture notes because you won't be able to understand her.  It's really an easy class.                                                      by Namen, "the ramen", Mehta

Super nice person, but not a great lecturer. Really smart and obviously knows her stuff, but goodness she is so boring. You don't really need to go to class. Interesting material and an easy A, but not great for lectures.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!!!! SO So boring....you will fall asleep. Not necessarily hard....but so boring.
The most boring class I've had this semester. Stay AWAY!!!!!!! Not necessarily hard, but you will fall asleep in every class.

Easy class.  REALLY boring lectures, but I never go and I do fine on the tests.  Also impossible to hear in lecture.  Tests are easy as long as you read.  Luckily, the reading is pretty interesting.  Not a bad choice.

The readings are really interesting but the lectures are super boring. Attendance at lectures isn't mandatory at all and usually on 25% of the kids show up. She's really boring and hard to hear in the back. I would however recommend going to discussion, that's where you actually learn. It's an easy A class.

I thought the material was interesting and even though she is not an outrageous lecturer, if you pay attention you can really learn a lot and she can actually be quite entertaining. I took two classes by her, both were easy A's because I enjoyed the material we covered.

A very easy non-western course where skipping lecture is not a problem. If you attend discussion section weekly and review the material in the study guide you're provided, this is an easy A. The paper was a breeze. As long as your wrote memorization skills are pretty strong, you'll have no trouble with this course.

Average lecturer, but she knows her stuff. I did my honors paper with her and she was extremely helpful. The class was interesting but relatively easy. She's always willing to help a student, though, and tries to make the lectures as interesting and clear as possible.
No Comments

I skipped this class all the time--I maybe attended 5 times, including the lecture where we had to turn in our essays.  She emphasizes going to class for all the pictures (which are used on the exam), but if you know the material by studying the book, you'll be able to guess what the picture is portraying.  Easy A!
Awful.  Impossible to understand and horrible instructor.
No Comments

I didnt want to do this class, she uses lots of powerpoint, but it was boring and it was hard not to fall asleep.  She tried to make some jokes in class but was occasionally hard to understand.  Not a hard class so it will be a good non-west gened if you have any interest at all.

The class is not hard at all, it really depends on how you are able to differentiate the names... You need to know many terms, concepts, and names for the exams. The instructor clarifies the readings as well as the major storie, if you go to lecture and discussions then you will be doing great!

Asian Mythology... I'm sorry, I can't stand to hear this woman lecture. Her voice is like nails on a chalkboard. Lectures are boring. Material is kind of interesting... but I wouldn't suggest this class to anyone.
Dr Sennett be flexing. Super insecure, mean to students, cold. Wouldn't take another class of her's.
Worst teacher ever. Dr. Sennet talks in circles, but always misses the point. Her inconsistent teaching paired with her lack of enthusiasm makes for the worst class at the U of I.

By far my best teacher so far. I've only spent a year with her so far and am looking forward to the next 3. Although she seems very business oriented, she seems to genuinely care. Totally knows all here stuff and is a supreme performer.

Prof Sennet is the best teacher I've ever had, hands down. She knows exactly what she's talking about and even if I didn't practice as much as I should she still makes the most of all the lesson time. Can't say enough good things about her, truly an exceptional teacher and musician.

The way she teaches this class is nauseating. No one knows what they are doing. The labs themselves are so unorganized, and paired with the amount of material we should be learning for the exams, there should be much more emphasis on teaching us the information rather than bumbling through lab procedure.
She did a terrible job as course coordinator. Would not recommend her at all.
not sure they know what their job is

She never responded to any emails and always ended her office hour way earlier than scheduled. I had to come to her office for so many times to ask her to correct my score. The course is very easy but the poor organization from the course coordinator is a burden. She doesn't have the capability to be in this position.
Not the best
Very disorganized, very unhelpful, not sure she knows what her job is
Extremely unhelpful
She's very understanding and accommodating
She's very chill and super willing to help out her students, she was very accommodating to me

The score for second week assignment was not entered until the reading day. There was always mistake about scores showed on atlas and be expected to come to her office to get it fixed as she never answered any emails. Easy class but didn't learn anything tbh.

I loved my TA for this class, but not Renee. The amount of spelling errors on both the homework and exams were horrible(ex: "proceedure, differental", etc). The exams were Zoom proctored and she started screaming at me over Zoom in front of everyone else because she thought I didn't have a second device set up. Not caring or willing to hear you out
she doesn't reply to emails at all... I struggled a lot due to the incorrect grades she entered and we haven't receive any grades about packback and group assignment since the first two week.

He is a tough cookie, but she has been teaching this class for decades. Therefore, everything is pretty much predictable and well organized. You will need to really pay attention and learn both practical and theoretical sides of the lap, because it will show up on the exams.
i'm a freshman and i think the only thing i learned from 101 was that students concerns arent taken srsly
worst class ever. this woman is soooooo disorganized. i'm so annoyed omg she doesn't even put in grades until the day before the final.
came in an mcb major and came out majoring in law cuz of arguing my grades... still haven't won the case wish me luck :( also do not take this class

Renee is the coordinator of my MCB301 course. She is a disaster. She does not respond to emails and forces everyone to argue over an incorrect grade she entered. She makes the class much more difficult than it is and does not care about the students. She's also quite mean and ignorant when you talk to her.
This is the worst course coordinator ever. Don't even bother emailing her because she won't respond. Also, incorrect grades are entered. The professors don't even help.

BEWARE. Don't take a class with her. I second that she doesn't respond to emails at all. Expect your response time to be months. I don't even think she communicates with the professor of the class too. Impossible to get in touch with and will put your grades in wrong. She is rude when you do speak to her face to face. Be READY to argue your grades.

Renee Alt is to blame for this being the worst class I've ever taken. The professors are fine, and the material is understandable. Renee, on the other hand, has entered incorrect grades into the gradebook for not only myself, but also for several other students in the class. The worst part is that she does not respond to ANY emails. ZERO.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took this for MCB 301, but I couldn't chose that option for this review. I had her as well in MCB 300. She is not good at all. She never responds to emails and told me i'd lose points if I missed class more than the allotted 2 days she gave me for COVID.

Rene Alt doesn't care about this class. She doesn't answer emails. Additionally, I have never had a lab that has had 4 exams with a two day lab practical. It is way too much work for 2 credit hours. I honestly wanted to learn something from this class. All I got was a headache.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Alt is unorganized, lazy, and doesn't care about her students' success. She won't even answer your email so don't bother. She messed up the exam key on the first exam and made everyone argue. their points back, which makes absolutely no sense. Terrible class, terrible professor, terrible department.

One of the most unorganized professors I've ever seen. This class was way too much work for 2 credit hours. The professor was so lazy, didn't do anything.. not even answer emails. You could tell the TAs were over it too. She doesn't care about her students and makes everything so much more difficult on everyone. Do not take this class.
The class follows a flipped format, with prelectures and weekly group activities. The prelectures which were quite long. The videos were quite dull and not explained especially well.

One of the only classes at UIUC where reverse classroom works due to many helpful TAs. Lecture videos are comprehensive and very similar to homework / exams. Many people complain about Exam Coding Questions, but really arent that challenging, usually arent similar to homeworks, require you to think outside the box but not overly difficult
her lecture videos are so bad at explaining things. GA's are not related to the content we learn and become about following mindless instructions

This class is a joke, and this professor is a joke. Her lectures don't help with learning anything. The exams are difficult for no reason. I genuinely feel like she wants her students to fail. Avoid this class if possible, and if you can't, GOOD LUCK.

She's the worst CS professor I've ever seen. none of the videos do a very good job at explaining the concept. this course need some linalg background, she never recap the key linalg concepts, keep saying review on your own. coding question in quizzes sometimes are too time consuming, course is overall doable but i'll never take her class again

The coding questions on the biweekly quizzes are awful. They tend to veer away from the course content that we have been practicing, and they tend to differ from the practice quizzes substantially. If you ask for help on Discord (our main method of communication), it is likely that you will get a snarky response from the professor or TAs.

The content of the coding questions on the quizzes are substantially different from the lectures, HWs, and GAs. Unless you have a REALLY good understanding of Python, you will struggle on the coding portion of the quiz. Practice quizzes are good for non-coding, but even then, questions completely different from the pq show up on the actual quiz.
Great professor:)

Mariana is a very supportive professor, and the course itself contains a lot of resources to learn. The course policy for late work is very fair, and I believe the content is a decent mix of informative and easy. While I do think improvements can be made on the material, I think it's in a pretty good state, and Mariana's a good reason why.

I had the online section, and it was very doable. The textbook is the most crucial thing in this class; it helps a lot for homework and especially quizzes. The GAs and MPs were also doable, and Discord helped answer any questions. The Python questions on the test were the hardest parts. Her videos are also helpful, but the relevance for HWs is tiny

The structure sucks, have a STRONG linalg background if not go back and review. start learning python especially the numpy library! the coding questions on quizzes are very difficult. Lecture videos suck and homework does not connect to test and those optional study halls USELESS!!! TAs can not respond in timely manner and theres A TON OF WORK.

Flipped structure means you watch lecture videos before class. The videos are good but don't relate too much to the HW. The tests are fine and you get access to the MC questions beforehand, as well has get the course textbook in the CBTF. She is also super nice and loves education. Overall a great class, but you need to be well versed in LinAlg.

Great professor, very patient and works with her students to help them be successful in the class! The class is very linear algebra and multi-variable calculus heavy so your strengths in those topics basically determine what you get out of the class.

The prof is very hard to follow and her material does not co-relate to the homework. I have used github as reference for these homeworks and have been good so far. Grind hard before quizzes and you'll be 'chillin

three weeks in and this course is already a disaster. the quiz had broken questions which the professor promised would be taken care of later, however now she is only giving the option to retake the entire quiz but won't give points for something that's her fault cause it could be unfair to other students.

The professor puts in no effort for this class. She runs the class using the same material from a previous professor from 9 years ago. That's why the TAs and the professor are clueless and leave it up to us to figure things out.
Least organized professor at the university. No flow between topics. Has outdated lecture videos which don't do a good job explaining.

Some people find this class to be tedious due to the PrairieLearn setup, however I found that it allows for students to get out of the course what they want to. The practice quizzes and quizzes have the same bank with unlimited practice quiz attempts. I think anyone that put in ~10 hours/week could receive an A.

This class should not have been this hard. The quizzes were always a pain when it came to the coding questions. How are people with very minimum coding experience in Python expected to know to just code with the math based leetcode type questions?
Professor Silva is an amazing teacher and person. I got to know her better during her office hours, and ultimately had her write me a letter of recommendation.
Awful note ecosystem. Frequently, the prerecorded videos will have typos, and / or are explained poorly.
Know your linear algebra before taking this class...
I did not find the lectures that helpful. If you do the homework, it should be sufficient to do well on the quizzes. The MPs could be documented better.
This class trolls hard

She did not care about student's opinions and feedback until way too far into the semester. At the end it turned out ok as long as you did the practice quizzes and exam but her as a prof would not recommend.

Besides being extremely petty, she's actually a good/fun professor. This class could've been a lot more boring but she made it fun and something to look forward to every week. It's an easy A if you do the work (which is not a lot).

The course website has a lot of useful resources (helped me a lot with learning Python), and lectures were decent. All the homeworks, group assignments, and quizzes/exams were on Prairelearn. Group assignments were doable, quizzes and homeworks were similar in difficulty, and she allowed us to use resources on the course website during quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This professor did not adjust well to online teaching. The course itself is fine, and pretty easy. However, there were at times frustrating issues with the online courseware this professor was using. It wasn't always clearly communicated. I am someone who reads the website, and is self motivated and I still struggled to figure out what she wanted.

Prof. Silva was a good professor. Her notes were really helpful for the quizzes and her lectures were decent. The homework prepared you for the quizzes. As long as you attend lecture and keep up with the homework, an A is not too difficult. She is really caring and even reviewed my buggy code personally on Piazza. One of the most underrated profs
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Some of the coding problems on the homework were hard to debug. The homeworks prepared you well for the quizzes. I found all the quizzes and the final managable. The lectures were not that good, and while I managed to figure out how to do the problems on the exams, I did not understand the theory behind many of the concepts.

Okay so I took this class in Spring 2020 (the semester of COVID). The first half of this class was in person as normal and it was fine. The beginning of the class was definitely a little tricky in terms of material though. After things transitioned online, Prof Silva was extremely accommodating and caring and I really admired that.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Mariana is unreasonable and super petty. Probably needs therapy (I mean same)

Professor Silva is many things, but a good teacher is not one of them. The quizzes focus on everything you didn't see in the homework. She has specifically said she searches the slides for things she did not put in homework to test on. Pretty unprofessional, always talking about how she knows students do not like her in a weird way
good prof!!!
Stuck up. Debugging is sometimes really difficult, absolutely garbage problems at times. Office hours is a complete joke
Too much in a hurry. Always talking faster than anyone can understand. Can't survive without rewatching lectures. Pretty fun at times though.
