reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Quizzes are so hard to pass and she will never give you a break from homework and quizzes. Very time consuming and even when you'll study for the quizzes you won't do well.

She made the class a LOT EASIER than before, since the Prairelearn NOW gives us the answers to the questions we can share with more people ahead of time. I'm a math major and I took it as Math 357, so I took the class for an easy A cuz I had the help of people ahead of me. She forgets to record her own voice but that doesn't affect my performance.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I wouldn't give her a high grade because she turns off the volume of recording intentionally to force students to take her class. Besides that, the rest is fine.

This class is a ton of lin alg later on, so be prepared for that. She's a good lecturer, so if you pay attention you should be fine. All lectures are also posted online which was super useful. Very steady workload, weekly homework and a CBTF quiz every 2 weeks. ALL other practice material is in the textbook, so get it if you think you'll need it.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Love that course materials are available online. Great lecture notes and explanation of difficult material.
Mariana (Silva) Sohn's last semester teaching TAM 251 :(, she has moved to CS so you'd better register for CS 357 next sem if you want to be taught by her! Best teacher ever!
Prof. Silva is the BEST I've had in Chambana. A pleasure in lecture and office hours, cares about students, answers emails (quickly), and fantastic lecture presence. Highly recommended!

I wouldn't say Silva is a great professor, but she makes it very easy to get a good grade. Quizzes are directly from the homework and the final is simple if you generally understand the concepts. At the end of the day, as long as you understand how to do each homework question this class should be an easy A.
Great teacher. Class isn't too difficult, although at times the online quizzes (taken at a testing center) can catch you off guard.
The lectures are easy to follow and she feels approachable as a person. The volume of homework seemed a little high, but don't let that keep you away from her classes.
Very well explained material.  If you stay on top of the homework and go to lectures, it isn't hard to get a high A.  Make sure you understand early topics since the material builds upon itself.
There is a lot of homework for 20% of the final grade

She's so friendly. You can tell she genuinely cares about the success of her students. Lectures were easy to follow and helped a lot with understanding. Plus, she records them and uploaded them online which is INSANELY helpful, especially around finals season. Highly recommended.

Best Professor I've seen in U of I. Amazing lectures offered, and very clear explanation. Dr. Silva is always open for students.  I took her TAM 211 for my first semester as a freshman in college. I get an A+ and end up with 102%. I am sure if you take her class, work hard and go to office hour often, you will perform as good as I did.
Dr. Silva is a great professor - very clear and structured lectures, fair exams (she gives major hints on exam content).

One of my best professors so far. Her presentation of the material is very clear –  I highly recommend her. Also, some advice: attend all review sessions. The example problems are almost identical to the ones on the exams.
Awesome professor! She makes everything understandable and tells you exactly what's going to be on the midterms. Go to lecture because she moves quickly.
so nice and helpful! offers lots of extra credit and the exams are very straightforward. she even tells you what's going to be on them. one of the best professors I've had.
so nice and helpful! offers lots of extra credit and the exams are very straightforward. she even tells you what's going to be on them. one of the best professor's I've had.

She is very helpful and teaches very well.  Her cheerful manner really helped since the class was at 9 am.  She is definitely one of my favorite professors.  Tests weren't really all that hard and there was plenty of extra credit available.  If you can take a class with her, take it.
She is really good and cares about her students. Helps a lot during the tests and wants her students to excel.

Awesome professor. She was very helpful throughout the entire course, and you can tell that she actually cares about her students' success.  She was really good at preparing us for exams, and would give a clear outline for what to expect on midterms and the final. I would definitely recommend taking a class with her.
Very kind and helpful. She definitely cares about her students.
Easiest TAM class Ive ever had. Plus she TEACHES other than teacher like Keane.

She is the absolute BEST tam professor I've had. She actually cares about her students and wants them to succeed. She posts helpful lecture notes and provides review sessions for her students (seriously, no tam professor has ever done that). She is really friendly and nice and most importantly, TEACHES! Take a class with her!
Lecture is a tad bit redundant but going or not going won't deteremine your grade.  Her tests are tough as they are purely theory based and no numbers are in any problems.
Explained the reading concepts very clearly. No homework other than the readings.

I took two courses with her and wish I could have taken more. She is incredibly knowledgable and passionate. She's also friendly, kind, and tries to make her class as fun as possible for students. Her lectures focus on the readings, which she's fabulous at explaining in depth, so reading out of class isn't a must.

This post updates a post I made Dec. 21st. when I was disgruntled at Professor Weinberg. I want to set the record straight. Professor Weinberg gives amazing lectures, which makes the material in the course seem simple, when it is incredibly complex. Professor Weinberg cares for her students and wants the best for everyone. -Shane Werner

Arrogant and Unprofessional at times when discussing certain topics. She appears self righteous and mixes her personal views with her professional teachings. Her lectures are boring and she wants you to stay focused. If you want to wake up every day coming to see someone who is in her 50s and often shows an angry face, take Weinberg.

One of the worst professors at uic, Weinberg always mixes her personal opinion with her professional opinion. Her lectures speaks of human logic but absent good hearts which reflects how often she hates Christianity. Stay away from this professor. Her class doesn't add any value to real life other than rant about how smart she is. 2 + 2 = 4

I wouldn't waste my FAFSA loan on taking her courses, there is nothing good that she can teach and to try to make it look that her class is worth it, she is a harsh grader. Her lectures are many times slow, she often comes with a mean demeanor to the class and hates it when students aren't paying attention because she is boring.

Professor Weinberg seems harsh, but her class is incredibly informative and well put together. If you talk to her she will absolutely help you. The papers are not graded too harshly either. Overall great intro class.

I don't usually leave reviews on here, but I just had to for Dr. Weinberg. Not only is she extremely intelligent and knowledgeable about what she teaches, but she also lectures in a way that makes it easy to understand some difficult course material. Papers are graded hard, but they are graded fairly with great feedback. Take PHIL with Dr Weinberg!

She's a fantastic professor and person. The only gripe I have is that she's not willing to say "yeah you've basically got it"—you've got to say EXACTLY what she's thinking in class for her to accept it. It's a little annoying, but it's a tiny flaw in an otherwise airtight teaching skillset. She's very personable.

Weinberg's class is easy and straightforward, but she can be very rude at points. She would give very little credence to the questions asked by students and would be very dismissive unless she liked you as a student.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Unprofessional at times when discussing certain topics (e.g. God) and fails to asses student understanding in lecture; but she gives room during class for questions and thorough feedback, and her TA grades fairly easy. Showing up to class is essential to pass the class. She goes really fast with the slides, so try and write as much as you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Shelley is a great lecturer and every class will be good to watch. You don't need to read the readings really except for paper writing. There are three papers to write and they are graded hard but fair. The papers are all the points.

The class itself is a bit confusing but Dr. Weinberg is super sweet and considerate. She words lectures in a way that we can understand and it's actually intro level. She keeps the class interesting and she's probably one of my favorites. Literally in no way mean, she's also super funny.

I don't love school at all, but I LOVEED this class. She is so clearly passionate about philosophy which made the lectures really interesting and genuinely enjoyable. She goes through the slides quite fast tho, so I recommend taking pictures of them if you don't think you'll be able to get it all down. The info on the slides were IMMENSELY helpful.

I took ACP and it was great! Prof Weinberg selects great TA's that are always willing to help you out on the 3 big papers that basically determine your grade. The reading material is fascinating and Prof Weinberg is an extremely inspiring and captivating lecturer. Her passion for philosophy is evident, and it makes you want to learn it all too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: good class
She does a good job of engaging the class and breaking down the important ideas of the text.

I love Professor Weinberg! She's a great lecturer, which is helpful considering the course is about different metaphysical and epistemological systems from the early-modern period. She'll answer any questions or objections in class and encourages class participation. Highly recommend going to paper review sessions with T.A. if you want to do well
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Shelly is such a good lecturer and you can really tell she's passionate about philosophy. I loved taking her course. She doesn't do any grading but she's always down to help you with your papers. It's best to go to lecture because anything you need for your papers is basically discussed in lecture. Strongly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She isn't the greatest lecturer, but she is sure knowledge. Not much assignments, but grades are VERY HARSH. Materials are quite interesting, but not interesting enough that motivates you to learn. I took it for advanced composition and regret much for such decision.

Shelley is just amazing. She has an excellent command on early modern empiricists (Especially Locke). She is great as a professor and LOVES philosophy and this love permeates through to her lectures as well. One of the best professors I have ever had the privilege to learn from

Professor Weinberg is one of the most amazing people I have met. I have learned a lot from that class, and her. Anyone seriously interested in learning a uniquely smart way of viewing philosophy should take any of her classes :)

Great Professor. The material was not exactly a walk in the park, but she does a wonderful job helping students understand. She is funny also. Enjoyed the course simply because of her, I could have done without Leibniz lol :)

Shelley is a wonderful instructor and I absolutely recommend taking her class. She is very reasonable when it comes to grading and submission dates. So long as you are invested in the course, she will be willing to help you succeed. Tells jokes in class all the time and always happy to be there.
she's brilliant. awesome!!!! TAKE HER!!!!!!!

Tough grader on homework, but the class isn't too bad. The lectures seem to mesh together since everything seems the exact same as the previous lecture. Second half of semester is very challenging. I kind of regret taking this class because I don't like thinking critically, but for others, it maybe be helpful.

This woman is so funny! I feel like every lecture is her venting about her life to all of the students. She hates my class because we don't really do a great job of staying attentive, but it's pretty fun. As for the curriculum, I feel that i made a huge mistake taking this class, it is way too simple.

This class was a lot of fun and really taught you how to look at argumentation. Beginning of the semester was really easy, but review often, because it gets a lot harder near the end. She takes attendance, but her pleasant awkwardness makes class fun and entertaining. I recommend this class.
She's good and cares about her students. Plenty of energy.

She is harsh on papers, but she's really helpful and wants you to understand the materials. I feel it's hard to not like her class, at least students in our class all say they enjoy it a lot. Her first few weeks are easy, but it suddenly gets hard, be prepared, don't complain after you get bad grade on a paper!
She is really nice and helpful, she inspires me to minor Phil!

She takes attendance for every lecture and discussion! There is not much work to do for the class but she wont give you A. The first assignment is misleadingly easy and from the second one on, she starts to give low grades. No finals, no exmas, but she(or maybe the TAs) is harsh on your papers.

I find her a great teacher.  She is living the life you would imagine a philosophy teacher to live.  This class has interested me in going into law, an unforeseen venture.  She selects great TA's too.

My main criticism is that the class moves a little slow sometimes; we had to cut out everything on Kant. She tries to make things as clear as possible and wants students to understand the material. Overall I enjoyed her class a lot. To succeed in the class you MUST do the readings!
Very outdated class. Decent guy but very bad professor.

He was horrible at explaining any content. Lecture slides were mostly images as he'd just yap. When there were equations - there would be no explanation of what the equation was for or what the variables represented. He also doesn't provide all the necessary equations to solve problems.

The class average for the first exam was 44%, Stark decided that it would be funny if he invited a previous student who he had given an job offer to. Had the previous student explain why she loved geotechnical engineering and then preceded to laugh as he told the class that he would never extend an offer like that to us because we were incompetent.

While he's kind funny, his lectures are pointless and it's more worth it to watch a youtube video from home where you can get explanations on how to actually do things. Hardest bit of calculus from his class is deriving what he's saying.

Tumanov is the worst professor I've ever had. Taking Calc 1? RUN!!! His lectures are pre-written notes with a heavy accent, zero explanation, and skipped steps. His tests are impossibly hard with brutal time limits. He's inflexible and seems to enjoy student misery. Save yourself the pain and pick literally anyone else. You've been warned.

This professor gave very difficult exams in a short time span, leaving you not being able to finish the exams most of the time. It was also very difficult to follow his lectures as he didn't follow the textbook and had prewritten notes which often skipped steps.

Consider taking a different professor if you don't want to struggle in this course. Tumanov uses pre-written notes to teach rather than working out problems on the board. His accent along with that makes it difficult to understand what he says.
Very difficult professor. I recommend studying a lot as his lectures don't prepare you for the exams at all .

learning like a dog and got a C
If you currently has no religion, I urge you to pick one and start praying if you are in his class.
Do not take with him
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst teacher I've had in my whole life, super hard to understand with his thick Russian accent. No one attends lecture because of how hard to understand it is. Getting an A in this class in nearly impossible. Switch out this class asap. His exams are extremely hard, and very hard to score high on.

Worst professor I have ever studied with; He only reads from lecture notes (which he post on canvas) and NOTHING ELSE. Does not really understand what a student is asking when they actually asked an in-depth question about the lecture. If this is actually the scenario for others, I do not recommend taking his class unless you have no other choice.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tumanov was the worst professor I have ever had in my entire life. He had a thick russian accent that was incomprehensible and would only read over his notes that he did a day prior. He often got confused by his own notes because he skipped steps. Do not take this professor. Drop the class and take it at a community college or another semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He doesn't speak a single ounce of English. If you have him please switch! It was very bad all semester!

Tumanov is a nice man but a bad teacher. His lectures aren't worth attending because he doesn't work through or explain problems, he only reads off his notes which are mostly proofs. Exams are also difficult. If you need to take 220, take it with any other professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He reads off his notes, never wrote a single thing on the blackboard. He has a heavy accent which is very hard to understand. His tests are way harder than his practice problems. Will never take a class with him.
The course provides enough materials and notes to do the self-learning but the lectures are really difficult to understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Whatever you do, PLEASE take a different professor for this course. I was lucky enough to have a great TA help me through the semester, but even he couldn't save me from Tumanov's messy and confusing lectures. I can tell Tumanov is passionate about calculus, but his teaching style is abhorrent; he never once worked through a problem by hand.
Just took my final

Very nice man, weak lecturer. Exams are tough. Expect to spend 30+ hours a week studying. Weekly quizzes are manageable. Your best bet is to the lecture notes, do the problems in the workbook, use outside resources. I also recommend looking over for mistakes and asking for regrades, he's very willing to do so.

there hasn't been a single lecture where this professor has written on the chalkboard and i usually end up more confused than actually learning anything. i highly recommend finding another professor that teaches math 220

If you are going to take Math 220 and this is the only professor left, forget about it and take it at a community college. This class is so disorganized. 75% of the time the professor is either babbling about some math nonsense that has nothing to do with the lesson or the note problems don't match the text book problems at all. Don't do it.
Absolutely the most horrible professor probably here at UIUC takes points off for the most random things

Your grade in Tumanov's class is very dependent on how much effort you put on your own time. He is a very passionate individual, but his teaching style during lectures lacks depth. I could not rely on his lectures alone to learn the material. PLEASE go to office hours and tutoring sessions at the Jeffries Center if you take his class.

Honestly the worst professor I've had so far. He has an extremely heavy accent, so you can barely understand him sometimes. On top of that, he has prewritten notes that sometimes have errors. He doesn't teach, he just reads through the notes. Doesn't ask questions to try and engage the class at all. I don't redolent taking with this professor!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Very hard to understand and won't work through problems shown will only talk about them. Make sure to do homework and fully understand it otherwise the class is a lost cause.
Does not know how to teach, only suing prewritten notes. Decent person, but horrible at teaching.
He's not teaching at all.
This guy cannot teach. Better to watch youtube lectures than actually go to his. You can barely understand him cause his thick accent and he uses messy pre-handwritten notes to teach.
Teaches off hand written notes and barely explains what he's doing.

It's very hard to understand what he's talking about because his English isn't very good. He reads from prewritten notes that often contain errors, leaving everyone confused. If you have him, good luck you'll probably end up learning from Khan Academy and YouTube instead.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He does not speak the greatest English and is very hard to understand. He is very arrogant when sending out emails, I had asked about some confusion, and he pretty much insinuated I did not go to lectures, discussions or work on anything. When he doesn't know what to say in English he just starts mumbling. He uses prewritten notes and goes by fast.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: just don't bruh...

Horrible accent, basically had only discussion to really understand anything, and no overall teaching is actually taking place, just reading off from the notes with no explanations. Tests every week and everything is so off schedule, lectures were one week ahead, discussions a couple days ahead and tests were one week behind. Don't recommend.

Very hard to understand his speech, he has a thick accent and broken english while also going through example problems much too quickly, moving onto new questions without even waiting. He makes calculus seem much, much more difficult than it actually is.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I literally dropped his 220 lecture while I was inside the lecture hall. He doesn't use a microphone, just tries to speak loudly, which makes it literally impossible to understand him as he also has a thick accent and broken English. I don't think those are inherently bad qualities of a professor, but you literally cannot hear the guy.

Tumanov is one of the kindest and most caring people I have met. Always smiling and encourages participation in his class. Hard class and his class structure was something I wasn't a fan of though. His exams are easy but there isn't really anything to pad your grades. His class is not for the faint of heart.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The worst professor ever

This class was very difficult, but I appreciated the lectures. Dr. Tumanov was very passionate about teaching, and explained things well.

If you have a very solid math and proof background, I think this class would be amazing. Otherwise, you might not have a good time. There wasn't a lot of filler points to boost your grade in the class.
Worst professor in the entire math department. He does not speak English.

MATH 448 - You need to build a strong intuition for the proofs to get an A, but course is generally more computation-heavy than a normal analysis course. Teacher reads off of lecture notes, but go to lecture and read through notes a second time. Teacher provides the type of questions before the test so you can build your own practice test.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: You should probably switch out of his class if you're in Math 285. You will be far better off with any other professor, even if you have to sign up for an 8 AM.
Does not really speak English and has a great difficulty helping students understand the materials. If you have chances to switch another section, go for it.

Doesn't teach out of the textbook assigned, not to mention the textbook is horrible at showing steps and giving clear explanations for problems.  Usually gives quiz and  test questions that require a "deeper understanding" of the concepts of the chapter which aren't entirely covered in the homework.  However, he is an easy grader of homework.

Tumanov quite simply cannot lecture. About halfway through the semester, I attended class but stopped paying attention. His accent makes him hard to understand not that without it, he'd be able to lecture well anyway. If you do the homework and study you will do well, but with a different prof, that level of work would be a letter grade higher.

You don't learn much from Tumanov as his explanations aren't great and he has a hard to understand accent. In addition to this, the book isn't exactly all that clear either. If you do end up taking this class, prepare to learn how to solve a specific problem for an exam rather than properly understanding the concepts. Generous curve though.

A professor of absolutely appalling quality. I learnt nothing from the course. He was incomprehensible with a heavy accent. Attendance was horrible-no one showed up. TA's were not helpful. No extra credit despite numerous requests.. and exams of unpredictable difficulty. Overall Fail.
nice man, available for help. homework and quizzes every week. exams weren't too bad, and the grade curve was generous
Nice person, available for help. homework and quizzes every week. exams weren't too bad and the grade curve was generous

one of the worst teacher one can have. Doesn't really speak English, and doesn't know how to teach either. No one ever goes to his lecture, you have to teach yourself. Exams are not that bad, have to do the previous exams

He did poor job explaining the theory and he jumped a lot. However, his grading is pretty fair, and he almost tells us every problem on the test, so grade is not a problem. If you review the stuff after each class and make sure you get everything on the homework, then A is in your pocket.My little tip is: find an excellent study buddy!That helps me

You get used to the accent but that doesn't help the fact that when you ask him questions he doesn't understand and answers something else. In lecture he didn't use enough examples and does way too much proof which made the hw extremely hard. However, he gives reasonable tests and lots of partial credit! Very fair exams

Cool accent, nice guy, available for help, not a very good teacher because he usually doesn't use good examples (just proofs). I took Math 241 Honors and he told us wat questions were gonna be on each test. Start the hw early and you should be fine

HORRIBLE professor. Most math professors here are bad but I've never been so confused in a subject like with him. lecture was pointless he just outlined the book, my ta was also bad and a hard grader. test averages have been ~65%, homework is ridiculous along with quizzes and the book does not help any, moral of the story FIND A DIFFERENT PROFESSOR

Tumanov is your average UIUC math professor; and by average I mean bad at teaching, and foreign with a thick accent. That being a said, the accent is not impossible; my advice is to take a seat front and center. The lectures have too many proofs and not enough well explained examples. The tests are difficult, but not impossible.

Good solid professor. Much better choice than the other professor(s) on campus. Calculus is difficult, there's no way around it, but he's very clear in examples and extremely thorough. Exams are not too difficult if you keep up with the hw and quizzes. Hw never due, but necessary to succeed.

Major beef is that he is extremely hard to understand. It honestly took me 3 weeks to get used to his accent. Meanwhile I had to study primarily from the book just to keep my head above water. He obviously knows his stuff, but teaches like he's the only one in the room, usually ignoring raised hands. Avoid if possible.

Isn't really too hard to understand, and if you pay close attention in class the material becomes more coherent. He gives very difficult quizzes and tests, but there is a steep curve on final grades. Go to class for the examples but study the concepts from the book before hand.
He isn't very helpful, seems quite apathetic toward his students, and speaks in a funky accent.

god awful. Doesn't know how to teach, and have a VERY strong accent. If you'd like to ACTUALLY learn math , I suggest you drop the minute you get in there. I regret I haven't chekced this before hand.

Hard to understand, and not just because of accent/mediocre command of English language.  NOT good at explaining/teaching.  Hard exams, inadequate curve for class (class average for final exam was a D WITH the curve)  Aviod this guy if you can, but since U of I won't let you see your math prof. when you sign up, it's really just a matter of chance.

VERY strong accent, hard to understand his words sometimes. there is NO curve in his classes  -  class average was a D+.  Proves every formula, and thinks every example he does is "trivial". TERRIBLE.

makes errors on practice exams. if you make those errors yourself, you usually earn 20%. felt like there was much handwaving and blamelaying in officehours. if he had an entry like this before i took the class, i would have not taken it with him.
Talks with a thick Russian accent, not very helpful or accommodating outside of class.
Extremely easy class. The lectures are super slow, but I found it easier to use the recordings and put them on 2x speed. I really cant comment on how he is as a lecturer too much.

If this class wasn't CBTF based, it would be much more enjoyable; however, professor Ravaioli gives fair questions on the exams outside of a few tricks. The Prairielearn homework is OK, sometimes going overboard on what is actually necessary for the exams. This course required the least amount of time for me this semester.
Best class I've taken this semester. He is a very nice guy, and the content is very straightforward. it was easy to get an A. my GOAT

GOAT Professor in ECE. Living Icon. Cares about what he is teaching and is very accessible for questions outside of class, both on class content and beyond. Very good teacher. Wish he taught more classes I could take.
Great professor, lectures are not mandatory and mostly consist of solving example circuits. Lectures are recorded and posted online. Funny guy.

ECE 205 content is so simple, it is not worth going to lecture to hear this man give 15 minute examples and long tangents. He's funny, but that's really about it. Consider watching the videos at 2x speed or just reading the annotated slides. Better yet, just try the prairielearn answers until you somehow get it right. It got me an A+.
very enthusiastic about teaching, but teaching methods aren't efficient. a lot of extra credit offered.
Nice professor! 4% extra credits, and exams are not overly hard. Do the work, and get the reward.
Teaches us cirquits better than ECE majors

He is a nice man and clearly cares. The class is not overly hard (I am bad at circuits and did fine) but can feel like a lot at times. The lectures can feel very boring but I personally found them useful. The extra credit was amazing.

Professor Ravioli clearly wants his students to succeed and is enthusiastic about what he teaches. He gives great lectures that are recorded, but I'd recommend going to class. Quizzes are pretty similar to the homework assignments. He also offers some extra credit that's definitely worth doing. Overall a very good professor that you need to take.

Prof Ravaioli loves what he does, and it shows. He's always so enthusiastic when he's talking about circuits. All of his lectures are recorded and put online, but I recommend going in person anyways. Be warned: he puts some extra content in his slides that you won't be tested on but he is still very good at presenting the content.

He is very passionate about teaching. He knows almost everything that students brought up during class. He can talk about something for a long time, and make sure he made everything clear. If you want to do research, he is definitely a good professor to seek.
Excellent professor, and pretty funny. Don't skip class, though. You'll regret it.
Good Professor, uses slides rather than the board - 'so no need to take notes' :) Very useful class, but dont skip, and u will be fine
I would rather spend some time discussing quantum physics with my friend's Samoyed than listening to this guy's lectures.
I actually had him for ECE220 over the summer and he was a great professor. Super great at explaining and was able to explain notes in a very comprehensive way.

Besides him being nice, the guy has nothing going for him. He just repeats what Lumetta says in lectures but worse. He gets confused while teaching concepts and says sry then spends 10 more minutes on one thing. As soon as the concepts got difficult, the professor became worse at teaching than your average Undergrad who took the class and got a B.

He is significantly better than other professors who got a 1.+ rating. I got an A- since I was too busy looking for jobs. ECE374 is not a very hard course, and easier with Prof. Umrawal. He explained the concepts pretty well and was nice to the students. He provides a chill vibe in the lecture, too. This guy doesn't belong in this rank.

Worst professor ever—he rips Kani's materials, tweaks them, and claims them as his own. His lectures are so unclear everyone learns from Kani's videos. He grades harshly: no curve on a tough final, splits 3 midterms into 4 brutal exams, and delays the final-drop decision. Pure incompetence.
CIA should use his lecture recordings as interrogation tools

Half the time I'm unsure if he knows what he's teaching. I wish that Lumetta dude was still teaching here, I honestly don't know why I even attend the lectures anymore. I only got through the first few weeks because I learned lots of basic content over break, and still he made things I've learned already seem confusing.
he is a nice guy but his teaching is crazy boring
perfect cure for insomnia

Some ppl don't like him but I think he is really nice. There were some things that could have been improved but he was incredibly understanding and receptive to feedback. 374 is a hard class whether you want to admit it or not but his exams were not bad and he tried his best. More humble than a majority of other profs here, cares ab students lots

Consistently put DIY on example problems, was inconsistent with THE SYLLABUS, and overall not great. There was always something unexpected with these exams. The curving he implemented made little or no sense.
DIY.
Professor skips slides during lecture very often, and concept w
DIY
He made jokes. I liked the way he taught.

Professor subbed in for Prof. Nickvash Kani during the Fall 2023 semester. He is okay. It's his first time teaching lectures ever. So I hope it gets better over time. Is awful at time management for lectures, reads off lecture slides and doesn't necessarily understand the lecture presentations made under Prof. Kani. Watch video lectures instead.
Great speaker

He is honestly one of the best lecturers out there. He really makes everything intuitive and easy to grasp - given you attend the lectures. The exams were in the CBTF which means no partial credit, which is a bit odd for a stats course. The homework felt a little too tedious for the course, and the discussion section is not useful at all.

Class was incredibly mismanaged this semester, each CBTF exam having grading issues/typos worse than the last. Despite this, there was a heavy air of condescension in emails and in-person communication concerning these issues. Almost half of the final exam was on topics that were not covered on any midterm. Decent lecturer and homework was fine
Take class with Yu
sucks dont take him

Good lecturer and engaging. Homeworks are a little tedious but not too time-intensive. The online tests (for STAT 400) were frustrating since they offered little/no partial credit, which is strange for a statistics class since it disproportionately penalizes small calculation mistakes on problems requiring many steps.

Very caring guy, material is rather easy but as mentioned by a lot before me, the exams and Ed Discussion are handled terribly. No responses and tedious exam grading. It did fortunately get regraded but still no communication to students. Be aware of this, its okay if you have prior Statistics experience, but otherwise steer clear.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Never felt more disrespected taking a class. Shows up 5 or more minutes late to lecture every single time. Questions on discussion forum are only ever answered by the same poor TA that can be bothered to log on (shoutout Adam Tonks fr). Material is incredibly easy but class is graded very strictly, particularly exams, and homeworks are so tedious.

Class is hanging by a thread. Super mismanaged class. Earlier, it used to be pen & paper, but now CBTF, which is where it has all gone downhill. Would recommend taking Professor Albert Yu's class instead, as it is still Pen and Paper!

I heard good things about this professor and class coming into stat 400 this semester but it turned out to be one of my worst experiences here so far. It feels like students aren't taken seriously because no one answers our questions on ED, midterms in cbtf have been hell, class in general has been very disorganized too.

I truly believe my degree is worth less for associating me with this professor.

CBTF exam questions consistently had incorrect answers and were met with absolutely zero response from the professor, despite literally 100+ Ed discussion posts asking about the blatant errors. Clearly doesn't care about his students whatsoever. Run fast and run far.

They ask u to post question on Ed, but important one never get answered on time, and the homework grading expectations are extremely unclear, u barely can get 100 in hw. If you can, take STAT 400 with any other professor.
worst organization skills in a professor I seen in a while

Right now, we're dealing with an AWS outage, so we can't access anything on Canvas. Tell me why he provides us with a drive with the stuff from canvas, but the homework due tomorrow or the exam material isn't posted. Like you cannot be this disorganized. On top of that, they don't respond to student messages about this issue. Genuinely, so stupid.

Currently taking the class, and the professor and his TAs take forever to respond to questions on Ed Discussion. Like there's questions from a month or like weeks ago that STILL haven't been responded to. So if you have a question or if there's any issues going on, good luck.
Easy exam content, but will take off points for the most tedious reasons. Easy to get an A though.

he is a nice man. however, his organization skills are horrendous. he frequently makes changes to the course's grading, homework submission rules, and he didn't even give information about when the cbtf exams are until at most a week before. have to go to lecture since he doesn't announce these changes on canvas or the forum.
Take what is said in the syllabus with a grain of salt.
I was his student in 2023. He is nice and knowledgable.

The professor isn't bad but I just self taught everything for STAT400 by using online resources. The homeworks and exam practices were significantly harder than the actual exam content. His lectures are readily available to watch online and from the minimal lectures I did attend in person hes pretty easy to understand
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Unger's STAT 400 section is pretty easy. The homework takes a while but isn't too bad. The midterms are always easier than the practice problems he posts so I wind up very prepared. Lectures aren't very interesting but that's because he paces things pretty slow to ensure a complete understanding of the concepts behind the formulas.
Awful grading policy!!

Overly strict marking mechanism. both TA and professor tried every possible way to get my marks deducted. Multiple requests for regrade were pointless. Completely changed the point that assignments are meant to help students understand and learn better. I am very anxious and overly stressed.

This class is just AP Statistics with calc 2 in the beginning but harder since Unger explains how the distributions are derived from, as well as basic probability questions with notations.

Ppl criticize Unger for being too easy, but if you haven't done stat, his class is helpful. Be aware of MLE, it is hard, but will not show up until final.

Great lecturer with a sense of humor. Enjoyable lectures (can study without attending), although lectures do become very important after the second midterm. Has 50% of your grade assigned on HW, which are not so bad (4-6 hours per week). The midterms were fair with a harder final. Overall the class is fine if you stay on top of your HWs.

One of my favorite professors so far, his lectures are great. He is accessible at office hours and him and his TAs were great at helping me understand things. He is great at using tech with recorded lectures and online gradescope grading. Exams are easy and HWs are of medium difficulty. Concepts are a great foundation of basic statistics.
Lectures are very engaging. One of my favorite teachers I've had so far. The participation points are easy to forget about though.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: He is a great professor. If you take STAT400, he is definitely better than Albert Yu.

(Actually had STAT 400) 
He's pretty good, not exceptional but did a good job explaining the concepts and breaking down the formulas. His homework can be tough, but it's bearable. I don't enjoy how he does participation, it's open after class until noon the next day, making it easy to forget.
I actually took him for stats 400. He was very good

I have to say that Professor Unger is easily one of, if not the best math lecturers I've had. He is very articulate in class and is happy to answer any questions, and is super knowledgeable. He's easy to follow along with during lectures. I felt that the homework required some self learning, but ultimately I enjoyed this class and would take again.

Very frustrating and time consuming class with overloaded homework and quizzes. Over half the semester is done and he has not released the grades for any of the homeworks. Awful experience. If you can take this class from a different professor please do as it might be less frustrating and more organized.

Professor Unger very clearly teaches you the material. The homework was very engaging and directly applicable to what was being taught in lectures. Due to this course and his teaching have certainly further developed my skills in R and have been properly introduced to SQL.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: For those who are not ready to teach themselves, do not take this course. For those who are not familiar with R, you will rarely learn anything. I do not like self-learning courses, but it is easy to get a high GPA. Thank you, Professor, so much

The professor's teaching method is more than effective. I am always amazed as to how much I have learned after taking a class with this professor. Thanks to his courses, I am able to say I am an advanced user of R and SAS.

Changed the final date by five days ahead. Announced on Monday in class that final will be on Wednesday the same week without sending an email or posting an announcement on compass. Regularly late 10 minutes for class. Often canceled classes until the last minute. Never post grades on time. Still do not know midterm grade in final week.
Unger was a great professor. If he is available, take his section for sure

I'm not sure if this was Unger's fault but the class this class was a joke; Unger basically just read off powerpoints basic SAS syntax;  most of the class finished without knowing a proc import statement. It really should be a 100 or 200 level class; take 448 instead. I would be 90% of the class got As. Its insane grad students are in this class
Very nice professor!

He is so engaging during lectures, no extra effort is needed to prepare for exams if you follow lectures carefully. Lectures are almost similar to SAS certification contents and illustrates nut& bolts of SAS programming. Except heavy home works this course is good, easy to score A.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Like him a lot, very nice, crystal-clear English, excellent analogies, describes and explains everything for any level right there in lecture. So far, just excellent.

David is a kind, emotionally stable instructor. If you see him in his office hours, he will treat you decently and go the extra mile to help you understand the material. His exams are pretty hard though while the classes/homeworks may not always prepare you for an A if that's what you want.
really nice teacher, no extra credit, no curves, very fair, good lecturer

I took a dual credit environmental science class in high school, so this course was really easy for me since it was basically the same stuff--actually probably less in depth than my first class, but if I didn't have that environmental science knowledge it would have absolutely sucked. There would be so much you'd have to read and watch.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: do not take NRES 100. the class takes around 12 hours a week and nothing is ever graded on time. the semester is about to be over and 50% of my grade is still unknown. you also will get docked points for things that don't even make sense. overall, would not recommend anyone take this class unless it is mandatory.

My major is not related to environmental science, and I took this course to fulfill a gen ed within 8 weeks. For my purposes, this class demanded more than I expected. Considering the course was completely online, Ugarte was not very involved, though she replies to emails. The class had more lengthy assignments than I expected.

Her class format is typically a week's worth of fast video lectures (lecture slides are uploaded) with a class discussion board, a writing assignment to do, and a simple quiz. I'd have gotten a better grade had I not forgotten to do two of the quizzes, but overall this class's work is very flexible to fit into wherever you have time.

Govt and NFP Actg was a great class and helped with FAR prep for the CPA exam! Dr. Ulla was wonderful and caring and made class very enjoyable. There is a lot of material, but as long as you stay on top of it, the workload is manageable. She ensured that we all succeeded and provided many resources to help us grasp the topics. Highly recommended!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She is THE best professor I have ever had. She is super caring and genuinely makes so much effort to remember everything about you. She personally cares about the success of each student in her class. She is super accessible every time and will do anything in her power to help and support her students.

Notes were available online and you just fill out all the info during lecture. Lectures were packed with information. Tests were hard and a lot of people ran out of time. Despite course material difficulty, Prof. Ulla is incredibly nice and cares about her students. One of my favorite professors at Illinois but you need to put in the work.

Prof Uribe was nice but she is the worst at responding to emails. Do not think you will get a response from her the entire semester because chances are, you will not. Not responding to important student emails in my opinion, is extremely unprofessional.

She is a somewhat okay professor but her lectures are extremely long and hard to follow. 12pt font and smaller on all of her slides and they are very confusing. When you want further elaboration through office hours, she gives you the feeling like you are not worth her time and she is strictly business only. One of my least favorite poli sci profs

She was a good professor. When asked questions, even off topic, she would try to engage them and give good responses. I personally thought her grading methods and assignments were clear. This was my first introduction to law material and she made it really easy. She's very useful if you have questions, especially if you're asking the right ones!

McGuire is great. Ive taken her 3 semesters in a row because I learn a lot in her class and it is very manageable. She is writing me my letter of reccommendation for law school and I feel like she prepared me well. Sometimes lectures felt long, but if you read the cases ahead of time and are interested in them, I found lectures to be interesting.

Take this website with a grain of salt. People generally only write a review if they really love or hate a professor, and it's usually the latter because of how school is. 

McGuire was one of my favorite professors at the University of Illinois. It's as simple as that.

Be prepared for long and boring lectures. She reads off a slide and notes, and finds a way to make interesting topics boring. Also, you can go the entire semester without her responding to a single email (I did). Not to mention extremely unclear grading procedures, that vary significantly from grader to grader. Worst professor I have had at UIUC.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: unclear grading criteria for case briefs/seemingly had no parameters for her graders other than telling them to command+f specific terms. if you didn't word things in the case brief precisely how she wanted, likely due to a lazy grading regiment that didn't require her or her graders to actually read your work, your homework grade would suffer.

This professor is just so boring. She has mandatory attendance but everything she says during lecture is just read off slides. Going to this class feels so unnecessary and boring. This class is all about memorization and incredibly stupid assignments. I really do not reccommend taking this class unless you have to for your major.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor McGuire is very knowledgeable about the Constitution and her lectures gives a lot of good information . She is pretty understanding and will acknowledge when she makes a mistake or when grading is off for an assignment. The case breifs are not bad, although some take while. But the midterm/final paper is very long and can be intimidating.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Uribe is very sweet, but she is not a good lecturer. Very boring, and presentations are vague. Her exams are hard and worded weirdly (she did let us redo some responses for points back). Studying for the exams, good luck finding the definition unless you went to every single class and paid 100% attention. Can't even find them in the book

I had this professor for two semesters in a row and i absolutely love her class. She was so helpful during covid and modified some things to make it easier on the students. Lectures can feel a bit long and tiring but the content is very interesting. She is hard to reach on emails but a little effort in this class goes a long way

As a student participating in her online class, I have reached out to her many times for assistance and she has responded to nearly none of my emails. It's quite disappointing when you sign up for an online class (COVID-19) and you need to get in touch with your professor and they don't give you any response. Not able to ask questions as a student.

I have had class with her for two semesters in a row and a few things became clear to me. She is very hard to reach, the grading is super inconsistent, and the text book is useless. Her lectures are very dry and not very interesting, but they have a lot of good information. Research HEAVY final paper.

An absolutely awful professor who does not know how to lecture. Four relatively easy case briefs, a mediocre midterm and final, and a research heavy final paper. The grading is probably the worst part of this course, it is not uniform at all. One paper will get a 90% and the same exact paper will get an 80%. Lastly, the book is a waste.

Lectures tend to be not very engaging and generally have low attendance. Briefs for the class are easy, the midterm is reasonable, and the final paper is an immense amount of work. Criteria for briefs, and especially the final paper are unclear and do not establish concrete expectations.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: For this class we wrote four short briefs (one page, pretty easy), and one long brief (10 pages, requires lots of work). There was a midterm and a final which were not too bad if you study. Overall not a bad course.

Professor Uribe-McGuire has amazing lectures, she really brings the issues to life whereas simply reading a book explanation of the issues can fail.  She gives the class a better understanding of The Constitution and the Supreme Court Cases that shape our current law.  Excellent professor.

I took her Separation of Powers class. I thought this class would be hard at first (especially when we went into game theory) but it wasn't as bad as I expected. Her slides are clear and concise-no extra bs. They have everything you need to know for exams. There's one group project  that was difficult, but she's a lenient grader.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Prof has excellent lectures. They're simple and not too flashy, but they really help for learning the materials. Super fair grader as well.

Goes way too fast during lecture. Can't understand what she is talking about. Not a good professor for someone who knows little to nothing about physics. She explains everything as if you should already know what you are doing.

She is one of the best professors I have ever had. Her experience in the field shows in her lectures and her explanations. I have taken her courses since my undergrad and glad to continue to have her in grad school. Marci is an inspiration to what I can become and who I want to be.
Very helpful, clear, and cares about her students inside and outside of class. One of my favorite instructors throughout college.
She's extremely helpful and wants everyone to do well. Be prepared to write tons of notes everyday.
Read the book. Go to class. Take notes. Study the way he suggests. Get an A+. You definitely have to think but he low key tells you exactly what to study and how. Just do what he says.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I like professor Rao. He actually makes stats interesting. Read the book and go to class and take good notes, the exam is all the things he says in class.

Didnt like him. He was constantly complaining about students talking in a 500-person lecture. I don't know what you expected. Kept trying to hold us over class time, and then tried to make people feel bad for leaving when class was over, and he kept talking.

The material feels like it shouldn't be difficult and makes perfect sense until you go to lecture. He acts like if you do everything, then you're guaranteed to do well and understand, but unfortunately that's not true. Most of what he talks about in lecture is actually irrelevant to the coursework, and just makes the class more confusing.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Teaches well, homework and textbook is super helpful. The exams aren't overly hard and the review sessions for the exams are really helpful too.
PROFESSOR RAO IS THE GOAT

I can't believe I got an A- in this class tbh. It feels like a lot of the answers to his questions are ambiguous. For the mandatory lecture, you need an iClicker which he uses to ask you questions. Unfortunately, if you get it wrong, you lose points. There are a lot of extra credit surveys you can fill out though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Had to teach myself everything, always yapping about apples to apples yet I still don't know what that means. save yourself

Hard class but professor does care a lot. Nice curves and cheatsheets as well as practice before exams. Follow the workbook/textbook and you'll be fine. Would be best to make friends and study in groups to understand the concepts better.

Professor Rao seems to get a lot of hate, but I don't think it's warranted. I got a D on my first exam and was frustrated, but then I started reading the book, paying attention in class, and I got an A on the next two exams. Prof Rao is a great guy who's personable and wants to see his students succeed. You'll get out what you put into the class.

I have mixed feeling on this class. I got an A on all exams but I almost entirely taught myself the material. I didn't find the lectures and his teaching style very helpful and I despise the way he does iClicker. Also the TAs are just as confused as the students. However, Prof Rao really cares about his students and wants to make you think.

Rao is the GOAT. I emailed him because I thought I was going to get a C, I did good on exams but missed a lot of homeworks and never went to class so had like a 0 participation score. He said because my exams grades were better than my homework he was going to adjust my grade to match my exam scores!! I went from a C to an A-. GOAT.

Read before class and his lectures make sense, otherwise it's hard to understand what he is explaining. You have to work to get an A but it's worth it. Everything he teaches shows up in all my other classes. Most valuable gen ed I have ever taken.

While I did get an A+, I think that his teaching needs ALOT work. I self taught myself the whole class because his explanations were confusing. This class took so much more time then it should have. The class would have been so much better if he improved his teaching skills. He was really kind, but just not a good professor at all.

professor rao is a great guy and very passionate about statistics and this course. he wants his students to get an A, but you do have to try somewhat in order to get it. he gives out a lot of extra credit that adds up at the end of the semester. you could lwk get a high c on every exam and then do all the extra credit and get an A.

Honestly anyone complaining about this class just didn't put the least amount of effort into this class. He makes it pretty easy to study for the exams as everything is available on canvas. Even if you don't do well on the exams, you can still manage a good grade, for example, my friends got Cs on the tests and still managed to pull out low As.
Don't take this class, it's useless info, and he made up the whole book himself. He also can't explain anything at all.

He does not know how to teach a class well, and this class will take up more time for you than it should. He is a nice person overall, but just a very bad lecturer. He made up this whole class himself, and then when you go to office hours, they don't seem to help. He makes it more confusing than it needs to be because he knows this is just a gen ed
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: the homework doesn't prepare you for what the tests look because the questions are written a lot differently. has resources for you to get help if you need it. He sometimes fails to explain topics that most of the class doesn't understand. must study outside of class to get a good grade!

Why is everyone hating on Rao? If you feel like he didn't help you it's low key your fault. Its definitely a lot though. My roommate and I both took it. She never showed up or read the book so obvi she was always confused and thought Rao is the worst. I actually went to class and took notes and got an A. It's really not bad if you actually try.
Show up to class because he does Iclickers, He does not know how to teach and you basically have to teach yourself the material so take the course online if you can. Always do the surveys.

This was literally the worst class I took this semester. If I'm gonna be honest, he makes everything up the whole class he made up himself. He wrote the book in 5 minutes, and you can tell by the number of mistakes he made throughout the book. He refused to help me understand the material, and sent me to the tas who got even more wrong then I did.

I was gonna write a horrible review, but then he lowkey gave us a crazy deal: he would raise the weight of our second or third midterm grade, and my 91% bumped to a 99% in the class despite getting a D on the last exam. Rao is not so bad. He is a miracle worker, and he's the GOAT. After a semester of suffering, this made it worth it.
Don't take

Stats can be intimidating, especially if you're a beginner. However, professor Rao is a great lecturer. He really explains concepts very well and he also makes stats very interesting and applicable. 
While there is a lot of reading involved, its clear he wants you to succeed and enjoy his class.

I went into this class thinking it would be a traditional, math based course. This was WRONG. There is minimal math- he typically pulls numbers from the air. However, Rao wants his students to think like him and question everything. This is a very conceptual class and if you don't do well with that, good luck and godspeed.

Prof Rao is really a caring professor. Sometimes he gets fed up with people being lazy or talking over him, but he does try and seems like a nice guy. Class content is hard, and he does not always explain well, but there are chances for extra credit. Come to office hours too!

Prof Rao is a great person overall and I will definitly use what I learned for classes/labs. While at points the class gets confusing like  "maybe " as a answer, I get that things arent always definite. Even in a giant lecture hall he will try and connect with students as a person and even sends congratulating emails if you did well on a test

Professor Rao is extremely understanding and reasonable with personal matters. He will make you feel important even in a class of 900. The class is not very difficult and the homeworks/labs are very easy, and PAL surveys allow for a lot of extra credit. He teaches very clearly and provides many resources.

I really like professor Rao, but some of the ideas here at the end are really confusing. He challenges your thinking, which I really like though, even if it is confusing. This is definitely the course that I learned the most from. Exams are doable, just make sure you study hard and go to office hours every week. don't fall behind!

You are going to sit in this class and wonder if any of this is even real. Here's the answer: literally everything feels made up. This is the only math course that I have ever taken where "maybe" was an answer on the exam. Rao wrote the textbook, so you have to think like him to do well on the exams. At heart, he is a good person and means well.

Rao definitely wants you to succeed, but omg, is he confusing. The exams are nothing like what you do in lecture and assignments. They are 10x harder. He has me feeling like I am in a philosophy class, with making us think "ish-ness" and having "maybe" as an answer. All I gained from this class was to be uncertain about everything.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Rao is a great guy, but his class made no sense most of the time. Lots of the answers to questions will be "maybe" or "it depends" which is annoying. Midterms get harder and harder so be prepared to study. Watch the lectures online and go to office hours for help. If you fall behind on the material, you've gotta hope for a miracle.

He talks quickly during the lectures, and I had a hard time understanding. However, if you do the surveys and study hard for the exams, you will be ok. Most of the content sounds made up and fake. My advice is to hold on and lock in. The only thing that really stuck with me was that compression socks may or may not make me run faster.

Easy A , very good intro course for stats. Lectures are very easy to understand, but most of the information was  intuitive. Few assignments, quizzes were easy, and he gave late credit for a lot of work. Midterm and final were a lot harder and will catch you off guard if you aren't properly prepared. Very sympathetic, understanding, and kind.
PROFESSOR RAO IS THE GOAT!

If you want to talk with a professor like two human beings, If you want a professor who sees you as a person and not just a student, If you want a professor who cares about you and your well being, You want Professor Rao.
Prof. Rao is the best. If you need to take stat 100, take it with him!!

Rao was genuinely one of my favorite professors I had my freshman year--he really cared that his students learned the content. I was sometimes confused so I would go to office hours and that was always very helpful. You can tell he really cares about his work, I have lots of respect for him as a person and educator.

SUPER caring, seems like a great guy outside of class. As a teacher, he was very difficult for me to learn from personally, and I went to almost every lecture. His teaching method was far too slow in the beginning and far too quick at the end imo.
Not an easy class, but it's very clear exactly what you need to do to get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Took his class over the summer and online. If you have the time and need the credit, I recommend it. You definitely will find yourself studying for the exams, but again, if you have the time, it's not too hard.

I'm not a math person and this course was not at all easy, but I liked Prof Rao's book. Some of it was confusing but it also didn't really have a lot of confusing math. Go to every office hours, I started after quiz 2 and wish I went all summer. The TAs are really helpful. Prof Rao also is really understanding and easy to email.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I thought this class was impossible to pass since I failed my first exam, but what had helped was going to office hours with getting that help. Ask questions. I made sure to study the whole week coming up to the exam date. He is a caring professor and if you need to talk to him. Also make sure to do all the extra credit, and there will be curves.
Hard but useful especially if you are in a lab
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: He makes the test much more complicated then it needs to be for a 100 level class. I had to study a good amount for a topic that seems like common sense. I took the online version of the class. Its easy to complete one week worth of homework the day before its due if you are on a time crunch. If your going to take this class you need patience.
Homework included a quiz and practice problems after every lecture and weekly problems and a data set quiz.

Gives LOTS of extra credit and highly recommends reading the chapter before coming to class. In-class lectures are a bit boring, and he rushes through topics at times. He can be nice, he has his moments where he is stern (phones away, no talking, don't do assignments last minute, etc.) The first exam 2 proctorU while the others were CBTF.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: My roommate and I took stats together. She was online, I was in person. She didn't like Prof Rao, but she never talked to him. You have to go to class or office hours and actually talk to him. He is so caring and supportive and will make time to help you. He just has a million students so if you don't talk to him it feels like he doesn't care.
this professor was really amazing! he made stat 100 actually enjoyable and gave a lot of extra credit. prepares you very well for the exam and cares about his students.

If you read the book and come to lecture you will be fine. The difficulty will sneak up on you so you better stay on top of your readings. Lazy students will have an issue with this class, others won't. Simple as that.

Rao is a super chill guy who you can tell is passionate about statistics and making sure his students succeed. I'm not a huge fan of statistics since I'm better with numbers but as long as you read the chapters and do the example problems this class should be a breeze.

I took the online version. Getting an A is definitely possible if you study hard and are good at math. overall the class is useless and as he says, you have to think like him. I didn't really learn anything that wasn't either already common sense or that could be used outside of this class. but a good thing is that that there's a lot of ex credit

Didn't really enjoy the class or find it useful. The second and third exams were quite difficult and I found it super frustrating how we couldn't view our exams or see our originals scores before the curves. It's not difficult to receive an A, you just have to study for it.

Didn't really learn anything and didn't really find anything useful. The second and third exams are quite difficult, but there was a large curve. It's not difficult to recieve an A in the class if you work for it.
He's alright. That is really the best i can describe him as

Professor Rao is the kindest. I did really bad on the second test, so I worked really hard went to every class went to every office hour and got an A on the third exam. And then I get an email from this man congratulating me on my hard work and saying he's proud of me. I low key cried. He's the only professor I've had who makes me feel seen.
Hard class, but I feel like I've learned more from professor Rao than any of my other classes

I hate Rao. It's an online class. But exams are in person? And then he tells me because I didn't follow his instructions he's going to take 10% off my exam if I take it late? And don't even try emailing him, he'll send you a grumpy message about the syllabus saying to email the class email account and not him.
he is alright

Professor Rao is a great professor. I'm taking the online Stats 100 class at the moment. I struggle with stats due to my weakness in conceptual understanding and trick questions, so I might end up with a C or lower. Still, I respect him he genuinely tries to help students. There's also lots of extra credit, so make sure to do them.
I love professor roa. The class is hard, but he does a lot to help students. And it feels really useful. Do all the extra credit. Go to all the office hours. And take good notes.
Soo much better than econ 202. There's a lot of reading and the exams are tough, but there's not so much equations and math which I like. Go to office hours every week and try to work with Rao.
some things are confusing, but there's so much extra credit, and you get unlimited attempts. love the ca's. theyre really nice and helpful
Take stat with Rao! I'm pre-law and didn't think about it would actually help me but turns out asking who's not here and what was compared is helping me on practice LSAT questions!
Amazing lectures he is very passionate
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: If you took AP Stats in highschool, this is nothing like it. This class amazing. Professor Rao truly cares about his students and he really takes the time to make sure everyone is learning and not falling behind. He gives you multiple opprotunites to acheive an A in this class and prepares you very well for the tests. Very easy to get an A!

Rao's class isn't a typical math class, which I loved!! He focuses on big picture ideas. I spent about 4-5 hrs every every week outside of class after midterm 1, reading the book and watching lecture recordings to take good notes. It's worth it though, I learned more from Rao than any of my other classes.

If you aren't a fan of not questions being vastly different on the practice problems than on the exams I'm not sure I would recommend him. He is very caring about his students and take advantage of office hours. However you can't look at your wrong answers on past tests or on any of the practice problems so how do you know what to study??

Professor Rao is the best. You have to work hard, but he makes it interesting and relevant. Every exam has a curve. And he sends you an email at the end of the semester if you get an A to congratulate you! So sweet ❣️

i took this class thinking it would be my easy A. wrong. rao's stats class was a lot of reading and understanding concepts instead of math, which i thought it would be. he is a very caring professor and you can tell that he genuinely wants you to do good, but his class was pretty hard. he does give a lot of extra credit towards the end so do those
I had a terrible semester. Professor Rao was very helpful and supportive. I am very grateful for Professor Rao.
Professor Rao's dedication to students is amazing. He made stats interesting instead of intimidating. He and the TAs also communicated great given how many students there are.

The tests cover material, but the questions are worded in an extremely confusing and overly complicated way. It often feels like the tests are more about deciphering what the question is asking rather than focusing on the content. Additionally, we don't go over problems, and I can't see previous tests. How am I supposed to learn from my mistakes?
Genuinely cares about students so much, literally wants everyone to get an A

I'm only writing because I'm surprised what other people are writing. If you take college seriously, and you want to learn a lot, and are willing to work hard, take Professor Rao. I'm so thankful I had him. The course pushes you, but Professor Rao and the CAs are incredibly supportive. 

If all you want to do is goof off, then don't take the class.
Lectures are so boring and unengaging so be prepared but his grading curve saved me for real. He gets really sassy if you try to leave lecture early so beware. Also study extra hard for midterm 3.

Professor Rao sent me an email congratulating me on getting an A on the midterm. I've never had a professor do that before. He's such a good professor and he's really kind and caring. Take as many classes with him as you can.

Exams are hard but it's useful. I have friend in the other section and they're just doing a bunch of math. We don't do calculations, it's a lot of reading. But I like that. I don't like math. Rao makes stats actually useful.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Would stop his lectures to wait for 600 people to be silent before teaching. Made participation checks for problems worked on individually in class for points, but then wouldn't always go over the correct answers afterward so I couldn't find out what I did wrong.
Stat 100 is my hardest course. I am learning a lot that shows up in my other psych classes, so it is useful. But I didn't think I'd have to work so hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He's good. Not a lot of equations and math.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Rao is the best

Easy class if you actually go to class every day and pay attention. I skipped class a lot and got a C on the first exam. Then I started going to class everyday and got an A on the next exam. Rao is really helpful if you ask questions. But he does expect you to do your work. If you're goofing off in class he'll call you out.

You can tell he genuinely cares about his students and what they are going through..I had a few issues and communicated with him directly about it and he was nothing but understanding and kind, clearly just wanting his students to succeed inside and outside of the classroom. His lectures are engaging and not boring either; highly recommend him!!

easy online class. unlimited attempts on homeworks. kind of tough exams but huge curve. pretty straight forward to get an A. and it's actually useful -- a lot of what professor roa talked about are showing up again in my  other clases
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: im in stat100 right now. his lectures are the best compared to my other intro classes. i like being able to ask the ta questions in class. we just got midterm grades. there was a huge curve. my friends that come to class we all did really well.

Easiest class ever. Professor actually wants you to succeed. So many grade replacement opportunities, I believe he curved every test, everything you need is in the workbook. Lectures are a bit boring but he records them all and doesn't take attendance. Pretty much stopped going in-person after 2 weeks. Just watch the videos to study.
HUGE curve. It moved me B+ to A. Study hard for final. My final replaced my.midterm score. Makes big difference in final grade
Do what he says you should do and you'll get an A
Just finished the summer online course. I thought the midterms were a little tricky, but the curve professor gave was AMAZING. Easy A, and low key kind of interesting.

I made a HUGE mistake and didn't realize our midterm was due in Saturday (I thought it was due Sunday). Professor Rao allowed me to take it on Sunday anyway without penalty. Instead of a C, thanks to professor rao's kindness, I'll be getting an A. I really appreciate how understanding professor Rao was. Plus he's a great instructor
Professor Rao is a great professor. You can tell he really cares about his students.

Professor Rao is the kindest and most generous professor I've ever had. I missed several assignments because of some health flare ups, and he accepted all of them without a penalty, and even assigned one of the TAs to work with me to make sure I did well on the exam. No other professor does that. I'm so thankful I had professor Rao

Rao's lectures are actually low key kinda interesting. Plus he drops your first midterm if you do better on the final.
